### "Chant Anyway" by Srila Bhaktivinod Thakur | Soulful Japa
**Speaker:** HDG Srila Prabhupada

#### chanting, obstacles, holy name
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/N9FXMsAY_50?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "You are making offenses in chanting. Chant anyway."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada quotes Bhaktivinoda Thakur, emphasizing the importance of chanting the Hare Krishna mantra regardless of any obstacles or imperfections in one's practice.

I will put one writing of Bhaktivinoda Thakur. You read that. Then you will see how we should be towards, how our attitude should be towards the general population chanting Hare Krishna. This is Bhaktivinoda Thakur's Sharanagati quoted from Adi Purana. It's called chant anyway. It starts like this: Your mind is wandering all over the universe when you chant. Chant anyway. Your mind is wandering to the past and future when you chant. Chant anyway. You are not able to concentrate on Krishna's names while you chant. Chant anyway. You have no taste for chanting. Chant anyway. You have lusty desires. Chant anyway. You are making offenses in chanting. Chant anyway. You are not praying to Krishna to help you chant better. Chant anyway.

**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada continues quoting Bhaktivinoda Thakur, explaining the unparalleled benefits of chanting the holy name, highlighting its power to grant liberation, peace, and eternal life.

You often chant late at night. Chant anyway. And why, why should you chant despite all the above obstacles? That is why. What is that? There is no vow like chanting the Holy Name. There's no knowledge superior to it. There is no meditation which comes anywhere near it and it gives the highest result. No penance is equal to it and nothing is as potent or powerful as the holy name. Chanting is the greatest act of piety and the supreme refuge. Even the words of Vedas do not possess sufficient power to describe its magnitude. It's continuing chanting is the highest path to liberation, peace, and eternal life. It's a pinnacle of devotion, heart's joyous proclivity and attraction. See it's a joyous heart's joyous proclivity and attraction and the best form of remembrance of the Supreme Lord.

**Summary:** The speaker discusses Prabhupada's statement about remaining in the universe until all his disciples are delivered, contrasting it with atheistic philosophy and recounting an interaction with someone questioning Prabhupada's accessibility.

Actually, in 1969, one devotee asked Prabhupada the question, what happened after the spiritual master departs, because the spiritual master, he says, he will deliver all the disciples. Prabhupada said, "I remain in the universe as long as all my disciples are not delivered." So this idea that Guru is dead and gone, this is Gnastika. You all of you know Hindi or Sanskrit, some Sanskrit, not Gnastika, that means this is atheist philosophy. Some time ago, we had some disputation with some of our brothers in San Diego, California in 1990, and one of such less intelligent people says, "Oh, why don't you send a letter to Prabhupada and tell us what he replies." Actually, this is Aparaj, but the reason we are repeating this is, first of all, that was made directly in front of Aditya Prabhupada, which just shows the ignorance of the people. So my reply is basically, I'm not going to tell you anything. I say, well, why don't you offer Krishna some prasadam, and after 15 minutes, why don't you see if it's still there? So if it's still there, do you conclude there's no Krishna? So all these arguments about living, this is actually due to a poor fund of knowledge.

### 063  Srila Prabhupada is the Summum bonum of all acharyas
**Speaker:** HDG Srila Prabhupada

#### hospitality, Vaishnava culture, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/rgFgDCnVDM0?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Your Mahaprabhu came to this house."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada narrates an experience in Sri Rangam where they were initially denied entry to the temple but were later welcomed by a Brahmin who was a descendant of Gopal Bhattagoswami and a devotee of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu.

What is the essence of all Shastra? The Prophet has taught the essence of all Shastra. One time we went to Sri Rangam. There used to be... Actually, when we first came to Sri Rangam, there was... They had a sign there, 'Non-Hindus Allowed.' There was one life member in Shishadipuram. He sent his car with us. So we went to Sri Rangam, and we did not know we were Westerners. So we tried to go over to Darshanam. He says, 'No, Non-Hindus Allowed. You are Mleches. You cannot go.' The only person who can let you in is this Archaka, this Rangaraj Bhatta. Rangaraj Bhatta at that time was the chief Archaka of the Sri Rangam temple. So where does he live? Since we went there. So we see this Brahmin, big, big Brahmin. Oh, very big. Must have been... He comes out of his house. He says, 'Oh, you have come.' We did not know him. He says, he offered his obeisances. He says, 'Come inside.' First thing we see inside, there's a picture of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. He says, 'What is this picture?' 'Oh, we are descendants of Gopal Bhattagoswami.' 'Really?' 'Oh, yeah. He lived... Your Mahaprabhu came to this house.' Actually, when Chaitanya Mahaprabhu visited Sri Rangam in about 1514, something like that, he stayed in that house to observe Chaturmasya. Very old house. So they worshipped Mahaprabhu. They worshipped Chaturmasya. They worshipped Mahaprabhu on the wall. They are direct descendants of the relatives of Gopal Bhattag. And he told us... Then he says, 'I will show you another deity.' He took us to another little street there. And they have very old Jagannath deities that Chaitanya Mahaprabhu worshipped when he was in Jagannath Puri. So then he sat us down. And he brought us to the temple. And he, of course, he spoke very good Tamil. And he chastised them, chastised the Brahmins. 'No, no, let them, let them.' Then he brought us. And we had the darshan of Lord Ranganath and everything.

So he looked at this. Of course, these pandits, they know Sanskrit very well. And he's looking, he was looking at all the pictures. He says, 'Actually, I want to tell you something.' #### translation, devotional ecstasy, commentaries
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/rgFgDCnVDM0?t=148
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees
**Key Quote:** "These books are my devotional ecstasies."
**Summary:** The Brahmin explains that Prabhupada's greatness lies in his ability to understand and synthesize the commentaries of various Vaishnava acharyas. Prabhupada's translation process is described as a deep analysis of numerous commentaries, resulting in purports that capture the essence of all the acharyas. Prabhupada emphasizes that his books are not just translations but expressions of his devotional ecstasies.

And we asked him, how do you say that? He says, because in order to do this, you not only do you have to know Sanskrit, but you have to know just not the Sanskrit. You have to be able to read all the commentaries of the acharyas. Just what we read today. Actually, when Prabhupada was here a few times, we had the chance at night in 1977 to watch Prabhupada translate the book. And he had this big, big book. It must have been like, it's a huge book that was published 200 years ago. It has all the Sanskrit commentaries of all the Vaishnava acharyas. It has the commentary of Vijaya Advaita. It has the commentary of the Vedic scriptures. It has the commentary of the Vedic literature. It has the commentary of the Madhva Sampradaya. It has the commentary of the Viragavacharya of the Sri Sampradaya. It has the commentary of the Vala Bhacharyas. It has the commentary of the Jiva Goswami. It has the commentary of the Sanatana Goswami. It has the commentary of the Prophet's Guru, Bhaktisiddhanta's commentary, Bhaktivinoda Thakur's commentary. So many commentaries all in Sanskrit. In order to produce these, you know, 25, 26 lines, the Prophet would sometimes take an hour and he's analyzing all of this. And he's giving these purports. So these purports of all the essence of all these Acharyas. Another time, one devotee asked, 'O Prophet, why don't you translate faster? We want to publish more books quicker, quicker.' The Prophet says, 'You do not understand. You do not understand.' He says, 'These books are my devotional ecstasies.'

#### karma, sin, atheism, punishment

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "For such people, the punishment is the most severe."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the severity of punishment for sinful acts depends on the level of awareness and belief in God, with atheism leading to the most severe consequences.

You see, the same act performed by a person who is completely ignorant, the resultant punishment is least severe. And if somebody knows the difference between what is pious and impious activity, still performs the impious activity, then his punishment is more severe. But the most severe punishment is given to one who performs sinful activities out of atheism. I don't care for God. There is no God. There is no next life. For such people, the punishment is the most severe.

### 096  Srila Prabhupada's is parampara understanding of philosophy
**Speaker:** HDG Srila Prabhupada

#### parampara, envy, self-realization, householder life
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/bLUgYC-iRho?t=7
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Prabhupada is giving the parampara understanding."
**Summary:** The speaker explains how Srila Prabhupada translates with the parampara understanding, using the example of 'envious householder' to illustrate the difference between a grahamedhi and a grahasta.

What is the condition of people in general? There are so many subject matters for discussing, but they don't discuss about Krishna because they are blind to self-realization. Why? Because they are attached to happy home, family life. And their time in family life is spent away both day and night in some activities which have no spiritual benefit. The lifetime, Prabhupada translates this verse as the lifetime of such an envious householder. He doesn't say just householder. So when we were translating or getting it translated this English to Kannada, the Bhagavatam, one translator objected to this. He says where in the verse, Sanskrit verse, it is said envious householder. He doesn't say envious householder. The word envy is not used in Sanskrit. Why is Prabhupada telling envious householder? So there is a mistake in the translation or there is some problem here. Actually, Prabhupada translates not just literally always. Prabhupada is giving the parampara understanding. The parampara understanding. You should not forget that. Prabhupada is not just taking the Sanskrit and writing the English. No. He is giving the parampara understanding. The Acharyas explain how a householder who is blind to self-realization, who is attached to family life, is an envious person. Elsewhere, Prabhupada says what is the difference between a grahamedhi and a grahasta? Grahamedhi is explained as an envious householder. Envious householder. Because such a person is busy always with his limited self-interest. What is the self, conception of the self? I am the body and my bodily connections are mine. Aham ameti. This is the conception of the householder who is blind to self-realization, who is materially attached to family life. Therefore such a person is described as an envious householder. So this translation says the lifetime of such an envious householder is passed at night either in sleeping or in sex indulgence. Bhagavatam is very strong.

#### offering, devotion, love
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/OCAkGM6MIsQ?t=0
**Verse References:** BG 9.26
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "What Krishna is really looking at is the feeling with which we are offering, the devotion with which we are offering."
**Summary:** We should always bring an offering to the temple, even something small like a leaf, water, or fruit, but more importantly, it should be offered with love and devotion.

Pravupada also mentions that whenever we go to the temple, we are going to meet the Supreme Personality of Godhead, and we should take something, some gifts or presents or some offering to the Supreme Personality of Godhead. We should never go empty-handed. That is why we see generally in all the temples there is a custom that whenever devotees go, they bring something, some flower, some fruit. Krishna is not expecting a big offering from us. Krishna says in Bhagavad Gita, "You can offer him a leaf, water, or a fruit." These things are very easy to get. We don't need to possess a lot of wealth or money. It is easily obtainable. But more than even this, what Krishna is really looking at is the feeling with which we are offering, the devotion with which we are offering.

#### love, devotion, relationship
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/OCAkGM6MIsQ?t=64
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is our Supreme Father."
**Summary:** Just as we offer the best to our parents out of love, we should offer our devotion to Krishna, our Supreme Father.

Yome Bhaktaya Prayachati. A devotee attitude. An attitude of, yes, he is my Supreme Lord, he is my father. Just like I would like to offer my parents. Generally all of us have this. We offer something very best, something very precious to our parents, right? And we have no issues about that. Rather we will be very happy. We will always be looking for how to offer the best to my father, to my mother, because I exchange a very loving relationship with my father and mother. So in the same way, Krishna is our Supreme Father.

#### offering, ownership, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/OCAkGM6MIsQ?t=95
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Everything belongs to the Lord, and I am only offering it back to the Lord."
**Summary:** Everything we have comes from Krishna, so our offerings are really just returning what already belongs to Him.

And everything that I have got is from my father. So actually our offering to the Supreme Personality of Godhead, in the strictest sense, is not really an offering from us. It is the Lord's own wealth, the Lord's own profit. Everything belongs to the Lord, and I am only offering it back to the Lord. And the Lord also actually is not in need of any offering, per se.

#### love, devotion, offering
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/OCAkGM6MIsQ?t=121
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "He is just looking for that love and devotion."
**Summary:** Krishna is self-sufficient and doesn't need our offerings, but He eagerly accepts the love and devotion with which we offer them, as illustrated by Sudama's offering of chipped rice.

He is self-sufficient. He is Swarat. He doesn't need anything. He has hundreds and thousands of servants, and he has all the opulences. Aishwarya, one of the qualities of the Supreme Personality of God is Aishwarya. He has enough of everything. But he is just looking for that love and devotion. A small little bit of offering that we give to him with love and devotion. He is more eager to take that than anything else. Just like Sudama, he got chipped rice. It is considered to be something very insignificant for Krishna, who is the King of Dwarka. But he relished that because Sudama brought it with love and devotion. So this is how we offer to Krishna, with love and devotion, whatever best we have, we can offer.

### 173  Prabhupada wrote books for us to advance
**Speaker:** HDG Srila Prabhupada

#### service, spiritual progress, disciplic succession, book study
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DMzA1IJ0UuM?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "But the key to success is somehow or other you should take to serve the mission of Srila Prabhupada."
**Summary:** Serving the mission of Srila Prabhupada, especially through studying his books, is essential for rapid spiritual progress and achieving perfection in this life.

It's very important for anyone who is aspiring to achieve success in spiritual life to engage in serving the Lord. Many of you may be coming for many years, some of you may be coming for one month, some may be coming for one year, or some may be coming for 5-6 years. But the key to success is somehow or other you should take to serve the mission of Srila Prabhupada. Srila Prabhupada is a bona fide spiritual master coming in the disciplic succession of Lord Brahma. And this is a rare opportunity in this material world to get a bona fide Acharya like that. And there is a mission of his going on here. So when we are coming here regularly, if we want to make rapid progress in spiritual life, then we have to do service. Coming to temple, having darshan, hearing kirtan, bhajan, honoring prasadam, everything is alright. But if you want to make quick progress, then we should also take up service. Some service, it can be anything. So we should be extremely eager to take up serving the spiritual master. In that way, we will achieve this affinity to hear the message of the Lord. Or else, we will not feel like reading. Prabhupada said, "We have spent many days and nights to write these books for us only. It's for us to read and advance in spiritual life." If we do not read these books, just not reading, we have to realize these things. Only when we completely realize the subject matter explained by Srila Prabhupada, we will be eligible to go back to God. So in order to achieve that complete perfection in one life, we have to begin serving the spiritual master.

### 181  Srila Prabhupada claims that he is the representative of God
**Speaker:** HDG Srila Prabhupada

#### Guru, Representative of God, Spiritual Vision
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jJ7C3r3Tv6c?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So there is no doubt about it that I am the representative of God."
**Summary:** A devotee recounts a dream where he saw a representative of God, and Srila Prabhupada confirms that he is that representative, citing scriptural support for the Guru being a manifestation of God.

There was one conversation, one devotee came to Prabhupada and he said, "They are telling me that you are going to see the representative of God. And when I came to that vision, I saw the person. At that time, I didn't know who he was. But I had some doubts in my mind and I was thinking, 'Oh, that's probably another kind of propaganda.' This was all in the dream. But when I came to that person sitting on the elevated seat in that forest, surrounded by many, many devotees, that person looked at me and proved to me that he was representative of God." So this person, devotee, was telling that I had a dream. And in the dream, there was a representative of God surrounded by many, many devotees. And he looked at me and proved that he was representative of God. But after that, I forgot. Maybe one year or something like that, one or two years after. Prabhupada, then Prabhupada replies, "So there is no doubt about it that I am the representative of God." So this devotee is saying, "I had a dream, representative of God was there, many devotees were there." So Prabhupada says, "There is no doubt about it that I am the representative of God." The devotee says, "Yes, but when I was..." Prabhupada, "Either you dream or not dream, I claim that I am representative." "Because I had a dream and some person was there, something similar to you. So therefore, you are a representative of God." Prabhupada says, "Whether you dream or you don't dream, I am claiming that I am representative of God." Prabhupada says, "Yes, but this thing was just confirming. When I saw you the first time in Amsterdam, you were the same person that I saw in my dream one or two years previously." Prabhupada says, "You have read Chaitanya Charita Amrita, Guru Tattva. So there is clearly said that Guru is the manifestation of God. So it is confirmed. So it is confirmed in the Shastra and all the Acharyas accept it. Yasya Prasadat Bhagwat Prasad."

### 214  What a great service Srila Prabhupada is doing
**Speaker:** HDG Srila Prabhupada

#### Guru, Srila Prabhupada, Deliverance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/RdTxnGCtMyM?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "He is the only hope for us, there is no other link for us between us and Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes Srila Prabhupada's role as a pure devotee who delivers fallen souls from Maya's influence and provides the only link to Krishna through his instructions.

Very tiny children without any energy, we don't have spiritual energy now to follow all the rules and regulations strictly, we are impelled by Maya's illusions and somehow we came in touch with a pure devotee like Srila Prabhupada who has taken the responsibility of delivering anyone who has come in touch with him. Can you see what a greatest service he is doing to the mankind? He has come from the spiritual world to please the Supreme Lord by delivering millions and millions of fallen souls of Kali Yuga for the next 10,000 years. So such a great responsibility Srila Prabhupada has taken. You can just imagine how fallen we are all. We are all coming from all sorts of sinful backgrounds and we are all rotting in this material world. And just by coming in touch with Srila Prabhupada's instructions, his shelter, we are really relieved from Maya's torture and we have a hope of going back to God just by following Srila Prabhupada's instructions. He is the only hope for us, there is no other link for us between us and Krishna. He is the only person because he is the only person who is able to tell us, you do this, you don't do this. Or else if you take scriptures on our own, you will not be able to do this. If you take scriptures on your own and try to read, you will be fully confused. We don't know what to follow and what not to follow, it's totally confusing. So without the help of a spiritual master, it's impossible to go back to Godhead. So it is our duty to somehow or other stick to the instructions given by Guru and then execute it to the best of our ability. In this way we will be able to overcome Maya's influence and we will come back to our normal condition. Thank you.

### Can We Serve Krishna in Our Own Way? | HDG Srila Prabhupada | Bg 1.45-46 | 03.08.2024
**Speaker:** HDG Srila Prabhupada

#### Arjuna's Dilemma, Duty, Compassion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/h8hMfQp6ZFs?t=0
**Verse References:** BG 1.45, BG 1.46
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Arjuna, having thus spoken on the battlefield, cast aside his bow and arrows and sat down on the chariot, his mind overwhelmed with grief."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada reads and explains verses 45 and 46 from the first chapter of Bhagavad-gita, describing Arjuna's reluctance to fight and his overwhelming grief.

OM NAMO BHAGAVATE VASUDEVAYA. Reading from Srimad Bhagavad Gita, Chapter 1, entitled, Observing the Army on the Battlefield of Kuruksetra, Verse No. 45: YADIMAM APRATITARAM ASASTRAM SASTRA PANAYAH DATARASTRA RANIHANYOS TANME SHEMATARAM BHAVET. Translation and Purport by His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Srila Prabhupada: I would consider it better for the sons of Dhritarashtra to kill me unarmed and unresisting rather than fight with them. Purport by His Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Srila Prabhupada: It is a custom according to Kshatriya fighting principles that unarmed and unwilling foe should not be attacked. Arjuna, however, in such an enigmatic position, decided he would not fight if he were attacked. He did not consider how much the other party was bent upon fighting. All these symptoms are due to soft-heartedness resulting from his being a great devotee of the Lord. Verse No. 46. I will read myself. Sanjaya uvacha evam uttvarjunasankhe rathopastupa visat visrajya sasaramchapam sotra samvitnam anasah. Translation: Sanjaya said, Arjuna, having thus fought on the battlefield, tossed aside his bow and arrows and sat down on the chariot, his mind overwhelmed with grief. Perfect. While observing the situation of his enemy, Arjuna stood up on the chariot, but he was so afflicted, he pleaded with lamentation that he sat down again, setting aside his bow and arrows. Such a trained and soft-hearted person in the devotional service of the Lord is fit to receive self-knowledge. I would consider it better for the sons of Dhritarashtra to kill me unarmed and unresisting rather than fight with them. Sanjaya said, Arjuna, having thus spoken on the battlefield, cast aside his bow and arrows and sat down on the chariot, his mind overwhelmed with grief.

---
#### Kshatriya Dharma, Surrender, Krishna's Order
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/h8hMfQp6ZFs?t=192
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "You can violate the laws on the order of Kṛṣṇa. This is surrender."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada explains the rules of kshatriya warfare and how they can be superseded by the order of Krishna, emphasizing the importance of surrender.

yadimam apratikaram asastram sastra-pāṇaya. It is the custom between the kṣatriyas, that in the fighting, if the other party hasn't got weapon to fight, this party will supply him weapon. Note that the other party without weapon and this party will take opportunity to kill him. This is not the rules and regulation of fighting. There are many rules and regulations of the fighting. Not that because he's my enemy, I shall kill him anyway. No. There are rules and regulations. If the enemy has broken his chariot, he's fallen down on the ground, he's been killed. The other party also will immediately get down from the chariot. Suppose he is fleeing, then if one is on the foot and another on the chariot, so he will be in superior position. Therefore the opposite party must also get down from the chariot and walk with him. That means the defeated enemy should be given all opportunities. So he may not think that unnecessarily, in weak position, he has been killed. No. So Arjuna says… Arjuna, of course, did later on. When he killed Karna, Karna was without weapon, and he fell down from the chariot. Karna, after falling down from the chariot, and he was trying to raise the wheel of the chariot from the mud. So Kṛṣṇa asked Arjuna that this is the opportunity to kill him. You kill him immediately. So at that time he had no weapons, and he was trying to kill Karna, and he fell down from the chariot. And in that position he was killed by Arjuna, by the order of Kṛṣṇa. This is violating the laws. So you can violate the laws on the order of Kṛṣṇa. You cannot do. This is surrender.

---
#### Surrender, Truthfulness, Krishna's Pleasure
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/h8hMfQp6ZFs?t=386
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "My principle should be, if Kṛṣṇa is satisfied, then everything is all right."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada continues discussing surrender to Krishna, using the example of Yudhisthira Maharaja and emphasizing that pleasing Krishna is the ultimate goal, even if it appears wrong from a mundane perspective.

When Kṛṣṇa says that you do this, although it is wrong, you have to surrender. You cannot do that. You cannot do that. You cannot do that. You cannot do that. You cannot do that. You cannot do that. You cannot do that. You cannot do it. There is no consideration. Just like in fight the commander says to the soldiers, do this. His duty is to do that. He should not think at that time what is right or wrong. Just like Yudhiṣṭhira Mahārāja… He was advised by Kṛṣṇa that Yudhiṣṭhira, you go to Dronācārya and inform him that your son is dead. It was a false information. Because Dronācārya will not die if he is not affected with some lamentation. So Kṛṣṇa asked Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira that you go, because you were recognized truthful, dharma-rāja. So when he will say this is also false, he will believe. But Yudhiṣṭhira hesitated. How can I tell lie? He disobeyed the order of Kṛṣṇa. And he wanted to become very truthful. For this reason, he had to see hell. So in the mundane consideration, there are so many things, right and wrong. So long you are on the mundane platform, you have to obey all these right and wrong. But in the spiritual world, you have to obey all these right and wrong. But in the spiritual platform, when it is ordered by Kṛṣṇa, He is above all these duality. He is absolute. So even if He says to do something wrong, because He cannot say anything which is wrong, God is all good. If you discriminate God's order from the mundane platform, then you will be misguided. Anyone who has got formed and fixed up faith that whatever God does, whatever God orders, even from mundane calculation it may be wrong, that is right. That is absolute understanding. So those who are in the spiritual platform, for them, there is no such consideration which is right and which is wrong. Their only consideration is whether Kṛṣṇa is pleased. That's all. If Kṛṣṇa is pleased, by inducing me to do something wrong, I will not hesitate to do it. I shall do it. Because it's Kṛṣṇa. It's Kṛṣṇa's order.

---
#### Spiritual Authority, Discernment, Guidance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/h8hMfQp6ZFs?t=589
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "You have to get it confirmed from the transparent medium, spiritual master."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada warns against concocting ideas about pleasing Krishna on the mundane platform and emphasizes the need for guidance from a spiritual master.

My principle should be, if Kṛṣṇa is satisfied, then everything is all right. But while we are in the mundane platform, we should not concoct the idea that Kṛṣṇa is pleased. Let me do that. No. That you cannot do. You have to follow the rules and regulations. But if you think that it is... You cannot think. Neither you can manufacture. So either you should hear from directly, from Kṛṣṇa directly, or that is not possible because we are not in touch with Kṛṣṇa at the present moment. We are trying to see Kṛṣṇa through the transparent medium of spirituality. So we cannot manufacture that this is Kṛṣṇa's desire. No. You have to get it confirmed from the transparent medium, spiritual master. These are the principles.

#### Arjuna's Reasoning, Non-Violence, Krishna's Plan
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/h8hMfQp6ZFs?t=664
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If you think that I don't fight, that they will take the opportunity and kill me immediately, that also I shall prefer. But I am not going to fight."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada returns to the verses from Bhagavad-gita, explaining Arjuna's reasoning for not wanting to fight, even if it means being killed.

So Arjuna says, yadimāṁ apratikāraṁ. Pratikāraṁ apratikāraṁ. Pratikāraṁ means counteraction. And apratikāraṁ means without any counteraction. So he is informing Kṛṣṇa, that if the other party, dhārta-rāśrā, the sons of Dhrtarāśrā, other party, they kill me while I am not prepared, but that is not the regulation of fighting. So therefore he says, apratikāraṁ. If I am not prepared and they kill me, all of a sudden. This is wrong. So I accept, even they kill me, apratikāraṁ, without I am fully equipped. Asastraṁ. In the fight, one must be equipped with all, all weapons. But while I am not equipped with weapons, or I am not willing to fight, because I am not willing to fight, this has been decided. I cannot kill my kinsmen. Therefore, I am not going to take the step of pratikāraṁ, counteraction. So even in this condition, they come and kill me. Yadimāṁ apratikāraṁ asastraṁ. I am not fully equipped with asa-sastra, weapons. Asastraṁ and sastra-pāṇayaṁ, they are fully equipped with weapons. Rās-rās-rās, rane, hanyu. So in this condition, the other party, my cousin brothers, sons of the rās-rās, if they kill me, rane, hanyu, I shall prefer them. I am not going to fight. If you think that if you don't fight, then they will take the opportunity. He is talking with Kṛṣṇa. He is already giving the counter-argument. Kṛṣṇa, if you think that I don't fight, that they will take the opportunity and kill me immediately, that also I shall prefer. But I am not going to fight. But I am not going to fight. But I am not going to fight. But I am not going to fight. This is my decision.

#### Material World, Danger, Counteraction
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/h8hMfQp6ZFs?t=917
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Material world means step by step, simply danger. That is material world. So you have to find out pratikāraṁ, counteraction, how to save yourself from danger."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada explains the concept of 'pratikaram' (counteraction) in the material world, where danger is present at every step, and the need to find ways to protect oneself.

So apratikāraṁ pratikāraṁ. There are two things. Pratikāraṁ means counteraction. Here in this material world, for everything there is counteraction. Just like if you are diseased, so you have to take the medicine. Pratikāraṁ. That is pratikāraṁ. The disease is caused by some, what is called, disarrangement within the body. So we have to arrange, we have to set up the disarrangement within the body. That is pratikāraṁ, medicine, counteraction. Similarly, if somebody is diseased, he has to take the medicine. If somebody is coming to kill you, so you also become prepared to kill him. This is pratikāraṁ. If somebody is drowning in the sea, then you have to give him some help, send some boat or some lifebuoy so that he can be saved. So we have got so many. This material world is going on. It is full of dangers. Yes. Padam padam yata vipadam. Material world means step by step, simply danger. That is material world. So you have to find out pratikāraṁ, counteraction, how to save yourself from danger. Just like when we walk, we see in so many doors, danger. So that you may be, you are warned. Sometimes the doors are, beware of the door. So you have to take care. No, I shall not enter. So everything, there is danger and there is pratikāraṁ, counter, counteraction, how to save yourself from this. This is called struggle for existence. In this material world...

#### Material Existence, Krishna's Purity, Rascaldom
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/h8hMfQp6ZFs?t=1055
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Kṛṣṇa is the purest. So whatever he does, that is pure."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada describes material existence as a cycle of danger and counteraction, contrasting it with the purity of Krishna and criticizing those who misunderstand Him.

In this material world... There is only this danger and counteraction. I am unhappy, so just to become happy, I have to work. I have to get money, whatever I want. So this is going on, pratikāraṁ, danger, vipadam. Padam padam yata vipadam. Padam padam yata vipadam, natesāṁ. So samāsthita ye pada-pallavam plavam. Anyone who has taken shelter of the lotus feet of Kṛṣṇa. Samāsthita ye pada-pallavam plavam. Mahat-padam kurna-jaso murāre. Murāre, murāre means Kṛṣṇa. So purna-jaso is simply glorified by pious activities. There is no impious activities in Kṛṣṇa. Kṛṣṇa is the purest. So whatever he does, that is pure. The rascals, they do not know this. They think Kṛṣṇa is immoral, and he has become a professor, teacher. He does not know, he does not understand Kṛṣṇa. So many big, big professor, learners, scholars, they do not understand what is Kṛṣṇa. And they're talking about Kṛṣṇa. They're taking the position of instructing about Kṛṣṇa. Just see how rascals are.

#### Krishna's Knowledge, Yoga Siddhis, False Claims
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/h8hMfQp6ZFs?t=1176
**Verse References:** BG 7.3
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Both of them are animals."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada emphasizes the difficulty of knowing Krishna, criticizes those who falsely claim to be God, and condemns those who accept such claims.

You do not know something. Perfectly, and still you are talking about it. This is going on. Purna-jaso murāre. What is Kṛṣṇa? Kṛṣṇa says, bholam janmalā mante jñānavāṁ mām kṛpady. We have to know Kṛṣṇa. Because as soon as you know Kṛṣṇa, you become immediately independent. So knowing Kṛṣṇa is not so easy thing. Therefore Kṛṣṇa says, manuṣyānāṁ śahaśreṣu kaścid yatati siddhayaḥ. First of all, you become siddha, perfect. Perfect means perfect knowledge. That is called siddha. And another siddha means very powerful, master of all yogic perfection. That is also siddha. Yogi is perfection, eight kinds of perfection. Manimā, lagimā, siddhi, prapti, yasitā, bhashitā, prāpaṁ mam. So they update eight kinds of siddhis. They are also called siddhas. From material point of view, a perfect yogi can counteract anything, and whatever he likes, he can do. That is called siddhi, astra-siddhi. But still, he is not as siddha as Kṛṣṇa. Kṛṣṇa's name is yogi-śara. He is the master of all the yogis. Yogi-śara, yatta-yogi-śara-kṛṣṇa. The yogis also, they are deficient with Kṛṣṇa's power. Still, some rascals, without any yoga-siddhi, they claim that I have become God. What is your qualification? Have you got all the yoga-siddha? Then how you claim that you are? But rascals, they claim to become God, and other rascals, they also believe that here is God. Both of them are animals.

---
#### Krishna's Power, Animalism, Crazy Claims
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/h8hMfQp6ZFs?t=1352
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "As good as Kṛṣṇa, this is craziness, animalism."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada continues to denounce those who falsely claim to be equal to Krishna, emphasizing Krishna's unparalleled power and condemning such claims as animalistic.

One who does not know actually what is Kṛṣṇa, if you accept somebody, some rascal as God, then he is also animal. That is also animal. One who is claiming that I am as good as Kṛṣṇa, he is certainly animal, crazy. He does not know what is the power of Kṛṣṇa, how much he is yogi-śara. Still, he is not. He is falsely claiming to become Kṛṣṇa, and one who accepts him as Kṛṣṇa, what is this? So Kṛṣṇa, one should understand first of all Kṛṣṇa. Without understanding Kṛṣṇa, if somebody remarks, if he thinks Kṛṣṇa as ordinary, human being, avajānanti māna-mūḍhā, mānuṣeṁ tanum āśritam, that is the mūḍhā's business, rascal's business. Kṛṣṇa, because He comes as ordinary human being, manuṣya-līlā, if we take Him, Kṛṣṇa, as like me, like ordinary, then I am a mūḍhā. How a human child, at the age of six or seven, seven years, one can lift the whole mountain, Govardhana. Then, first of all, try to understand Kṛṣṇa, how much powerful He is. How a three months old child can kill a big demon, kūtanā. These things are there in the Bhāgavatam about Kṛṣṇa's life. How do you claim to become? As good as Kṛṣṇa, this is craziness, animalism.

#### Krishna's Order, Arjuna's Role, Bhagavad-gita's Purpose
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/h8hMfQp6ZFs?t=1456
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Arjuna is playing like that, how this Bhagavad-gītā will come?"
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada explains that Arjuna's apparent bewilderment is a deliberate act to facilitate the revelation of Bhagavad-gita for the benefit of the world.

So Kṛṣṇa's order must be final. If He says, then everything is all right. So here Arjuna's position is, Kṛṣṇa has already arranged the battle, and He knows that Kṛṣṇa wants this battle. Still He says, that yadīvāṁ apratīkāraḥ, He decides in His own way, this is karmāyāṁa. He knows what is Kṛṣṇa's desire, and still He is speaking His own philosophy. That means Arjuna is placing himself on the position of ordinary man. Ordinary man does not know what is Kṛṣṇa. And what is Kṛṣṇa's desire. And he manufactures his own philosophy and thoughts. That is ordinary man. But Arjuna, he is always friend of Kṛṣṇa, constant friend. His name is Gurā-keśa, above all darkness. Certainly he must be one who is Kṛṣṇa's friend, directly. How there can be ignorance? There cannot be. Then why Arjuna is playing that part? He knows Kṛṣṇa's desire. Still He says, that yadīvāṁ apratīkāraḥ, asastraṁ śastra-pāṇayaṁ, and nūṁ tan-veṣeṁ ata-raṁ. He is playing like that. Because without playing, Arjuna is playing like that, how this Bhagavad-gītā will come? This is just like, just like playing on the stage. One very rich man, he is in a drama, he is playing the part of a very poor man. But actually he is not poor man. But in the stage he is playing the part of a poor man. Similarly, Arjuna is not builder. But he is playing the part of a poor man. Similarly, Arjuna is not builder. But for the time being he appears to be bewildered by Kṛṣṇa's māyā. Because Kṛṣṇa wants to speak Bhagavad-gītā for the benefit of the whole world. So what is spiritual knowledge?

#### Counteraction, Krishna's Sanction, Prahlada Maharaja
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/h8hMfQp6ZFs?t=1613
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "That will be also failure if Kṛṣṇa does not sanction it."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada explains that all material counteractions are ultimately ineffective without Krishna's sanction, using the example of Prahlada Maharaja.

Therefore this is a stage arrangement. So, prati-kāraṁ and aprati-kāraṁ. So, prati-kāraṁ and aprati-kāraṁ. This whatever we have manufactured, prati-kāraṁ, counteraction, that will be also failure if Kṛṣṇa does not sanction it. That is realized by Prahlāda Mahārāja. He says, my dear Lord, Hīraṇyakāršipu, that people have manufactured so many prati-kāraṁ, counteraction. counteraction. But tanu-vṛtāṁ those who have accepted this material body they are simply manufacturing things to counteract dangers. But even though they have all these counteracting machines or counteracting agents, still without your sanction these counteracting machine or agent will not be fruitful. Similarly Arjuna is being protected by Kṛṣṇa. So even though Arjuna is not well equipped of prati-kāra, still he will be saved. Because Kṛṣṇa is there. tāvat vibhūtaṁ vṛtāṁ tad-peksitāṇā And even one is well equipped and if Kṛṣṇa does not like to save him, he will not be saved. Always mind this. rāke kṛṣṇa māreke māre kṛṣṇa rākeke This is ordinary. If Kṛṣṇa wants to kill you, nobody can save you. And if Kṛṣṇa wants to save you, nobody can kill you. This is the position.

#### Krishna's Protection, Surrender, Bhagavad-gita Philosophy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/h8hMfQp6ZFs?t=1737
**Verse References:** BG 18.66
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Real pratikāraṁ is Kṛṣṇa."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada concludes by emphasizing that true protection comes from surrendering to Krishna, as taught in Bhagavad-gita.

So the examples I have given by Prahlāda Mahārāja. bāla-śaṇīha-saranaṁ pitarau nṛsiṁha My Lord, Nṛsiṁha-deva, the parents of children, they are not actually protector of the children. Because generally we think that this child, this boy has got his father. Father and mother, he is well protected. No. Their father and mother, every father and mother wants to protect the child, but still the child has to meet some danger and dies. There are so many experiences. Similarly, bāla-śaṇīha-saranaṁ pitarau nṛsiṁha The father and mother is a counteraction for the dangers of the child. But Prahlāda Mahārāja said, no, they are not counteraction. It is not that if a child is in danger, because the father and mother is very strong, very rich, he will be able to give protection to the child. No, that is not possible. Then, nārtyasya-cāgadha The medicine, a person is suffering from some disease, some fatal disease. If you think, I shall engage first class physician and I shall supply first class medicine, if you think then it will be, the patient will be safe. No, that is not possible. That is not possible. We have got many experiences like that. Then, no rich man would have died. Because he has got money, he can employ first class physician, first class medicine, and simply by engaging such things, counteracting, pratikāraṁ. He cannot be safe. There are many examples like that. So the conclusion is, tāvat vibhūtaṁ vṛtāṁ tada pe kṣitānāṁ If you neglect, if you have decided, that this person, this child cannot be safe, then any amount of pratikāraṁ will not help. Will not help. Therefore, real pratikāraṁ is Kṛṣṇa. Real pratikāraṁ is Kṛṣṇa. Counter it. So, better we take shelter of Kṛṣṇa. As Kṛṣṇa says, sarva-dharmān parityajya mām ekaṁ śaraṇaṁ vrajaḥ. You must always know that no counteraction will be beneficial for us unless we are protected by Kṛṣṇa. So therefore, our business is fully surrender to Kṛṣṇa and seek His protection. Kṛṣṇa says, yes, ahaṁ tvā sarva-pāpebhaṁ akṣuṣyaṁ, I shall give you protection. This is Bhagavān's Bhagavad-gītā philosophy. We have to believe on the assertion of Kṛṣṇa that He is prepared to give me all protection provided I have fully surrendered unto His lotus feet. Thank you very much.

### Following the Path of Six Gosvamis | Srila Prabhupada | ISKCON Bangalore
**Speaker:** HDG Srila Prabhupada

#### devotional service, karma, jnana
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/s4RqNWMjb7w?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Devotional service should not be contaminated by jñāna-kāṇḍa, karma-kāṇḍa, by fruitive activities or mental speculation."
**Summary:** Devotional service should not be mixed with fruitive activities or mental speculation, but should be taken in the line of great authorities like the six Gosvāmīs, and is dependent on the mercy of Kṛṣṇa and His devotees, not on pious or impious activities.

You cannot achieve the platform of devotional service by karma-kāṇḍa and jñāna-kāṇḍa processes. Devotional service should not be contaminated by jñāna-kāṇḍa, karma-kāṇḍa, by fruitive activities or mental speculation. Devotional service should be taken exactly in the line of the great authorities. We have to follow the principles of these six Gosvāmīs. That will make us happy. Otherwise, if we take devotional service as one of the pious activities, subha-kriya, that is offense. We should not take devotional service of chanting of Hare Kṛṣṇa mantra equal to some Vedic pious activities, ritualistic ceremony. We should simply follow the path chalked out by the... Devotional service is not dependent on subhā-subha-karmā. It is simply dependent on the mercy of Kṛṣṇa and His devotees.

### Gaura Mandala Parikrama 2024 | Srila Raghunatha Dasa Gosvami: His Pastimes and Legacy
**Speaker:** HDG Srila Prabhupada

#### birthplace, lineage, wealth
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jQgypRuR8Bw?t=0

**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Srila Raghunath Das Goswami's intense devotion attracted the special mercy of Sri Radha and Krishna."
**Summary:** This segment describes the birthplace of Raghunath Das Goswami, his wealthy family background, and the early influences in his life.

So, this is the birthplace of Dasaragunath, who was very much connected to the Paani Hatti festival, and one of the six Goswamis, Rupa Raghunathpade. So this Raghunath, this is the birthplace of Raghunath Goswami. We all know that he was born in a very, very rich family, a big Jamindar's family. So this must have been a big, big palatial bungalow with a lot of things. Daytime you could see the ruins; a lot of ruins are there in this site. Sri Raghunath Das Goswami, one of the six revered Goswamis of Vrindavan, was born in Krishnapur, located in Bandel. So this is the Bandel area, one of the seven villages comprising Saptak Gram. Srila Raghunath Das Goswami's intense devotion attracted the special mercy of Sri Radha and Krishna, who appeared to protect him from tigers at Radha Kund and provided shade during hot days while he chanted his rounds. Srila Raghunath Das, the combined incarnation of the potencies of Hrithi Manjari and Rasa Manjari and Bhanumati Devi, was born into a wealthy Vaishnava family in Krishnapur. He received guidance from stalwart Vaishnavas like Sri Yedunanda and Sri Krishna. He was also the father of a very rich family. In his childhood True Bharat Lauream Lord Yudhuvu Sri Raja of Kriya replied by saying that Sri Yudhunanda Acharya and Balaramacharya were types of...

#### renunciation, devotion, surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jQgypRuR8Bw?t=148
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Raghunath Das' life of austerity and devotion remains eternal."
**Summary:** This segment discusses Raghunath Das Goswami's attempts to renounce, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's advice, his eventual escape to Puri, and his exemplary devotion.

Despite repeated attempts to leave home for Jagannath Puri to accompany the Lord, he was sent back. Chaitanya Mahaprabhu advised him against false renunciation, Markata Vairagya, urging him to engage in befitting renunciation, Yukta Vairagya. Raghunath Das married as per his parents' wish but remained internally devoted to Lord Chaitanya. Raghunath Das harbored a deep longing to reunite with Lord Gauranga. Ceasing an opportunity to escape, he fled to Puri, surrendering to Lord Chaitanya. In Puri, he exhibited exemplary renunciation, pleasing Mahaprabhu who gifted him Govardhanashila and Gunjamala. Raghunath Das' life of austerity and devotion remains eternal. He is an enduring source of inspiration, epitomizing the essence of surrender and devotion to the Lord. While most cannot imitate his extreme renunciation, his example encourages seekers to cultivate deeper spiritual dedication and humility in their own lives.

No, there are sluice gates in the dam. In the dam, controlled manner, water is sent out. Is it not? Similarly, once you take initiation from purification, your devotee, he builds a dam across your karma and he plays with your, he and the chaiti guru, they play with the sluice gates. How much water should come out? That is doubt-tailing. They guide you how to doubt-tail your entire attachments and everything, how to doubt-tail it. So there is a control. #### duty, Krishna's nature, material vision
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Ie-wW_KaeQA?t=193
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is not subjected to your examination or your test; He is above all, He is transcendent."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada explains how Krishna induces Arjuna to fight, the ignorance of those who criticize Krishna, and how to connect with Krishna through chanting His name, emphasizing that Krishna is beyond ordinary understanding.

Especially for you, you are my friend. What people will say, so give up this weakness of the heart and stand up, take courage. You just see how Krishna is inducing Arjuna to fight. People are very much ignorant, and they sometimes criticize that Krishna is exciting Arjuna. He is very gentleman, non-violent, and Krishna is exciting him to fight. This is called jara-darsana. Jara-darsana means material vision. Therefore, shastra says, atasri-krsna-namadi namaved graiham indriyai sri-krsna namadi. We become in touch with Krishna by chanting his name, Hare Krishna. That is the beginning of our connection with Krishna. Namadi, the shastra says, atasri-krsna-namadi. Adi means beginning. We have no connection with Krishna, but if you chant Hare Krishna maha-mantra, immediately our first chance to contact Krishna begins. So it has to be practiced, not that immediately idealize Krishna. That is not, of course, if one is advanced, it is possible immediately. So, shri-krsna-namadi, nam means name. So Krishna, He is not only name, adi, that is the beginning, but form, activities, just like shravanam, kirtanam, glorifying or describing about Krishna. So He has got His form. So, nam means name, and then root means form, nam root lila means pastimes, goma means quality, entourage, His associates, all this adasya shri-krsna-namadi na bhavet na bhavet grāhyam indre. By ordinary senses, we cannot understand either Shri-Krishna's name. We are hearing that our through the oral reception Krishna's name, but if we keep our ear without purification, of course, by hearing, it will be purified. We have to help, help means to avoid the offenses, ten kinds of offenses. So in this way, we shall help the purificatory process. Just like if I want to ignite fire, so I must help the igniting process by drying the wood. It will very soon get fire. Similarly, simply chanting, that will help also. It will take time, but if we avoid the offenses, then it will be very quickly purified. The action will be very quickly there. So this behavior of Krishna, how ordinary man can understand because they have got their ordinary senses. Therefore, they mistake why Krishna. Here He is directly enticing parantapa.

#### duty, leadership, weakness of heart
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Ie-wW_KaeQA?t=611
**Verse References:** —

**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada explains the duty of a Kshatriya to chastise mischief-mongers, contrasting it with modern leniency towards criminals, and emphasizes the importance of following the orders of superior authorities without weakness of heart.

Parantapa, this word is used, that you are a kshatriya, you are a king. Your business is to chastise the mischief mongers. That is your business. You cannot excuse the mischief monger. Formerly, the kings were so, the king himself used to judge. A criminal was brought before the king, and if the king thought it wise, he would take his own sword, immediately cut his head. That was the duty of king. Even 100 years ago in Kashmir, the king, as soon as a thief was caught, he would be brought before the king, and if he is proved that he was a thief, he is stolen, immediately the king will cut off his hands personally, chopped up. Even 100 years ago, he was a thief, even 100 years ago. So all other thieves warned, this is your punishment. If there was no thieving, there is no stealing, no burglary in Kashmir. Even somebody lost something on the road, it will lie down. Nobody will touch it. The order and care for, you cannot touch it. The man who has left it, he would come, he would collect. You cannot touch it, even 100 years ago. So this capital punishment is required. Nowadays, the capital punishment is excused. Murderers are not harmed. This is all mistake. No mercy. Why a human killer, even an animal killer, should be immediately hanged. That is the kingdom. The king should be so strict. So this sympathy is like Arjuna's sympathy. This sympathy now, the state is sympathizing with the murderer, not to be killed. This is Arjuna's, that is, that is not duty. One has to discharge the duty ordered by the superior authority very strictly without any consideration.

#### sense control, spiritual master, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Ie-wW_KaeQA?t=943
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If you cannot control your tongue, if you control your anger, control your mental concoction, then how you can become even spiritual master? That is not possible."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada emphasizes the importance of controlling the tongue, mind, anger, belly, and genitals for spiritual advancement, stating that one who controls these six is fit to be a spiritual master. He explains how the tongue can be engaged in the service of the Lord through chanting Hare Krishna and glorifying Krishna.

And these rascals, they say, no, you can eat whatever you like, it has nothing to do with religion. This is instruction that anyone who has become competent to control the tongue, to control the mind, to control the anger, to control the belly and control the genital, if six kind of control is there, he is fit for becoming spiritual master. He can make disciples all over the world. And if you cannot control your tongue, if you control your anger, control your mental concoction, then how you can become even spiritual master? That is not possible. Pritivimsh sashishyat o anv desh, that is called goshyami. Goshyami or shami. Master of the senses. Master of controlling these six kinds. So beginning is the jiva. Sevan mukhe hi jiva do, samay mein vaspuratt do, seva. The tongue can be engaged in the service of the Lord. How? You chant Hare Kṛṣṇa, always glorify bachan shi vaikunth kunanu varnane. Bachan shi, means talking. Talking is the business of tongue. And tasting is the business of tongue. So you engage the tongue in the service of the Lord by glorifying. Whenever you take a vow that whenever I shall speak, I shall simply speak glorifying Kṛṣṇa, nothing more. That is tongue control. If you don't allow your tongue to speak anything nonsense, gramm katha. We sometimes sit together, we talk so many nonsense. And that should be controlled. Now I have engaged my tongue for the service of the Lord, so we shall not talk anything of sense gratification. This is controlling the tongue. I cannot eat anything which is not offered to Kṛṣṇa. This is controlling the tongue.

### Mahabharata: The History of Greater India | HDG Srila Prabhupada | Bg 2.1 | 05.08.2024
**Speaker:** HDG Srila Prabhupada

#### ignorance, compassion, self-realization
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3AxaWNAZbWU?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Compassion for the eternal soul is real self-realization."
**Summary:** This segment focuses on Sanjaya's description of Arjuna's lamentation and Krishna's role in dispelling ignorance through the Bhagavad Gita, emphasizing that true compassion lies in understanding the eternal soul, not the material body.

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya. In Srimad Bhagavate Gita, Chapter 2, Contained Bhagavate Gita, Summarized, Verse 1, Sanjayo Vacha. Tam Tatha krupaya vishtam, ashrupurna kulekshanam, vishidantam idam vakyam, vacha Madhusudana. Translation in Perpetual Bhai's Divangresh. Translation: Sanjayo said, seeing Arjuna full of compassion and very sorrowful, his eyes brimming with tears, Madhusudana, Krishna, spoke the following words. Perpetual compassion, lamentation, and tears are all signs of ignorance of the real self. Compassion for the eternal soul is real self-realization. The word Madhusudana is significant in this verse. Lord Krishna killed the demon Madhu, and now Arjuna wanted Krishna to kill the demon of misunderstanding that had overtaken him in the discharge of his duty. No one knows where compassion should be applied. Compassion for the dress of a drowning man is senseless. A man fallen in the ocean of nescience cannot be saved simply by rescuing his outward dress, the gross material body. One who does not know this and laments for the outward dress is called a Shudra, or one who laments unnecessarily. Arjuna was a Kshatriya, and this conduct was not expected from him. Lord Krishna, however, can dissipate the lamentation of the ignorant man, and for this purpose the Bhagavad Gita was sung by him. This chapter instructs us in self-realization by an analytical study of the material body and the spirit soul. As explained by the supreme authority, Lord Shri Krishna, this realization is made possible by working with the fruity being situated in the fixed conception of the real self. Thus ends the Bhaktivinoda Purport.

---
#### devotional service, liberation, Krishna's position
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3AxaWNAZbWU?t=255
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "That position is greater than to become one with Kṛṣṇa."
**Summary:** This segment describes the setup of the Bhagavad-gita, with Krishna as Arjuna's charioteer, and contrasts the impersonalists' desire to merge with Krishna with the devotee's desire to serve Him, highlighting that commanding Krishna is a higher position than becoming one with Him.

Now, in the first chapter of the Bhagavad-gītā, it is the setup. Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna, according to the order of Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna, Kṛṣṇa placed the chariot in between the two soldiers, two flanks of soldiers. Śreṇāya-rūbhāya-maddhe rata-sthāpaya me acyuta. So Arjuna was respectful to Kṛṣṇa. Kṛṣṇa has accepted to be charioteer, inferior position than Kṛṣṇa. Kṛṣṇa is on the chariot, sitting on the throne, and Arjuna is sitting on the throne, and Kṛṣṇa has taken the inferior position, driving the chariot. So this is very nice position for devotional service. Those who are not devotee, they aspire to become Kṛṣṇa. Their aspiration is to merge into the existence of the Supreme, or to become one with Kṛṣṇa. But in devotional service, it is not the desire of the devotee to become one with Kṛṣṇa, but sometimes to make, to make Kṛṣṇa as the order-carrier of the devotee. To become one with Kṛṣṇa, it may be a very great position, but to become the commander of Kṛṣṇa, that is another thing.

#### liberation, devotion, material desire
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3AxaWNAZbWU?t=403
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Just pure devotion means without any aspiration of any kind of material gain or even spiritual gain."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the five kinds of liberation, emphasizing that pure devotees do not desire any kind of liberation, material gain, or even spiritual gain, but only desire to serve the Lord, as exemplified by Caitanya Mahaprabhu's prayer.

That position is greater than to become one with Kṛṣṇa. So there are five kinds of liberation, sājūrya. The first liberation is supposed to be sājūrya, means to become one with the Supreme. The Māyāvāda philosopher, monist, they aspire after sājūrya-mukti. But the devotees, Vaiṣṇavas, they do not aspire to become one with Kṛṣṇa. They aspire to become one with Kṛṣṇa. They aspire to become one with Kṛṣṇa. And at that time after sājūrya-mukti, there, for them, there are other four kinds of mukti. Sa rūpaḥ salakkaḥ sāsthiḥ sa mipyaḥ. And those who are still further advanced, they do not want any kind of mukti. Neither of these five points of mukta is… Just like Caitanya Mahāprabhu, He prays, na dhanam na janam na sundariṁ kavitāṁ vā jagad-dīśa-kāmāyam. This is pure devotional prayer. The devotee does not approach the Supreme for any material gain. Just pure devotion means without any aspiration of any kind of material gain or even spiritual gain. Anyāvilāśitā sunyam jñāna-karmāda-anāvṛtaṁ ānukulyena kṛṣṇaṇu-śrīlanam bhakti-uttamāṁ. Uttamāṁ bhakti, first-class bhakti. What is that? Anyāvilāśitā sunyam. Without any kind of desire than to serve the Lord.

#### transcendental form, material conception, nirakara
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3AxaWNAZbWU?t=553
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When we use this word nirākā, that means He is not anything of this material world."
**Summary:** This segment explains that Krishna, Vishnu, and Narayana are transcendental and not of this material world, clarifying that 'nirakara' means the Lord's form is not material, but He possesses a transcendental form (sac-cid-ananda-vigraha).

And in the Śrīmad-Bhāgavatam also it is said, sabhi-puṁśāṁ paro dharmaḥ. Paro means transcendental. Beyond this material conception, Kṛṣṇa or the Absolute, Nārāyaṇa, that is paro, Nārāyaṇa-paro of bhakti-āyam. Nārāyaṇa is not anything of this material world. Nārāyaṇa, Kṛṣṇa, Viṣṇu, the Absolute Personality of Godhead, He is not anything of this material world. When we use this word nirākā, that means He is not anything of this material world. But He has got His form, that is transcendental form, sac-cid-ānanda-vigrahaḥ. Nirākār means He has no such form as we have got this material form. This material form is neither of the three transcendental bliss, sac-cid-ānanda-vigrahaḥ, this is asat, acit, and nirānanda-vigrahaḥ. This body, this material body is asat, acit, and nirānanda-vigrahaḥ. Therefore when in the Vedic literature on an authorized statement we find nirākār, that means His form does not belong to this asat, acit, or nirānanda-vigrahaḥ.

Īcchā means desire, and deśa means envy, enviousness. ícchā deśa samad-tena. When we become envious of Kṛṣṇa and we want to enjoy the good of the average Muslims, we cannot en possession of radiance. enjoy this material world, then we come to this material creation. Icchād deśa-samuttena sarve jānti paraṇṭhaḥ. So any one of us who is in this material world.

#### body identification, ignorance, animal life

**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** This segment explains that Arjuna's distress in the battlefield stemmed from identifying himself with his body and family relationships, a state of ignorance likened to animal life, where one is attached to the material body.

So that thing happened to Arjuna in the battlefield. He identified himself as the body. He thought himself that he belongs to the Kuru family, and his family relatives, his other side, his brother, nephews, or his grandfather. So he refused to fight. My dear Kṛṣṇa, after placing the chariot between the two parties, senaya rubhayor madhye rata-sthāpaya meyocchuta, and then he became very much disturbed. He thought, I have to kill the other side, my brother and my nephews, my grandfather. No, no. Kṛṣṇa, I cannot. This is not possible. I shall not fight. This is the stage of Bhagavad-gītā. Kṛṣṇa became very much dissatisfied. Of course, Arjuna played the part of a conditioned soul. A conditioned soul is under the impression that he is the body. That is animal life. In the śāstra it is said, anyone who is identifying himself with this material body, he is animal, go-kharā. Go-kharā. Sa eva go-kharam. Go means cow, and kharo means ass. Yaśyātma-buddhi-kunapeti-dhātoke sadhik-kalatra-disubhau maiyyadhi Ya tīrtha-buddhi-salilena karhiśe janesu abhignesu sa eva go-kharam Yaśyātma-buddhi-kunapeti-dhātoke sadhik-kalatra-disubhau maiyyadhi Anyone who is identifying another one, another one, another one, this himself, with this body, which is made of tri-dhātu.

**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the importance of presenting the Bhagavad-gita as it is, without misinterpretations or whimsical concoctions, and explains that interpretation is only necessary when things are not clearly understood.

Now, sañjaya-vāca. The sañjaya is speaking to Dhritarāja. So further on he says, smile. Well, it is clear for each giving anything in this life, isn't it? Shine. And he is smiling. And if there is any—where is he? Next. Didn't you come from the Arjuna comme nellazya? Why, you have come so far. You have come from home to Arjuna. Have you ever been in your home? Have you ever seen a felt-alive child? That is the personal application. You have no prior experience, even then? Yes. Well, that is not the matter. We are all capable of them. blind, and his secretary, Sañjaya, he was speaking. He was seeing the activities in the battlefield. Dhrtarāṣṭa inquired from him, dharma-kṣetre, kurukṣetre, sama-vetā-yuduṣyavata, māmakaḥ pāṇḍavāś caiva kim akūrvata-sañjaya. He was asking Sañjaya, what did they do? Kim akūrvata-sañjaya? That was the question. And first of all, Sañjaya described the arrangement in the battlefield, and then he's speaking. Now, sometimes Bhagavad-gītā is misinterpreted that these battle, I mean to say, dharma-kṣetre, kurukṣetre, or means this body. We do not misinterpret in that way. There is no question of misinterpretation. We are presenting Bhagavad-gītā as it is. We do not change by our whimsical imagination, concoction. We do not interpret the words of the Bhagavad-gītā according to our own desire. No. Actually, from literary point of view, interpretation is required when things are not understood very clearly.

And this Mahābhārata, Mahābhārata means the history of greater India. That is Mahābhārata, history. It is history. It is not a fiction. It is history. Mahābhārata. This planet was formerly known as Bhārata-varṣa. This is Mahābhārata. This planet, the whole planet, not that the piece of land, as we are calling now Bhārata-varṣa, no. Before that, this planet was known as Ilābhati-varṣa. And after the reign of Mahārāja Bharata, the son of Viśavadeva, this planet became Bhārata-varṣa. So Bhārata-varṣa means the whole planet. But we have laws. Just like we have lost a portion of the present Bhārata-varṣa as Pakistan. Everyone knows. Twenty years before, there was no such thing as Pakistan. But circumstantially, we have lost. So the whole Bhārata-varṣa has been partitioned as this portion is called America, this portion is called Europe, this portion is called, this is called Asia. These are modern names. Actually, the whole planet was Bhārata-varṣa. And the whole planet was being controlled by Vedic culture. So as we have lost our Vedic culture, as we could not control the others, other people in other part of the world, by our culture, by our political manual.

Another thing, the dharmakṣetra, effect of dharmakṣetra, was visible in Arjuna. Dharmakṣetra. He, because he is devotee of Kṛṣṇa, yasyāsthi bhakti bhagavati akañcanaḥ sarvai guṇaitatra samāsati siddhā. Because he is devotee of Kṛṣṇa, therefore he felt, oh, what is this? Why is there a fight? Why is there a fight? Why is there a fight? Why is there a fight? Why is there a fight? Why is there a fight? Why is there a fight? I killed this, my brothers. Because he was devotee. This sentiment came into the mind of Arjuna, not on the other side, Duryodhana. He never thought. Although they were placed, both of them placed at dharmakṣetra, the effect of dharmakṣetra was manifest in the body of Arjuna, not Duryodhana. This is the... If one is pure, then the effects of dharma becomes manifest very quickly. Kṛṣṇa says that those who are too much sinful, sinful, simply their life is sinful, duṣkṛtina, duṣkṛtina, kṛti, kṛti means one who has got good brain. But duṣkṛtina, but the brain is applied for mischievous activities. For mischievous activities, it also requires good brain. And similarly, for pious activities, that also requires brain. But those who are applying their brain for mischievous activities, they are called duṣkṛtina. So duṣkṛtina, na māṁ duṣkṛtu na mūḍhā. Why? They do so because they are mūḍhā, rascals. If one has got good brain, he should apply for good work. But sometimes they are utilized. Just like a thief, he has got good brain. A rogue, he has got good brain. But he is applying for mischievous activities, for making people unhappy. That is not the right use. Jñāna-khala. They are called jñāna-khala, one who has got nice knowledge. It should be utilized for better purpose. So the effect of kuru-kṣetra, dharma-kṣetra, was visible in the person of Arjuna, not in the person of Duryodhana. That is the difference.

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** This segment explains that Arjuna's emotional distress was due to his compassion as a devotee, contrasting it with the material world's envy, where people are unhappy to see others happy.

Therefore, he was crying. So I am put in such a position that I have to fight and I have to kill my brothers, my nephews, my grandfather. He was too much affected. Although it is weakness, but it is not actually weakness, it is compassion. Arjuna was not a coward, neither he was less heroic than the other side, but out of compassion, because he was devotee. Devotees, they are para-duḥkha-duḥkhi. The symptom of a devotee is they are unhappy by seeing others unhappy. That is the symptom of devotee. Generally, person, if he sees somebody happy, he becomes unhappy. Matsarata. That is the world situation. If I see my brother, he is very happy. He has improved in his material condition. Then I become unhappy. He has advanced so much, and I could not do so. This is material situation. Materials are envious. Griha-medhi. Everyone is envious. Either you take person to person or neighbor to neighbor, their sympathy is lip-sympathy. Actually, everyone is envious. Businessman to businessman, nation to nation, this is material world. Therefore spiritual advancement means for person who is not envious, not envious. Parama nirma-sarāṁ satāṁ vāstyava-vaṣṭu-vedyaṁ. In the Sema-bhāgavatam it is said that if you want to know the real truth, vāstyava-vaṣṭu, then one must become parama nirma-sarāṁ, nirma-sarāṁ. Matsara, matsara means envious, and nirma-sarāṁ, not envious. And parama, first-class nirma-sarāṁ.

It is not for the persons who are entangled in cheating religious system. Cheating, dharma-kaitava. Kaitava means cheating. So cheating type of religious system are kicked out from this book, Sema-bhāgavatam. But it is meant for persons who are not envious. Parama nirma-sarāṁ satāṁ. Vāstava-bhāstu, one who wants to learn real reality, not false reality. Here in this material world, everything is false reality. Just like we are trying to find our water in the desert. That is the example, my race. False, there is no water. But the animal, he sees that there is water, fast water, and he runs after it and dies. So here in this material also world, every one of us running after the false mirage. There is happiness, there is happiness, there is happiness. This is called material condition. And we are envious. Yes, this is the position. And therefore Kṛṣṇa begins.

#### Bhakti-rasa-amrita-sindhu, Spiritual Bliss, Material Enjoyment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TJTn0IJoxRE?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "That is the difference between spiritual bliss and material enjoyment."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada introduces the Nectar of Devotion, explaining it as an ocean of mellows (rasa) that is unlimited and ever-increasing, contrasting it with the limited and ultimately unsatisfying nature of material enjoyment.

Today we have a class by Sthivayangreshwara Prabhupada on Nectar of Devotion. It's divided into four parts, just as the ocean is sometimes divided into four parts. And there are different sections within each of these four divisions. Originally, in Bhakti Rasam of the Siddhari, the ocean is divided like the watery ocean, to east, west, north and south, while the subsections within these different divisions are called waves. As in the ocean, there are always different waves, either on the eastern side, the western side, the northern side or the southern side. Most similarly, Bhakti Rasam of the Sindhu… That Bhakti Rasam Brta Sindhu, the Sanskrit word, we have shortcut the translation, Nectar of Devotion, but the Rupa Gosvami's purpose is to present the ocean of Bhakti Rasam. Rasam means mellow, the juice. It is like an ocean. This ocean has limitation, but that is unlimited. Ānandā-buddhi-vardhanam. Caitanya Mahāprabhu said, this ocean is increasing. Now, this material… This material ocean, it has got limit. It cannot increase unlimitedly. That is not possible. Otherwise, how we could build? This house is on the beach. No. It is ordered not to come forward beyond this line. But the spiritual bliss, that ocean, is increasing. Ānandā-buddhi-vardhanam. That is the difference between spiritual bliss and material enjoyment.

ānanya-cinvaya-rasa-pradibhāvitābhi stābhir ya yeva-nija-rūpa-tayākalābhi Kṛṣṇa's enjoyment. It means Kṛṣṇa's expansion. Just like in Bhāgavata, you know, when Kṛṣṇa's cowherd boys, calves and cows were stolen by Brahmā, Kṛṣṇa again expanded Himself in the similar forms, so that the mother of the cowherd boys, the mother of the calves, they may not feel there is separation. Kṛṣṇa immediately replaced. That is Kṛṣṇa. So the blissful enjoyment with Kṛṣṇa, it is unlimited. That is called bhakti-rasa-amṛta-sindhu. Rasa and amṛta. Amṛta, that is not dying. That is not possible. Formation of material enjoyment. It is not possible. But because we are preaching Kṛṣṇa consciousness, there is still a persistence. Yes. This is the difference. You will feel more and more enthusiasm. In material world, the more you work, you feel tired. And the spiritual world, the more you work, you will feel enthused. That is called ānandāmbudhi-vaddhanam. That is the test. If our workers are feeling tired, then that means he is working material. And if he is feeling enthused more and more, that is, he is working spiritually. That is called ānandāmbudhi-vaddhanam. That is the spiritual test.

Vara-mante yogina anante satyānande cidātmane. That is cidātmane. That is spiritual, not material. So here we are trying to enjoy materially. So how we can be happy? That is not possible. Materially we cannot be happy. Therefore Bhagavad-gītā gives us indication, sukhaṁ ātyantikam yat tat atindriyat grāhyam. If we want happiness, happiness is our priority, every individual soul, happiness. Because Kṛṣṇa is sac-cit-ānanda-vigrahaḥ, eternal, blissful ānanda, ānanda-blissful, citta, knowledge, and sac-icārya. That is Kṛṣṇa. Sac-cit-ānanda-vigrahaḥ. Īśvaraḥ paramaḥ kṛṣṇa sac-cit-ānanda-vigrahaḥ. Vigrahaḥ means four, but not this four. Those who have no knowledge about Kṛṣṇa, they are thinking Kṛṣṇa is māyā. These bhaktas, they are worshiping the māyā form. Their philosophy is when Kṛṣṇa comes, when God comes, He takes a material form. No, that's not true. Kṛṣṇa said, sambhavāmi ātma-māyayāṁ, sambhavāmi ātma-māyayāṁ, the ātma-māyayāṁ means chit-potency. These Māyāvādī philosophers, they have no information that chit-potency. That is Allādhina-śakti. That is Rādhā-nāyā. They have got experience of this material potency, external energy, durgā. But they have no information of the chit-potency.
