#### forms of life, evolution, human life
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/SqSgsKehYQI?t=455
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "So by gradual evolution we are coming to the human form of life."
**Summary:** Living entities come in different forms through perspiration, eggs, embryo, and seeds, and by gradual evolution, we come to the human form of life.

Seda-ya, udvija, and jarā-yuja, and anda-ya. Seda-ya means by perspiration, through perspiration. That is like bed bugs. Bed bugs, because if you keep your bed nasty, do not keep it very clean, then by your perspiration there is generation of these bed bugs. They are called seda-ya, by perspiration. And anda-ya, through the eggs, just like the birds, the living entities coming through the eggs. That is called anda-ya. Seda-ya, anda-ya, jarā-yuja, embryo. Just like we are coming from the embryo, anda-ya, jarā-yuja, and udvija. Coming from the seeds, the trees, plants. So within these four groups, all different living entities are coming. So by gradual evolution we are coming to the human form of life.

#### relationship with God, duty, aim of life

**Audience:** Newcomers, General public

**Summary:** In human form, there is a chance to know God and our relationship with Him, which leads to the execution of duty and the ultimate aim of life, which is to go back to Godhead.

So in the human form of life, here there is a prerogative, there is a chance to know what is God. And what is my relationship with Him. That is called sambandha-jñāna. Sambandha-avideya. Then execution of the duty. Just like sambandha, we can understand, an unmarried girl, an unmarried boy, there is first of all sambandha. The father-mother makes the relationship. Then there is function between husband and wife. That is called avideya. And why? No, because there is a necessity. What is that? To get children. Sambandha, avideya, preya-ja. Preya-ja. Every sambandha, every relationship is made, every action is done with the aim, the goal, the preya-ja. So Vedic literature means sambandha, avideya and preya-ja. That is to be studied in the human form of life. Vedaisya sarvair aham eva vedyam. To study Vedas means to understand what is my relationship, what is Kṛṣṇa, what I am, and what is my relationship, and how to act in that relationship, and what is the aim of life. The aim of life is to go back to home, back to garden. That is aim of life. We are fallen in this material condition of life. You are suffering. But we do not know. We are so full, just like animal. We do not know what is the aim of life. Aim of life, that is also described in the Bhagavad-gītā.

Jarma-mṛtyu-jarā-vyādhi-duḥkha-dūśānudarśanam. When we can understand that this process of repetition of birth, death, old age and disease, this is not wanted by me, nobody wants to die, but the death is forced upon him. He does not think that this is my problem. I do not want to die, but death is as sure as anything. So this is the problem. Nobody is careful how to solve this problem. They are simply engaged in the, I mean to say, temporary problems. The temporary problems are not problems. Real problem is how to stop death, how to stop birth, how to stop old age and how to stop disease. That is real problem. That can be done when you are liberated from this material world. This is our problem. So Kṛṣṇa comes here again. Yadā yadā hi dharmasya jñāni bhavati bhārata. Dharmasya jñāni. Jñāni means when it is distorted. So people are manufacturing in the name of so-called religion. This is our religion, this is Hindu religion, this is Muslim religion, this is Christian religion, this is Buddha religion, and this is Sikh religion, this is that religion, that religion. They have manufactured so many religions, so many religions. But real religion is dharmanta-sākṣād bhagavat-pranam.

#### religion, surrender, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/SqSgsKehYQI?t=760
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Religion means the codes and the laws given by the Lord, given by God. That is religion."
**Summary:** Religion is made by God, and God's religion is to surrender to Him and become a devotee, and any religion without the conception of God is a cheating religion.

Religion means the codes and the laws given by the Lord, given by God. That is religion. Simple definition of religion is dharmanta-sākṣād bhagavat-pranetam. Just like law is given by the state, by the government. You cannot manufacture law. I have repeatedly said. Law is made by the government. Similarly, religion is made by God. If you accept God's religion, then that is religion. And what is God's religion? If you stand, you can stand here. Other people are sitting. God's religion is, you'll find in the Bhagavad-gītā, sarva-dharmān parityajya mām ekaṁ śaraṇaṁ vraja. This is God's religion. You give up all this nonsense religion. You become a devotee, a surrendered soul unto Me. That is the religion. One who does not know what is God, one who does not know how to surrender to Him, he is not religious. Any religion without the conception of God, without knowledge of God, without knowing the surrendering process, that is called, described in the Śrīmad-Bhāgavatam as cheating religion, dharma-projjhita-kaitava-atra. The so-called religious system, which is cheating only, that kind of religion is completely thrown away, kicked out. Because religion means to develop your dormant love for God or to execute the laws of God. That is it. The laws of God is, Kṛṣṇa says, sarva-dharmān parityajya mām ekaṁ śaraṇaṁ vraja.

And how to achieve that? That also Kṛṣṇa said. man-manā bhava-bhaktah mad-yājī māṁ namaskuru. Four principles only. Only. Always think of Me, Hare Kṛṣṇa. If you chant Hare Kṛṣṇa, then you are remembering Kṛṣṇa. Man-manāḥ. Hare Kṛṣṇa, Hare Kṛṣṇa. This is the religion, at least of this age. Hare nāma, Hare nāma, Hare nāma eva kevalam kalau nāsteva nāsteva nāsteva gati hraṇyata. There is no other way. You chant, then any scripture will find. Sabdyād anāvṛtti, in the Vedānta-sūtra. Sabdyād, simply by chanting, vibrating, you become liberated. In the Śrīmad-Bhāgavatam, kīrtanā deva kṛṣṇaṣya mukta-saṁha-paramprajeta kalau dosa-nidhe rājanaḥ asti yeku mahān guṇam. Śukadeva Gosvāmī said, Mahārāja, forgive. This age of Kali is full of faulty things. It is a ocean of faulty things. But there is one profit, benefit. What is that? Kīrtanā deva kṛṣṇaṣya mukta-saṁha-paramprajeta. If you chant the holy name of Kṛṣṇa, then you become liberated and you are promoted to the spiritual world. Paramprajeta. That is our age.

Purāṇa means very old. na jāyate na abhryate vāt kadācit nitya-śāśvatayam purāṇa. We have to accept that purāṇa. We are purāṇa, eternal. na hanyate hanyamāne śarīre. We are not destroyed simply by destruction of this body. We remain. We accept another body. tathā dehāntara-prāptir dhīras tattva-numayate. This is Bhagavad-gītā. Nobody wants to know this.

They manufacture their own commentary. That way you will never understand. You will understand as Kṛṣṇa says. sa eva ayam purātanaḥ. The same thing. What is that same thing? That I am God. I am Kṛṣṇa. You are my part and parcel. This is eternal relationship. It cannot be broken. But you have forgotten. You have forgotten your relationship with Kṛṣṇa. You have made your relationship with your family, so-called family, so-called country, so-called society, and so on, so on, so on, so on. This is all temporary. Suppose I am Indian today. You are American today. But is there a guarantee after your death you will become American or I will become Indian? Or I will take my birth in the same family? No. According to my karma, I may become cats and dogs. You may become demigods. You may become something else. But dehāntara-prāptir. Dehāntara-prāptir, you will have to accept another body. And there are 8,400,000 species or forms of body. Any of them you will have to accept. Prakṛteḥ kriyamārāṇi guṇaiḥ karmaṇi sarvaśaḥ. You are wasting your time as the family member or as a national or this or that. But there is no guarantee that next life we will be same countrymen or same family. No, there is no such guarantee. Dehāntara-prāptir. You will have to accept one body.

#### karma, temporary problems, real problems

**Summary:** According to your karma, you will have to accept one body out of 8,400,000 species, and you are wasting your time with temporary problems instead of focusing on the real problems of death, birth, old age, and disease.

And that body means any out of these four, according to my karma. karmaṇā daiva-natreṇa yanto deha-upapatti. So these are the instructions of the śāstras. And this is very scientific and very important thing. But unfortunately you are not taking care of these things. You are very busy with the temporary problems. Temporary problems are not problems. That is also stated in the Bhagavad-gītā. āgama-pāina anityātāṁ titikṣaśya bhārataḥ mātrāś paraśāstha-kaunteya śrī-daśna-śukha-dakkha-dāntaḥ. Your temporary problems, so-called happiness and distress, these are āgama-pāina. They come and go. That is not very important. Just like the seasonal changes. The winter season comes and again goes on. For the time being you may feel very cold, but it will go. Similarly, summer season also. It comes and goes, any seasonal changes. We should not be disturbed with the seasonal changes of happiness and distress. We should be very serious about the problems of life. janma-mṛtyu-jarā-vādi. That is spiritual advancement. Unless we come to the serious point, unless you have become very intelligent, that I do not want to die. Why death is there? This is intelligence. You may solve your temporary problem, but you cannot by your so-called materialistic activities. The problem of death. Nobody wants to die.

mṛtyu-sarva-harasya-haṁ. Death will come. But the people have become so much unintelligent, they do not take up seriously the real problems. Just like animals. This bag of three elements, kava-vitva-vayu, if I think that I am this body and the comfort of this body is my happiness, sa eva go-kharo, you are no better than animal. This is animal conception of life. You must know that I am not this body. I am spirit soul. Circumstantially I have been put into this body, and again I will be put into another body. #### devotion, definition of bhakti, hearing and chanting
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/SqSgsKehYQI?t=1452
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Bhakti means ānukulyena kṛṣṇa-anusilaṇaṁ anyā vilāśitā sunyaṁ jñāna-karmādana-vṛtaṁ ānukulyena kṛṣṇa-anusilaṇaṁ bhakti-vṛttamaḥ"
**Summary:** Bhakti is defined as favorable cultivation of Krishna consciousness, and it involves hearing and chanting about Krishna, which is also kirtana.

Bhakti. Bhakti means ānukulyena kṛṣṇa-anusilaṇaṁ anyā vilāśitā sunyaṁ jñāna-karmādana-vṛtaṁ ānukulyena kṛṣṇa-anusilaṇaṁ bhakti-vṛttamaḥ. This is the definition given by Rūpa Gosvāmī. Similarly, in Nārada-pañcarātra, the bhakti is defined. sarva-pādhi-vinirmaktaṁ tat-paratyena nirmanam rīśi-keṇa-rīśi-keśa-sevanam bhaktir ucyate. These are the definition of bhakti. Bhakti, without bhakti, nobody can become bhakti. Bhakti means one who is executing bhakti. That is bhakti. And the beneficiary, the beneficiary is the Bhagavān. So those who are engaged in this process of bhakti, sarva-nām kīrtanaḥ Viṣṇu, Viṣṇu means Bhagavān, or Kṛṣṇa. So sarva-nām kīrtanaḥ, you have to hear about Kṛṣṇa. That is sarva-nām. And kīrtanaḥ, one has to chant, This is also kīrtanaḥ. I am speaking to you. This is also kīrtanaḥ. When you are hearing, this is bhakti. Sarva-nām kīrtanaḥ, about Kṛṣṇa. The chanting, dancing about Kṛṣṇa, this is bhakti. Sarva-nām kīrtanaḥ, Viṣṇu.

#### remembering Krishna, temple worship, seeing God
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/SqSgsKehYQI?t=1548
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "There is no difficulty to see God."
**Summary:** Remembering Krishna involves fixing our minds on His lotus feet, which can be facilitated through temple worship, and there is no difficulty in seeing God if you want to see Him.

Smaraṇaṁ, and remembering about Kṛṣṇa. Man-manā bhava-bhakti. If you engage your mind, savai manaḥ kṛṣṇa-padāravindayaḥ we have to fix up our mind always to the lotus feet of Kṛṣṇa. So if you do not accept this object, this temple worship, how you can become always be engaged in the lotus feet of Kṛṣṇa? These are the processes. These are not sentiments. This is the scientific process. If you want, your business is to become bhakti, that is perfection. And because Arjuna was bhakti, therefore Kṛṣṇa is speaking to him. So if you become, it is not monopolized by Arjuna. If you also become bhakti, Kṛṣṇa will speak to you. Now he says, where is God? Where is God? Here is God. God is speaking. Why don't you see God? God is speaking. You'll find in the seventh chapter, if you cannot see God, if you cannot see Kṛṣṇa in the temple, if you cannot see Kṛṣṇa within Bhagavad-gītā, then Kṛṣṇa says, raśo'haṁ apsukam teyaḥ prabhāsaṁ miṣaśa-śūryaḥ. You see while you drink water. Or anything you drink, you can see Kṛṣṇa there. Raśo. That taste is Kṛṣṇa. So there is no difficulty to see God. These rascals, they said, where is God? Here is God. You are drinking water. Why don't you see? Give me water. So Kṛṣṇa can be seen always, provided you want to see God. But you don't want to see God. That is the difficulty. Otherwise you can see.

#### saintly person, sinful activities, leadership
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/SqSgsKehYQI?t=1674
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "If you become saintly person, but if you remain rogues, thieves, cats and dogs, then how you can see God?"
**Summary:** To see God, one must become a saintly person by giving up sinful activities such as illicit sex, meat-eating, intoxication, and gambling, and sinful leaders cannot lead people properly.

śrīlaḥ śaṅtaḥ svartho svarthaḥ. In the Brahma-saṅgita it is said śantaḥ śrīlaḥ svartho svartho śāntaḥ. If you are śaṅtaḥ śaṅtaḥ means saintly person. If you become saintly person, but if you remain rogues, thieves, cats and dogs, then how you can see God? You have to become a santah. Premāñjana-carit-bhakti-vilocanena santah sadaiva hṛdayeṣu viloka-yajña-jaṁśāva-sundaraṁ acintakunasya-rūpaṁ govinda-mādi-puruṣaṁta-māṅgayaḥ. So you have to become santah. If you remain always addicted to sinful activities, how you can see God? That is not possible. You have to give up this, just called sinful activities. What are the sinful activities? Illicit sex, meat-eating, intoxication and gambling. That's all. You have to give up this. If you become leader, so-called leader, rascally leader, and you are addicted to illicit sex and intoxication and meat-eating, you must be seated. If you are sinful, how you can lead person?

### Srimad Bhagavatam I Srila Prabhupada I SB 1. 2 .5 I 27.11.2023
**Speaker:** HDG Srila Prabhupada

#### Kṛṣṇa-kathā, peacefulness, relief
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CPaYBnLBlsg?t=30
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The world is like the blazing fire in the forest, so this Kṛṣṇa-kathā, this Kṛṣṇa consciousness movement, will give them relief."
**Summary:** Engaging in Kṛṣṇa-kathā, talking and questioning about Kṛṣṇa, brings peacefulness and relief from the blazing fire of the world.

So, questioning about Kṛṣṇa and answering the question is Kṛṣṇa-kathā. So here it is recommended that if we are constantly engaged in Kṛṣṇa-kathā, about Kṛṣṇa, talking about Kṛṣṇa, questioning about Kṛṣṇa, then ātmāṣu prakṛtati. We are hankering after peacefulness of our heart, peacefulness of our atmosphere. So here it is recommended that simply by inquiring about Kṛṣṇa and taking answer of the question, both the questioner and the answer-giver, both will be pleased. Jaya ātmāṣu prakṛtati. Therefore Caitanya Mahāprabhu ordered, yāre dākṣa tāre kaha kṛṣṇa-apare. The world is like the blazing fire in the forest, so this Kṛṣṇa-kathā, this Kṛṣṇa consciousness movement, will give them relief. Jaya ātmāṣu prakṛtati.

#### Kṛṣṇa, auspiciousness, questioning
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CPaYBnLBlsg?t=136
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "It is simply questioning about Kṛṣṇa and hearing the answer."
**Summary:** Questioning about Kṛṣṇa is a pious activity that brings auspiciousness, and the Kṛṣṇa consciousness movement embodies this principle.

And Vishuddha Gosvāmī says, your question is very pious question. That's it, sādhu, because it is about Kṛṣṇa. The question was, after the departure of Kṛṣṇa, dharma, kaṁ, what is that, sarnā-gata, kārthaṁ, what is that? This is the inquiry. The answer is given there. Sir, your inquiry, Kṛṣṇa comes for re-establishing the principles of religion. That's it. Lillian means the order set by the Supreme Personality of God. That is lillian. You do like this, just like king's order, or the government order, do like this. Loose and, does not, So that is dharma. If you follow the do's and do nots given by Kṛṣṇa, that is a lie. You cannot manufacture it. That is bogus. So munayam svayabhu kṛṣṇa-bhagavad-vī-loka-maṅgalam. This question, just like our Kṛṣṇa consciousness movement, is the same. It is simply questioning about Kṛṣṇa and hearing the answer.

#### attraction, liberation, activities of Kṛṣṇa

**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

**Summary:** Talks about Kṛṣṇa are attractive because Kṛṣṇa is all-attractive, and studying and hearing about His activities guarantees liberation.

It is loka-maṅgalam. Anywhere this vibration will go on, there will be all auspicious, loka-maṅgalam. Kṛṣṇa saṅkṣu, cīnātmā suprasūdhī. Another feature is, Kṛṣṇa is all-attractive, therefore talks about Him is also attractive. In our Kṛṣṇa book, there are so many topics about Kṛṣṇa. Janma karma-mṛdivyam, about His birth, about His transference from real father's house to another foster father. Then the attack by the demon upon Kṛṣṇa, kaṁsau, all these activities. If we simply study and hear the Kṛṣṇa saṅkṣu-prasṇā, then we are liberated. Without any doubt, our liberation is guaranteed simply by hearing about Kṛṣṇa. Kṛṣṇa therefore comes, so many activities. Na māṅg karmānī limpaṇṭhī na me kaṇva-pareśa-prihāḥ. Kṛṣṇa says that He has nothing to do. What He has to do? But still He is killing so many demons, He is giving protection to so many devotees, because He has come to reestablish what is religious principle. So by His personal activities, He's telling, otherwise, Kṛṣṇa has nothing to do. Nā mā rāma kṛṣṇaḥ. He is self-satisfied. He has nothing to do. But to teach us, He comes. In this way we should live, from the very beginning of His childhood. This is the first point.

#### Absolute Truth, Kṛṣṇa, Paramātmā
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CPaYBnLBlsg?t=435
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The ultimate Absolute Truth is Kṛṣṇa, the person."
**Summary:** The human form of life is meant for inquiring about the Absolute Truth, which ultimately is Kṛṣṇa, and understanding Kṛṣṇa leads to understanding other features of the Absolute Truth.

Athāta brahma-jīgānta. Athā. Now, this is the time for inquiring about the Absolute Truth. This is the time, means, this human form of life. Animals cannot inquire. So therefore Vedānta Sutra says athā, athā, brahma-jīgānta, inquire about the Absolute Truth. Brahma, brahmeti, paramātmeti, bhagavān eti sadyati. The ultimate Absolute Truth is Kṛṣṇa, the person. Paramātmā is plenary expansion. And brahma, he is impersonal, effulgent. So if one understands Kṛṣṇa by question and answer, then he understands the other three features. But simply by understanding the impersonal feature, brahma effulgence, one cannot understand Kṛṣṇa. Neither by understanding or seeing the Paramātmā, one can understand Kṛṣṇa. To see the Paramātmā is the business of the yogis. dhyānā-vasthita-tad-gatena-manuṣā patsyante saṁyam yogiḥ. Yogina, the yogi, they are trying to see Kṛṣṇa or Viṣṇu within their heart by meditation. Meditation means this. dhyānā-vasthita-tad-gatena-manuṣā. patsyante saṁyam yogiḥ. And the jñānīs, they want to stop the material varieties, make it impersonal, and marge into the existence of brahma effulgence.

#### spiritualism, materialism, Kṛṣṇa consciousness

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "When we forget Kṛṣṇa, that is materialistic. And when we constantly remember Kṛṣṇa, that is spiritual."
**Summary:** The distinction between materialism and spiritualism lies in whether we remember Kṛṣṇa; forgetting Him is materialism, while remembering Him is spiritual.

Now the question is, how the foods and stores which is matter, become spiritual? Isn't that the question? We say one man is constructing this type of a building with wood and stone. We say this is materialistic. They can also challenge you that you are also interested in wood and stone and constructing a huge church or temple. Why? Why in your... Yes, spiritualism. Now, you can compare. This is spiritualism means in this stone and earth and wood you remember Kṛṣṇa. I am constructing something for Kṛṣṇa. Therefore it is spiritual. So the conclusion is when we forget Kṛṣṇa, that is materialistic. And when we constantly remember Kṛṣṇa, that is materialistic.

#### bhakti, karma, sense gratification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CPaYBnLBlsg?t=912
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Materialism means forgetfulness of Kṛṣṇa. Otherwise there is no materialism."
**Summary:** Actions performed for the satisfaction of Kṛṣṇa are bhakti and spiritual, while actions performed for sense gratification are karma and materialistic, ultimately materialism is forgetfulness of Kṛṣṇa.

But the same thing done for the satisfaction of Kṛṣṇa, that we shall construct this temple for Kṛṣṇa's inauguration. We shall construct this building for providing devotees of Kṛṣṇa so that they may be able to chant Hare Kṛṣṇa mahā-mantra without any disturbance. It appears the same thing, karma and bhakti. But bhakti is always in relation with Kṛṣṇa, and karma is always in relation with sense gratification. Therefore the thing, the ingredient, is originally spiritual, because it is Kṛṣṇa's energy. But ahaṁ mamṛti, when we try to impose our proprietorship, that is materialism. Materialism means forgetfulness of Kṛṣṇa. Otherwise there is no materialism.

#### dependence on Kṛṣṇa, saintly persons, economic policy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CPaYBnLBlsg?t=1442
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Until the devotee is completely dependent upon me, I carry personally whatever he needs."
**Summary:** Saintly persons are content with what Kṛṣṇa provides and focus on Kṛṣṇa consciousness, and Kṛṣṇa personally provides for those who are completely dependent on Him.

People will not agree to this, but actually if anyone wants peace of the mind, jñātmā-śutra-siddhanti, then this process will be allowed. This is practical also. There are many saintly persons. They do not try where to go, where to take money, or where to eat. They are busy in Kṛṣṇa consciousness. If Kṛṣṇa sends some food, I shall eat. Otherwise it doesn't matter. I shall starve. Then what is their determination? Spirit. They do not go anywhere. But Kṛṣṇa sends it, everything. Just like Gīla Maṁgata. Just like Gīla Maṁgata. He was not going anywhere. He was living in Vrndavana. Kṛṣṇa personally was coming to give him something. He became blind. He could not see. So Kṛṣṇa would come just like a boy. Gīla Maṁgata, I think you are hungry. Will you take some milk? I am not very good. All right, so give me little milk. Next day he comes. Then he would ask, who is this boy? I cannot see. He comes and gives me milk daily. Yuga-kemaṁ bhāmaḥ. In the Bhagavad-gītā he said that, until the devotee is completely dependent upon me, I carry personally whatever he needs. Yuga-bhūṇāṁ vṛnda-kāmaḥ. That one person is supplying all the necessities of all the living entities.

### Srimad Bhagavatam I Srila Prabhupada I SB 3. 26. 42 Part 2 I 25.11.2023
**Speaker:** HDG Srila Prabhupada

#### purpose of knowledge, infallible purpose, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/x4pl2rq9V70?t=39
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If you can explain the activities of Kṛṣṇa, how He is acting, how the chemical process is going on under His direction, if you can write a thesis on this subject matter, then ultimately Kṛṣṇa is behind that, then your study of chemistry is perfect."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the true purpose of knowledge, including scientific knowledge, is to understand and glorify Krishna, recognizing Him as the ultimate cause and director of everything.

Now, śāstra says that avicchito artha, artha means purpose. If somebody asks what is the purpose of becoming a scientist, what is the purpose of becoming a physicist? So different men will give different answers. It is meant for this purpose, it is meant for this purpose. But kavi bhi, those who are actually learned, advanced learning, they have said that avicchito, avicchito means without any failure, without any contradiction. You say that chemistry is required for this purpose. I say chemistry is required for this purpose. Another man says chemistry is required for this purpose. But difference of opinion, that is not accepted. Avicchito, infallible purpose. What is that infallible purpose? Avicchito artha. Kavi bhi nirupita. Nirupita means it is already settled. You haven't got to make research anymore. Nirupita means it is already settled. It is already concluded. What is that? Yad uttama-sloka-guṇa-anuvarnanam. If you can explain the activities of Kṛṣṇa, uttama-sloka, how He is acting, how the chemical process is going on under His direction, if you can write a thesis on this subject matter, then ultimately Kṛṣṇa is behind that, then your study of chemistry is perfect. Aviccita-artha-kavirbhi-nirupitam.

---
#### Krishna consciousness, perfect education, scientific knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/x4pl2rq9V70?t=145
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "You are greater scientist when by your scientific knowledge you prove the existence of God and the existence of the world."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that true intelligence and perfect education lead to the understanding of Krishna as the cause of all causes, and that scientific knowledge should be used to glorify Him.

Nirupitam, it is concluded. No more argument. That is the purpose. That means if you are intelligent enough, actually, then through any source of knowledge you come ultimately to Kṛṣṇa. That mayādhyakṣeṇa prakṛtiḥ śrūyate śrī-cāryaḥ. Kṛṣṇa says, but if by your education, if you come to the same point, that here is Kṛṣṇa, He is in the background, sarva-kāraṇa-kāraṇaṁ, cause of all causes, then education is perfect. Otherwise it is śrama eva hi kevalam. Otherwise it is simply laboring for nothing. Waste of time. This is the purpose. Aviccita-artha-kavirbhi-nirupitam. Yad uttamaḥ śloka-guṇānubhanyanam. So parāśa-śakti-bhībhi-daivaśyate. Very, very big chemistry, physical laws, mathematics, everything is required to understand the potency of God. How He is working. Actually everything is being done by His potency. Here there are different tests. The material is the same, but the method of mixing the material, different tests are coming out in existence. So how that expertly management of mixing is going on, if we can explain by our chemistry, by our mathematical values, then our studying of the material is going to be very, very good. So the understanding of chemistry is perfect. Then we are perfect.

#### humility, God's existence, material scientists
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/x4pl2rq9V70?t=261
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "You are greater scientist when by your scientific knowledge you prove the existence of God and the existence of the world."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the humility of great scientists like Einstein, who acknowledged God's presence, with the arrogance of lesser material scientists who deny God's existence, emphasizing that true knowledge leads to the glorification of God.

idam nipuṁśaḥ tapasas śrutasya bhāgau. Another place, atapuṁ vidhijaḥ śreṣaḥ varnāśrama-vibhāgaśaḥ sānuṣṭitasya dharmasyaḥ śaṁsiddhī haritośanam. Haritośanam is the ultimate goal. sakarmānātama-bhārcaḥ śaṁsiddhī-vṛndate naraḥ. The Bhagavad-gītā. sakarmānātama-bhārcaḥ. You become a chemist, it doesn't matter. You become physicist, mathematician, it doesn't matter. Unfortunately, due to the Kali-yuga, as soon as one become a big chemist, doctor of chemistry, he says, I am God. There is no need of God. As soon as little, saparī-jalamātve naḥ farfarāyate, you will see the small fishes, they are practically on the edge of the water and making farfarāyate. The big fishes, they are down the water. So these so-called material chemists, they are nothing, small fish. Those who are big chemists, big scientists, just like Professor Einstein, he used to accept. He used to accept God's curiosity is the end of the brain. How God's brain is working. He appreciated. He was a great scientist. And the ordinary, fanatics, sāyāsadeva thinks that now we are, we are able to do everything without God, therefore we are bigger than God. That is not fact. You are greater scientist when by your scientific knowledge you prove the existence of God and the existence of the world. That is not a factor. If you understand the simple single-minded thought in the of God and how He is working so finely that varieties of things are coming out of material nature. That is the perfection of knowledge. Otherwise, sānuṣṭitasya dharmasya saṁsiddhi haritauśana.

#### devotion, glorification of Krishna, humility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/x4pl2rq9V70?t=387
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A devotee neither claims anything, neither claims anything. He is not taking any credit for himself. Neither it is possible. Even he may be very, very big devotee, he will never claim any credit for his glorious activities. His glorious activities means to make Kṛṣṇa glorious."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that a true devotee uses their knowledge to glorify Krishna, taking no credit for themselves, and that Krishna is pleased when He sees that the knowledge He has given is being used for His glorification.

That is haritauśana. Unless Kṛṣṇa sees that you are trying to glorify Him by your knowledge, then He becomes satisfied that I have given Him some knowledge, and He is utilizing this knowledge for my glorification. He is my devotee. Devotee's business is to glorify. He doesn't take any credit for himself. Actually there is nothing to be taken, credit. All credit goes to Kṛṣṇa. A devotee neither claims anything, neither claims anything. He is not taking any credit for himself. Neither it is possible. Even he may be very, very big devotee, he will never claim any credit for his glorious activities. His glorious activities means to make Kṛṣṇa glorious. Not that like the so-called materialist, he takes, wants to take the credit. No. Sakarmanā tam abharcho saṁsiddhī davate naraḥ. Sakarmanā. You may be engaged in any pattern of work, any department of work, but by dint of your work, you establish the existence of God, Kṛṣṇa, and whatever is being done, it is being done by the expert management of Kṛṣṇa.

#### Krishna's management, divine order, supreme control
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/x4pl2rq9V70?t=474
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Don't think the sun is working so nicely. Automatical, not automatical. The master is there, Kṛṣṇa."
**Summary:** The speaker illustrates Krishna's expert management through the example of the sun's precise movements and temperature regulation, emphasizing that nothing happens automatically but is under the Supreme's order.

The sun is rising exactly in the time, and it is setting exactly in the time, and the temperature, according to the different season, the movement, uttarāyaṇa, dakṣināyaṇa, everything is managed so expertly under the order of the Supreme, mayādhyakṣeṇa, prapṛti. Don't think the sun is working so nicely. Automatical, not automatical. The master is there, Kṛṣṇa. The sun is so powerful substance within this universe. There are many millions of suns. This is one sun only. But it is carrying out the order of Kṛṣṇa.

#### Krishna's potencies, unlimited energy, spiritual reality
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/x4pl2rq9V70?t=541
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Similarly, the Supreme Lord, by expanding His potencies by His energy, He remains the same. He does not diminish."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the unlimited nature of Krishna's energy, using the sun as an analogy to explain that even after giving unlimited heat and light for millions of years, it remains the same, and similarly, Krishna's potencies are inexhaustible.

yaccha kṣuresa śavitā śagala-grhānā rājā samastha śra-mṛti aśeṣu tezā Aśeṣu tezā. Unlimited. Light. Unlimited fire. Unlimited heat. Aśeṣu. Aśeṣu tezā. There is no compression. With the sunlight, sunset, there is no compression within this universe. Unlimited. For millions and millions of years, it is from the sun, the light and heat is coming out, but there is no diminution. They, it is the same as it was millions of years ago, and after giving you light and heat for millions of years, the same quantity of light and heat is still there. So if it is possible for a material thing, that after giving unlimited heat and light, it remains the same. Similarly, the Supreme Lord, by expanding His potencies by His energy, He remains the same. He does not diminish. Purnasya purna-mādāya purna-mī-bhāva-śuṣyate. So if we can see in the material object that the heat being emanated from many millions and millions, it remains the same heat. It maintains the same heat, same life. Why it is not possible for the Supreme? Therefore, Īśvara-bhūta-paṇiṣāda informs us that purnasya purna-mādāya purna-mī-bhāva-śuṣyate.

#### real God, unlimited potency, modern gods
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/x4pl2rq9V70?t=661
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Kṛṣṇa can make hundreds of thousands and millions of Kṛṣṇa, still He is Kṛṣṇa. That is Kṛṣṇa. The potency is never exhausted."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the unlimited potency of Krishna with the limited power of modern 'gods' who diminish when they give their power away, emphasizing that Krishna's potency is never exhausted.

If you take the whole energy of Kṛṣṇa from Kṛṣṇa, then still the whole energy is there. But you'll be surprised. Nowadays, modern gods, there are so many modern gods. I do not wish to name, but one modern god, he gave his power to his disciples. And when he came to Kṛṣṇa, he gave his power to his disciples. And when he came to consciousness, then he was crying. The disciples inquired from the guru, why you are crying, sir? No, I have finished everything. I have given you everything. I have given you everything. Therefore, I am now finished. That is not spiritual. That is material. I have got hundred rupees. If I pay you hundred rupees, then my pocket is empty. But Kṛṣṇa is not like that. Kṛṣṇa can make hundreds of thousands and millions of Kṛṣṇa, still He is Kṛṣṇa. That is Kṛṣṇa. The potency is never exhausted. That is called purnasya purna-mādāya purna-mī-bhāva-śuṣyate.

Purnam eva śuṣyate. So this imitation, God will not help us. The real God, real God, Īśvara-parama-kṛṣṇa, sa-cid-ānanda-vigrahaḥ, anādhi-rādhi-govindaraḥ, sarva-kāraṇa-kāraṇam. Sarva-kāraṇa-kāraṇam. He is never exhausted. He is never exhausted. It is said that yasyaika-niśya-śutakālam athāva-nambha-jīvanti-lobha-vilajā, jagad-anna-nāthā, viṣṇu-mahāna-śayaya-yasya-kalā-viśeṣa, govinda-mādi-puruṣaṁ tamāṁ jāyate. The millions of universes are coming out with his breathing, period. And again they are annihilated. When the breathing is taken inside, in this way the universes are coming out. Jagad-anna-nāthā. Jagad-anna-nāthā. Jagad-anna means the universe. And nāthā, the lord of the universe, means Lord Brahmā. So he has got a duration of life. And what is the duration of life? The breathing period of Mahā-Viṣṇu. yasyaika-niśya-śutakālam athāva-nambha-jīvanti-lobha-vilajā, jagad-anna-nāthā, viṣṇu-mahāna-śayaya-yasya-kalā-viśyeṣa. Govinda-mādi-puruṣaṁ tamāṁ. That is God. By breathing, unlimited number of universes are coming out and going in.

And the duration of existence of each and every universe is the duration of life of Brahmā. That we cannot calculate. Even we cannot calculate His twelve hours. So that is God. We accept such kind of life. We accept such kind of God. Not a little magician. No. That is not possible. We cannot accept such cheap gods. We accept Kṛṣṇa, the sarva-kāraṇa-kāraṇam, and we want to be eternal servant. That is our actual position, jiveśvarūpa-nitya-kṛṣṇa-dāsa, by His unlimited potency, energy. Unlimited universes are coming out with unlimited number of varieties of planet. And each planet is differently situated, different atmosphere, different temperature. So in this way, varieties. That is ānanda. Kṛṣṇa has created these varieties because He is ānanda-mayo vyāsāt. Ānanda-mayo. Unless there is variety. So we are partakers. We are part and parcel of that variety maker. We are also one of the varieties. Jīva-śakti. We are also one of the varieties. So how we can become variety-less, nirvīśeṣa? That is not possible. Even artificially we try to become nirvīśeṣa, variety-less. Our constitutional position is that we want variety.

#### Maya, service, Krishna

**Summary:** The speaker explains that the impersonalists fall down from their position of oneness because they lack the ānanda found at Krishna's lotus feet, and that everyone is serving either māyā or Krishna, and true success lies in transferring service from māyā to Krishna.

Therefore, in the śāstra it is said, so-called māyāvādī, impersonalist, monies, āryajya krccheṇa-param padanta-taḥ. Although they get up to the position of oneness, monies, but from that position they fall down. Why? āryajya krccheṇa-param padanta-taḥ, patanti adhā, ānādita jagособas vlogs. Why? Because you cannot become jīvadora. You are going to become s Sitting-abh Knights. You cannot become a teacher, good teacher, teacher, But you cannot become an ascetic. Because you cannot become a teacher, good teacher, good monk, Junga. So this is the definition of vaiṇādita-jisma-dhangrāja. Because they have no information of the lotus feet of Kṛṣṇa, where to enjoy ānanda, ānandamaya-bhāsā. And they are by nature seeking after ānanda, the so-called oneness, monism, that will not please them. So they will require again ānanda. But because anādhita-yusmataṁ grhayaḥ, they have not worshipped Kṛṣṇa's lotus feet, they come down again in this material world and worship the feet of māyā. That is their tradition. ārīja-kṛcchreṇa-parampadam tato patanti adhaḥ. And as soon as you are engaged in the service of māyā, that is your adhapatam. Adhapatam. As we, all these materialistic persons who are within this material world, you are serving māyā. Everyone is serving. Nobody is without serving. But they are serving māyā. So you have to transform, transfer your service from māyā to Kṛṣṇa. Then your life will be successful, and you will taste different varieties of prasādam perpetually and enjoy life eternally and blissfully. Thank you very much.

#### Krishna's Identity, Devotional Service, Causeless Mercy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jsbOXtHRf6Y?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "All these truths were explained to Sanatana Goswami by the Lord himself out of his causeless mercy."
**Summary:** This segment introduces the lecture, focusing on Shri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's instructions to Sanatana Goswami regarding Krishna's identity, conjugal love, opulence, and the mellows of devotional service, all given out of causeless mercy.

Jaya Jaya Shri Chaitanya Jaya Nithyananda. Jaya Dvaita Chandra Jaya Gaurabhakta Vrinda. We are reading from Shri Chaitanya Charita Amrita, Madhya Leela, Chapter 20, entitled Lord Shri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu Instructs Sanatana Goswami in the Science of the Absolute Truth from verse from 97 to 99. I will read 97 and 98 and will repeat verse 99. Krishna Swaroopa Madhuri Aishwarya Bhakti Rasashriyam Tatvam Sanatana Yesha Kripa Yopadi Desha Saha. Shri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, the Supreme Personality of God, had personally told Sanatana Goswami about Lord Krishna's real identity. He also told him about the Lord's conjugal love, his personal opulence and the mellows of devotion service. All these truths were explained to Sanatana Goswami by the Lord himself out of his causeless mercy.

---
#### Humility, Material Fall, Surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jsbOXtHRf6Y?t=68
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Although they are fallen, if they simply submit themselves at the lotus feet of Shri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, they will be saved from sinful reactions."
**Summary:** This segment covers Sanatana Goswami's humble submission and self-identification as fallen, followed by an explanation of how he, despite being from a brahmana family, considered himself fallen due to association with meat-eaters and materialists, and how surrender to Shri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu can save one from sinful reactions.

Text 98. Putting a straw in his mouth and bowing down, Sanatana Goswami clasped the lotus feet of Shri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu and humbly spoke as follows. Text 98. Putting a straw in his mouth and bowing down, Sanatana Goswami clasped the lotus feet of Shri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu and humbly spoke as follows. Translation. Sanatana Goswami said, I was born in a low family and my associates are all low class men. I myself am fallen and am the lowest of men. Indeed, I have passed my whole life fallen in the well of sinful materialism. Actually, Shri Sanatana Goswami belonged to a brahmana family because he belonged to the Saraswati division of the brahmanas and was well-cultured and well-educated. Somehow or other, he accepted a ministership in the Muslim government. Therefore, he had to associate with meat-eaters, drunkards and cross-materialists. Sanatana Goswami considered himself fallen for in the association of such men, he also fell victim to material enjoyment. Having passed his life in that way, he considered that he had wasted his valuable time. This statement about how one can become fallen in this material world is made by the greatest authority in the Gaudiya Vaishnavasam Pradaya. Actually, the whole world is presently fallen into material existence. Everyone is a meat-eater, drunkard, woman-hunter, gambler and what not. People are enjoying material life by committing the four basic sins. Although they are fallen, if they simply submit themselves at the lotus feet of Shri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, they will be saved from sinful reactions. This ends the Bhaktivedanta Parbat.

---
#### Krishna's Nature, Devotional Service, Opulence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jsbOXtHRf6Y?t=237
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** This segment discusses the subject matter of instruction Sanatana Goswami received from Shri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, focusing on Krishna's svarupa (actual position), madhurya (special attractiveness), and aisharja (opulences), emphasizing that these can only be understood through devotional service.

Sanātana Gosvāmī is taking instruction and lessons from Śrī Caitanya Mahāprabhu. And what is the subject matter of instruction that is described herein by the author of this Caitanya-caritāmī? The subject matter is Kṛṣṇa's svarūpa. What is the actual position of Kṛṣṇa? Kṛṣṇa's svarūpa, mādhujya. And what is the special attractiveness of Kṛṣṇa? Mādhujya. Kṛṣṇa's svarūpa, mādhujya, aisharja. And what are the opulences of Kṛṣṇa? Kṛṣṇa's svarūpa, mādhujya, aisharja. And what are the opulences of Kṛṣṇa? Bhakti-vāsākram. And all these, what is Kṛṣṇa? What is His special qualification for attraction? And what is His opulence? These three things can be understood through devotional service by no other means.

#### Devotional Service, Understanding Krishna, Material Existence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jsbOXtHRf6Y?t=315
**Verse References:** BG 7.3
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Out of many millions of human beings, somebody tries to make a perfection of his life."
**Summary:** This segment uses the analogy of understanding a great personality through service to explain how one can understand Krishna through devotional service, referencing the Bhagavad-gita to emphasize the difficulty in understanding Krishna and how few people strive for perfection in life, contrasting them with those engrossed in animalistic activities.

Just like I have several times cited the example that if you want to be in confidence of a great personality, then you must satisfy him by your service. You cannot understand a big man by your speculation, even in this material world. If you want to know a big man, what he is, then you must satisfy him by your service, attitude, by friendship, by love. And he will disclose to you what he's. So the same process is bhakti-vāsākram. If you want to know what is Kṛṣṇa, what is His opulence, what is his special feature for attraction, then you should try to understand by no other means anything other than Kṛṣṇa, anything. Understand through the devotional service of Kṛṣṇa consciousness. In the Bhagavad-gītā also the Lord says that bhakti-āmāma-bhijānāti tattvatā. Yāvān yasyāmi tattvatā. In another place, the Lord says that to understand Me, actually what I am, is very difficult. Manuṣyānāṁ sa śreṣu kaścid yaḥ. Out of many millions of human beings, somebody tries to make a perfection of his life. Otherwise they are all working jail cats and dogs. That's all. Simply āhāra-vidrā-vāya-mṛti-viṣaḥ. They have no other information. Work hard, eat and be merry and enjoy. And making, that's all. So out of millions and millions of persons, they are all working. Therefore they are all working. They cannot be made devoid of the sacraments of the Lord. And what is the profession of a person who is engaged in animal profession?

#### Devotional Service, Perfection, Understanding Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jsbOXtHRf6Y?t=447
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If you want to know Kṛṣṇa, then you have to understand through your devotional service."
**Summary:** This segment continues the discussion on understanding Krishna, emphasizing that even among those striving for perfection, only a few can truly understand Krishna through devotional service, as stated in the Bhagavad-gita, and that no other process can lead to this understanding.

Even for a person like this, engaged in animal profession of life, only a few persons, selected persons, because the illusory energy has grasped under her clutches very tightly. To get out of the clutches is very difficult. So somehow or other, a few people, out of many millions and thousands, they come to the understanding of God. And then the rest of them, they are not able to understanding that what is the real perfection of life. Now, those who are actually perfected, out of them, out of millions of them, one can understand Kṛṣṇa. That is the statement of Bhagavad-gītā. Manuṣṭhānaṁ sahasreṣu kaścid yatati siddhaye yatatām api siddhānaṁ Siddhānaṁ means those who have already achieved perfection. Out of them. Not these imperfect fools. Those who are actually attained perfection, they can understand what Kṛṣṇa is actually. Tattva. And that actual position can be understood in another place of Bhagavad-gītā state, bhaktyā mām abhijānāti. What he sees actually, that can be understood by this devotional service. No other process. No other process will help to understand Kṛṣṇa. You can, by other processes, you can achieve some other thing. But if you want to know Kṛṣṇa, then you have to understand through your devotional service. Bhaktyā mām abhijānāti.

#### Spiritual Kingdom, Devotional Service, Instruction

So you will have entrance into the spiritual kingdom, into the abode of Kṛṣṇa, when you have perfectly… We cannot understand perfectly, but as far as possible, as far as you have got the capacity, if you understand Kṛṣṇa as He is, then the entrance will be there. Otherwise there is no entrance. Therefore Lord Caitanya instructed Sanātana Gosvāmī about Kṛṣṇa through devotional service. This instruction is very important. You should very carefully hear and, if possible, note. This is directly the instruction. Just like the Bhagavad-gītā, Lord Kṛṣṇa is directly instructing or doing. But in the Bhagavad-gītā there are many phases of instruction, fruitive action, philosophical speculation, and yoga system, and jñāna system, all kinds of different paths. Ultimately, of course, Lord Kṛṣṇa instructing him that sarva-dharmān parityajya mām ītaṁ sarvaṁ bhajaṁ. That is the most confidential part. But here Lord Caitanya, He is not instructing any superfluous thing.

#### Mercy, Surrender, Love of Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jsbOXtHRf6Y?t=658
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Even what Kṛṣṇa could not give, what Kṛṣṇa could not, or Kṛṣṇa hesitated to give...Here Caitanya Mahāprabhu, His mercy is that He directly gives this highest instruction."
**Summary:** This segment highlights Lord Caitanya's immense mercy, stating that He directly gives the highest instruction of surrendering to Krishna, which Krishna hesitated to give in the beginning of the Bhagavad-gita, and that Lord Caitanya is considered the most munificent because He directly offers love of Krishna.

So that is the blessing of Lord Caitanya. Even what Kṛṣṇa could not give, what Kṛṣṇa could not, or Kṛṣṇa hesitated to give, because Kṛṣṇa hesitated that if I speak in the beginning of the Bhagavad-gītā, sarva-dharmān parityajya mām ītaṁ sarvaṁ bhajaṁ, people would not accept it. There was no necessity of describing Bhagavad-gītā in so many phases of instruction. Because the last instruction, most confidential instruction was that you give up everything and just surrender unto Him. That is the actual instruction of Bhagavad-gītā. But that was not spoken in the beginning. Because there are many phases of human being. They are under different stages of soul. Or they have different stages, karma, jñāna, yoga, so many things they have been discovered. Here Caitanya Mahāprabhu, His mercy is that He directly gives this highest instruction. What Kṛṣṇa could not give, but Kṛṣṇa Caitanya, or Kṛṣṇa in His incarnation as Lord Śrī Caitanya Mahāprabhu, He gave up. Therefore He is considered the most munificent, charitable personality, man of charity. Rūpa Gosvāmī offered His respect that namo mahā-vadānā, you are the greatest munificent personality. You are directly offering Kṛṣṇa, which is very difficult for you. It is very difficult to understand. So many scholars, so many philosophers, so many great personalities, they could not understand Kṛṣṇa. But your mercy is so broad that you are directly offering Kṛṣṇa. Therefore mahā-vadānā, Kṛṣṇa prema-pradāya, directly you are giving love of Kṛṣṇa.

#### Bhakti-yoga, Humility, Instruction

**Key Quote:** "So Sanātana Gosvāmī in this way directly received instruction from Lord Caitanya about bhakti-yoga, what is bhakti."

So Sanātana Gosvāmī in this way directly received instruction from Lord Caitanya about bhakti-yoga, what is bhakti. So this is called tattvaṁ sanātanāya īśa, kṛpayāpadi-veṣa īśa. īśa means the Lord, the Lord, Supreme Controller. So He is also there. Just like Arjuna received instruction directly, similarly Sanātana Gosvāmī also received instruction directly from the Supreme Lord. So here how to receive instruction, that is given by example by Sanātana Gosvāmī. tave sanātana-prahūr-carane dharīya dainya-vinati-kare dante-triṇalaiya Sanātana Gosvāmī very humbly fell down on the lotus feet of Caitanya Mahāprabhu. And most humbly He submitted. What is that submission? nīca-jāti nīca-saṅgī-patīt adhaṁ pūpiṣaya pūpe parī gāyaṇu janam My dear sir, I have wasted my time in simply sensual gratification. That is my qualification. And I belong to the lowest caste. Nīca-jāti. Nīca-saṅgī. And my association is also of the lowest type. And patīt. I have fallen and I am the lowest of all. This is my qualification.

#### Humility, Social Status, Service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jsbOXtHRf6Y?t=917
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Because service is meant for the śūdras, not for the brāhmaṇas, kṣatriya and vaiśya."
**Summary:** This segment elaborates on Sanatana Goswami's self-identification as belonging to a lower caste, explaining that although he was born into a brahmana family, his acceptance of a ministership in the Mohammedan kingdom, which was considered service, placed him outside the brahmana community, as service was traditionally meant for the sudras.

Now, why He is submitting himself? Nīca-jāti. Nīca-jāti means those who are born in lower family. They are called nīca-jāti. They are śūdras or less than the śūdras. According to Vedic system, the brāhmaṇa, kṣatriya and vaiśya, they are higher caste. And the śūdras, they are lower caste. And less than śūdras, they are lowest. So Sanātana Gosvāmī, actually he belongs to the brāhmaṇa family. He, his forefathers belonged to the śārasya-brāhmaṇa family. There are different set, gaura-brāhmaṇa and śārasya-brāhmaṇa. So śārasya-brāhmaṇa, actually, they belong to the Aryan, āryavartā, the Panyāra side and Kashmir side. They are considered high-class brāhmaṇas. So he belongs to that community. But somehow or other, their forefathers… I mean, make their domicile in Bengal. Because they had some property in Bengal. They are very rich, aristocratic family. And this Sanātana Gosvāmī accepted ministership in the Mohammedan kingdom. So in those days, amongst the brāhmaṇa community, if any brāhmaṇa would accept service, he becomes at once a śūdra. He becomes at once. Because service is meant for the śūdras, not for the brāhmaṇas, kṣatriya and vaiśya. They would never accept anybody's obligation as service. They would starve. That was the principle. Only the śūdras could accept service. So because these Sanātana Gosvāmī and Rupa Gosvāmī accepted government office, although it was government office, but it was service, so they were outside the brāhmaṇa community.

#### Humility, Spiritual Master, Qualifications
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jsbOXtHRf6Y?t=1059
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Everyone, unless he is conscious of his position that he is the lowest, he cannot become the highest."
**Summary:** This segment discusses how only Caitanya Mahaprabhu accepted Sanatana Goswami due to the strict social conditions of the time, and that everyone must be conscious of their position as the lowest to become the highest, and that one should not approach a spiritual master puffed up with so-called qualifications but as a blank slate.

So only Caitanya Mahāprabhu accepted. That is Caitanya Mahāprabhu. Otherwise, at that time, social condition was very strict. You have heard the name of Rabindrīnātrava. They were also in that way, hospitied. They were out of brāhmaṇa community. They also belonged to the brāhmaṇa community. So therefore he is presenting himself that nityāti. Nityāti. I am lower caste. Why? Because my associates are niche. My associates are Mormonian. They are not Brahmins. They are not highly qualified. Because my associates are lower, so I am lower also. And patīr, I have fallen down from the standard of my fraternal position. Patīr, I have fallen. Ādhāṁ. And ādhāṁ means I am the lowest of the human beings. So, of course, because Sanātana Gosvāmī was under certain condition, it is not simply particularly for the Sanātana Gosvāmī. Everyone, unless he is conscious of his position that he is the lowest, he cannot become the highest. One should not think, while approaching his spiritual master, one should not be puffed up with his so-called qualifications. He should be a blank slate. That is the, that is requirement. He should forget that whatever nonsense he had learnt, he should forget. Otherwise, there will be no benefit by approaching spiritual master. He should forget. If he keeps his personality, I believe, I cannot, there is no necessity. With such nonsense things, nobody should approach a spiritual master.

#### Humility, Sense Gratification, Beneficial Knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jsbOXtHRf6Y?t=1192
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "I do not know what is actually beneficial to me."
**Summary:** This segment continues the discussion on humility, emphasizing the need to become a blank slate before approaching a spiritual master, and that Sanatana Goswami admits to wasting his time in sense gratification and not knowing what is actually beneficial to him.

One should become a blank slate. So, I am fallen. I am the lowest. And I am lower born. So many things are good qualifications. This is my position. Nityānā. And ku viṣaya kupe parī gāiṇu-janaḥ. I have now wasted my time simply by sense gratification. Viṣaya means āhāra-nidrā-bhaya-maitur. These are called viṣaya. Objects of sense gratification. Eating, sleeping, mating, and defending. These are called viṣaya. And we are engaged only how to eat very nicely, how to sleep very nicely, how to give me protection, and how to have nice mating. This is our business. So, Samātana Gosvāmī says that I have simply wasted my time in this way. And my position is this. So, therefore, actually, āpanār hita hi kisi na hi yati. Because I am so much fallen, because I have wasted my time in sense gratification, because I am ostracized, because my association is so lower, therefore my position is that I do not know what is actually beneficial to me. Āpanār hita hi kisi na yati.

Now, Samātana Gosvāmī was minister, educated, he was learned scholar in Parsi and Sanskrit. So, how it is that he is placing himself in that way? Is he, I would say, imitating something, such humbleness? But actually he was known? Yes. Actually he was. No. Actually he was so. A devotee does not say anything falsely unless he feels. He feels himself like that. Why does he feel? He was educated. He belonged to the brāhmaṇa aristocratic family. His association was all Nabhāvas and Yamindārasa aristocratic family. And still he says like this. Why? What is the defect? That he felt like that. Now he is expressing. Āpanār hita hi kisi na yati. I do not know what is actually beneficial to me. I am thinking that if I am posted in a service like this, and if I have got some aristocratic association, I have got some enchantment for sense gratification, that is materialism. So I have passed my, wasted my time in that.

#### Social Etiquette, Vedic System, Communities
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jsbOXtHRf6Y?t=1376
**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

**Summary:** This segment explains the Indian system of addressing different communities, with brahmanas addressed as pandita, kshatriyas as Thakura, vaisyas as sēdjī, and sudras as mahajana, and that Sanatana Goswami is addressed as pandita as a matter of social etiquette.

But actually I do not know what is beneficial to me. So grāma-bhāvahara paṇḍita tāi sattva-māne. In the Indian system, a brāhmaṇa is addressed as paṇḍita. But as you know, Paṇḍita Jahannālaya, although he had no brāhmaṇical qualification, but because he was born in a brāhmaṇa family, so this title was Paṇḍita. Paṇḍita. So a brāhmaṇa is addressed as paṇḍita. A kṣatriya is addressed as Ṭhākura. And a vaiśya is addressed as a saint, sīrjī, a rich man. The vaiśyas, vaiśyas are generally very rich men. For us, as you have in your country, the Rockefeller, Carnegie and Ford, similarly in our country you have got many rich men. Just like Billard, Dalmya, Bangar and so many. So they are called sēdjī. So sēdjī, Ṭhākura-jī and paṇḍita-jī. And the śūdras are called mahājana. Because if they are called śūdras, they will be angry. And mahājana means great personality.

#### Social Harmony, Krishna Consciousness, Humility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jsbOXtHRf6Y?t=1454
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

**Summary:** This segment discusses the social harmony that existed among different communities in villages due to their shared religious culture and Krsna consciousness, and that Sanatana Goswami is addressed as pandita by social etiquette, but he does not possess the knowledge of a true pandita.

So these are some etiquette for addressing different communities. Among the different communities, although there are such classifications, still there was no enmity. They lived in village, although in their position, but the relationship was very friendly. Just like brothers, just like brothers, actually. Because the culture was religion. Culture was the Kṛṣṇa consciousness. So they depended on the culture. There was no provincialism. Because they depended on culture. The culture is the thing. The Kṛṣṇa conscious speech can… Even there are materially so many high and low classifications. When you come to this culture, Kṛṣṇa consciousness, they will be all in the same place. So he said, the grāhma-bhābhāre paṇḍita. And as a matter of social etiquette, I am addressed as paṇḍita. Paṇḍita means planet. Planet scholar. He is called paṇḍita. Paṇḍita asama-darśana. He has read in the Bhagavad-gītā. Paṇḍita means he sees everything on equal eyes. Why? Because he is brāhmaṇa. He has known what is grāhma and what are these living entities. He has got such knowledge. That is called paṇḍita. So Sanātana Gosvāmī had no such knowledge. At least he is pretending like that. So he said, the grāhma-bhābhāre kahaye paṇḍita. By social etiquette, I am addressed as paṇḍita. But… And I also accept that I am paṇḍita.

#### Humility, Illusion, Spiritual Master
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jsbOXtHRf6Y?t=1576
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So this is blank slate, admitting that I am fool number one."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the illusion of accepting false qualifications and how Sanatana Goswami admits to being a fool, and that this mentality should be sacrificed before approaching a spiritual master.

If somebody falsely calls me that your majesty… So it is an insult. It is not honoring him. I am an ordinary man. If somebody calls me your majesty… So a man who has no qualification… If he is visualized in that way, that is an insult. But we are so much poor that if somebody calls me your majesty, I shall feel, I am majesty. Yes? I am his majesty. That is our nature. If some false qualifications are awarded to me, I shall be very glad to accept it. I shall think, yes, I am. So Sarātana Gosvāmī said that actually I am not paṇḍita because I do not know what is my benefit, what is beneficial to me. I do not know the goal of my life. I simply wasted my time in sense gratification. I do not know. And still people say, you are paṇḍita. And I accept it. Just see my position. So this is blank slate, admitting that I am fool number one. But people say, I am learned and I accept it. This is our nature. This is called illusion. He will never think that I am fool number one. He will always think, oh, who can be greater than me? I am the… I can think myself. What is the necessity of a spiritual master? I can… I can become a religious leader. I can become… This, this is our mentality. So this mentality should be sacrificed first, before going to a spiritual master.

#### Spiritual Master, Surrender, Qualifications
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jsbOXtHRf6Y?t=1696
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "He left his very lucrative job, minister seat, and just like a very poor man, he approached to Caitanya Mahāprabhu."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the process of selecting a spiritual master, emphasizing the need to understand whether the person is fit and then offering oneself as a student, and that Sanatana Goswami first heard from Caitanya Mahaprabhu and then retired from service, approaching Caitanya Mahaprabhu as a blank slate.

First of all, you have to select who can be your spiritual master. That requires some knowledge. Or you have to behave with a person to understand whether he is fit to be a spiritual master. If you become my spiritual master, then you should offer yourself to be a student. That is the process. Not that all of a sudden you shall go to a person, oh, please accept me as my student. No. You should first of all try to understand whether he is actually fit. Then offer yourself. So just like Sanātana Gosvāmī, when he first saw Caitanya Mahāprabhu, he did not offer himself. He just… But when he heard from Caitanya Mahāprabhu, and he understood, yes, it is a very nice thing, so I should now retire from service, and I should wholly devote to Caitanya Mahāprabhu. He left his very lucrative job, minister seat, and just like a very poor man, he approached to Caitanya Mahāprabhu. And he… And he placing, submitting himself as blank place. That these are my qualifications. Please accept me. Thank you.

#### Haridasa Thakur, Lord Brahma, Gauranga Mahaprabhu, Holy Name
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZboVSgUlJxk?t=29
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "in future when Lord appears in Kali Yuga as Gauranga Mahaprabhu, Lord Brahma's desire will be fulfilled that he will appear as one of his close associate and assist in the pastimes of Lord Chaitanya in Gauranga Leela and that time Brahma will be called, will be known as Haridas and he will appear in a family of Mlechas so that he wouldn't be proud of his position as a creator of this universe."
**Summary:** This segment narrates the story of how Lord Brahma, after realizing his mistake of testing Krishna, desired to participate in Krishna's pastimes and was told that he would appear as Haridasa Thakur in the age of Kali to assist Lord Chaitanya.

With awareness about the pure activities of pure devotees, Supreme Lord Krishna will manifest his lotus feet in our hearts. Today, we are in this holy place of Puleya, where a pure devotee Haridas Thakur lived, glorifying this place by chanting three lakh holy names every day and performed many wonderful pastimes. Shilabhakti Vinod Thakur reveals in Navadvipa Dhamamahatmya, after Lord Brahma stole the calves and the cowherd friends of Lord Krishna to test the supremacy of the cowherd boy in Vrindavan. He realized his mistake that the boy in Vrindavan is none other than his master, Supreme Lord Sri Krishna. He was feeling so repentant, he came and offered his prayers to Lord Krishna and Lord mercifully forgave him. But Lord Brahma was not satisfied with this. He came to Navadvipa Dham and performed severe auspiciousness. Lord Brahma was pleased with his austerities to atone his offences in Antaradvipa. Being pleased with his austerities, Supreme Lord Krishna appeared before him and gave his darshan. Lord Brahma, seeing the form of the Lord, submitted a request that he wishes to be part of his leelas. Then Lord showed him the Gauranga form and said, in future when Lord appears in Kali Yuga as Gauranga Mahaprabhu, Lord Brahma's desire will be fulfilled that he will appear as one of his close associate and assist in the pastimes of Lord Chaitanya in Gauranga Leela and that time Brahma will be called, will be known as Haridas and he will appear in a family of Mlechas so that he wouldn't be proud of his position as a creator of this universe.

#### Haridasa Thakur, Puliya, Chanting, Miracle
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZboVSgUlJxk?t=232
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Understanding their fear, Haridasa Thakur spoke to the snake and told them, My dear snake, because of the fear of you, devotees are hesitant to come here and chant with me."
**Summary:** This segment describes Haridasa Thakur's life in Puliya, his dedication to chanting 300,000 names of Krishna daily, and the miracle of him asking a poisonous snake to leave the cave, which increased the villagers' devotion to him.

Lord Brahma appeared as Haridasa Thakur, stayed here in this Puliya village at this place. There used to be a cave in this place during those days and a tree where Haridasa Thakur would spend most of his time chanting the names of Lord Shri Krishna. He would chant 300,000 names of Lord Shri Krishna every day. He would cry. He would dance. Sometimes he would fall unconscious chanting the names of Lord Shri Krishna. Villagers would come here to take association of Haridasa Thakur and chant with him. But the villagers also noticed there was a black poisonous snake living in the same cave which posed a great threat to Haridasa and other visitors. So they were very hesitant to visit the place. Understanding their fear, Haridasa Thakur spoke to the snake and told them, My dear snake, because of the fear of you, devotees are hesitant to come here and chant with me. If you don't mind, could you please go somewhere else and live or if you are uncomfortable leaving this place, I would like to go and be somewhere else. With the words of Haridas Thakur, the black snake immediately left the place and went away. Seeing this miracle, the villagers were struck with awe and reverence. Their respect and devotion to Haridas Thakur increased immensely. This wonderful pastime happened at this place where Haridas Thakur stayed and performed his Nama bhajana.

---

Once, Maya Devi herself came to test Srila Haridas Thakur here. With exquisite beauty, the illusory energy approached Haridas Thakur to allure him. Srila Haridas Thakur transformed Maya Devi with his association by chanting Hare Krishna Mahamantra. He requested Maya Devi to sit down and after his bhajan, after his chanting, he will come and attend to her. The whole day went on chanting Hare Krishna Mahamantra. Mayadevi, hearing Hare Krishna Chanting Hare Krishna Mantra, had a heart transformation. In fact, Mayadevi had no impact on Haridas Thakur. At that time, Haridas Thakur was a young man. With her exquisite beauty, illusory energy could not distract Haridas Thakur from his determination to worship Supreme Lord, but only with the help of Prasadu.

on those who take my shelter. #### immortality, intelligence of God, Hiranyakasipu
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/OevVtXN2A5k?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "God is always more intelligent than ourselves. We should always remember."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada explains that everyone in the material world must die, and Hiranyakasipu tried to avoid death in a materialistic way, but Krsna is more intelligent and cannot be cheated.

Wherever you go within this material world, either in the Brahma-loka or in the Patala-loka, you have to die. That is the problem. So Hiranyakasipu wanted to solve this in a materialistic way, but that is not possible. So in a negative way, he wanted that I shall not die on the land, I shall not die in the air, I shall not die in the water, I shall not be killed by any man-made weapon. In so many ways, negatively, he thought that he has become immortal. But Krsna is so intelligent that keeping all the words or the blessings given by Lord Brahma to Hiranyakasipu, He cannot kill anyone. So we cannot cheat God. That is not possible. However scientifically we may be advanced, God is always more intelligent than ourselves. We should always remember.

#### violence, devotional service, Krishna's satisfaction

**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada explains that the violence in Bhagavad-gita is not for Arjuna's satisfaction but for Krishna's, highlighting the difference between ordinary work and devotional service.

Driven by selfish motives, one may be inclined to such sinful acts as the killing of one's own brother, father or mother. There are many such instances in the history of the world. But Arjuna, being a saintly devotee of the Lord, is always conscious of moral principles and therefore takes care to avoid such activities. The father's archon作者 Leånen Dumar. Translation. Alas, how strange it is that we are preparing to commit greatly sinful acts, driven by the desire to enjoy royal happiness. Nā Magnātī NāPartu Jigā Emmanuel Emmanuel. So sometimes Arjuna is accused, Bhagavad-gītā is also accused that there is violence. There is violence. Bhagavad-gītā is full of violence. Yes, it is full of violence, the war field, but here the Vaisnava thinking, Arjuna is thinking that it was arranged for his love, rājya-sukha, yad rājya-sukha-lobhī, loving. It was arranged for the satisfaction of Arjuna so that he could enjoy the kingdom and the happiness thereof. Actually it was not so. It was arranged by Krsna for his satisfaction, not for Arjuna's satisfaction.

#### devotional service, Krishna's mission, misunderstanding
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ewOaJaDBsak?t=203
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada contrasts devotional service with ordinary work, addressing the common misunderstanding that Bhagavad-gita promotes violence, and emphasizes that Krishna's actions are to fulfill His mission.

Yes. So that is the difference between ordinary work and devotional service. Devotional service and ordinary work, they look almost equal. Just like we are living in this house, the neighbors, they may think that something is wrong. Some people are living here, chanting, dancing. We also dance. We also sometimes sing and eating. They are also eating. So what is the difference? They may think that what is the difference between devotional service and ordinary work? It looks almost equal. Therefore people misunderstand that Bhagavad-gītā is ordinary warfare, violence. But it is not that. It is arranged by Krsna because to fulfill His mission, His mission is paritrāṇāṁ sādhunāṁ vināśāya ca dṛśkṛtām.

Simultaneously, two things. paritrāṇāṁ sādhunāṁ vināśāya ca dṛśkṛtām. So kinsman does not mean, in higher sense, only my brother or my sister or my father or my uncle. #### universal kinship, Krishna as Father, compassion

**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada elaborates on the concept of universal kinship, stating that Krishna is the Supreme Father of all living beings, and therefore a devotee of Krishna does not want to harm any living entity.

Actually, everyone is our sa janam because if God is father, as Krsna claims, ahaṁ vīja-pradapitāḥ, if He is the Supreme Father, not only He claims, at least any fine religious system claims God is the original Father. That's the fact. ahaṁ sarvasya prabhava matta-sarvaṁ prabhartate. Everything has come from Him. He is the Supreme Father. If Krsna is the Supreme Father, He is father of everyone. sarva-joni-sukha-antaya sarva-joni-sukha-antaya. In all species of life, in all forms of life, they are all our sa janam, kinsmen. How it cannot be? Because Krsna is the original Father. This is Krsna consciousness. Therefore, a devotee of Krsna does not want to commit a little harm to any living entity. That is Krsna consciousness. sarva-joni-sukha-antaya sarva-joni-sukha-antaya sarva-joni-sukha-antaya sarva-joni-sukha-antaya sarva-joni-sukha-antaya sarva-joni-sukha-antaya sarva-joni-sukha-antaya sarva-joni-sukha-antaya sarva-joni-sukha-antaya sarva-joni-sukha-antaya sarva-joni-sukha-antaya sarva-joni-sukha-antaya Yukta-cuse.
