#### spiritual vision, soul's dimension, karma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ewOaJaDBsak?t=574
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The living part, the soul is the same dimension either within the ant or in the elephant."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada explains that a Krishna conscious person sees everyone as part of the same family, regardless of their external form, because the soul within each being is of the same dimension.

The same member of the same family. It does not matter if one has become ant or one has become elephant. The living part, the soul is the same dimension either within the ant or in the elephant. This dimension of the soul we have several times discussed. One ten thousand part of the top of the air. kesagra satavagasya satadhā kalpitasya ca jīva-bhāga-sabhījña sa anantāya kalpate. There is the dimension. In śāstra we get the dimension of the soul. Very, very minute. One ten thousand part of the top of the air, just a minute. So that portion is within the ant and within brahmā and within elephant. Therefore, one who is pundit, one who knows what are these soul spiritual parts, part and parcel of Krsna, if he has got full knowledge, then his vision is vidyā-vinaya-samparni brāhmaṇi govī-hasthini śunite ca sapāke ca paṇḍitāḥ samadarsana. Samadarsana means equal vision. A learned brāhmaṇa is most intelligent man in the human society. And a dog superficially, externally, there is much difference. Here is a dog, a street dog, and here is a learned brāhmaṇa. But one who is pundit, one who is Krsna conscious, he sees that the pundit and the dog, they are the same, because they are also the same spiritual part. By his karma, he has become a learned pundit. And by his karma, he has become a dog.

#### soul, external behavior, sinful acts
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ewOaJaDBsak?t=690
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If we want to kill anyone, any living entity, for my satisfaction, either my tongue satisfaction or any sense satisfaction, it is mahāpāpaṁ, great sinful act."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada continues to explain the vision of a learned person, emphasizing that while external behavior may differ, internally, all beings are spiritual parts of God, and killing any living entity for personal satisfaction is a great sin.

But within the different body, dehi na smin jathā dehi kaumār, smin dehe, in this body, there is the soul. That is his vision. Of course, externally it is not that I shall behave equally with the brāhmaṇa and the dog. That is external behavior. But internally we should know that both the brāhmaṇa and the dog, they are spiritual parts. This is called brahma-jñāna. Brahma-jñāna means the knowledge of spiritual self. That is called brahma-jñāna. So when one attains this brahma-jñāna, then brahma-bhūta-prasannātmā na śocati na kānta samas sarveṣu bhūteṣu samatā equal. That is brahma-jñāna. So in this verse, Arjuna says that yad rājya sukha-lobhena hantuṁ śva-janam pūrdhataḥ. So when we are killing animals for the satisfaction of our tongue, this is mahāpātaḥ. Arjuna says ahavatau mahat-pāpaṁ mahat-pāpaṁ, great sinful act. Great sinful act. If we want to kill anyone, any living entity, for my satisfaction, either my tongue satisfaction or any sense satisfaction, it is mahāpāpaṁ, great sinful act.

#### universal family, sense gratification, real happiness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ewOaJaDBsak?t=800
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Unfortunately, everyone is killing for his sense satisfaction, gratification, in the name of religion."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada expands on Arjuna's limited view, explaining that all living entities are part of our universal family, and killing them for sense gratification is a great sin, even if done in the name of religion. He then contrasts material happiness with real happiness.

Because they are all śva-jana. You cannot kill. Either you take this sense or that sense. But Arjuna is speaking in a limited sense. He is thinking of his own family members. But if one is actually in knowledge, brahma-jñāna, he thinks in the same way that the lower animals, they are also our family members. And if I kill him for my satisfaction, my sense satisfaction, it is great sinful act. Unfortunately, everyone is killing for his sense satisfaction, gratification, in the name of religion. In the name of religion, in the name of religion, and although it is prohibited, still they are killing. Thus, imagine how much sinful activity they are doing and how they can be happy. Happiness, of course, a hawk also thinks that he is very happy, that he is eating stool, living in filthy place, and because he has got the facility of sex life without any discrimination, he may think happy life. But that is not happiness. Happiness is different thing. sukhaṁ ātyam tṛtaṁ yatraṁ atindriya grāhyam. If you want to feel happiness by your, this blunt material senses, that is not happiness. Happiness is beyond your material senses. ātyam tṛtaṁ. That is real happiness. Real happiness means it will never end. And you will never feel satiation. There is no more want. That is real happiness.

#### material vs. spiritual happiness, purified senses, Krishna's satisfaction
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ewOaJaDBsak?t=917
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Whatever we do for our own sense satisfaction, that is mahāpā, sinful activities. But if we do the same thing for Krsna's satisfaction, that is spiritual advancement."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada contrasts material and spiritual happiness, explaining that real happiness is beyond the material senses and is achieved when our senses are purified and used for the sake of Krishna.

Material happiness, there is no such thing. That you will feel immediately satiation. After enjoying any material happiness, a few minutes, you will feel again another, again another, again another. So, therefore, in the Bhagavad-gītā it is said, sukhaṁ ātyam tṛtaṁ yatraṁ. So, real joy, what is real happiness, that is not felt by these blunt material senses. What is that sense? That is purified senses. sarva-pādhi-vinirmuktaṁ tat-paratyena nirvana. When our senses are purified, tat-paratyena, for the sake of Krsna, when our senses are employed for the sake of Krsna, that is purified senses. rīśi-keṇa rīśi-keśa-sevanam bhakti-rīcchate. That is wanted. So, here, in the beginning, Arjuna is thinking in terms of his blunt senses. But the same thing he will do. He will do. ātyam tṛtaṁ atindriyaḥ purified senses. Just try to understand. This is Krsna consciousness. Arjuna is now thinking yad rājya-sukha-lobhena. For the matter of getting kingdom and sense gratification, I am going to kill my kinsmen. So it is great sin. That's a fact. If the warfare, in the Kuruksetra battlefield, was for Arjuna's sense satisfaction, then it was a great sin. But actually it is not being done for Arjuna's satisfaction. It is to be done for Krsna's satisfaction. So therefore the conclusion should be that whatever we do, if we do it for our own sense satisfaction, that is mahāpā, sinful activities. But if we do the same thing for Krsna's satisfaction, that is spiritual advancement. This is the difference. So outsider, they think that a karmī is working for his own satisfaction, and a devotee is working for Krsna's satisfaction. Although two things are, similarly, externally it appears the same thing, but there is great difference.

Whatever you do for your own satisfaction, for the satisfaction of your whims, that is mahāpā, great sin. The same thing, when you do for Krsna, that is opening your path to liberation, back to home, back to God. This is difference. You have to change the consciousness, what you are doing, for whom you are doing, for yourself or for Krsna. This is Krsna consciousness. And that is the perfection of life. In whatever position you are, whatever you are doing, it doesn't matter. That is confirmed in the Śrīmad-Bhāgavatam. adapyaṁ vīdīyaśreṣṭa-varṇāśrama-vibhāgaśaḥ sānuṣṭhitaśya-dharmanāṁ saṁsiddhī-haritauśanaṁ haritauśanaṁ means to satisfy the Supreme Lord. That is perfection. It doesn't matter what you are doing. varṇāśrama-vibhāgaśaṁ. First of all, whatever doing, it does not mean whatever nonsense you are doing. That will be accepted. No. varṇāśrama-vibhāgaśaṁ. God is the one who makes you happy. He is the one who makes you happy. He is the one who makes you happy. He is the one who makes you happy. He is the one who makes you happy.

#### Varnashrama, Vedic civilization, satisfying Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ewOaJaDBsak?t=1179
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "But if you act for the satisfaction of Krsna according to the prescription of your position, then your life is perfect. That is wanted."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada defines Varnashrama according to Vedic civilization, emphasizing that activities must be done according to prescribed duties, and perfection is achieved when those duties are performed for the satisfaction of Krishna.

So that's the definition of varṇāśrama. According to Vedic civilization, there is division of varṇa. Brāhmaṇa, kṣatriya, vaiśya, śūrya. So activities must be done according to the varṇāśrama prescription. A brāhmaṇa is ordered to do like this. Satyamādhama titikṣhā, aryava. So that is, therefore, it is called varnāsana. The prescribed duties are already there. Therefore perfect human society means, first of all, there must be this division. Brāhmaṇa, kṣatriya, vaiśya. And when they act the duties of that particular position, brāhmaṇa, kṣatriya, for the satisfaction of Krsna, that is perfection of life. It doesn't matter whether you are a śūdra or you are brāhmaṇa, but if you act for the satisfaction of Krsna according to the prescription of your position, then your life is perfect. That is wanted.

#### human civilization, coordinated divisions, Krishna's actions
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ewOaJaDBsak?t=1254
**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada explains that a perfect human civilization should be based on coordinated divisions of society, with everyone trained to satisfy Krishna, and clarifies that Krishna's actions are transcendental and not sinful.

The whole human civilization should be based on this principle. There must be division. The division is already there. They should be coordinated. Systematized. Not that everyone is brāhmaṇa. Brāhmaṇa means the intelligent man. So we should pick up the intelligent man. They should be trained as brāhmaṇa. Those who are martial, having fighting spirit, they should be selected as kṣatriya. Those who are for increasing money, mercantile mentality, they should be also collected. Similarly, śūdras. And they should be trained, everyone, to satisfy Krsna. Varnāsamo vibhāgasaḥ sānuṣṭitasya dharmasya saṁsiddhiḥ hṛtośaḥ. So here Arjuna is a fighter. He is engaged in fighting. Now he is thinking that is proper. He is Vaisnava. He is devotee. He is properly thinking that for my sense gratification, I am going to kill my kingdom. What a great sinful activity I am going to do. But actually Krsna is not engaging His devotee to act sinfully. No. That is not Krsna's business. Although superficially it appears that Krsna is engaging Arjuna to fight in the sinful activities. No. That is not sinful. Whatever Krsna does, it is not sinful. It is transcendental. The most pure activity. Therefore one, the rascals who do not understand Krsna, they say that Krsna is immoral.

#### sun analogy, purification, Krishna's influence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ewOaJaDBsak?t=1456
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Similarly, even though somebody comes to Krsna with some purpose which is not moral, but the man or woman who comes there, he becomes, he or she becomes purified."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada uses the analogy of the sun evaporating water from both clean and filthy places to illustrate that Krishna's influence purifies even those who approach Him with impure motives, without Krishna becoming tainted.

He, just like the sun, is very powerful. As we see in this material world, the sun, a material thing, and it is very powerful. But the sun is soaking water, taking water from the sea as well as from filthy place. See, He is also taking, what is called soaking? Evaporating, yes. Evaporating water from urine also. And fill the place where ditches. He is evaporating water as well as from the sea. But it does not mean by evaporating water from the sweat, ditch and urine, the sun is becoming polluted. No. Rather He is turning that place, what is called, prophylactic, antiseptic, by His sun sign. Similarly, even though somebody comes to Krsna with some purpose which is not moral, but the man or woman who comes there, he becomes, he or she becomes purified.

### Who is Humble and Meek? | HDG Srila Prabhupada | Bg 1.43 | 22.07.2024
**Speaker:** HDG Srila Prabhupada

#### Bhagavad Gita, Family Tradition, Hell
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xQNqH9zvCXQ?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "O Krishna, maintainer of the people, I have heard by disciplined succession that those who destroy family traditions dwell always in hell."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada reads verse 43 from Chapter 1 of the Bhagavad Gita, along with its translation and purport, emphasizing the consequences of destroying family traditions.

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya. Reading from Srimad Bhagavad Gita, Chapter 1, Observing the Armies on the Battlefield of Kuruksetra, Verse number 43: Utsanna kula-dharmāṇāṁ manuṣyāṇāṁ janārdana narake niyatam vāso bhavatīty anuśuśruma. Translation and purport by His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Srila Prabhupada: O Krishna, maintainer of the people, I have heard by disciplined succession that those who destroy family traditions dwell always in hell. Purport by His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Srila Prabhupada: Arjuna bases his argument not on his own personal experience but on what he has heard from the authorities; that is the way of receiving real knowledge. One cannot reach the real point of factual knowledge without being helped by the right person, one who is already established in that knowledge. There is a system in the varnasrama institution by which one has to undergo the process of ablation before death for his sinful activities. One who is always engaged in sinful activities must utilize the process of ablation, called the Prayaschitta. Without doing so, one surely will be transferred to the right path. One who is engaged in sinful activities will not be transferred to the left path. One who has been engaged in sinful activities should be transferred to hellish planets in order to undergo a miserable life as a result of sinful activities. This is Bhaktivedanta's purport. Bhaktivedanta Swami Srila Prabhupada. O Krishna, maintainer of the people, I have heard by disciplined succession that those who destroy family traditions dwell always in hell. Bhaktivedanta Swami Srila Prabhupada.

#### Goal of Life, Sadhya-Sadhana, Darwin's Theory
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xQNqH9zvCXQ?t=234
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Sādhya means what for this human form of life we have got. That is called sādhya, the goal, the goal of life."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada introduces the concepts of sādhya (the goal of life) and sādhana (the means to achieve that goal), contrasting it with Darwin's theory, which he criticizes for lacking a clear understanding of life's purpose.

Therefore in the Viṣṇu Purāṇa, when Caitanya Mahāprabhu was talking with Rāmānanda Rāya, so Caitanya Mahāprabhu inquired, what is the actual aim of life? Sādhya-sādhana. Sādhya and sādhana. What is the aim? Sādhya means what for this human form of life we have got. That is called sādhya, the goal, the goal of life. And sādhana. Sādhana means the activity by which we can attain, we can achieve that goal of life. This is called sādhya-sādhana. The Darwin theory, although they are given some idea of progressive evolution, but he does not know what is the goal of life, why this progress is there. That these rascals, they do not know. They simply just imagine it.

**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xQNqH9zvCXQ?t=330
**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada discusses the concept of transmigration of the soul and the struggle for happiness, emphasizing the need for a defined goal of life (śādhya) and the means (śādhana) to achieve it, as questioned by Caitanya Mahāprabhu.

Taking some hints from the Padma Purāṇa, Brahma Vaibhata Purāṇa. Asyadeṁ saturāṁ caiva jīva-jātiṣu. Lakkhāṁ stāṁ jīva-jātiṣu. There are 8,400,000 types of jīva-jāti. Asyadeṁ saturāṁ caiva lakkhāṁ stāṁ jīva-jātiṣu. This is already there. Brahmadvī. In this way, the living entity is wandering in the cycle of transmigration of the soul. So from this, Mr. Darwin might have taken some hints, but he could not explain properly. He could not catch up that Brahmadvī, who is wandering. So it is a chaos. So anyway, why this living entity is wandering, not fixed up? So there must be some goal. He is hankering after that. There must be some goal of life. To achieve that goal of life, that is called śādhya. Why we are struggling here? For happiness or something else, we are struggling. So this question was raised by Caitanya Mahāprabhu. What is the goal of life? Unless there is goal of life, why there is struggle? Why there is? There must be some goal of life, śādhya. And śādhana. śādhana means the means by which we can achieve that goal of life. That is called śādhana. śādhana.

#### Humility, Authority, Vedic Principle
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xQNqH9zvCXQ?t=472
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Everyone should think that, I am a pig, what is my value? That is really large. Humble and meek."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada criticizes the modern tendency to give opinions without proper knowledge, contrasting it with the Vedic principle of quoting authorities. He emphasizes humility, using examples of Caitanya Mahāprabhu and the author of Caitanya Caritamrita, who presented themselves as lower than a worm in stool.

So Rāmānanda Raya quoted, because when there is talk between two learned persons, they, just like nowadays, it has become a fashion, in my opinion, I think in this way. What nonsense? Why you can think? What is your knowledge? But he is very proud. Everyone writes that. The other day, Sir Alistair, Alistair Hardy came. He also said, it is my opinion. Nobody thinks that he is a nonsense number one. What is the value of his opinion? Nobody thinks. But this is the Vedic principle. Even Caitanya Mahāprabhu, he, when he was asked by Prakāśananda Sarasvati, that you are a sannyāsī, you do not engage yourself in the study of Vedānta. You are simply chanting Hare Kṛṣṇa, Hare Kṛṣṇa. So, because sannyāsī is supposed to be always reading Vedānta philosophy especially and all other philosophies. So what is this that you are chanting? Like a sentimental person. So Caitanya Mahāprabhu replied, yes, my Guru Mahārāja, spiritual master, saw me a fool number one. So Caitanya Mahāprabhu, it was known to Prakāśananda Sarasvati was a great learned scholar in his student life. So he is posing himself as a fool number one. So, this is the way, Caitanya Caitāmṛta, the author of Caitanya Caitāmṛta, he is presenting himself as, puriṣer ki thaita muhiṣe logiṣṭa, I am lower than the worm of the stool. Puriṣer ki thaita muhiṣe logiṣṭa, logiṣṭa means lower. Lowest. Jagai madhāi haite muhiṣe pāpiṣṭa. Jagai madhāi was taken. They are drunkards, woman-hunters, therefore they are sinful. So Caitanya Caitāmṛta, the author, says that jagai madhāi haite muhiṣe pāpiṣṭa. Although, this is the way, nobody thinks himself as, one has become very big man, and he has his own opinion to give. This is rascala. Everyone should think that, I am a pig, what is my value? That is really large.

#### Humility, Authority, Spiritual Progress
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xQNqH9zvCXQ?t=686
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Everyone should always think in fool number one. Therefore, they never will go on to become perfect."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada continues to emphasize humility, warning against thinking oneself perfect or above rules. He highlights Arjuna's example of quoting authoritative sources and contrasts it with giving one's own opinion.

Humble and meek. Nobody should think that, now I have learnt everything. I can surpass everyone. I have become above all rules and regulations. Now I have become paramāṁśa. This is rascala. Everyone should always think in fool number one. Therefore, they never will go on to become perfect. If we think that, now I have become perfect, paramāṁśa, then the spiritual regulatory principles will never be followed, and he will fall down. So, here Arjuna also says, nara ke niyataṁ vāsa bhavati iti anuśuṣyumaḥ Krishna, I have heard it from authoritative sense. He never says, Krishna, in my opinion, if it is done like that, then people will go to hell. He does not give his own opinion. He says, iti das anuśuṣyumaḥ abhā. This is called paramāṁśa-siṣṭha. Nobody should give his own opinion. He must quote the authoritative statement to support his proposition. So similarly, when Caitanya Mahāprabhu asked, what is the aim of life? And how to achieve it?

#### Authority, Goal of Life, Satisfying Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xQNqH9zvCXQ?t=803
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

So Rāmānanda Rāya, he did not give his own opinion. That in my opinion, like this. Here also, Arjuna says, iti anuśuṣyumaḥ I have heard it. Heard it means, suṣyumaḥ means heard from authority. So Rāmānanda Rāya said, that, the real purpose of life, goal of life, is to satisfy the Supreme Personality of God. Just like, as we are citizens, what is our duty? We want to satisfy the authority, the government. When one serves the government nicely, in this country, he is recognized as knight, he is recognized as Earl, as Lord, as, so many, they have got titles. Every country, when a person is a very exalted citizen, then he is recognized by the God. So if this is the system in our ordinary life, then, the aim of life should be to satisfy the supreme governor or supreme God. It is very common sense.

In each planet, there is government, there is authority, and above all of them, there is the supreme authority, Krishna, Isvara Parama Krishna, Svacchidananda Vidra. This is the Shastric Indian. There are many controllers. In this planet, there is controller. There is another planet, another planet. Even Brahma is the controller of the whole universe. Just like in our government, there is system. One department, several departments is being managed by another director. Several director is being managed by another secretary. And the same system. Krishna is the supreme proprietor. Sarva Loka Maheswara. And he has got many secretary's assistants. They are called Brahma. So Brahma means the manager of one Brahma universe. And there are millions of Brahmas. Millions, trillions. They are numberless. Ananta Koti. Jasya Prabha Prabhavatu Jagadanya Koti. Koti. Koti means unlimited. Jagadanya. Jagadanya. Jagadanya means universe. Brahmaanda or Jagadanya. Anda.

In the human life, if they do not follow the regulative principles of family life, family tradition and systematic arrangement of four division of the society, Brahman, Kshatriya, Bhakti. Jati-dharma means, if you are born in the family of a Brahmana, you have got particular duties. Simply, you have to engage in this duties. Simply if you think yourself that I am in, I am born in a Brahman family, my duty is all finished. Now I can do whatever nonsense. They think like that. I have spoken. They are very much proud of taking birth in Brahmana family. So when others are engaged in devotional service, they say, oh, you are born in lower family. You have got to do this. We are born in Brahmana family. We have nothing to do. But they do not know that jati-dharma-kula-grhana, utsanna-kula-dharmana. If you are actually Brahmana, you must keep your family tradition. That is Brahmana. Otherwise, if you sacrifice everything of your family tradition, just like in your country, the lower family, the lower family is maintained. The government, the lower family has got money deposited with the government. And government gives the interest so that the family tradition may be maintained. The aristocratic style. #### Chaos, Sense Gratification, Goswami, Sense Control
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xQNqH9zvCXQ?t=1874
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Instead of becoming servant of the senses, you have to become master of the senses. That is called gosvam."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada explains that the world is in chaos because people are eager to serve their senses instead of Vishnu. He contrasts being a servant of the senses (godas) with being a master of the senses (gosvam), which is achieved by engaging the senses in the service of Krishna.

Otherwise they are never like that. Therefore, the whole world is in chaos. They are not eager to have Visnu. are simply eager to serve their senses. Godas, servant of the senses. So instead of becoming servant of the senses, you have to become master of the senses. That is called gosvam. If you remain servant of the senses, then you are godas. Kamadinankati dhanakati. That is animal life. And when you become master of the senses, how it is possible? Master of the senses. Now, if you employ your senses always in the service of Krsna, your senses are already controlled. Just like your tongue. Your tongue asks you to go to the restaurant and eat some nice thing. But if you are a Vaishnava, then you are not a Vaishnava. You are not a Vaishnava. If you have this vow that I shall not allow my tongue to eat anything except prasadam, it is already controlled. Gosvam. That means to control the senses means to engage the senses in the service of the Lord. This is control. Otherwise, by force, just like the yogis, they try artificially. By some force, they try artificially. But some gymnastics. That may be possible for some time. But if it is, there are many instances, it becomes impossible. Because by force, you cannot control the senses. That is not possible.

You must give better and just better. If I force, you think that I shall control my tongue, I shall control my eyes, I shall control my eyes, or I shall control my belly. Artificial. But maybe possible. But sometimes. But they are so strong. The senses are so strong. Artificially, it can have this power. Param drstasyam. Stated in the Bhagavad-gita. That is, give the senses better engagement. Then you can control. Risi-kena risi-kesa sevana-dvaha sarva-pādipi-nenmukta-tattva-tena. You have to purify the senses. How? By engaging the senses in the sarva-paratvena. In fact, then you will be able to purify the senses. And purified senses, when you are engaged in the service of the Supreme, that is called bhakti. So if we are actually human beings, manushyana, pulo-dharma, this family tradition is not meant for the cats and dogs. If you live life, cats, no question of family tradition. Yes. Yes. Yes. Yes. Yes. Yes. Yes. Yes. Yes. Yes. Yes. Yes. Yes. Yes. Yes. Yes. Yes. Yes. Yes. Risi-kena risi-kesa sevana-dvaha sarva-pādipi-nenmukta-tena. So if you want to live like human beings, manushyana, then this system must be karyatā puruṣiṇa-paraptam āyata-manjmaya-sviṣṭhaṁ dhuna-gana-vihāgya, then society must be divided into four classes. As we have got, four divisions in the Mahākārīya, Vaisya, Śūdra must be there. And each one should serve according to his guṇa, karma, quality and capacity to work. ### Why Seek the Shelter of the Supreme Lord? | Srila Prabhupada
**Speaker:** HDG Srila Prabhupada

#### devotion, material desires, benefits of Krishna worship
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/616K-1_1lPo?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Kṛṣṇa will fulfill your material desires and at the end will give shelter at His lotus feet."
**Summary:** This segment explains that while people seek temples for material gains, worshiping Kṛṣṇa fulfills desires and ultimately grants shelter at His lotus feet, unlike worshiping demigods which offers temporary and finite benefits.

People generally go to temples and churches for mitigating some material wants. Even if you have material desires, still you take shelter of Kṛṣṇa. Kṛṣṇa will fulfill your material desires and at the end will give shelter at His lotus feet. This is the advantage of devotion and service. Whereas if you worship other demigods, you may get some temporary benefit, but at the end the benefit will be finished, you will be finished, and the demigod who has given you the benediction, he will also be finished. But even one has got some material desires to be fulfilled, if he worships Kṛṣṇa, that desires will be fulfilled. At the same time, he will get shelter under the lotus feet of the Lord. This is the advantage.

**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is the Supreme Personality of Godhead, no one is equal to or above him, he is the primeval Lord or Bhagavan known as Govinda and he is the supreme cause of all causes."
**Summary:** This segment explains the three phases of understanding the Absolute Truth: Brahman, Paramatma, and Bhagavan, using the analogy of the sun and establishing Krishna as the Supreme Personality of Godhead.

Of life they do not lead to higher planets but to infamy report by Srila Prabhupada. Krishna and the Supreme Personality of Godhead are identical, therefore Lord Krishna is referred to as Bhagawan throughout the Gita. Bhagawan is the ultimate in the Absolute Truth. Absolute Truth is realized in three phases of understanding, namely Brahman or the impersonal all-pervasive spirit, Paramatma or the centralized aspect of the Supreme within the heart of all living entities, and Bhagawan or the Supreme Personality of Godhead. In the Srimad Bhagavatam this conception of the Absolute Truth is explained thus: vadanti tattva vidas tattvam yajnana madhvayam brahmeti paramatmeti bhagavaneti shabdhyate. The Absolute Truth is realized in three phases of understanding by the knower of the Absolute Truth and all of them are identical. Such phases of the Absolute Truth are expressed as Brahman, Paramatma, and Bhagawan. These three Divine aspects can be explained by the example of the sun, which also has three different aspects, namely the sunshine, the sun's surface, and the sun planet itself. One who studies the sunshine only is the preliminary student, one who understands the sun's surface is further advanced, and one who can enter into the sun planet is the highest. Ordinary students who are satisfied by simply understanding the sunshine, its universal pervasiveness, and the glaring effulgence of its impersonal nature may be compared to those who can realize only the Brahman feature of the Absolute Truth. The student who has advanced further can know the sun disc, which is compared to knowledge of the Paramatma feature of the Absolute Truth, and the student who can enter into the heart of the Sun planet is compared to those who realize the personal features of the Supreme Absolute Truth. Therefore the bhaktas or the transcendentalists who have realized the Bhagawan feature of the Absolute Truth are the top most transcendentalists. Although all students who are engaged in the study of the Absolute Truth are engaged in the same subject matter, the sunshine, the sun disc, and the inner affairs of the Sun planet cannot be separated from one another, and yet the students of the three different phases are not in the same category. The Sanskrit word Bhagawan is explained by the great authority Parashara Muni, the father of Vyasa Deva: the Supreme personality who possesses all riches, all strength, all fame, all beauty, all knowledge, and all renunciation is called Bhagawan.

---
#### Krishna's Supremacy, Aryan Values, Liberation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/e3SxWTcHSKk?t=240
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Only Krishna can claim this because he is the Supreme personality of Godhead."
**Summary:** This segment continues to establish Krishna's supremacy, contrasting Him with other powerful beings, and then discusses Aryan values, emphasizing spiritual realization and liberation from material bondage.

There are many personalities possessing the qualities of Bhagawan, but Krishna is the Supreme because none can excel him. He's a Supreme person and his body is eternal, full of knowledge and Bliss. He is the Primeval Lord Govinda and the cause of all causes. In the bhagavatham also there is a list of many incarnations of the Supreme personality of Godhead, but Krishna is described as the original personality of Godhead from whom many many incarnations and personalities of Godhead expand. Krishna is the one who is eternal and full of knowledge and bliss, he is the Supreme personality whom many incarnations and personalities of Godhead expand. All the lists of the incarnations of Godhead submitted herewith are either plenary expansions or parts of the plenary expansions of the Supreme Godhead, but Krishna is the Supreme Personality of Godhead Himself. Therefore, Krishna is the original Supreme Personality of Godhead, the Absolute Truth, the source of both the Supersoul and the Impersonal Brahman. In the presence of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Arjuna's lamentation for his kinsmen is certainly unbecoming, and therefore Krishna expressed His surprise with the word, kutas, wherefrom? Such unmanly sentiments were never expected from a person belonging to the civilized class of men known as Aryans. The word Aryan is applicable to persons who know the value of life and have a civilization based on spiritual realization. Persons who are led by the material conception of life do not know that the aim of life is really to achieve the goal of life. They are led by the realization of the Absolute Truth, Vishnu or Bhagavan, and they are captivated by the external features of the material world, and therefore they do not know what liberation is. Persons who have no knowledge of liberation from material bondage are called non-Aryans. Although Arjuna was a Kshatriya, he was deviating from his prescribed duties by declining to fight. This act of cowardice is described as befitting the non-Aryans. Such deviation from duty does not help one in the program, nor does it even give one the opportunity to become famous in this world. Lord Krishna did not approve of the so-called compassion of Arjuna for his kinsmen.

#### Bhagavan, Vyasadeva, Krishna's Supremacy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/e3SxWTcHSKk?t=395
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** This segment emphasizes that Krishna is the Supreme Personality of Godhead, as established by Vyasadeva's writing in the Bhagavad-gita, and refutes the idea that anyone can claim to be Bhagavan.

The Supreme Person, Bhagavan, said, My dear Arjuna, how have these impurities come upon you? They are not at all befitting a man who knows the progressive values of life. They do not lead to higher planets, but to infamy. So, in this verse, Bayas Dev is writing, of course, the speaking is through, but the original writer is Bayas Dev. For in other shlokas he writes Arjuna Uvaj, Sanjaya Uvaj, like that. Similarly, he could write here Krishna Uvaj. He could write, no, he is writing Bhagavān Uvaj. So, Krishna is the Supreme Personality of Godhead. By this writing, it is established, see Bhagavān Uvaj. He cannot be equal with Arjuna or Sanjaya or anybody else. He cannot be equal with Arjuna or Sanjaya or anybody else. Nobody is equal to him. Nobody is higher than him. Everyone is lower. That is the meaning of Bhagavān. Nobody can claim, I am Bhagavān.

**Verse References:** —

**Summary:** This segment defends Vyasadeva against criticisms from Mayavadi philosophers, emphasizing his authority as an incarnation of Narayana and the importance of receiving knowledge through disciplic succession.

But nowadays there are so many rascals. They are claiming that everyone is Bhagavān. But Vyāsadeva is mistaken. Actually, these Māyāvādī philosophers, they are so great rascals, they sometimes say like that, that Vyāsadeva is also mistaken. They have got the audacity to say, Vyāsadeva is accepted as incarnation of Nārāyaṇa. Who has given us the Vedic literature. So many books he has given. The Vedas, the Puranas, Mahābhārata, Śrīmad-Bhāgavatam, Vedānta-Śūtra. Everything is given by Vyāsadeva in writing. And he is accepted as Nārāyaṇa, incarnation of Nārāyaṇa. He is also Mahābhūnī-kṛte. He is also Vyās-mūṇī. But he is also said Mahābhūnī-kṛte. There cannot be any mistake in the words of Vyāsadeva. This is the difficulty. If one does not come through the channel of disciple succession. So there. In so many ways mistaken Vyāsadeva is above all this. He is not an ordinary writer for material description, for material name and place. He cannot be mistaken as Bhagavān. Kṛṣṇa cannot be mistaken. Similarly Vyāsadeva, incarnation of Bhagavān, he also cannot be mistaken. Neither devotee of Kṛṣṇa can be mistaken. Devotee of Kṛṣṇa, he does not say anything as his own opinion. He never says what Kṛṣṇa says. He says, he may be not perfect, but what Kṛṣṇa has said, that is perfect.

#### Pure Devotee, Perfect Knowledge, Bhagavan's Qualities
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/e3SxWTcHSKk?t=679
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A pure devotee who does not say anything beyond which was spoken by Kṛṣṇa, therefore his statement is also without mistake."
**Summary:** This segment explains that a pure devotee's statements are also without mistake because they only repeat what Krishna has said. It contrasts this with the four defects of those in the material world: committing mistakes, being illusioned, having imperfect senses, and cheating.

Therefore a pure devotee who does not say anything beyond which was spoken by Kṛṣṇa, therefore his statement is also without mistake. Common man within this material world, he commits mistake. To any human, even big big personality, they commit mistake. But Nārāyaṇa para, he is transcendental. Kṛṣṇa is transcendental. There cannot be any mistake. There cannot be any illusion. Those who are within this material world, they have got four defects. They commit mistake, they are illusion, and their senses are imperfect. Brahmapramādvī and they are cheaters. Just like modern day scientists and philosophers, they propagate so many branches of knowledge, but when on the question of knowledge, the crucial point, they are caught. They say, I do not know perfect. We are trying to know. In future we shall tell you the perfect. But if you are not in perfect knowledge, why should you take the post of a teacher? If your knowledge is imperfect, then whatever you speak, that is imperfect. Therefore with imperfect knowledge, why you should become a teacher? That is cheating. Therefore purposefully Vyāsadeva is writing śrī-bhagavān vāca, where there is no cheating, no imperfection, no illusion, no mistake, four things: no mistake, no illusion, no cheating, and no imperfection. This is bhagavān.

**Summary:** This segment explains why the Bhagavad-gita is taken so seriously, contrasting it with other books written by defective authors. It emphasizes the importance of the parampara system (disciplic succession) for receiving perfect knowledge.

Why you are taking bhagavad-gītā so seriously? There are so many other books we can read, so many theories, so many philosophers, big big philosophers, but we cannot take them because they are defective. The author is sure to commit mistake, he is illusioned, because his senses are not perfect, therefore imperfection. So with all these defects we cannot accept anyone's knowledge. This is Vedic process. This is called paramparā system, disciplic succession. We receive knowledge perfectly from the supreme bhagavān. And if I receive the knowledge from bhagavān, and if I distribute the same knowledge as bhagavān has said, without any interpretation of my cheating policy, then the knowledge which I distribute, that is also perfect. I may not be perfect, but the knowledge which I have taken from Kṛṣṇa, if I present it as it is, without any interpretation, then what I give you, that is perfect. It is very easy to understand.

#### Spiritual Master, Caitanya Mahaprabhu, Superior Authority
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/e3SxWTcHSKk?t=921
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Even without any qualification one can become spiritual master."
**Summary:** Using the analogy of a peon delivering a money order, this segment explains how anyone can become a spiritual master by following the order of a superior authority, as emphasized by Caitanya Mahaprabhu.

I have given several times, just like a peon has brought a money order for you, thousand dollars. I have given a thousand dollars for you. Just like a peon has brought a money order for you, thousand dollars. Just like a peon has brought a money order for you, thousand dollars. So he is giving you. So you cannot say, here is a peon, he is a poor man, how he can give me one thousand dollars? He is not giving. The money is sent by somebody else. He is simply handing it over. Therefore Caitanya Mahāprabhu says āmā rāgdhāya guruhaiyā tāraḥ sarva-deśaḥ, he is asking everyone to become a spiritual master. So how everyone can become spiritual master? A spiritual master have sufficient knowledge and so many other qualification? No, even without any qualification one can become spiritual master. How? The process is Caitanya Mahāprabhu says āmā rāgdhāya guruhaiyā tāraḥ sarva-deśaḥ. Even without any qualification one can become spiritual master. How? On my order. That is the crucial point. One does not become spiritual master by his own wings. That is not spiritual master. He must be ordered by superior authority, then he is spiritual. Āmā rāgdhāya. Just like in our case, our superior authority, our spiritual master, he ordered me that you just try to preach this gospel, whatever you have learned from me in English. That I have tried to execute the order of superior authority. That's all. The only qualification is that I have tried to execute the order of superior authority. This is the secret of success.

#### Bhagavan, Impersonalism, Absolute Truth
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/e3SxWTcHSKk?t=1416
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "भगवान means person."
**Summary:** This segment explains that Bhagavan means person, refuting impersonalism, and describes the three features of the Absolute Truth: Brahman, Paramatma, and Bhagavan, using the analogy of fire.

So here, भगवान वाज. वैस्तिम् दोस्तों नहीं कहते है कि कृष्णा वाज. If you would have said कृष्णा, then people would have misunderstood. He is directly speaking भगवान वाज, the Supreme Personality of Godhead. So anyone who is impersonalist, how he can understand भगवाद गीता? भगवान means person. भगवान is not imperson. The absolute truth is manifested in three features. ब्रह्म is the beginning, impersonal. Because just like fire, fire is burning somewhere, but its heat and light is impersonal. Suppose here is big fire, just like we got fire place, that is in one corner, where the whole room you are feeling heat. That heat is impersonal. But the fire place where there is blazing fire, that is personal. So impersonal conception is the offshoot of the person. That will be explained in the 13th chapter. That everyone, everywhere I am spread. I exist everywhere. How does he exist? By his energy. That energy is impersonal. But the Supreme Person, he is not impersonal. He is personal.

It also discusses Krishna's criticism of Arjuna's compassion from a material point of view.

Therefore it is said, भगवान means who is full with six kinds of appearance: richest, the most famous, the most learned, the most beautiful, the most strong, and the most renouncing, he is Bhagavān. So Bhagavān is the ultimate understanding of the Absolute Truth. Just like when you feel temperature, just like we feel temperature from the sunshine, heat, and light. The sun is giving heat and light. Everyone can enjoy the light and heat. But if you trace where from this heat and light is coming, then you go to the sun planet. That is localized. That is not important. And again, if you enter into the sun planet, then you will see the sun gone. We were shunned. So we should not conclude final simply by heat and light. So Brahmā understanding, impersonal understanding of the Absolute Truth is imperfect understanding, partial understanding. It is not full understanding. Full understanding is Bhagavān. Therefore it is stated here, śrī-bhagavān vāca. There cannot be any mistake that is final. So Bhagavān is criticizing. Arjuna became very good man. Why is he like this? I cannot kill my kinsmen. From material point of view, people will very much appeaset. He is Arjuna, he is so nice, Don't Fourth. He is purgohing. He's claiming. He has gimla. He is asâtrā daa, bow and arrows by legs, lips, teeth. He is no longer fighting. He has decided not to fight with his kinsmen. He has decided not to kill his own man. So from material point of view, Arjuna is an animal.

#### Killing, Self-Defense, Progress
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/e3SxWTcHSKk?t=1853

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

But if a tiger comes to attack you, you must kill, that is self-defense and that is not himself. So a devotee knows, a Krishna conscious person knows when to kill and when not to kill, but it is not that because we accept not killing, therefore in every case killing should be stopped. No. If there is necessity, killing should be accepted. Therefore Arjuna decided not to kill, not to fight, and Krishna says that this is anārya-duṣṭam, this kind of decision is made by the uncivilized rasa. Anārya-duṣṭam. Asargaṁ. Akhirti-karam. So many things. Asargaṁ. Because the aim of human life should be to make progress. That is called Arjuna.

Progressing, marching. Now we have got this human form and knowledge. Progressing from the lowest species of life in aquatic, then trees, plants, insects, birds, beasts. Eight million four hundred thousand. Now I have got this civilized form of body. Then my endeavour should be how to make further progress. This further progress is described in the Bhagavad-gītā. That you can go to the higher planetary system. Higher planetary system. In the middle planetary system. Those who are contaminated with the passion quality, they remain without it. And those who are miscreant, जघन्न गुणवित्तिष्ट, most hatefully accustomed. This is hatefully accustomed. Illicit sex, meat-eating, gambling, intoxication. These are habits of hateful nature. They go down. They become animals of the same kinds of lower planetary system. So, आर्ज्य means must make progress. अनार्ज्य means one who does not know what is progress of life. They think there is no life after death. So, I have got this life now. Let me enjoy my senses to the best capacity. This is अनार्ज्य, demon.

#### Guru qualification, order of Guru, rubber-stamped guru
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/S9Nfm8KMcZc?t=350
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The key principle here is that to become guru, one must be ordered by one's guru."

And in small temple you have guru. That's all. No obligation, no preaching, simply brings a foodstuff, my Guru Mahārāj, joint mess, place for eating, our ātya khana, joint mess. Exactly. So we see here, Śrīla Prabhupāda emphasized that a rubber-stamped guru won't be effective. The key principle here is that to become guru, one must be ordered by one's guru. In other words, any person ordered by his guru to become guru is authorized. And any person who is not ordered by his guru, but he becomes guru, some or the other, either by himself or somebody else tells him, or there is a body of some people who give him a no objection to become a guru, some or the other, he becomes guru, then he is not bona fide, he is rubber-stamped or unauthorized guru.

#### Srila Prabhupada's health, Vrindavan, will
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/S9Nfm8KMcZc?t=459
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker transitions to May 1977, detailing Srila Prabhupada's deteriorating health, the decision to move him to a more suitable location, and his subsequent instruction to make his will, indicating his decision to leave the material world.

Now, let us move on in history to the month of May in the same year, 1977. This month means that one has to take the terms of Jayasabila and Chandrasurga for every business Day of Ram. What had happened was, by May of 1977, Śrīla Prabhupāda's health had deteriorated considerably. Therefore, devotees had decided to shift Śrīla Prabhupāda to a more salubrious place, so that Śrīla Prabhupāda could recover from his limp念. So, they chose H capsule ministry. H capsule ministry in Uttarakhand? H capsule ministry in Uttarakhand? was the place which was chosen for Srila Prabhupada to recuperate. On the 7th of May in 1977, Srila Prabhupada left for Rishikesh by a night train. Overnight, they travelled from Delhi to Rishikesh. And there in Rishikesh, they had taken a place on rent and some few devotees and Prabhupada was staying there. While they were staying there, on the 14th of May, in the night, there was a fierce thunderstorm. There was a power cut. It was pitch black in the night and there was lightning, thunder and strong winds were blowing the whole night. Devotees were all shaken up. The next day, on May 15th, Srila Prabhupada called the devotees and instructed them to make his will. So now, this was a clear indication that Srila Prabhupada had decided to leave the material world.

#### Vrindavan, GBC meeting, arrangements
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/S9Nfm8KMcZc?t=585
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker continues the narrative, describing Srila Prabhupada's desire to die in Vrindavan, the arrangements made to transport him there, and his subsequent order to the GBCs to come to Vrindavan.

Now, the very next day after that, on the 16th of May, Srila Prabhupada again called the devotees. At 2.30 am, Prabhupada was sitting up straight on his bed and he said, if I am going to die, then let it be in Vrindavan. Now, it was more clear what Srila Prabhupada had decided. So, the devotees immediately started making arrangements and maybe they could not arrange train tickets at such a short notice or for whatever reason, they got a car to take Srila Prabhupada to Vrindavan. So the same day, Srila Prabhupada was taken to Delhi by car. And there is Delhi in the evening. They stayed in Delhi for the night. And again in the morning, the next day, May 17th, they left from Delhi by car and went to Vrindavan. And in this way, Prabhupada reached Vrindavan on the 17th of May, 1977. There many of you who would have visited the Krishnabalram temple in the guest house, there is Prabhupada's quarters. And Prabhupada was staying there. Five days after reaching Vrindavan on the 22nd of May, Srila Prabhupada said, it is better to plan for the worst. And he called all the GBCs to come to Vrindavan. So based on this order of Prabhupada on 22nd of May, by May 28th, most of the GBC members had reached Vrindavan.

#### GBC meeting, questions, future of ISKCON
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/S9Nfm8KMcZc?t=673
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "After due deliberations in the meeting, the GBC chose five questions that they wanted answers from Srila Prabhupada for the time after his departure."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the GBC meeting in Vrindavan, noting that only 18 out of 23 members were present, and outlines the five questions they prepared to ask Srila Prabhupada regarding the future of ISKCON after his departure.

And they held a meeting in Vrindavan. At that time in ISKCON, there were 23 GBC members. But only 18 were able to make it to Vrindavan for the meeting. After due deliberations in the meeting, the GBC chose five questions that they wanted answers from Srila Prabhupada for the time after his departure. So the 18 GBC members were present there, noted down the five questions they wanted to ask Srila Prabhupada. What were those questions? The questions were, number one, how long should GBC members remain in office? Number two, how can GBC members who leave be replaced? So actually, you see, these two questions are relevant only in Prabhupada's disappearance. Because as long as Prabhupada was there, the GBC members were continuing and whoever was leaving, Prabhupada was replacing them. So this question was coming up because in future, when Prabhupada is not there, how will they do it? Question number three, in the absence of Srila Prabhupada, what is the procedure for first, second and sannyas initiations? Question number four, what is the relationship of the person who gives this initiation to the person he gives it to? And question number five, the last question, is there any provision for publication of other translations of Vaishnava scriptures by the BBT after the departure of Srila Prabhupada?

#### Rithviks, July 9th directive, Srila Prabhupada's appointment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/S9Nfm8KMcZc?t=1237
**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that Srila Prabhupada's response to question three was to appoint officiating Acharyas or Rithviks, not Diksha Gurus, and that this was confirmed by the July 9th directive, which is not a temporary arrangement but meant for the time when Srila Prabhupada is appointed.

If Shaila Prabhupada wanted them to become Gurus, he could have said that he is going to recommend some of them to act as Diksha Gurus or Gurus. But no. The moment the question was put to Shaila Prabhupada, how initiations will be conducted in the future, particularly at that time, when Prabhupada is no longer with us, Shaila Prabhupada, without hesitating for a moment, or waiting to think, or considering different options, should we do like this, or should we have this system, or we have that system, there was no such consideration. Prabhupada immediately responded with officiating Acharyas. A clear, straight question and a clear, straight answer. And we all know that Shaila Prabhupada did choose eleven people to act as Rithvik, representative of the Acharya, which, in other words, is officiating Acharya. And when did Shaila Prabhupada do this? He did this in the July 9th, 1977 directive. Hence, the July 9th directive appointing Rithviks is not a temporary arrangement, but it is meant for the time, particularly when Shaila Prabhupada is appointed. It is not a temporary arrangement. But it is meant for the time, particularly when Shaila Prabhupada is appointed. It is meant for the time, particularly when Shaila Prabhupada is appointed.

#### Guru, initiation, Rithvik, Srila Prabhupada
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/S9Nfm8KMcZc?t=1424
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The person who is giving initiation, the initiator is the Guru. And the officiator is the Rithvik."
**Summary:** The speaker analyzes Srila Prabhupada's response to Satsvarupa Dasa's question, highlighting that Prabhupada reiterates the principle that without one's Guru's order, no one can become Guru, and clarifies that the person giving initiation (Srila Prabhupada) is the Guru, while the officiator is the Rithvik.

So, now, let us see what is Prabhupada's response to Satswaroop Dasa's question. Prabhupada, he is Guru. He is Guru, Satswaroop. But he does it on your behalf. Prabhupada, yes, that is formality. Because in my presence, one should not become Guru. So, on my behalf, on my order, Amar Ajnayi Guru Haya. Be actually Guru, but by my order. So, here, we can clearly see that Prabhupada is reiterating the same principle which he discussed in the April 22nd meeting, April 22nd discussion between Prabhupada and Tamalkrishna Goswami. Without one's Guru's order, no one can become Guru. Shaila Prabhupada was stating this principle. Another point to note here is that Satswaroop Dasa wanted to ask what is the relationship between the Rithvik and the devotee who is getting initiated. But instead, he ended up asking, what is the relationship between the person who gives initiation and the disciple? Actually, in Shaila Prabhupada's mind, it is clear that there are two people. One who gives initiation, which is Shaila Prabhupada, and the other one who officiates, which is the Rithvik. So, when Satswaroop Dasa asked what is the relationship of the person who gives initiation, and the disciple, Shaila Prabhupada immediately said that he is Guru. What does that mean? The person who is giving initiation, the initiator is the Guru. And the officiator is the Rithvik. Initiator is Shaila Prabhupada and he is Guru. It is very clear, abundantly clear.

#### scripture, introduction

#### divine will, devotee
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/weLxSeRIE6Q?t=34
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A devotee, once accepted by the Lord, can never fall down. That is the conclusion of this incident."
**Summary:** The speaker reads the translation and purport, emphasizing the Lord's approval of events and the devotee's security.

Translation: The Lord then said to His attendants, Jaya and Vijaya, depart this place, but fear not. All glories unto you. Though I am capable of nullifying the Brahmanas' curse, I would not do so. On the contrary, it has my approval. Purport: As explained in connection with Text No. 26, all the incidents that took place had the approval of the Lord. Ordinarily, there is no possibility that the four sages could be so angry with the Dharmasur, nor could the Supreme Lord neglect His two doorkeepers, nor can one come back from the Vaikuntha after taking birth there. All these incidents, therefore, were designed by the Lord Himself for the sake of His pastime in the material world. Thus, He plainly says that it was done with His approval. Otherwise, it would have been impossible for inhabitants of Vaikuntha to come back to this material world simply because of Brahmanical curse. The Lord especially blesses the so-called culprits. All glories unto you. A devotee, once accepted by the Lord, can never fall down. That is the conclusion of this incident. Thus ends the Bhaktivedanta Purport.

---
#### divine will, suffering, faith
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/weLxSeRIE6Q?t=135
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Without the Lord's approval, not even a blade of grass can move."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the concept of the Lord's approval in all events, including evil, and how this can challenge one's faith.

So, these are the words spoken by Lord to Jaya and Vijaya: Depart this place, but fear not. All glories unto you. Though I am capable of nullifying the Brahmana's curse, I would not do so. On the contrary, it has my approval. So, here Prabhupada mentions that all the incidents that took place had the approval of the Lord. We all have heard of this common phrase, that is not a blade of grass moves without the Lord. Without the Lord's approval, not even a blade of grass can move. Many people who have faith in the Lord, and when they hear this statement that not a blade of grass moves without the sanction of the Lord, they find it difficult to reconcile that why there is so much evil in this world. It is not a blade of grass that moves without the Lord's sanction. Terrorism is on rise. Suicide bombing is on rise. So many innocent people, apparently innocent people are getting killed. If not a blade of grass moves without the Lord's sanction, then why all this is happening? So, some people who are skeptical, with Komala Shraddha. Shraddha is there, but it is very tender. They think that probably things have gone out of control of the Lord also, because of Kali Yuga. The influence of Kali Yuga is so, maybe things have gone out of control. Or, maybe the Lord is not so powerful to deal with Asuras in this Kali Yuga, or so many of evil minded. So, all kinds of doubts may cloud a person's thinking or consciousness, when we see these incidents happening.

#### devotee, karma, divine will
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/weLxSeRIE6Q?t=277
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Even the so-called things happening in this world, crime, terrorism, innocent people getting killed, it has Lord's approval."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the example of Jaya and Vijaya's curse to illustrate how even devotees experience difficulties with the Lord's approval, and how this can be bewildering.

Even here we see, Jaya and Vijaya are being cursed. And, although on one side the Lord says that, Naame Bhakta Pranishchati, a devotee of mine will never perish. Krishna also says, Yad Gattva Na Nivartante Taddhamam Paramam Mama. After having gone there, the Vaikuntha, Goloka, Vrindavan, the spiritual planets, we don't come back. So, here Prabhupada says, it would have been impossible for inhabitants of Vaikuntha to come back to this material world. But here we see, Jaya and Vijaya coming down. Here we see, ardent devotees of the Lord. To be a doorkeeper of the Lord, is not an ordinary thing. Just like to be a personal assistant, or even a stenographer, or even a peon in the office of, let's say Prime Minister, is not a small job. So, unless one is an exalted devotee, he will not get any personal service to the Lord. Here Jaya and Vijaya are, personal attendants of the Lord, doorkeepers. And the Lord has sanctioned, they getting cursed, and coming down to this material world. So, sometimes these situations are very bewildering. But here, in the verse it's mentioned, all this has my approval. Even the so-called things happening in this world, crime, terrorism, innocent people getting killed, it has Lord's approval. It is not that it is out of control of the Lord. The Lord has sanctioned it.

#### karma, devotee, divine intervention
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/weLxSeRIE6Q?t=397
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In the material world, when one is not a devotee, it's pure law of karma working. As you sow, so shall you reap."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the difference between how karma affects devotees versus non-devotees, emphasizing the Lord's active role in directing the lives of devotees.

In the material world, when one is not a devotee, it's pure law of karma working. As you sow, so shall you reap. And Krishna is just, impersonally sanctioning. Somebody has Punya, Krishna approves it. He gets the results of Punya, in the form of prosperity, happiness, good times. And somebody has done lot of Baba, Krishna, just impersonally, sanctions, approves, misery. Whereas in case of devotees, just like here, Jaya and Vijaya, and in the case of the Four Kumaras, Lord is not, impersonally, sanctioning. Lord is not just the witnesser, and sanctioner. Lord plays a very important role, in the lives of a devotee. He is a witnesser, a witnesser, and a witnesser. And when the Lord talks about, witnesser, sanctioner and director. Somebody who is not a devotee just has witnesser and sanctioner. A third component comes in play in the life of devotees when someone tries to surrender to the Lord or surrenders to the Lord, the Lord becomes witnesser, sanctioner and director. Many times devotees may go through some challenges, may not be because of their karma. It's very intricate to find out but may not be because, just like the Pandavas, they were so pure, so righteous, completely devoted to the Lord, completely surrendered but we see that they are facing so many calamities. It has Lord's approval. Here Jaya and Vijaya are being cursed and they have to fall down. The Lord has approval. So the Lord has witnesser, sanctioner and director. In case of devotees life, Krishna directs, even the hitting of karma is directed by the Lord. If according to karma, devotees are supposed to be very prosperous, very, you know, go through a very pleasant good times.

#### detachment, divine will, example
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/weLxSeRIE6Q?t=551
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When too much of bhoga and aishwarya comes, too much of enjoyment of prosperity comes, we can forget Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Krishna may withhold prosperity from devotees to protect them from forgetting Him, using Srila Prabhupada's struggles as an example.

Krishna feels that the devotee is not matured to handle that prosperity because sometimes when too much of bhoga and aishwarya comes, too much of enjoyment of prosperity comes, we can forget Krishna. So Krishna may not sanction prosperity or good times for a devotee. He has to suffer a lot and Krishna feels that he is not ready, his faith is still a little tender, he may lose faith, he may get shaken, Krishna may not allow that karma to act. Just like Shri Prabhupada before going to America, he was quote unquote in one way, it's all the Leela of the Lord, he was meeting so much obstacles, so much struggle, it's not that Shri Prabhupada was not pure or is just after going to America or after going to Jaludutta, things changed drastically, no. So the Lord is, it all had Lord's approval. Today if we look back, all that struggle even glorified. We were reading the sacrifice of Shri Prabhupada and his devotion to the Lord. We were reading Jaludutta diary, Shri Prabhupada having heart attacks. Krishna proved that. And today when we read that, our faith in Shri Prabhupada, our conviction, the sacrifice he took to spread this message, we get so inspired, get so motivated. If he at the age of 70 years could do all that, what are we?

you see it's all very bewildering. Something is being told here, something is being told there and they don't tally, they don't reconcile. Prabhupada says, you come to the temple, here is Shyama Sundara's form, you can see always. We have many pastimes where the Lord comes, they meet the devotee, there is a conversation and the Lord departs. We have many pastimes where the Lord comes, they meet the devotee, there is a conversation and the Lord departs. Whereas, see of our good fortune, the Lord has descended personally in the form of Archa Vigraha, He is there always for us to go, to have darshan, to offer obeisances, to circumambulate. #### Deity worship, Krishna consciousness, meditation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/weLxSeRIE6Q?t=1077
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Deity worship is so essential for neophyte, he can always, the opportunity to think of Supreme Lord, by the impression of Deity within the mind."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the importance of Deity worship for neophytes, emphasizing how it helps to develop Krishna consciousness and facilitates meditation.

In another place Prabhupada says, if we don't have proper consciousness, even worship of Deity will become Galagraha, a pain in the neck. If we have proper consciousness, Krishna consciousness, then we will not see Deity as made of metal, stone, we will see Krishna personally standing. See the Deity nicely dressed, in this way the impression of the Lord will be within your mind. Dhyana vastita tad gatena. And when you have got this impression, you always meditate upon Krishna, as Krishna advises, man manabhava madbhakta, always think of me. That is perfect yoga system. Seeing Deity always, at least daily, you have some impression, our temple Deity is like this. We should understand, every day as part of a schedule, we have Darshan Aarti, we have Mangal Aarti, and various other, this thing. That impression, either you are sitting in one place, without any activities, sthitam, while walking on the street, you can always think of this Deity. So this Deity worship is a benediction, that Lord will bless you, that Lord which is Adokshaja, beyond our sense perception, the Lord has descended in a form, which we can perceive with our eyes, and we can capture the impression of the Lord, and think of the Lord. There is no difficulty, either you sitting, or walking, or standing, anyway, because the mind is there in Krishna, in Krishna's form. Therefore Deity worship is so essential for neophyte, he can always, the opportunity to think of Supreme Lord, by the impression of Deity within the mind.

#### attachment, Krishna, story
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/weLxSeRIE6Q?t=1197
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "It is only Lord Shri Krishna, who is our eternal friend, eternal relationship we have with Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker narrates a story about a TC on a train who finds a purse with a picture of Krishna, leading to a realization about the importance of attachment to Krishna.

One time there was a TC in a train, he was going around the compartment checking tickets, and he came across a wallet, a purse, very old looking, in a bad shape. So he picked up the purse, and thought maybe one of the passengers would have dropped it. Just like when we find something, natural thing is to find out who has dropped it, and return it to that person. So he picked up the purse, and opened the purse, just to see if there is some listing card, or some photograph, so that he can identify who the owner is, and return the purse to him. When he opened the purse, there was hardly any address there, no photograph, no photograph of a person. There was a picture of Krishna. Small picture of, in purse you have that, you know, small window kind of thing. So he saw a picture of Krishna, and very few, very little money. So then he raised the purse, and said, is this belonged to, is this purse belonged to anyone? So an old man, very frail, in a very, you know, frail voice said, yes, this belongs to me. This purse is mine. So now he wanted to ascertain, it really belongs to him, or he is just simply for the sake of, because, what's the harm in getting a purse? Yeah, it belongs to me. So he says, what's the proof? What's the evidence? What's the identity that this belongs to you? So he says, in that purse, there is a picture of Lord Krishna. So he says, anyone, Krishna is so famous, anyone can have a picture of Krishna in the purse. By the way, why don't you have your picture? Generally in the purse, people have, you know, their picture. By the way, why don't you have your picture in the purse? So, on that, this person became little, you know, very, sad voice, he said, let me tell you the reason why I don't have my picture. He says, when I was young, when I was in school, my father gifted this purse to me. And, naturally, I was very, very, dear to my father. When I went to the college, he used to send lot of money to me, pocket money. And, because I was attached to my father, I put the picture of my father in the purse. And that remained for many years, till, I grew up. And, I became enamoured by my looks. I had nice thick black hairs, nice haircut. And then, one day, I replaced the picture of my father with, my picture. And then, years rolled by, I got married. I had a beautiful wife. She was very handsome. And, one day, I replaced, my picture with my, wife's picture. And, I used to spend lot of time, seeing that picture. She was very, very handsome. Very, very beautiful. Then, afterwards, I got married. And, then, years rolled by, I got married. I had a beautiful wife. She was very, handsome. And, one day, I got married. Then, after that, life changed. After marriage, we got a beautiful child. Very cute. And, I got so attached to him. Many a times, just to spend time with him, I would go late to the office, and come back early, to play with that child. And, naturally, out of affection, the child picture was placed in the purse. And, now, many years back, my father passed away. After few years, even my wife, expired. My son now has grown up. He has got married. He has his family. He is staying separately. He hardly remembers me now. He hardly comes to meet me. Now, I am all alone. And, now, after reading Gita, I have realized, that it is only Lord Shri Krishna, who is our eternal friend, eternal relationship we have with Krishna. So, now, I have replaced the picture, the picture of Krishna. And, with tears in his eyes, he says, alas, the same affection, the same attachment, I would have showered on Krishna. I would have been so lonely today. And, while he was speaking this last sentence, tears started gliding his eyes. Even the TC became emotional, and gave the purse back to this person. No, no, no, you would keep the purse. And, next station came, the train stopped, the TC went out to the platform. There was a book shop, which used to sell all religious books and all. He went and asked, can I have a picture of Krishna? And, he opened his purse, and put the picture of Krishna in his purse. Whatever picture was there, got replaced with picture of Krishna.

#### Krishna consciousness, detachment, analogy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/weLxSeRIE6Q?t=1552
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "If we are conscious of Krishna, we will be untouched with the miseries of this material world."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of a lotus leaf to illustrate how Krishna consciousness allows one to remain detached from the miseries of the material world.

So, Prabhupada, in one lecture was mentioning, if we are conscious of Krishna, if we are Krishna conscious, we will be untouched, with the miseries of this material world. Prabhupada gives a very nice example, striking example. He says, just like the lotus leaf, it is untouched by water. It is in water, surrounded by water. You put a drop of water, it slips off. Lotus leaf surrounded by water, untouched by water. You put a drop, it slips off. Exactly like this, we all get, so to say, we mess up our lives, because of various kinds of attachment, because of material consciousness, and because of these attachments, different kinds of fears, different kinds of anxiety, different kinds of miseries. Here it is said, the Lord approves it. Lord approves it. It is actually out of pain.

We want it. We want life independent of Krishna. We want to enjoy independent of Krishna. Krishna approves it. But all along, he sends his representatives, gives his instructions, personally comes, to bring us back to the consciousness, original consciousness, which is Krishna consciousness. And when we become conscious of Krishna, when we have impression of Krishna, when we are thinking of Krishna, man-mana, bhav-mad-bhakto, mad-yajik, mam-namaskuru, all this material consciousness has no place. Even the so-called miseries, so-called this thing, the miseries can strike us, can hit us, can affect us, only if we are not Krishna conscious. It's not that miseries will not be there. In devotees' life also, old age will be there, diseases will be there, death will be there. But like a lotus leaf, it cannot touch the consciousness. The example is given of Pandavas. How in spite of all the miseries, they are more and more Krishna conscious, to the extent that Kuntimaran is saying, Krishna, please sanction more miseries. So, just like here, the Lord, out of His so, out of His causeless mercy,
