#### Krishna consciousness, motivation, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/weLxSeRIE6Q?t=1711
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The amount of effort involved is very less compared to what we are going to get in return."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by emphasizing the good fortune of remembering Krishna and the motivation it provides for sincere spiritual practice, highlighting the Lord's protection and approval.

His devotees, where one side Krishna says that they do not fall down, namae bhaktaye pranashyati, Krishna is saying here, all glories unto you. On one side He is saying glories, and then He is approving them to fall down. So we also have a very, very good fortune now, to remember Krishna, be conscious of Krishna, get attached to Krishna. And when we do that, then all this maya, tamas, andhakaar, darkness, ignorance, attachments, various kinds of miseries, which are bewildering us, will have no place in our life. We will be blissful in consciousness of Krishna. Now, if we know that this is our prerogative, this is what we can, this state of consciousness we can achieve, that will motivate us. That will motivate us to be serious in spiritual life. Be sincere in doing the sadhana which Prabhupada has prescribed, our daily program. Because the results, what we get in return, or whatever little bit small thing we do, manmana, think of Krishna, madhyachi, maam namaskru, offer obeisances to me, remember me. The amount of effort involved is very less compared to what we are going to get in return. So, when we hear these statements, how the Lord is so dear to His devotees, how He goes out of His way to protect His devotees, sometimes for the devotees' sake, for this pastime, He approves something. Just like here, it's been approved by the Lord. So, when we have that consciousness of the Lord, we will be very, very, so to say, enthused and enlivened to render devotional service to Krishna. Let's stop here. Vrantraj shri madbhagavatam ki shri Prabhupada.

### 2015-09-02 | SB 3.16.33-35 | HG Amitasana Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### Fall down, Maya, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XLVPd2o5f68?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Anyone of us can be arrested by Maya at any moment if we are not strongly attached to Krishna consciousness."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the concept of 'fall down' from spiritual life, both in a gross and subtle sense, emphasizing the ever-present danger of Maya and the need for constant vigilance and strong attachment to Krishna consciousness.

Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto No. 3, Chapter No. 16, Text No. 33-35. 33 and 34 I will read myself and 35 will chant together. Tautu Girvana Rishabhau, Dustara Dharidokataha, Hatashriyau Brahmashapad, Abhutam Vigatasamayu. Translation: But those two gatekeepers, the best of the demigods, their beauty and luster diminished by the curse of the brahmanas, became morose and fell from Vaikuntha, the abode of the Supreme Lord. Translation: Then as Jaya and Vijaya fell from the Lord's abode, a great roar of disappointment arose from all the demigods who were sitting in the splendid airplanes. Text No. 35. Tau eva hi adhuna, taap tau, paashada paravarao hari, diter jathara nirvishthyam, diter jathara nirvishthyam, kashyapam teja ulbanam. Translation and purport by His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swamishila Prabhupada. Translation: Lord Brahma continued, those two principal doorkeepers of the personality of Godhead have now entered the womb of Diti, the powerful semen of Kashyapa Muni, having covered them. Purport: Here is a clear proof of how a living entity coming originally from Vaikuntha Loka is encaged in material elements. The living entity takes shelter within the semen of a father, which is injected within the womb of a mother, and with the help of the mother's emulsified ovium, the living entity grows a particular type of body. In this connection it is to be remembered that the mind of Kashyapa Muni was not in order when he conceived the two sons, Hiranyakasha and Hiranyakashyapu. Therefore the semen he discharged was simultaneously extremely powerful and mixed with the quality of anger. It is to be concluded that while conceiving a child, the mind of Kashyapa Muni was not in order. One's mind must be very sober and devotional. For this purpose the Garbhodana Samskara is recommended in the Vedic scriptures. If the mind of the father is not sober, the semen discharge will not be very good. Thus the living entity wrapped in the matter produced from the father and mother will be demoniac like Hiranyakasha and Hiranyakashyapu. The conditions of conception are to be carefully studied. This is a very great thing. Thus ends the Bhaktivedanta. So here in this verse it is being mentioned that how Jaya and Vijaya, their lustre diminished by the curse of the Brahmanas, became morose and fell from Vaikuntha, the board of the Supreme Lord. So in this particular case, Jaya and Vijaya, they grossly, literally fell from the spiritual world, they came to the material world. Fall down can be very gross, in the literal sense, somebody leaving the association of devotees. We have examples of some devotees who were there, but for various reasons, they left the association of devotees. So this is an example of a gross fall down. Somebody may leave the association of devotees, may cease to be a devotee, goes back to his old ways of life, old ways of eating, old association, prior to his coming to Krishna consciousness. And there could be also subtle fall downs. Subtle fall downs could be the fall down in the consciousness. There is a spiritual platform, and there is a mental platform, and there is a material platform. On a daily basis, we do our sadhana, chanting, reading, hearing, the various aspects in the morning sadhana, to give us the spiritual strength, so that we can be situated in the spiritual platform. So gross fall down can be very gross, where someone becomes so infected, that he can leave the association of devotees. And on a daily basis, yes, there will be ups and downs as far as consciousness is concerned. Sometimes somebody is not careful, whole consciousness dips, and he is totally at the material platform, completely bewildered, with complete material consciousness. Material calculations, fear, anxiety, what will happen to me, all material calculations can overpower a person. So Prabhupada in the one place says, anyone of us can be arrested by Maya at any moment. This is a baseline. Anyone of us can be arrested by Maya at any moment. Any moment. If we are not strongly attached to Krishna consciousness.

#### Humility, Sadhana, Maya
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XLVPd2o5f68?t=600
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Ideal chanting is when we chant for giving pleasure to the Lord."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the importance of humility and pure motive in spiritual practice, using the story of Tukaram to illustrate that external displays of devotion are meaningless without genuine internal consciousness focused on pleasing Krishna.

Sometimes we can become complacent, where one might think that I am a devotee for so many years, I am doing good sadhana. The example is given of Bharat Maharaj. However, moments inattention. He was a king, he renounced his kingdom, went to the forest, all good intentions, to renounce opulence, to sacrifice all the comforts. It is not an easy thing. He did all that, with the intention of rising to the spiritual platform, pure consciousness of Krishna. But moments inattention, he got attached to a deer. Compared to Bharat Maharaj, we may not be that exalted. The amount of sacrifice, what he did, may not be, we may not compare, when it comes to Bharat Maharaj. We are practicing the process of Yukta Vairagya, where day in and day out, we are dealing with lot of material energy, in that sense. Although material energy, when used in service of the Lord, gets spiritualized but, because of our anarthas, many times we can get attached to so many things, attached to comforts, attached to the facilities, which have been provided to us. We can become complacent. So Prabhupada says, so my request to you, and all others, is to follow the principles of Krishna Consciousness, adherently, and then there is no danger, however strong Maya may be. Maya is very strong, Krishna says, devi esha gunamayi mamamaya dhurataya. The more we advance, Maya would also give us higher, or rather, more severe, more stronger tests. So if someone remains humble, doesn't get popped up, does not, sometimes, even somebody may, because of his sadhana, may get popped up. I am very strong in my sadhana. Every day I attend Mangal Aarti. That's very good. But even that, Maya is very subtle. A pride can come in. I am a great, great devotee. When you see, compare with others, I am better than them. My sadhana is best. Maya is very strong, because many times, it is not the external which matters. Somebody may be very good in sadhana, but what's going on in the consciousness? Sometimes, somebody may do sadhana, because it gives him, he feels good. He feels peaceful. It's all, at a very subtle level. That's why many times, devotees, when some service is assigned, immediately, no, no, no, very particular about the chanting, because, after I finish my chanting in the morning, I feel very peaceful. Have you heard this comment? I feel very, so, one may be doing sadhana, one may be doing chanting, that's why in chanting it's said, chanting for whose pleasure? Ideal chanting is when we chant for giving pleasure to the Lord. The other day we were hearing in a class, when we control the senses, not just abnegation, not just, restraint, but to give pleasure to the super soul, who is there, it's a choice. On one side, is the senses, looking for sense pleasure, the other side, devotee says, no to that, to give pleasure to the super soul, becomes pleased that, yes, for me, he is foregoing, sensual pleasure.

#### Maya, Krishna Consciousness, Preaching
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XLVPd2o5f68?t=1200
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The only means of protecting us from the attack of Maya is to be fully Krishna conscious."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the constant battle against Maya, emphasizing the need for full Krishna consciousness as the only means of protection, and uses the analogy of health and disease to illustrate the importance of being careful and taking precautions.

One time, Tukaram, all of you have heard of, Tukaram, he was, he would spend lot of time, speaking about the Lord, Hari Katha, and it is said that, he was such an advanced devotee, that when he would start speaking, he would get so absorbed in the topics, related to the Lord, the past times of the Lord, he will become, completely oblivious of the time. Generally we have class, you know, we have, starts at this time, gets over at this time, but moment he would start speaking, he would close his eyes, and he would be so immersed in the topic, Hari Katha, about the past times of the Lord, that he would completely forget, what's the time. So one time, in the evening, the Katha started, around 7.30, 7 o'clock, 7.30, and it was supposed to be, for one, one and a half hours, and, while he started speaking, eyes closed, completely immersed, it became, 8.30, 9, 9.30, and since people had to go back home, catch up with their household work, and you know, dinner, and all those things, so people, they were reluctant, because, Tukaram was speaking, it would be an insult, in between the class, you get up and go, but since the eyes were closed, some of them, you know, started leaving, 9 o'clock, 9.30, 10 o'clock, 10.30, some people, out of respect for Tukaram, Tukaram is speaking, somehow they, stayed back, but 10, 10.30, they started dozing, started, feeling very sleepy, and then, not knowing when Tukaram will stop, even, those, slowly, gradually started getting up, 11 o'clock, some 5 people were left, 12 o'clock, one person was left, night 12 o'clock, one person was left, Tukaram opened the eyes, and he saw one person is sitting, so in his mind, he thought, such a nice devotee, so much desire he has, for hearing Hari Katha, that somehow, I totally become oblivious of time, and see 12 o'clock, I started at 7, 7.30, 5 hours he is hearing, so he told, such a nice devotee, you have such an attraction, you have for Hari Katha, you are so blessed, you are such a great devotee, so the devotee, very gently, folded his hand and said, no, no, no, no, actually, tell you the truth, I was just waiting, actually, the mat on which you are sitting, belongs to me, I was just waiting, when you will stop, and I can take the mat back to my home, so this shows that, externals, yes, sadhana, chanting, hearing, reading, all that, is important, but, each one of us, may not be very pure in our motive, just like here, we have different point system and, just to monitor sadhana, that's why sometimes, devotees punch and, you know, what's the motive, for what are we doing that, unless the motive is pure, unless we are very conscious that, I have come here to be conscious of Krishna, my motive is to be conscious of Krishna, pure Krishna consciousness, no doubt when we are part of an organization, there will be some systems, so that, not everyone, may be pure in the motive, so organization helps, somebody who is weak, somebody has, is not with the right motive, even to encourage that person, even to, safeguard that person, even to, enthuse that person, some systems are made, so here it says, unless one follows the principles of Krishna consciousness, adherently, in spirit, not just, for the sake of following, be too attached to rules and regulations, and forget the spirit behind those rules, the spirit is, to always remember Krishna, and to not forget Krishna, and if one, inherently follows, is very consensuous, always trying to, catch the lotus feet of the Lord, always trying to remember the Lord, always there is an internal struggle going on, how I should, always be conscious of the Lord, then Prabhupada says, then, there is no danger, however, strong Maya may be, because Krishna is protecting you, you cannot expect that, you will be out of danger, no doubt, Krishna's protection is there, Krishna says, but, the very fact that we are in the material world, Prabhupada says, this we should always remember, in other words, we cannot be complacent, we cannot be overconfident, now I am safe, I am very nicely situated, we should not be so fool that, I am living very comfortably now, no sir, you are in danger, that is said by Krishna, dukkha alayam ashashvatam, you do not take this material existence, as comfortable at any moment, even in spiritual life, sometimes we can settle down, we can, so to say, become comfortably situated, then you will not be able to make progress in spiritual life, as soon as you think, that I am very comfortable here.

#### Humility, Risk, Krishna's Protection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XLVPd2o5f68?t=1800
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "For a devotee, it is always good to think that I am weak."
**Summary:** This segment concludes by emphasizing the importance of humility, constant prayer, and seeking Krishna's protection, highlighting that recognizing one's weakness and dependence on Krishna is essential for spiritual progress and safety from Maya.

Sometimes devotees also get into a paradigm of, you know, not stepping out of their comfort zone, any service, so long as it is very comfortable, very peaceful, not too much of anxiety, not too much of headache, not too much of going against the likes and dislikes in the mind, always choosy about such kind of services, that means we are in a comfort zone, as soon as one thinks, I am very comfortable here, then you are spiritually fall, then you are spiritually fall down, that is Maya, so as I said, many of us may not even know, there are subtle traps, actually we may think, I am a great devotee, I am very good in sadhana, but what is going on in the mind, we may be looking for comforts, we may be looking for peace, we may be looking for, you know, cozy life, free from botheration, free from anxieties, we have examples, even during Prabhupada's time, many devotees, who were very, quote unquote, advanced, very senior devotees, they also fell in Maya, these are examples, just to, show us that how, age is not the real, this thing, I am 20 years old, I am 25 years old, in the movement, the senior most devotees, at time, during Prabhupada's time, they also, fell prey to Maya, regarding Kirtananda, there is nothing to be said about, but one should be very careful, not to fall sick like him, in the material world, there is a constant fight, between Maya, and the living entity, Maya is very strong, and we can fall victim, at her hand, at any moment, the only means, of protecting us, from the attack of Maya, is to be fully, Krishna conscious, the proportion, in which we fall back, in Krishna consciousness, is filled up, by the influence of Maya, now Krishna consciousness, is not a binary logic, binary means, you are Krishna conscious, or you are not Krishna conscious, just like, black and white, the grades of shades, so likewise, even Krishna consciousness, there are some, activities, which will propel us, to spiritual platform, and there are some activities, which will, pull us down, falling of regulatory principles, we break the regulatory principles, we are pulled down, the only means, of protecting us, from the attack of Maya, is to be fully, Krishna conscious, the proportion, in which we fall back, in Krishna consciousness, is filled up, by influence of Maya, Tau Pat gives example, just like health, if you neglect your health, if you are not cautious, if you are not careful, you can fall sick, these days, dengue fever is there, if you are not careful, no doubt, bite can happen, accidentally also, in spite of we being careful, but if you are not careful, if you are not taking precautions, just like here it says, we should be, careful, in the night, when we sleep, have the mosquito nets, use repellents, and all that, we have to do our duty, in spite of we doing our duty, what is there, in store for us, we cannot avoid, so Prabhupada says, one who is careful, the person who is very careful, about his health, generally does not fall ill, similarly, a person who is always fixed up, in Krishna consciousness, cannot be defeated by Maya, sometimes, in spite of our full Krishna consciousness, we fall a victim to Maya, but that is temporary, just as a seasonal change, such calamities, do come and pass away, and we have to endure them, in spite of we being careful, just like example, in spite of we being careful of health, sickness can come, I was just reading an article on dengue, it is not just because, you don't have good immunity, that virus, that mosquito bite, with that virus happens, even though you have good immunity, you may be good health, but you will fall sick, so here it says, if Kirtananda had, ever sincerely served Krishna, and his spiritual master, he will not fall down, the temporary systems will fall down, without delay, we should all pray to Krishna, for his steady recovery, my grief for Kirtananda, isn't anything personal, but I am sorry, that he has become like a Mayavadi, in spite of my best efforts, to help him, so we all will get lot of help, from different sources, from devotees, from various kinds of association, just like many devotees say that, when we go back, when we read the quotes on the notice board, immediately what we are, what's going on in our mind, we get an answer, so, Krishna, Prabhupada, in various ways, dynamically will guide a devotee, if we are sincere, will try to help us, will try to pull us, from Maya, that way, in one sense, we are, we can be very hopeful, because we have so much help, so much support, so much guidance, accessible to us, but if we get so much in Maya, that even those guidance, support and all those things, we are not willing to take, we become overconfident of our own opinions, our own thinking, we start committing offence, we become rebellious, we stop listening, we stop taking, we stop taking advice, sometimes devotees can also fall in a paradigm, saying that, whenever I go, they'll simply tell us philosophy, they'll simply tell chant Hare Krishna, regarding Damodar's flight, we are very sorry, but we must always expect, that some of the weak soldiers, may fall down in a fight, wage against the great Maya, external energy of the Lord, so it's like a fight, just like in a war, casualties happen, soldiers, so here the war is between us and Maya, casualties will happen, the sex attraction is very great in the material world, but in the spiritual world it is different, so the greatest form of Maya, is the attraction to the opposite sex, especially in today's world, with so much of sense objects around us, the glare of material energy, is all the more captivating, for a conditioned soul, their hips and attractive smiles, cannot stimulate sex urge, passion of a man or a male devotees, in the Vaikuntha planet, the opulence is very great, and yet they are all engaged, in the transcendent loving service of the Lord, when we come under Maya, when our consciousness gets pulled down, then helplessly we will get attracted, on one side is the beauty of Krishna, that will fail to capture our attention, we will be captivated by, the false illusory beauty of the opposite sex, if someone is helplessly getting attracted, overpowered by lusty desires, it is one symptom that, yes I am in Maya, when one is Krishna consciousness, actually the attraction to opposite sex, it fades away, raso varjam, raso pi asya, param drishtva nivartate, so these are all symptoms, just like Dengue has a symptom, from the symptoms doctor can make out, so likewise the symptoms of Maya is, Krishna's beauty does not attract us, when we go in front of the deities, just boring, it is very difficult to conquer over the influence of Maya, but who is firmly fixed in Krishna consciousness, cannot be victim of Maya, so in one sense, we have a war going on, Maya is very powerful, any moment we can fall down, so somebody may think, what is the hope for me, I am a weak devotee, but one who is firmly fixed up in Krishna consciousness, cannot be victim of Maya, anyway we cannot stop our fighting with Maya, neither we can expect that all our soldiers will remain unhurt, some casualties will be there, you know Arjun's son Abhimanyu was killed in the battle, in which Bhishma Deva was a party, fight for Krishna's cause sincerely, and you will be glorified with victory, even when we are dabbling with material energy, we go out, we meet all kinds of people, we can get infected, but if we are fighting for Krishna's cause, Prabhupada in one place says, we have to do so much of managerial work, but Lord Chaitanya promises, because it is required for propagation of Krishna consciousness, he will give us special protection, again, taking this statement, we cannot be overconfident, Lord Chaitanya has said, just like somebody said, we were going and preaching in some place, so we were told that, why we have to take unnecessary risk, somebody wants to go and preach in a bar, so just like the example I have shared earlier of a Kabaddi match, there is always a trade-off between risk and the gain, even in the stock market, even in the world, the more the risk, the better the gains, there are some stocks where the risk is very very high, and your money can multiply in one year, double, it happens, risk is so high, your money can become half, or it can become double in one year, and there are some, you put in fixed deposit in a bank, in a state bank, it will increase in six years, seven years, eight years, it will double, no risk, very very stable, and the gain also is this thing, so sometimes we have to be very cautious, we can become overconfident in the name of preaching, in the name of spreading Krishna consciousness, there is a very thin line, we have to strike a good balance, just like the Kabaddi match, a person goes, if he goes deep within the enemy, the opposite camp, trying to touch more people, quite possible he will not come back, so when the person is going on the opposite side, he has to be always careful, how much risk he can take, there is a trade off between risk, more the risk, more the gain, even people very cautiously when they invest money, constantly evaluating risk, when should I pull the money, when should I withdraw from this stock, otherwise everyone will put the money in the place where greatest risk is there and double it, always call, there are people who always play safe, so for Krishna's cause we have to take risk, because it's a preaching mission, we have to go and meet all kinds of people, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu said he was unwilling to meet even a materialist, King Pratap Rudra, but we many times go and meet all kinds of people, it has to be done for Krishna's cause, but we have to be, that evaluation constantly we have to do, suppose two days, three days sadhana is not there, reading is not there, chanting has not happened, consciousness is down, we know that this is the point where I should not take risk, that doesn't mean when sadhana is good you can take full risk, just that you have to be cautious, just like in a marathon, you know you have to run 42 km whatever that distance, people preserve their energy, it's not like 100 meter sprint, where in 100 meters you finish off the whole, so we also have joined to be here for the whole life, to serve the mission of Krishna, Prabhupada, so we have to strike a good balance, on one side we have to take risk, we have to do our assigned services, other side simultaneously we have to always be vigilant, am I taking risk which can even jeopardize my spiritual life, in the name of preaching, in the name of Krishna consciousness, so if we are very conscientious, very careful, very alert, always praying to the Lord, always being humble that yes, Maya is very strong, any moment I can be overpowered by her, with that mood, clinging on to the lotus feet of the Lord, begging, praying, pleading, then one is safe, if there is some earthquake or there is some danger, naturally spontaneously we pray, but we forget it, the tendency of offering prayer to the Lord at the time of danger is automatic, another beauty of Maya is, it will make us feel that there is no danger, just like here it says, when earthquake is there, everyone prays, when there is no earthquake, nobody prays, there is a famous saying, Dukh mein sumiran sab kare, Sukh mein sumiran kare na koe, so we should know that we are in danger, we are in this material condition of life, which means we are in danger, therefore we should always pray, so, to have that feeling that yes, Maya is very strong, I am weak, for a devotee, it is always good to think that I am weak, for a devotee it is always good to think that I am not advanced, sometimes the number of years can also give us pride, I am so many years, I am senior devotee, it is always good to remember and be humble that, I am a weak devotee, and with that mindset, with that attitude we will be always, you know in the mood of, seeking Krishna's protection, I need Krishna's protection, I need help, I need guidance, and when we are, in that frame of mind, with that consciousness, then Krishna, Prabhupada, will surely give their protection.

#### Universe, Science, Scripture
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DgH5mf8NZm4?t=455
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Otherwise, even if somebody is humble, and uses common sense, one will be open-minded, will be not very rigid about his views in terms of who is the creator."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the vastness of the universe and contrasts the humility required for spiritual understanding with the often rigid and sometimes flawed theories of modern science, emphasizing the reliability of scriptures.

in its own family of planets. And all this form a milky way, the galaxy called the milky way, and like that the billions of galaxies. So what we know of this universe, only out of pride, only when we are deluded, bewildered by illusory energy, we can say that, we can confidently say, my understanding of the world is perfect. Otherwise, even if somebody is humble, and uses common sense, one will be open-minded, will be not very rigid about his views in terms of who is the creator. Some so-called learned scientists have said that, if you are a scientist, you are a scientist, you are a scientist, you are a scientist, very confidently propound their theories. Again, maya parita jnana, Krishna says, they may be intelligent, but maya has stolen away their intelligence. So our scriptures here, very nicely they are describing, and how it tallies with the present day understanding. Sometimes we may say, it may not tally with what the present day science says, it is quite possible that as, just like there was a time, there was a time when science said that the earth is flat, but all along our scriptures say bhugol, gol means ghramanda, anda. So there could be, as, since we are students of Bhagavatam, Bhagavad Gita, all authorized scriptures, sometimes we may also get bewildered when we may hear something which, according to scientists, is contradictory. Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavad Gita.

#### Scripture, Cheating, Knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DgH5mf8NZm4?t=571
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So that way we can understand that with these inherent four defects, the world which we are living, the knowledge which is being taught, is also contaminated with these defects."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the trustworthiness of scriptures as apaurushaya, not man-made, contrasting them with the four defects of human knowledge: cheating propensity, limited sense perception, mistakes, and illusion, using the Volkswagen scandal as an example of cheating propensity.

Convicting what is mentioned here in the Bhagavad Gita. But we should have confidence that these scriptures are apaurushaya, they are not man-made. This knowledge is given by the Lord and it is not subject to the four defects. The four defects are, we all have cheating propensity, we have limited sense perception. We commit mistakes and we are subject to illusion. Recently in the newspapers, you would have read about a very big car maker company, Volkswagen. Millions and millions of cars worldwide. We are talking about cheating propensity. They, so to say, installed a software which faked, or so-called cheated, the emission testing process. Because each car has to be subject to emission tests and all that. And in millions, now they have filed a suit. The company, the dam. The dam is worth 19 billion dollars. So cheating propensity, it can be at that scale. One of the best brands in the market. Millions of cars being sold all over the world. And the brains behind that car, they want to cheat. So it can happen with the scientists. There is a book written by some of the devotees called Forbidden Art. It is a book about the history of archaeology. How many of the so-called facts which emerge when the scientists are trying to prove or trying to find out the truth. Or some evidences which come up. How they have been set aside if they don't tally with the profound theories, present day theories. They deliberately put under the carpet. They deliberately put under the carpet. They deliberately put under the carpet. They deliberately put under the carpet. So that way we can understand that with these inherent four defects, the world which we are living, the knowledge which is being taught, is also contaminated with these defects. Sometimes knowingly, sometimes unknowingly. Sometimes intentionally, sometimes unintentionally. So here it is being nicely described that how this is a science.

#### Devotion, Complacency, Jaya and Vijaya
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DgH5mf8NZm4?t=809
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We are staying in the association of devotees. We have an opportunity to engage full time in the service of the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker transitions to the story of Jaya and Vijaya, drawing parallels to the lives of devotees and warning against complacency, even when favorably situated in devotional life.

So here we see that how Jaya and Vijaya being cursed, they are taking birth as twins to Prajapati Kashyapa. And in continuation to what we discussed in my previous... We had touched upon that, that how just like Jaya and Vijaya are devotees of the Lord and not ordinary devotees. To be doorkeepers of Vaikuntha, personal attendants to the Lord, it is not an ordinary thing. And we see that how Jaya and Vijaya committed a mistake and how they were cursed. And because of that they had to take birth as Hirnakashyapu and Hirnakshah here. So as devotees we can also have some important lessons from this past time. We may be very nicely situated. When we see people around us, when we see people outside practicing Krishna consciousness, various kinds of struggles, we may see that yes, we are far more favorably situated. We are staying in the association of devotees. We have an opportunity to engage full time in the service of the Lord. We have an opportunity to every day get up in the morning, attend Mangala Aarti, attend Bhagavatam class. All the programs. It's a great, good fortune. For somebody who is trying to practice Krishna consciousness outside, even to get an opportunity like this, it's not easy. Even so-called many initiated devotees, maximum they are very, sometimes the pressures of life or the jobs are so high that even if one is able to complete sixteen rounds, it's a big deal. So to get five hours, six hours of time where we can focus completely on remembering the Lord, chanting His holy names, attending Aarathis, doing various kinds of spiritual activities, it's a big luxury. So we are in this situation, but we cannot be complacent.

#### Spiritual Life, Maya, Caution
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DgH5mf8NZm4?t=975
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Practicing spiritual life is like walking on a razor's edge."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of walking on a razor's edge to describe the precarious nature of spiritual life, emphasizing the constant battle against maya and the potential for both gross and subtle fall downs.

We cannot take it for granted. Just like Jaya Vijaya. This material world is, Prabhupada says, practicing spiritual life is like walking on a razor's edge. Then Prabhupada describes razor's edge means small in attention and there is blood. Although these days razors have improved, we don't see much blood because everything is, but typical barber razor which they use, I mean if, we see many times we get cuts also. So, small in attention and it's blood. Same is the case in the spiritual life. Grossly and subtly. Grossly could be somebody who could lose devotional service. He can go out of company of association of devotees. That's gross. Even subtle, subtle fall down can happen. Small mistake and your good consciousness which we had is gone. We are back to material consciousness. Material calculations, material worries, material anxieties. After good morning sadhana, we are, so to say, propelled. The consciousness is propelled into higher dimension. Our thinking is, quote unquote, we experience bliss. Our thoughts are sublime. And we make a blunder, small blunder, moments inattention. Krishna bhulia jiva bhoga bhancha kare, nikatastha maya tare jhapatiya dhare. Jhapatiya, it's not something which happens nikatastha, maya is there, right there. Jhapatiya dhare, jhapati means it pounds on you. So that way, we cannot be complacent. Just like Jaya and Vijaya. They didn't, they were not arrogant. They were not, so to say, just they came, the Kumaras came, a mistake happened. And they got cursed.

**Summary:** The speaker continues to discuss the battle against maya, highlighting the allurements of the illusory energy and contrasting them with the true bliss of Krishna consciousness, emphasizing the constitutional position of the jiva as sat chit ananda.

And see what they are going through. Born as demons. No doubt, we have to have a rider that it's all the pastime of the Lord. So, even during Prabhupada's time, many devotees, when they were practicing Krishna consciousness, unfortunately, were trapped by maya. So Prabhupada says, regarding Damodara's plight, Damodara was a devotee, we were very sorry, we are very sorry, but we must always expect that some of the weak soldiers may fall down in a fight waged against the great maya, external energy of Krishna. So spiritual life is fighting a battle, battle between on one side is maya, the allurement of maya, the other side is Krishna. Right now, we haven't got the taste. We are more attracted to the illusionary energy of the Lord. bhogaishwarya prasaktanam tayyaparita cetasam That's our present state. Why do we get attracted to maya, or the illusionary display of maya, the variegatedness in this material world? Because we all are amsa of the Lord, and our constitutional position is sat chit ananda. We are blissful, there is a sense of permanence because we are the atma, which is eternal, there is no fear of death, we are eternal, because we are blissfully amsa of the Lord, which is eternal, there is no fear of death, chit, and full knowledge, knowledge about our constitutional position, knowledge about we being amsa of the Lord, and Lord being our protector, fearless, abhaya. That's our constitutional position, that's what we are meant to experience.

#### Maya, Illusion, Enjoyment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DgH5mf8NZm4?t=1247
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Maya has two aspects, covering potency and bewildering."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the constitutional position of sat chit ananda with the current state of asat, achit, and nirananda, explaining how maya covers the real knowledge and understanding, pulling attention from Krishna's domain and placing it in illusory variegatedness.

But see our present state now, asat, always fearful, fearful of death, various kinds of insecurities, various kinds of fears, various kinds of ignorance, so much body of knowledge, so much research is happening because we are finding that because of various kinds of limitations, ignorance, various kinds of ajnaan, ignorance, we are not in control of things. Various diseases torment us because we don't have knowledge how to counter those diseases. So achit and nirananda, we are not happy. So what maya does, is when the jiva, krishna bhulya jiva bhoga vancha kare, when jiva wants to enjoy, minus krishna, iccha dvesha samuthena dvandamohena bharata, first thing maya does is, pulls krishna, pulls the living entity's attention from krishna's domain, thinking of krishna, consciousness of krishna, understanding that I am amsa of krishna, I am meant for pleasure of krishna. It pulls that understanding, covers that understanding. Maya has two aspects, covering potency and bewildering, avanatmika and prakshapatmika shakti. So first it covers that real knowledge, real understanding, that we are meant for pleasure of krishna, we are amsa of krishna, it covers that knowledge, pulls our attention from that domain, krishna's domain, and the next step is, takes that attention and puts in illusory variegatedness, which is a perverted reflection of variegatedness in the spiritual world. In present time we see lot of variegatedness, lot of allurements, various sense objects, everything looks real. That's why in spite of we wanting not to gratify our senses, simply we get pulled, because the attraction is real. That is maya, takes that attention and puts it in the variegatedness, which is perverted reflection, so to say replica of the original pleasure in the spiritual world. That original pleasure is replicated, but in a perverted way, it is illusion, just like water and a mirage.

#### Enjoyment, Maya, Spiritual Warfare
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DgH5mf8NZm4?t=1425
**Verse References:** BG 3.27
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Maya is cheating us with illusory enjoyment, but we are entitled for real enjoyment."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the world's allurements are a perverted reflection of true spiritual enjoyment, and that the battle in spiritual life is between the real enjoyment of Krishna consciousness and the illusory enjoyment offered by maya, which steals our intelligence.

So all along we see in this world, you tell somebody who does not have this understanding, or who has not practiced spiritual life, that there is no happiness in the world, then you feel that you are crazy. Or you feel that, you know, why are you unnecessarily depriving yourself of happiness? What's wrong in being happy? What's wrong in enjoying life? Doesn't make sense to them, because we all are meant for enjoyment, that's the constitutional position, you cannot deny, spirituality doesn't deny happiness. Anandamayo bhyasat. And the other side, we say don't enjoy. When we get this spiritual understanding, we understand that, yes, enjoyment is not denied, but there is illusory enjoyment, which we are getting cheated with. Maya is cheating us with illusory enjoyment, but we are entitled for real enjoyment. So when we have come to spiritual life, there is always a battle, on one side we have allurement of maya, the enjoyment which appears very real, which is very glaring, and we have taste for that, we are bewildered, bhogaishwara prasaktana, our intelligence, real intelligence has been stolen, the other side is real enjoyment for which we have no taste. We have no faith. It's mythology. Krishna is mythology, spiritual world is mythology. So here it is said, and maya's greatest weapon is the sex desire, attraction to the opposite sex. So we cannot expect that all our soldiers, some of the soldiers may fall down and fight, wage against the great maya, external energy of Krishna. The sex attraction is very great in the material world, but in the spiritual world it is different. So sex attraction, it's there even in the spiritual world.

#### Spiritual World, Sex Desire, Opulence

What we see here, opposite sex in the forms, it's a replica. From Srimad Bhagavatam we know that the opposite sex, namely the female members of the vaikuntha are many many times more beautiful than they are in the heavenly planets. We all heard of Apsaras and the hips and attractive smiles cannot stimulate sex urge, passion of the man or male devotees. In Vaikuntha also, they are great, you know, like here it says, female members of Vaikuntha. But the attractive smiles cannot stimulate. Why it cannot stimulate? Because the jiva who is there in the spiritual world is experiencing real Sat Chit Ananda. There is no requirement for sending out your tentacles to derive Sat Chit Ananda from external sources. In relationship with the Lord, mamahi vamso jiva loke, the jiva is experiencing completeness. Today we are having a deficit of Sat, Chit and Ananda. And because of that deficit we are having hope that I'll get here, I'll get here, I'll get there. That's why the whole material existence is to bridge the gap which is the gap of the deficit using various material environments. But they cannot stimulate sex urge, passion of the man or male devotees. In the Vaikuntha planet, the opulence is very great yet they are all engaged in the transcendental loving service of the Lord. Sometimes people say that spiritual life means we should not be amidst opulence. In Vaikuntha, so much of opulence is there. But they are not bewildered. bhogaishwarya prasaktana because they are connected to the Lord, they are conscious of the Lord.

#### Krishna Consciousness, Maya, Shelter
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DgH5mf8NZm4?t=1671
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is very difficult to conquer over the influence of maya, but one who is firmly fixed in Krishna consciousness cannot be victim of maya."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that while conquering maya is difficult, being firmly fixed in Krishna consciousness reduces the deficit of sat chit ananda, and that taking strong shelter of Krishna's lotus feet and following the process diligently guarantees protection from maya.

It is very difficult to conquer over the influence of maya, but one who is firmly fixed in Krishna consciousness, when we are fixed in Krishna consciousness, the deficit of Sat Chit Ananda proportionately comes down. The more and more we are Krishna conscious, the more and more we are situated in our constitutional position of being Sat Chit Ananda. But who is firmly fixed in Krishna consciousness cannot be victim of maya. Anyway, we cannot stop our fighting with maya. Now, the present state is we are not Krishna conscious. We are allured by maya. We are practicing sadhana bhakti. And while we are not pure, we are engaging in various preaching activities, meeting various people, dabbling with various things. Anyway, we cannot stop our fighting with maya. Neither we can expect that all our soldiers will remain unhurt. It's a fight. Fight with maya. And maya is very powerful. mamamaya dhuraptaya. So in that sense, we cannot be complacent. Any time we cannot be complacent. Any time maya can overpower us. But that does not mean that we should live in a state of fear or uncertainty. If we are taking the medicine, if we are following the process, Srila Prabhupada in many many places mentions, avashe rakshive krishnae vishwasa palana. Although maya may be very very strong, but if we have taken strong shelter of krishna's lotus feet, following the process diligently.

#### Devotee Protection, Compassion, Preaching
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DgH5mf8NZm4?t=1785
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "One who is interested in his own salvation is not as advanced in Krishna consciousness as one who feels compassion for others and who therefore propagates the Krishna consciousness movement."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the protection offered to devotees, even if they fall down, and contrasts self-centered salvation with the compassion of propagating Krishna consciousness, which earns special protection from Krishna.

Then their protection is guaranteed. You know Arjuna's son Abhimanyu was killed in the battle in which Bhishma Deva was party. Fight Krishna's cause sincerely and you will be glorified with victory. Devotees generally don't fall down, but in circumstances, but if circumstantially they do, the Lord because of their strong attachment to Him gives them protection in all circumstances. So this is a very nice balance. On one side we have to be very cautious. We have to be alert. We cannot be complacent. Other side we should not be leading a life out of fear, anxiety. Sometime devotees have this thing, you know, what is the guarantee that I will be safe, so better let me secure myself now or do now. What if I fall down in future? These kind of thoughts can cross. Chitraketu fell down and became a demon, but he too was protected. Thus even if one falls down from the path of bhakti yoga, one is ultimately saved. So there is no harm. One who is interested in his own salvation is not, now somebody may go to the other extreme that I will not take any risk. Going out, meeting people, now we see all kinds of billboards, it's all very risky. Or preaching, sometimes we have to encounter, deal with Mataji's. It's very risky. So somebody can say, okay, nothing doing all this. So one who is interested in his own salvation, yes, one can be very self-centered, concerned only about his salvation, his spiritual advancement, but that's not the mission of Prabhupada. One who is interested in his own salvation is not as advanced in Krishna consciousness as one who feels compassion for others and who therefore propagates the Krishna consciousness movement. Such an advanced devotee will never fall down, for Krishna will give him special protection.

#### Guru, Responsibility, Commitment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DgH5mf8NZm4?t=1919
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When we take initiation, it's a very serious commitment. On one side, we promise to follow the regulated principles, on the other side, the spiritual master takes the responsibility to deliver the disciple."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that we are standing due to the protection of the Acharyas and Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, and that while blunders can lead to helplessness, initiation is a serious commitment where the disciple promises to follow regulated principles and the spiritual master takes responsibility for their deliverance.

We are today standing not because of our own spiritual strength, because, Prabhupada, one more place says, Lord Chaitanya promises, because we have to do so much managerial work to propagate this Krishna consciousness movement, Lord Chaitanya promises that we will not fall down. So that protection of the Acharyas, that protection of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, protection of Krishna is there with us. Yes, if we make blunders, very grossly, very grossly, don't follow their instructions, take all kinds of risk, then, we are helpless. That is the sum and substance of this Krishna consciousness movement. It is my duty to deliver you the right things in right earnestness and it's the duty of the receiver to act on the standard spiritual regulation. When you left us, I simply prayed to Krishna for your return to Krishna consciousness because that was my duty, Prabhupada is writing. Any good soul who approaches me once for spiritual enlightenment is supposed to be depending on my responsibility to get him back to Krishna, back to home. When we take initiation, it's a very serious commitment. On one side, we promise to follow the regulated principles, on the other side, the spiritual master takes the responsibility to deliver the disciple. The disciple may misunderstand a bona fide spiritual master, so under pressure of Mayas, sometimes we misunderstand, we may not have faith in his instructions. Again, it's because of Maya's influence. But a bona fide spiritual master never let go as a devotee once accepted. Even a disciple misunderstands a bona fide spiritual master, the master regrets for his inability to protect the disciple.

#### Guru, Maya, Protection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DgH5mf8NZm4?t=2030
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So this is some insubstance that on one side we are practicing spiritual life, it's a war on Maya, Maya is very powerful, but if we somehow pray to the Lord, stick on to his lotus feet, follow the process given by the Acharyas diligently, then their protection is guaranteed."
**Summary:** The speaker shares a story of his Guru Maharaj lamenting the loss of a disciple who had taken sannyas, emphasizing the spiritual master's deep concern and the importance of following the process given by the Acharyas to guarantee protection from maya.

And sometimes he cries with tears in the eyes. So, right now we are not able to see that battle, we are not able to see how we are overpowered by Maya, we are not able to see the tricks of Maya. But spiritual master can easily see that, because he is not in illusion. So when he sees that the Maya has taken away some of his disciples, or tricked some of his disciples, he feels very sorry. We had an experience while my Guru Maharaj was alive, one of his disciples who had accepted sannyas was one day forcibly dragged by his wife. My Guru Maharaj lamented with tears in his eyes, saying that he could not save the soul. So this is some insubstance that on one side we are practicing spiritual life, it's a war on Maya, Maya is very powerful, but if we somehow pray to the Lord, stick on to his lotus feet, follow the process given by the Acharyas diligently, then their protection is guaranteed. And if we are protected by them, if we have their blessings, just like Prabhupada said, it's my responsibility. It's a dual thing, one side we have to follow the principles, we have to obey the instructions, the other side, the spiritual master takes the responsibility. So if we do that, then we don't have to have fear, we can practice spiritual life, and in due course of time, the influence of Maya, the allurements which we fall prey to, will subside.

#### Conditioning, Spiritual Process, Balance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DgH5mf8NZm4?t=2128
**Verse References:** BG 2.47
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The whole spiritual process is to undo that conditioning, and revive our constitutional, original pure consciousness."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the soul is not inherently lusty, but conditioned by maya, and that the spiritual process is to undo this conditioning and revive our original pure consciousness, emphasizing the importance of sincerely following the process and maintaining a balance between caution and faith.

In a conditioned state, in a conditioned state sometimes, we say, I am very lusty. Actually, the soul is not lusty. Conditioning means, Maya has covered the real knowledge, and helplessly we are getting attracted to Maya. The whole spiritual process is to undo that conditioning, and revive our constitutional, original pure consciousness. It cannot happen overnight. Till such time, we will get attracted. Till such time, there will be habitual responses to sense objects. There will be some occasional fall downs. Just like Krishna says, you have the right to do your duty, you are not entitled to the results of your activities. We have to see, am I taking the medicine sincerely? Am I following the process diligently? We may sometimes even break the regulative principles, helplessly. That's a result. That's an outcome. Sometimes we get very disappointed and depressed. Still Maya is attacking me. Still I am falling down. It will happen. Only check we should be, am I taking the medicine sincerely? Am I seriously following the process? Or am I doing some short circuits? Am I, just like today we read, am I partially following the process? Just like you go to a doctor, the doctor says, take this course for ten days, you take for one day, and you say, leave it. Enough. So if we are following the process, even though we may be overpowered by Maya, we come in illusion, we fall down occasionally, we should get up, have courage, have conviction that, Prabhupada is with us. If we follow his instructions, he will protect me. Avashya Rakshivya Krishna. E Vishwasa. We don't have to live in fear and anxiety. Nor we should go to the other extreme of being complacent. So this right balance, if we do that, we can practice this spiritual life and come out of this illusion. Let's stop here.

## HG Amitasana Dasa

### 2015-10-13 | SB 3.17.19 | HG Amitasana Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### Hiranyakashipu, benediction, pride
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yp6RiH3xOYA?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "He was proud and puffed up due to his benediction and was able to bring all three planetary systems under his control."
**Summary:** This segment introduces Hiranyakashipu, his benediction from Brahma, and his subsequent pride and control over the three planetary systems.

Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto No. 3, Chapter No. 17, Text No. 19. Translation and purport by His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swamishlaprabhupada. Translation: The elder child, Rinukashipu, was unafraid of death from anyone within the three worlds because he received a benediction from Lakshman. He was proud and puffed up due to his benediction and was able to bring all three planetary systems under his control. Purport: As will be revealed in later chapters, Rinukashipu underwent severe austerity and penance to satisfy Brahma and thus received a benediction of immortality. Actually, it is impossible even for Lord Brahma to give anyone the benediction of being immortal. But indirectly, Rinukashipu received the benediction that no one within this material world would be able to kill him. In other words, because he originally came from the abode of Vaikuntha, he was not to be killed by anyone within this material world. The Lord desired to appear himself to kill him. One may be very proud of his material advancement in knowledge, but he cannot be immune to the four principles of material existence, namely, birth, death, old age, and disease. It was Lord's plan to teach people that even Rinukashipu, who was so powerful and strongly built, could not live more than his destined duration of life. One may become as strong and puffed up as Rinukashipu and bring under his control all the three worlds, but there is no possibility of continuing life eternally or keeping the conquered booty forever. So many emperors, who have ascended to Paa, and they are now lost in oblivion, that is the history of the world. Thus ends the Bhagavad Gita. So here in this verse, Rinukashipu, who had received a benediction from Lord Brahma, and the benediction was that no one within this material world would be able to kill him. So Prabhupada mentions that although Brahma cannot give the benediction of Trinakashrupa being immortal, but indirectly he got that benediction. And because of the benediction that he cannot be killed by anyone in the material world, he was proud and puffed up, and was able to bring all three planetary systems under his control. He was also unafraid of death from anyone within the three worlds.

---
#### humility, pride, instrument
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yp6RiH3xOYA?t=217
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Even when we become proud, or when we achieve something, or when we accomplish something, or when we do something big in the service of the Lord, unless we remember that we are just nimitta mātra, we are just an instrument in the hand of the Lord, actually all credit goes to the Lord, unless we consciously remember, unless we consciously give due credit to the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the qualities of Hiranyakashipu with the qualities devotees should aspire to, using the analogy of a pencil being guided by a hand to illustrate humility and recognizing oneself as an instrument of the Lord.

So, given Trinakashrupa here, unlike the qualities which we should be aspiring for, the quality of being humble, the quality of being tolerant, the quality of being subservient to the devotees, to always consider oneself meek and humble, unlike that, because Trinakashrupa was puffed up, he was embodiment of just the opposite of these qualities. Unafraid of death. One time there was a lady, she had in her home, her grandchild who had come from a distant place for a vacation. So every morning when the child would get up, he would come to his grandmom and she would be writing something. Maybe she was an author or something. She would be always writing something, always with a paper, and she would write with a pencil. So one day this child, this small child became inquisitive and asked, what are you writing? Is it about me? Is it about us? So the lady said, what I am writing is relatively unimportant. But since you have asked the question, let me tell you that this pencil with which I am writing, whatever I am writing, it is playing a very, very important role. And before you leave, after the vacation gets over, I will gift you this pencil. So the child, you know, he had been also writing with pencil, so he was a little surprised. What's the big deal about a pencil? How is it different from the rest of pencils which is available in the market so easily? He said, I will give you this pencil and I want you to imbibe, you will imbibe the five qualities what you see in this pencil for the rest of your life. And if you do that, you will become a great man. So again the child was somehow intrigued what the mother is, what the grandmother is trying to tell. So she says, the first quality in the pencil, you see it is an ordinary pencil, but the pencil just remembers that there is a hand guiding it. Using a pencil, a writer, an author might write some wonderful text like Shakespeare wrote, wonderful, you know, whatever his writings, his poems, so famous he is for all his writings, he would have used a pen or a pencil to write it. But the credit goes to the Shakespeare. Although, what was literally written in the paper was written by a pen, but the pen or a pencil never took any credit. It was always conscious, had it been conscious, assuming that the pen is conscious or a pencil is conscious, it is very, very clear that I am being used. Something was being used, something wonderful may come up, but it will never ever even think of taking a credit. So likewise, a pencil always remembers that there is a hand guiding it. Here, Himata Shubha, we see, is getting puffed up, is proud, because he is not able to see the hand. Even when we become proud, or when we achieve something, or when we accomplish something, or when we do something big in the service of the Lord, unless we remember that we are just nimitta mātra, we are just an instrument in the hand of the Lord, actually all credit goes to the Lord, unless we consciously remember, unless we consciously give due credit to the Lord. sarvasya cāhaṁ vidhi-saṇi viṣṭo matasme ter jñānaṁ apok. The ideas, the thought, the inspiration came from the Lord. Unless we remember that, we will also become proud. So here we are saying, Himata Shubha is proud because he is totally oblivious of that he is amsa of the Lord. The Lord is the supreme controller. Because he has got three planetary systems under his control, he is thinking he is great, he is immortal. Nobody can touch him.

---
#### suffering, tolerance, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yp6RiH3xOYA?t=576
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Likewise, a devotee also, with his Kṛṣṇa consciousness, is willing to go through pains and pleasures, sorrows, knowing that the Lord is his well-wisher, knowing that Avashya Rakshabhi Kṛṣṇa, knowing that the Lord is in control of my life, knowing that the Lord is taking me through all this to make me a better devotee, to make me more dependent on Him."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the second quality of the pencil, its ability to bear pain when sharpened, and relates it to a devotee's willingness to endure suffering in Kṛṣṇa consciousness, trusting in the Lord's guidance.

But a devotee knowing that, in fact in the fourth chapter, twentieth verse, Kṛṣṇa mentions, Prabhupāda explains that a devotee, when he does anything, he is not attached to the results because he knows whatever he is doing is done by the Lord. Why the devotee is not attached to the results, it is not some artificial imposition in the mind, because of this consciousness that it is being done by the Lord through him. He is just an instrument, just like the pencil, just an instrument. The second quality of the pencil is, it learns to bear, it learns to bear pains and sorrows. After every few hours of writing, the person who is holding the pencil, the grandmother would use a sharpener and sharpen the pencil. And although the pencil may go through some pain, when you are, so to say, being sharpened, but after that pain, after that so-called suffering, the pencils become sharp, and it drives better. So likewise, a devotee also, with his Kṛṣṇa consciousness, is willing to go through pains and pleasures, sorrows, knowing that the Lord is his well-wisher, knowing that Avashya Rakshabhi Kṛṣṇa, knowing that the Lord is in control of my life, knowing that the Lord is taking me through all this to make me a better devotee, to make me more dependent on Him, same as the case being for Kunti Mahārāj, the Lord gives us more and more troubles. Unlike Rina Kashyapu here, who is trying to ward off all troubles of birth, death, old age, disease, by becoming immortal, a devotee of the Lord does not make such endeavours. He is rather endeavouring to be dependent on the Lord. Knowing the dangers in the material world, this material world which Kṛṣṇa says is duḥ khālaya māśāśvatam, rather than investing all his time and energy making this place a wonderful place to stay in comfortably eternally, realising that it is temporary and it is a place of misery, he invests his time and energy in becoming conscious of the Lord, dependent on the Lord. Even if he goes through some pains and pleasures, he takes it in stride, learns the lessons, depends on Kṛṣṇa more and more, and becomes a better devotee. So the pencil also, when it is sharpened, it does not rebel. It allows to be sharpened, although it goes through some pain, although it gets shortened, but in the process, it becomes better, better in writing.

---
#### humility, correction, feedback
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yp6RiH3xOYA?t=795
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Whereas a devotee, just like the pencil, allows itself to be rubbed in case you have made a mistake. We all make mistakes to arrest human."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the third quality of a pencil, its ability to be erased, and connects it to a devotee's humility and willingness to accept correction and feedback.

The third quality of a pencil is when you write with a pencil, it allows you to rub in case you have made a mistake. This is also a good quality of a devotee, where a devotee, unlike what is being depicted by Hruna Kashyapu, the qualities, a devotee is humble and he allows to be corrected. If suppose he comes across instructions in the Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavatam, and he realises that he is not behaving properly or the qualities what he is exhibiting is not in line with the qualities of a saintly person, or someone gives a feedback, if a person is pavdaad, he says, how dare you tell me? What do you think of yourself? Who do you think you are? Whereas a devotee, just like the pencil, allows itself to be rubbed in case you have made a mistake. We all make mistakes to arrest human. Even with good intentions, if we go about leading our lives, still we will make mistakes. We all have blind spots. We don't know that we don't know. Many times, the mistakes will be pointed out to us. Our behaviours will be corrected. Somebody senior would point out that this is not the way to act. This is not the way to behave. This is not the way to speak. And if you are not humble, then we will rebel. We will not take correction and stuff. We will not be open to feedback. We will get angry. We will be hatred. So like a pencil, it allows to be corrected. Yes, mistake has happened. One takes the feedback, sees if there is a grain of truth, and then corrects himself and move forward. So this is the third quality of a pencil.

---
#### consciousness, inner qualities, self-control
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yp6RiH3xOYA?t=924
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Although there may be different designs, different colours, different shapes, different sizes, ultimately what is important is, the lead inside the pencil."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the fourth quality of a pencil, emphasizing that the lead inside is more important than the external appearance, and relates it to the importance of inner consciousness and self-control for a devotee.

The fourth quality is, although we may see pencil different designs these days in the marketplace, different colours, different designs. These days, let's say if Chota Bean is a serial coming, you will have a pencil with Chota Bean cartoon on top of it. All kinds of designs are there. So much innovation has happened to make it attractive so that children fall for it and buy more and more different kinds of pencils. Although there may be different designs, different colours, different shapes, different sizes, ultimately what is important is, the lead inside the pencil. So devotee also, what is important is not our colour, not our complexion, not our nationality, not our caste, not our creed, not how we look. What is really important is, what's our consciousness? What's inside us? What's our character? What's our values? So devotee of the Lord also, see Hiranyakashipu, he is trying to control the world outside. He got control of three planetary systems, but he didn't control himself, his own senses, his own mind. So devotee of the Lord is more interested, what profit the man if he wins the whole world but loses his own soul? What profit the man if he wins the whole world? Today actually this is the problem, people are very active, very busy, very passionate, very hardworking, but more is to control the world around them, control the environment, control the people, control situations. But very few are wanting to control themselves. Hiranyakashipu, because he was in control of his excellence, he thought that he is immortal, he is great, because of that he is puffed up. A devotee of the Lord, realizing that Krishna is the supreme controller, everything is moving as per his wish, he doesn't get artificially puffed up, he is more busy in purifying his consciousness, in thinking of Krishna, remembering Krishna. In the 18th verse in the 4th chapter, Krishna says, the one who sees action in inaction and inaction in action is intelligent among men. A devotee may be just sitting in one place, chanting Krishna's name, remembering, apparently doing nothing, inaction, but that is real action. That is real thing. Just like in a pencil, the lead inside, the pencil may be attractive color, but what really matters, what finally you can do with the pencil is dependent on the quality of the lead inside. So we should also not be very conscious of our excellence, we should be more conscious of our, what's going inside us, what's our consciousness.

---
#### accountability, consequences, discrimination
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yp6RiH3xOYA?t=1128
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Likewise, our actions also leave a mark, good or bad. Every action has an equal and opposite reaction, Newton's third law of motion. As you sow, so shall you reap."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the fifth quality of a pencil, its ability to leave a mark, and connects it to the concept of karma and the importance of acting with discrimination and awareness of the consequences of one's actions.

And the fifth quality of the pencil is, it is conscious that everything will leave a mark. When you put a pencil on a paper, it leaves a mark. Likewise, our actions also leave a mark, good or bad. Every action has an equal and opposite reaction, Newton's third law of motion. As you sow, so shall you reap. So let's be humble enough, just like Rinne Kashyapu here is puffed up, he's reckless, the way he treated Prahlad. Where is your Lord? Let's always remember, they are the stringent laws of nature, and they are very stringent. Every action we are accountable for. So having understood this, we should be humble and act with discrimination. Our shastras are pointing out what we should do, what we should not do, what we should be careful of, what we should avoid doing. So knowing that every action, every word we speak, sometimes recklessly we may speak, we may hurt someone, although we may ask forgiveness, but the damage is done, just like a glass, if it cracks, although we may use quick fix, we may join together, but still the crack remains. So now you have every word, every word, every word we speak, every action of ours leaves a mark. So let's not be reckless, let's be very careful, and let's be very discriminative in leading our lives based on Krishna conscious principles. So this Grandmother tells this Grandson that I would give you this pencil, and I wish and pray that you also imbibe these qualities in your life.

---
#### Hiranyakashipu, negative qualities, materialism
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yp6RiH3xOYA?t=1242
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "And while I was going, going through this, we see the exact opposite of these qualities in the character of Hrana Kashyapu. And that's why he is Hrana Kashyapu."
**Summary:** The speaker summarizes how Hiranyakashipu embodies the opposite of the five qualities of the pencil and emphasizes the futility of material pursuits and hoarding.

And while I was going, going through this, we see the exact opposite of these qualities in the character of Hrana Kashyapu. And that's why he is Hrana Kashyapu. He is not remembering there is a hand guiding him, he is not tolerant to pains and sorrows, wants to lord over, wants to control things, so that there is no pain, there is no trouble, he is immortal, there is no death, there is no old age, he is not willing to correct himself. Lord Maharaj tried his best to give him some instructions, he was not willing to correct, he was not attentive to what's going on inside, he was more attentive to what is outside, and he was not conscious of his actions, he was reckless. Now here Prabhupada says, no matter how strong a person may be, just like Hrana Kashyapu, these are all very wonderful pastimes just to show, it's a Lord's plan to teach us that even Hrana Kashyapu was so powerful and strong, compared to Hrana Kashyapu, what we see present day, so called big people, they are nothing. He controlled the three planetary systems, whereas here we see some people controlling a particular company or a particular state, a particular country. So it was Lord's plan, to teach people that even Hrana Kashyapu, who was so powerful and strongly built, could not live more than his disdained duration of life. One may become as strong as Prabhupada, as Hrana Kashyapu, and bring under his control all the three worlds, but there is no possibility of continuing his life eternally, or keeping the conquered booty forever. People want to control things, accumulate things, hoard things, but whatever our booty, booty means, you know, when you loot something, that's called a booty. Whatever the booty may be, whatever we have so painstakingly gathered, accumulated, we'll have to leave and go.

---
#### impermanence, time, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yp6RiH3xOYA?t=1390
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, General public
**Key Quote:** "So many emperors have ascended to power and they are now lost in oblivion."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the example of Atal Bihari Vajpayee and historical civilizations to illustrate the impermanence of material power and fame, emphasizing that everything is eventually lost to time.

Just last Sunday we were discussing, Atal Bihari Vajpayee, we all have heard about him, he was the Prime Minister of the country, very charismatic Prime Minister. He was known for his speeches. People would get inspired by his speeches. He was very bold, very courageous. And when he was the Prime Minister, naturally headlines every day, whatever he is speaking, all his remarks, all his comments, all his views, all his opinions, all newspapers, all over the world, he was as charismatic as our present PM, or even more than that. And Prime Minister is very powerful. Today he is still alive. But no one is talking about him. You hardly read anything about him. Because of old age, because of disease, he is there, in his place, in his house, no doubt taken care of. He was recently awarded Bharat Ratna. He could not come and collect the prize, so people went there to confer this Bharat Ratna to him. He must be bedridden, cannot move, cannot talk, I am sure must be going through various pains which accompanies old age, diseases, invalidity, cannot see properly, cannot walk properly. So here it is said, just try to imagine 10 years back, or 15 years back, he was so charismatic, he would come and speak and move the things, move the situations, control a lot of things, the whole country was practically under his... So many emperors have ascended to power and they are now lost in oblivion. Big, big civilizations have come and gone. In the history books, we read about Mesopotamian, Harappan, Indus Valley, different civilizations, so-called advanced civilizations have come and gone. That is the history of the world. People come, they accumulate power, they become puffed up, for a while they are in control of things, they feel that they are the Lord, and then Kala comes, a wave of Kala comes, and it's lost in oblivion. People don't even remember. It's a very critical, classic case. Prime Minister of the country, we don't even hear of him these days. He's still alive.
