#### devotion, material existence, tolerance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/yp6RiH3xOYA?t=1595
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A devotee also gets disease. A devotee also becomes old. A devotee also dies. But, because of the consciousness, Krishna consciousness, he goes through, just like the pencil example allows, a devotee goes through all this material existence, but all these things intensify his, dependence on Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that devotees are not exempt from the four principles of material existence, but their Krishna consciousness helps them to tolerate and depend on Krishna more deeply.

So, devotee of the Lord, it is not that when we become devotees, just like here it says, no one is free from the principles of material existence, four principles of material existence, namely birth, death, old age and disease. It is not that when we become devotees also, we'll get freed from this. A devotee also gets disease. A devotee also becomes old. A devotee also dies. But, because of the consciousness, Krishna consciousness, he goes through, just like the pencil example allows, a devotee goes through all this material existence, but all these things intensify his, dependence on Krishna. So, Prabhupada says, even during the presence of Krishna, when Nanda Maharaj and other kahud men had the Supreme Personality of Godhead in their presence, there were disturbances. So, devotee of the Lord, he is not, he is not, because of his knowledge, because of his consciousness, he is not having a false hope that, now because I am a devotee, Krishna is protecting me, so I don't have to go through all that. Disturbances will come. tam titikshu sabharata Learn to tolerate without being disturbed.

#### verse recitation, translation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lcpU4oOmWc0?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Maitreya continued: The proud and falsely glorious Daitya paid little heed to the words of Varuna, O dear Vidura. He learned from Narada the whereabouts of the Supreme Personality of Godhead and hurriedly betook himself to the depths of the ocean."
**Summary:** The segment contains the recitation of Srimad Bhagavatam verse 3.18.1, followed by its English translation, setting the stage for the lecture.

OM NAMO BHAGWATE VASUDEVAYA. 3 CHAPTER 18 TEXT 1. MAITREYAV VACHAM. TADEVAM AKARNAYA JALESHA BHASHITAM. MAHAMANASTAV VIGANAYA DURMADHAHA. HARER VIDITVA GATIMANGA NARADHA. VASATALAM NIRVISHESHAT TVARNAN VITAHA. Translation and Procurement by His Divine Grace A.C. R.I.P. Translation. Maitreya continued: The proud and falsely glorious Daitya paid little heed to the words of Varuna, O dear Vidura. He learned from Narada the whereabouts of the Supreme Personality of Godhead and hurriedly betook himself to the depths of the ocean.

#### pride, intelligence, demoniac mentality
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lcpU4oOmWc0?t=77
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Since demoniac persons are less intelligent, they dare to fight with Vishnu, who is known as Ajita, or one who has never been conquered."
**Summary:** This segment explains Hiranyakashipu's demoniac nature, his eagerness to fight Vishnu despite knowing Vishnu's power, and contrasts this with the behavior of an intelligent person who considers the enemy's strength.

Perpet. Meteorolistic warmongers are not even afraid to fight with the mightiest enemy, the Personality of Godhead. The demon was very encouraged to learn from Varuna that there was one fighter who could actually combat him, and he was very enthusiastic to search out the Supreme Personality of Godhead just to give him a fight, even though it was predicted by Varuna that by fighting with Vishnu, he would become prey for dogs, jackals, and wordchords. Since demoniac persons are less intelligent, they dare to fight with Vishnu, who is known as Ajita, or one who has never been conquered. Thus entered the demonic Personality of Godhead, and he was very encouraged to learn from Varuna. So here in these words, Hirnaksha is being referred as falsely glorious Daitya. He was a Daitya, and he was proud, and falsely glorious. Varuna had actually told him that there is one fighter who can actually combat him because this Daitya was not an ordinary demon. He was very powerful, and Varuna told him that there is one fighter who can actually combat him, and rather than hearing to the words of Varuna, and he also told that he is none other than the Supreme Personality of Godhead, and he also told that by fighting with Vishnu, he would become prey for dogs, jacals and vulchers, but instead of heeding to the advice of Varuna not to take head on the Supreme Personality of Godhead, understanding that the Supreme Personality of Godhead is Ajita, is unconquerable, defeat is inevitable. But this Daithya, out of pride, goes in search of the Supreme Lord so that he can fight him. Such demoniac persons are less intelligent. Why they are called less intelligent? Because an intelligent personality is not a personality. A person will keep in mind the circumstances, the might of the enemy.

---
#### intelligence, pride, materialism

#### pride, disconnection, maya
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lcpU4oOmWc0?t=341
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "How does this pride come? When we become disconnected from the Supreme Lord, when we come under the influence of Maya, then naturally we forget our constitutional position, we try to be the Lord, we try to be the master, we try to be the controller."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that pride originates from disconnection from the Supreme Lord and being influenced by Maya, leading to forgetting one's constitutional position and trying to become the controller.

So this pride comes about originally Jeeva. We all are amshas of the Supreme Lord and we are subordinate to Him. We are subservient to Him. We are servants of Krishna and our constitutional position is to humbly serve the Supreme Lord, to obey the Supreme Lord, to please the Supreme Lord. How does this pride come? When we become disconnected from the Supreme Lord, when we come under the influence of Maya, then naturally we forget our constitutional position, we try to be the Lord, we try to be the master, we try to be the controller. And the more your person is in Maya, the more he thinks that he is the controller, to the extent that he thinks that God is not there, I am God.

---
#### disconnection, analogy, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lcpU4oOmWc0?t=429
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Because it is disconnected from the root. There is no supply of nutrients, water, moisture. It is disconnected."
**Summary:** The speaker narrates an incident where Prabhupada explains that dry grass is yellow because it is disconnected from the root, drawing a parallel to how disconnection from the Lord leads to anomalies in behavior.

So that is what is happening to Jeevas. In Sri Lanka here, Prabhupada one time was going with some devotees in a morning walk. And they were walking in a garden and they saw that some portion in the garden there were green grass and small portion in the side there was some dry grass. It was yellowish in color. Sometimes we see grass which gets dried, especially in the summer season, it becomes yellow. Even in the Ghoshala we have that dry grass. It becomes yellowish. So Prabhupada asked, why is this grass yellow? So devotees started giving different explanation. And then finally Prabhupada said, because it is disconnected from the root. There is no supply of nutrients, water, moisture. It is disconnected. Just like if you cut the grass, the grass which is green, you cut it, in no time it will become yellow or it will dry up because it is disconnected from the root.

#### devotion, humility, qualities
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lcpU4oOmWc0?t=505
**Verse References:** SB 1.2.6
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Genuine humility comes when one understands that I am an insignificant beggar. I am a puppet in the hand of the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that pride, lust, greed, and anger are symptoms of disconnection from the Lord. He contrasts external displays of humility with genuine humility, which arises from understanding oneself as an insignificant servant of the Lord.

So likewise all these anomalies in our behavior, pride, lust, greed, anger, these all are symptoms of our disconnection with the Lord. That's why they said, yasyastika, whosoever becomes a pure devotee or devotee of the Lord, all good qualities starts manifesting in his behavior. It automatically starts manifesting. I was talking about humility. Humility is not some artificial, some sentimental alignment of our minds. You know, we speak very softly. We always keep our heads down. We don't scold anyone. You know, generally we have another, he is very humble, very soft spoken. But genuine humility is something different from the external symptoms which we are aware of. Prabhupada at times, he chastised his disciples. He used harsh words. At times he got angry. Somebody might construe that Prabhupada was not humble, but it's not true. The real humility, Prabhupada is an insignificant beggar. I have no power to speak. So genuine humility comes when one understands that I am an insignificant beggar. I am a puppet in the hand of the Lord.

#### pride, analogy, humility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lcpU4oOmWc0?t=607
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "I am a lighthouse, you turn 20 degrees."
**Summary:** The speaker tells a story about a captain of a big ship who, out of pride, demands that another vessel change course, only to discover that he is communicating with a lighthouse, illustrating the foolishness of pride.

This is exactly what Hiranaksha is not having. I am the Lord. I can fight the Lord. I can overpower the Lord. I can defeat the Lord. One time there was a ship. This was earlier, many years back. These days you have modern, you know, navigation systems. Just like the GPRS and all that. So there was a time when the ship, huge ship, especially when it would be lot of fog in the ocean. So they will have very sharp beam of light. And that's how you can identify if somebody, some ship is coming from the opposite direction. And that's why sometimes ship would change course or slow down so that there is no collision. So one time there was a big ship moving in the water, in the ocean. And this ship, the captain saw a beam of light coming from the opposite direction. So he, it was evident that it may be some kind of a ship. So because this captain was captain of a very big ship, naturally he was proud. Generally when a person drives a big car, he is relatively more puffed up than a person, hey rickshawala, because it gives him a sense of that I am big. So the captain sent a message that I am captain of a big ship. He named the ship, he says turn, you turn 20 degrees. He wanted the other person to turn 20 degrees. So back comes the message, you turn 20 degrees. Many times we also have, you are wrong, no you are wrong. You know we accuse each other, nobody is willing to budge. So he says you turn 20 degrees. So he gets angry, he says you know who I am, I am an A class officer. I order you, you turn 20 degrees. So back comes the answer, I am a C class worker, you turn 20 maybe. He gets all the more angry. Being a worker, how dare he tell a captain of a big ship to turn 20 degrees. He says I will get you dismissed from the job. How dare you tell me to turn 20 degrees. He says sir I plead you, I beg you, I am a lighthouse, you turn 20 degrees. In earlier times there used to be lighthouse. You know the buildings, top buildings so that it can give directions to the ships. And seeing the lighthouse the ships could direct. Those were earlier times, not the present times. So he says I am from the lighthouse, you turn 20 degrees.

#### pride, materialism, bhakti
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lcpU4oOmWc0?t=802
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The whole process of bhakti is somehow to give up this pride, to give up this enjoying tendency and come back to our constitutional position of being a servant of the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker connects the lighthouse story to modern advancements that can lead to pride and challenging God. He emphasizes that the more we move away from our subservient position to the Lord, the more we suffer, and that the process of bhakti is about giving up pride and returning to our constitutional position as servants of the Lord.

How can the lighthouse move? So that way, even we, in the name of advancement, like here he is saying, falsely proud. In present time because we have got control of things, to some extent. We can diagnose some diseases. We can monitor a lot of things. We can to some extent forecast, weather forecast, we can tell when it is going to rain. To that extent, you know we have in one way got control over things. And this brings some kind of a pride. And out of pride, many times we even challenge ourselves or challenge God or come to a conclusion that there is no God. The more and more we are disaligned or not in alignment with God's law, God's principles which are like the lighthouse principles, the more and more we go away from our constitutional position of being self subservient to the Lord, acting as a dasa, the more and more we will suffer in this material world. The more and more we are in alignment with our constitutional position, subservient to the Lord, the position of being a dasa, the more and more we will actually experience the bliss we want to experience. But being conditioned soul, somehow, lording over, enjoying tendencies, very very deep rooted, even after becoming devotees still. Deep within that enjoying tendency, I am the enjoyer, that false ego is so prominent. The whole process of bhakti is somehow to give up this pride, to give up this enjoying tendency and come back to our constitutional position of being a servant of the Lord.

#### demoniac mentality, kali yuga, rebellion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lcpU4oOmWc0?t=927
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Demon doesn't mean sharp claws and horns on the head and black face. It's a demoniac mentality, the consciousness. The mentality of challenging the Supreme Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker clarifies that a demon is not defined by physical appearance but by a mentality of challenging the Supreme Lord. He observes that in Kali Yuga, despite technological advancements, there is an increasing rebellion against God.

That's why here it's called daitya, it's a demon. Demon doesn't mean sharp claws and horns on the head and black face. Generally in serials which we see in television, they portray demons or villains, ugly looking. It's a demoniac mentality, the consciousness. The mentality of challenging the Supreme Lord. The mentality of not agreeing to serve the Supreme Lord. So here we are seeing this in the behavior, the character, the words of Hirnakshan. In Kali Yuga, we are seeing more and more in present times, although as I was mentioning earlier, we are advancing in many fronts technologically. In so many areas we have advanced. There is no doubt about it. But more and more we are advancing, the more and more we are becoming in one sense rebellious to God. There was a time when man, naturally when you are helpless, you tend to remember God. The more and more we are in control of things, the more and more we think that God is obsolete, not required.

---
#### kali yuga, symptoms, story
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lcpU4oOmWc0?t=1032
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So-called God-men, so-called spiritualists will speak sweet words, will appear to be the well-wishers but they will exploit the followers."
**Summary:** The speaker narrates a story about the Pandavas asking Krishna about the symptoms of Kali Yuga, and Krishna responding by having them retrieve arrows, each leading to a symbolic scene representing the degraded state of Kali Yuga.

We can manage without God. One time these four Pandavas, Yudhishthira Maharaj was not there, the four Pandavas, they were with Krishna and they were having a conversation and they asked Krishna, what is going to happen in Kali Yuga? What will be the symptoms of people in Kali Yuga? So Krishna instead of replying, he took four arrows and shot those arrows in four different directions and told them, kindly go and get me those arrows. You will get answers to your question. So Arjuna went in one direction in search of arrow and finally he saw the arrow and he saw a very cute cuckoo bird singing very melodiously. Cuckoo birds, the sound is very very pleasant. So Arjuna was, when he heard that voice, he was charmed, so to say, by that sweet little word from the cuckoo bird. When he went near the bird, near the tree, he saw the cuckoo bird actually was eating the flesh of a live rabbit and the rabbit was wriggling in pain. Somehow he was surprised what kind of scene he is seeing. Cuckoo bird, sweet little bird, supposed to sing very melodiously and here is eating flesh of a live rabbit and the rabbit is wriggling in pain. He immediately took the arrow and came back to Krishna, not able to comprehend what he has seen. Then Bhima went in another direction. He saw the five wells. Four wells and the one well in between these four wells. Each of these four wells is swimming with water. Water is overflowing, sweet little water is overflowing from the wells and the well in the center, the fifth well is completely dry, completely empty. He was also surprised by this sight. What is this? All five wells are together, four wells are overflowing with water and the fifth well is completely dry. Not able to comprehend, he comes back. Nakula goes in another direction, tries to pick up the arrow and here he sees a cow has given birth to a baby calf or whatever calf and after birth it seems the mother cow licks the calf till it gets clean and keeps on licking the calf, keeps on licking. Even though the calf has become completely clean, keeps on licking. Finally, the cowman has to pull the calf away from the cow. By then, the calf had become wounded. There were wounds in the body because of excessive licking by the mother cow. So he was also surprised, how can mother cow hurt her own calf? And Sahadev goes in another direction. He picks up the arrow, it's at the foothills of a hill, big mountain and he sees from the top of the mountain a big boulder, big rock is rolling down the hill. Big boulder. And as it's rolling down, all the big, big trees, rocks, whichever is coming on the way, it's smashing it. It's smashing all things which comes in its way. And finally the boulder is coming down. And to his surprise, when it almost reaches the down, there is small little grass and the boulder stops. Stops rolling down and stops. That small little grass holds the boulder. Again he is bewildered, what's going on? Big, big trees are uprooted and small little grass has stopped this boulder and it's not moving down.

---
#### kali yuga, exploitation, disparity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lcpU4oOmWc0?t=1306
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "In Kali Yuga there will be extreme disparity in distribution of wealth."
**Summary:** Krishna explains the symbolism of each scene: exploitative God-men, extreme wealth disparity, spoiling children with excessive affection, and the degradation of character stopped by the chanting of the holy name.

So they all come back to Krishna and says, this is what we saw. So Krishna explains, the first thing of the cuckoo bird singing very sweetly but eating live rabbit, says that in Kali Yuga, so-called God-men, so-called spiritualists will speak sweet words, will appear to be the well-wishers but they will exploit the followers. The people who will surrender to them, this we see very often happening in Kali Yuga. So many scams happen, so many so-called God-men, people are gullible. Although not many are spiritually inclined, even though they are spiritually inclined, they get cheated because the real knowledge is not there. What is genuine spirituality is not there. Unless we are fortunate to get in touch with a bona fide spiritual master coming in a bona fide parampara, we will also get cheated. Today we are here not because we are intelligent enough to discriminate who is a bona fide God-man, it's Prabhupada's causeless mercy. So there will be God-men who will speak sweet words but they will exploit, they cheat, they eat their so-called followers. Then the five wells, four wells overflowing with water and the fifth well completely dry signifies that in Kali Yuga there will be extreme disparity in distribution of wealth. On one side we will have people who are filthy rich, we see this happening in this world, in fact the disparity is on the rise. It says, 90% of Americans hold some 90 or 95% of the total wealth in America. There are billionaires, people like Bill Gates, 40, 50, 60 billion dollars. And simultaneously in the same place you might also see people living who are completely poverty stricken.

---
#### kali yuga, parenting, affection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lcpU4oOmWc0?t=1461
**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Parents also when you show love, when you exhibit qualities which is not backed by shastric understanding, like I mentioned, humility means just to be kind, not speak harshly, not scold, to speak always pleasing words, to not reprimand anyone, not to get angry."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the cow licking the calf represents parents showing excessive affection to their children, spoiling them. He emphasizes that love should be backed by shastric understanding.

The third arrow, Nakula, when he went and saw that calf, cow licking the calf, indicates that the parents, they will be very affectionate to their children, they will show so much of affection to their children, so much of love, and that love will actually spoil the children. Parents also when you show love, when you exhibit qualities which is not backed by shastric understanding, like I mentioned, humility means just to be kind, not speak harshly, not scold, to speak always pleasing words, to not reprimand anyone, not to get angry. In the name of being gentle to the child, in the name of giving a good upbringing, parents pamper their children and they get spoiled. Sometimes they wonder, what wrong we did? We gave everything he wanted. Gave so much of love.

#### kali yuga, degradation, holy name

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker explains that the boulder falling from the hill represents the degradation of character in Kali Yuga, but the small grass stopping the boulder symbolizes that chanting the holy name of Krishna can stop this degradation.

That's not enough. Our, the way we live, the way we act in this world should be backed with right knowledge. Just the intention is not enough. And the last, the boulder falling from the top of the hill and all the trees getting crushed when it's moving down, and finally one small little grass holding the boulder, it indicates that the Kali Yuga, the character of people will degrade, like the boulder coming from the, it will fall, the character will fall actually. People will degrade, Kali Yuga, Doshan, Nidhe, Rajan. But finally the boulder is stopped by a small little grass, insignificant grass. What does that grass indicate? Kali Yuga, Doshan, Nidhe, Rajan, Astireko, Mahaguna. By chanting the name of Krishna, that small little sound vibration, appears to be insignificant sound vibration, that sound vibration can stop the degradation, that fall of character, the degradation which is there, which we are observing in the character and behavior of Hirinaksha.

#### holy name, association, protection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lcpU4oOmWc0?t=1618
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Actually, when we are in association of devotees, when we are chanting, when we are hearing, when we are attending the sarnaks like a dam, these forces of Kali Yuga actually are stopped."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of chanting the holy name and associating with devotees as a means of protection from the forces of Kali Yuga, comparing it to a dam holding back these forces.

In one sense, we also like demons, if we analyze our consciousness. The saving grace is what can really stop us is, if we take to the holy name, chanting of the holy names of the Lord, then this falling from grace, we say, falling of character, it can stop. In one sense, there was mentioned in one of the classes, many times devotees, we take all what we are doing for granted, and sometimes grass is greener the other side, we feel that, you know, it can be practiced outside also. Sometimes we face some kind of discomfort. Actually, when we are in association of devotees, when we are chanting, when we are hearing, when we are attending the sarnaks like a dam, these forces of Kali Yuga actually are stopped. This dam is holding the forces of Kali Yuga, or forces of Guna, Kala, Karma. We don't know what all forces we have to encounter in life. Actually, the sadhana, the chanting of the holy name, association, Nityam Bhagavata Seva is holding that force. The moment this dam opens, we will be inundated with Guna, Kala and Karma forces. If we stop chanting, or for some reason the chanting gets affected, we start chanting mechanically day after day, lose interest and all that, then the forces of Guna, Kala, Karma will overpower us, overwhelm us, to the extent that all these demoniac qualities will again start resurfacing us. To the extent we are following this process very diligently, the forces are checked at bay, and all the good qualities will start gradually manifesting in our behavior and character. Actually, in one sense, yes, we are demoniac, we are Kali Yuga products, in one sense. Varna Sankara, this word is used, Kalau Shudra Sambhava, Shudras, in that sense. The saving grace is the Lord's holy name. If we take shelter of, by the mercy of a pure devotee of the Lord, we Shudras, we in one sense, Varna Sankaras also, have a hope, that this boulder of degradation, the Kali Yuga, which is hitting all of us, we can be saved. So, these are a few insights we get from this verse.

#### guidance, humility, purification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lcpU4oOmWc0?t=1757
**Verse References:** BG 2.47
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Prabhupada, Krishna, they act very dynamically. They all want us to improve, become nice, pure devotees. From time to time, guidance will come."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by emphasizing the importance of heeding guidance from devotees and scriptures, contrasting it with Hiranyakashipu's refusal to listen to Varuna and Narada. He highlights the opportunity for purification and the path to happiness through aligning with our constitutional position as servants of Krishna.

On one side, we have Rinakasha demons challenging the Supreme Lord, and in spite of Narada telling, in spite of Varuna telling, he is not giving heed to their instructions. Falsely glorious Daitya paid little heed to the words of Varuna. Now, when we hear Bhagavatam daily, or when we are an association of devotees, we also have Anarthas, and we will be advised. We will be told what to do, what not to do. Our shortcomings will be pointed out. Our Anarthas will be pointed out. We will be advised. But if we are demoniac mentality, then here it is said, paid little heed to the words of Varuna. Prabhupada, Krishna, they act very dynamically. They all want us to improve, become nice, pure devotees. From time to time, guidance will come. Sometimes people wonder, we require a physical guru. We are practicing spiritual life, and the Lord, Supersoul, Chaita Guru, can act in mysterious ways. We can get directions from so many sources. And if we are humble, if we are properly situated, we will take heed to the words of, you know, if we get some advice from some senior devotees, some senior Vaishnavas, we are hearing a lecture of Prabhupada, something we hear, we will take those words seriously. And if we are infected, if we are demoniac mentality, we take no heed to. Somebody is saying we should chant attentively. Somebody is saying we should attend all the programs. Where is this thing? Chalta hai. So, we are very fortunate that we have an opportunity, although we may have this kind of mentality, we have an opportunity to, glorious opportunity, to rectify, to purify. And once we get purified, then as I mentioned earlier, the more and more we are aligned to our constitutional position as servants of Krishna, the more and more we will be happy. The more and more we will be blissful. The more and more we are disaligned, the more and more miserable we will be. The miseries of this material world, guna, kala, karma, will impact us. So with these words we will stop here. Granth Raj, Himmat Bhagavatam, Kishla Prabhupada.

### 2015-11-19 | SB 3.18.2 | HG Amitasana Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### incarnation, demoniac mentality, misconception of the Lord
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/UpJcbinR1uE?t=48
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Persons who have no intelligence deride the transcendental form of the Lord as material."
**Summary:** This segment discusses how demons misunderstand the incarnations of the Lord, considering them to be material and challenging their existence.

Translation and Purport by His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Shilaprabhu Paat. Translation: He saw there the all-powerful personality of Godhead. In a previous chapter, we have discussed the incarnation of the Supreme Personality of Godhead as Varaha, the boar. While Varaha, with his tusks, engaged in uplifting the submerged earth from the depths of the water, this great demon, Hiranyaksha, met him and challenged him, calling him a beast. Demons cannot understand the incarnations of the Lord. They think that his incarnations of fish or boar or tortoise are big beasts only. They misunderstand the body of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, even in its human form, and they direct their attention to the body of the Supreme Personality of Godhead. In the Chetanaya Sampradaya, there is sometimes a demoniac misconception that the descent of Nityananda's Prabhu. Nityananda Prabhu's body is spiritual, but demoniac persons consider the body of the Supreme Personality to be material, just like ours. Persons who have no intelligence deride the transcendental form of the Lord as material. Thus ends the Bhaktivedanta Papad.

#### impersonalism, maya, misunderstanding the Lord
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/UpJcbinR1uE?t=275
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** This segment discusses the impersonalists' view of the Supreme Lord, their acceptance of a supreme power but denial of His personal form, and their misunderstanding of the Lord's pastimes.

They think that when the Lord also descends, in one of the lecture Prabhupada mentions that impersonalists or mayavadis they may accept that there is some supreme power but their understanding is that Lord, since He is omnipresent, He is present everywhere, then He cannot be localized. He cannot be a person. This is a mundane logic, just like if you take a sheet of paper and tear it into parts, small small pieces, then the original sheet ceases to exist in its form. So once there are gross atheists who don't believe in the Supreme Lord, there are others who may believe in the Supreme but they say the Lord is impersonal, He is not a person, He is present in the form of His energy. They are impersonalists who even talk about the pastimes of the Lord. They may say that the Lord is impersonal, Lord is that energy, but then when the Lord descends, the form of the Lord is not spiritual. That's why the word is used mayavadis.

#### maya, surrender, illusion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/UpJcbinR1uE?t=378
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** This segment delves into the concept of Maya, how it influences even intelligent people, and how it distorts the understanding of surrendering to Krishna.

avajananti mamudha. Krishna derides such people. avajananti mamudha manushim tanumashrita. When they descend in human-like form, just like the Lord, for His enacting His various pastimes, appears in different forms. Just like the requirement was there to lift the Mother Earth, so it took the boar form. So Lord may also take for enacting pastimes, pleasing pastimes with His devotees, just to give a glimpse of the relationship and the wonderful pastimes of the Lord and the devotees in the spiritual world. The Lord may take a human form and avajananti mamudha, mudha, fools deride me. They think that the human form, the Lord, is also maya. Ultimately, Lord is impersonal. And when Krishna says, surrender unto me, they even say that it is not to the Lord, but to the ultimate absolute truth, the impersonal supreme within Krishna. This is all manifestation of the influence of maya, or illusion. Even big, big scholars, even most intelligent people, maya, paritta, jnana, the real knowledge, the real understanding of the Supreme Lord gets covered.

#### Krishna consciousness, maya, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/UpJcbinR1uE?t=474
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Even though we may be very, very powerful, but when we switch off our engine, engine of Krishna consciousness, then we are tugged by maya."
**Summary:** This segment uses the analogy of a tugboat towing a large ship to illustrate how even powerful individuals can be influenced by Maya when they disconnect from Krishna consciousness.

One time Prabhupada with his disciples, they had visited a harbor where all the ships come and they get, so to say, loaded, unloaded, all that. So Prabhupada was walking with his disciples, and they saw one very, very big ship. Generally in the airports also it happens that when the airplane comes, sometimes it's towed. Maybe because they don't want to use the fuel. So it's towed by small, different vehicles. They tow the airplane. So here also in this harbor, they saw one small little boat. It's called a tug boat. It was towing the ship to the harbor. Big ship, but it was towed by a small boat. And the engine of the ship was off. So Prabhupada saw that, and Prabhupada who is always Krishna conscious, everything you see, Prabhupada connects it to Krishna. So Prabhupada says, even though we may be very, very powerful, but when we switch off our engine, engine of Krishna consciousness, then we are tugged by maya. A small maya, apparently maya, avajananti mahmoodha. Even big, big people, scholars, appear to be very learned, good analytical skills, good comprehension, very knowledgeable, maya prithagyana.

But maya tugs away their understanding. And they are not able to understand the supremacy of the Supreme Lord. He is very powerful. And because of his power, because of his influence, he is able to take the Supreme Lord head on. At least he is thinking so. He is challenging the Supreme Lord. Many times when people also get a lot of money, a lot of power in this material world, they can get blinded. bhogaishvaya-prasaktanam taya-prita-cetasam vyavasatmika-buddhi The steady intelligence, that understanding that, yes, Krishna is the Supreme, we have to surrender to Him, doesn't come about. But Krishna says, even if a person does not surrender, does not accept the supremacy, finally at the point of death, one has to accept. mṛtyu-sarva-rascaham At the time of death, no matter how big a person may be, how much power, how much position, how much wealth a person may be, finally he has to surrender to Krishna.

#### detachment, wealth, Krishna consciousness

**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "In the end, wealth is only a fact of life, that I'm accustomed to."
**Summary:** This segment shares the reflections of Steve Jobs in his final days, highlighting his realization that wealth and success are meaningless without joy and a connection to the Lord.

This is a very interesting article. These are the words of Steve Jobs. We all know Steve Jobs. He is known all over the world for his business acumen. It's because of him today, Apple is what it is today. World over people are talking about Apple products. And it's one of the most valuable companies in the world. Billions of dollars of cash reserves, most profitable. And although it's being run now, but everyone knows, it's the work of Steve Jobs. He set into motion the processes, the whole company, the innovation, the products. So now what Steve Jobs is saying, when he was, in the last days, some of you may be aware, he was suffering from pancreatic cancer. So he was bedridden. He was hospitalized. So he writes, I reached the pinnacle of success in the business world. There can't be anyone more successful than him. Although there are many Fortune 500 companies, successful companies, but someone, if they can remember top CEOs of the world, he'll be one among them. I reached the pinnacle of success in the business world. In others' eyes, my life is an epitome of success. It's like a benchmark. Success means this is success. However, aside from work, I have little joy. In the end, wealth is only a fact of life, that I'm accustomed to.

#### detachment, wealth, God-consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/UpJcbinR1uE?t=806
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "At this moment, lying on the sickbed and recalling my whole life, I realize that all the recognition and wealth that I took so much pride in have paled and become meaningless in the face of impending death."
**Summary:** Continuing with Steve Jobs' reflections, this segment emphasizes the insignificance of worldly achievements in the face of death and the importance of pursuing matters beyond wealth, such as love and God-consciousness.

So here I'm talking about somebody may have power, position, may have all the wealth in the world, but unless that relationship to the Lord is there, unless Krishna consciousness is there, there'll be always something lacking, nagging, something is missing. This is what Steve Jobs realizes, as we see in the following words. At this moment, lying on the sickbed and recalling my whole life, I realize that all the recognition and wealth that I took so much pride in have paled and become meaningless in the face of impending death. Have paled and become meaningless. So all power, position, name, fame, what we hanker for, especially the demons, get so much puffed up with, they all pale into insignificance in due course of time. In the darkness, I look at the green lights from the life-supporting machines and hear the humming mechanical sound. He was in the ICU with all kinds of medical gadgets attached to his body. I can feel the breath of God of death drawing closer. Now I know when we have accumulated sufficient wealth to last our lifetime, we should pursue other matters that are unrelated to wealth. We are so fortunate we are directly pursuing that. Should be something that is more important than wealth. Because after having achieved the epitome of success, something is missing, something more than what wealth can give us. Non-stop pursuing of wealth will only turn a person into a twisted being, just like me. In one sense, he was a nice-hearted person. He came to India. He also learned yoga. He was practicing Buddhism. Some element of God-consciousness was there. God gave us the senses to let us feel the love in everyone's heart, not the illusions brought about by wealth.

The wealth I have won in my life, I cannot take with me. Whichever stage in life we are at right now, with time, we will all face the day when the curtains come down. And it covers the real knowledge and makes us feel proud. Makes us feel great. To the extent that we can even dare challenge the Lord, or dare deny the existence of God. #### devotion, spiritual vision, illusion

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** This segment discusses the importance of spiritual vision, contrasting it with material vision, and how illusion covers our real knowledge, preventing us from seeing the Lord and holy places as they truly are.

So here the Lord is being mentioned that the Lord is taking the Mother Earth, upholding on the ends of his tusk, trying to protect it, and the demon is making fun of him. He laughed, oh an amphibious beast. So demons, so Prabhupada mentions when the Lord comes in the form of fish incarnation or tortoise or the demons, because they have, no respect for the Lord. They are not seeing from shastra chakshu. They are seeing with their material vision. Just like many times people see the deities as made of metal or stone, or they call it idol worship. Sometimes people say, why do you worship statues? So if that devotion is not there, that bhakti is not there, that love for the Lord is not there, that shastric vision is not there, then we will see the Lord is in the form of a beast. How can this be a Lord? How can boar be a Lord? How can a fish be a Lord? Just like yesterday we were discussing, how can the place like Vrindavan with all the ganda nalas, all the dirty water, all the sewage strewn all over, all kinds of dirty pigs moving around, how can that be a holy place? Only when we have spiritual vision, with the help of what we hear from the shastras, guru, sadhu, shastra, then we are able to see things as they are. Otherwise, illusion covers our real knowledge.

**Key Quote:** "And the journey would take many, many days together. So they would pack everything, the food articles and, you know, the ingredients for making offerings to the Lord, the jewellery, the dresses. The service of the Lord would continue."
**Summary:** This segment begins the story of a Swamiji traveling with his disciples and a deity of Lord Narasimha, highlighting their dedication to serving the Lord even during a long and arduous journey.

One time, it seems the 33rd year Swamiji of Ahuvillam Math, this was I think many years back, that time there was no transport. So Ahuvillam was, even now for that matter, it's a forest. Although now it's, lot of people have started going, so some amenities have. So this, Swamiji with his disciples, they were travelling from Ahuvillam to Mysore. Maybe they had another Math there. So they were travelling, they were walking, there were no vehicles those days. And, he had a small deity of Lord Narasimha with him. And the journey would take many, many days together. So they would pack everything, the food articles and, you know, the ingredients for making offerings to the Lord, the jewellery, the dresses. The service of the Lord would continue. So they would add huge boxes, many disciples, some of them carrying jewellery, some of them carrying the ingredients. And they would stop at a place, maybe some tent or something they would put. They'll cook, offer the Lord, change the dress, worship the Lord. All that would continue.

#### faith, protection, Lord Narasimha
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/UpJcbinR1uE?t=1267
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Real test will come when we encounter danger."
**Summary:** Continuing the story, this segment describes the disciples' fear of an impending attack by dacoits and the Swamiji's unwavering faith in Lord Narasimha's protection, emphasizing that the real test of devotion comes in times of danger.

So one time they were passing through a forest, and suddenly there was a commotion. All the disciples got rattled, and one of them came, rushing to this Jeer Swamiji and said, Looks like dacoits. They're going to attack us. So there was a whole commotion among the disciples, that dacoits are going to attack. They are, you know, armed with guns and all that thing. And they were all alone in the forest. That was a time when the evening aarti had to happen to the Lord. Offering had to happen. So the Swamiji, after hearing all his disciples quite agitated, quite fearful, he said, Don't worry. Lord Narasimha will protect. It's easy to say when there is a danger. Real test comes at that time. Prabhupada also says, It's very easy to say, even a parrot, you can teach him to say, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna. But finally, when you catch the neck, ka, ka, ka, it will say this. So even we should not be very proud of our devotion. Real test will come when we encounter danger. It's very easy to chant Hare Krishna. Otherwise, yeah, we depend on Krishna. Krishna is a protector. We can chant shlokas. Heart of heart, we should always feel I have no devotion because it's a fact. We don't know whether we'll be able to remember Krishna when death comes.

So, so the Swamiji had a lot of faith. He said, Krishna, Narasimha will protect. Don't worry. So he got up. He was about to sit down for worshipping the Lord, worship the Lord. He got up and he went directly to the chief of the bandits, the dacoits, and spoke to him. He said, what do you want? He said, don't speak anything. All what you have, hand it over. Basically, he was interested in, they knew that, you know, the jewelry of the Lord, the mukut, a whole lot of things were there, sometimes made of precious stones, diamonds, hand over everything. Again, very unperturbed, he said, I have faith that Narasimha will protect. He said, sure, nothing belongs to us. It is all the Lord's property. I have no inhibition in giving it to you. Whether it's with me or it's with you, it's the Lord's property. It belongs to the Lord. Just give me a little time. It's a time when, you know, the worship has to happen of the Lord. Naivedya has to happen. Give me some time. You wait for a while and after this is over, you can take it.

So, the bandit also, you know, okay. So, all other, his, you know, other bandits, they were all hiding in the forest. Always there is element of fear of getting caught, of some kind of retaliation. So, they were hidden and this bandit somehow waited that the puja get over and he can take the entire jewelry and all the, whatever they had with them. So, this Swamiji, unperturbed, started worshipping the Lord. Couple of disciples, they even started cooking shakar prangla for offering to the Lord, for Naivedya. They used to cook also. But they were shivering, you know, the bandit is sitting and, you know. So, the worship happened. Aarti happened. The Naivedya happened. And while all these things were going on, all the dacoits, who were hidden in the forest, they started running and coming towards, save us, save us, save us. And they fell at the feet of the Swamiji. He says, what happened? He says, never we have experienced this. We are seeing so many lions coming from different directions, trying to attack us. Please save us from these lions. Please save us from these kind of, never experienced this kind of a thing. So, the Swamiji understood what these lions are. So, he prayed to the Lord, Lord Naishengadev, Please protect. And, as I said, the lions, whatever, whether they were coming or, whatever they experienced, they retreated. And all this bandit fell at the feet of Swamiji. And, thank them for protecting them. And it seems after that, these dacoits, they escorted. Because they were scared. They were scared. They were scared. They were scared of the Dacoits. They were scared of the Dacoits. So, they went, and they came. They came, they came, they came, they came, they came, they came, they knew forests, there could be other parties of dacoits. So they escorted Swamiji and disciples.

#### protection, supremacy of the Lord, maya
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/UpJcbinR1uE?t=1578
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Because of their knowledge, although the Lord is supreme, he exhibits his supremacy, exhibits his kind protection."
**Summary:** This segment concludes the story, emphasizing the Lord's eagerness to protect His devotees and the eventual vanquishing of those who challenge His supremacy, while also noting how Maya can obscure this knowledge.

Till Mysore, left them there and went back. So this is small, this thing that how the Lord, always the Lord is eager to protect his devotees. And the demons who are always challenging the Lord, not accepting the supremacy of the Lord, will get vanquished in due course of time. Because of their knowledge, although the Lord is supreme, he exhibits his supremacy, exhibits his kind protection. But unfortunately, when Maya covers the real knowledge, when we are overpowered by pride, illusion, then we are not able to experience all that. So only when we are overpowered by pride, illusion, then we are not able to experience all that.

#### maya, humility, complacency
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/UpJcbinR1uE?t=1626
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Little bit we become complacent. Little bit we become proud. Little bit we think that, you know, I am great. I don't need any help."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the importance of humility and the dangers of complacency, explaining how Maya seizes opportunities to overpower us when we become proud or think we don't need help, often without us even realizing it.

Only when we follow this process, like Prabhupada here is saying, Prabhupada also gives example of Nityananda Prabhu. He is all spiritual. Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, Nityananda Prabhu. But demoniac people, whose knowledge is covered, consider the body of supreme personality to be material, just like ours. Avajananti Maa Mooda. Persons who have no intelligence deride the transcendental form of the Lord as material. So we should also have an element of this element of fear within us. We cannot become complacent. This Maya also, just like we were talking about the three modes of material nature, Rajoguna, Tamoguna, Satoguna. And Krishna in the Bhagavad Gita says these modes always compete with one another. Sometimes we find ourselves very Rajasic, overpowered by Rajoguna. Sometimes we find ourselves overpowered by Tamoguna. Likewise, just like we mentioned one of the previous, Maya Jhapatiya Dhare. Maya needs an opportunity. Little bit we become complacent. Little bit we become proud. Little bit we think that, you know, I am great. I don't need any help. Sometimes it may not be even in words. We may not even say it in words. Our action speaks louder than words. Krishna is very there in our heart. He is sensitive to what we are thinking, our consciousness. The pride may not be in our words. Pride, we may not challenge verbally. But it may manifest in our actions. And when that happens, Jhapatiya Dhare, you're overpowered by Maya. And the best part about Maya is not best part. The worst part is we don't even know that we are in Maya. Only when I say When eyes get opened, we come out of illusion, we realize we were in maya. Before coming to Krishna Kancha, we were not thinking that we were in maya. We were blissful in ignorance.

---
#### maya, surrender, protection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/UpJcbinR1uE?t=1757
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "His only business is let me take shelter of the Supreme Lord, let me catch hold of the feet of the Lord, let me catch hold of the feet of the Lord very tightly."
**Summary:** This segment concludes by emphasizing the dynamic nature of Maya's attacks and the importance of humility, dependence on the Lord, and surrender as the means to receive Krishna's protection and overcome illusion.

Only when we look back with the knowledge what we have today, and when we look back what we were, what our consciousness was, what our thought process was, what our longing was, then we realize we were in maya. Now this is something very gross. As we practice, as a sadhaka, when we practice, the attacks of maya is very dynamic. Sometimes gross, sometimes subtle. It just penetrates into our consciousness. So that way a devotee is always very humble, realizes that he is very fallen, dependent on the Lord, always conscious, realizes that it is only by the mercy of the Lord, today he has some Krishna consciousness. Realizing that he is fallible, realizing that maya is very powerful, it's energy of the Lord. Although maya is this thing, it's not fearful because Krishna says, I will free you from maya, don't worry. His only business is let me take shelter of the Supreme Lord, let me catch hold of the feet of the Lord, let me catch hold of the feet of the Lord very tightly. Attacks of maya will be there, but so long as I am surrendered to the Lord, Krishna will give me protection. Krishna will surely protect me. Stop here. Granth Raj, Srimad Bhagavatam ki, Srila Prabhupada ki.
