## HG Amitasana Dasa

### 2015-12-03 | SB 3.18.6 | HG Amitasana Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### Lord's feelings, Mayavadi philosophy, Devotee protection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gVyhsE-q17U?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The conclusion is that God is as sentient as we are."
**Summary:** This segment covers the translation and purport of Srimad Bhagavatam 3.18.6, focusing on the Lord's feelings and how Mayavadi philosophers deny them, and how the Lord protects his devotees.

OM NAMO BHAGWATE VASUDEVAYA. Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto No. 3, Chapter No. 18, Text No. 6: SATURBYAMANU ARIDURUKKATOMARAYA, TAMSTRAGRAH GAMGAM APALAKSHABHITAM, TODAMRISHAN NIRGAD AMBUMADYAD, RAHATASAKARENUR YATHEBHA, RAHATASAKARENUR YATHEBHA. Translation and purport by His Divine Eminence, A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Srila Prabhupada. Translation: Although the Lord was pained by the sharp-like abusive words of the demons, He bore the pain. But seeing that the earth on the ends of His tusk was frightened, He rose out of the water, just as an elephant emerges with its female companion when assailed by an alligator. Purport: The Mayavadi philosophers cannot understand that the Lord has feelings. The Lord is satisfied if someone offers Him a nice prayer. And similarly, if someone decries His existence or calls Him by ill names, God is dissatisfied. The Supreme Personality of Godhead is decried by the Mayavadi philosophers who are almost demons. They say that God has no head, no form, no existence, and no legs, hands, or other bodily limbs. In other words, they say that He is dead or lame. All this is not true. All these misconceptions of the Supreme Lord are a source of dissatisfaction to Him. He is never pleased with such atheistic descriptions. In this case, although the Lord felt sorrow from the piercing words of the demons, He delivered the earth for the satisfaction of the demigods, who are ever His devotees. The conclusion is that God is as sentient as we are. He is satisfied by our prayers and dissatisfied by our harsh words against Him. In order to give protection to His devotees, He is always ready to tolerate insulting words from the Atheists. Thus ends the Bhaktivedanta purport.

---
#### Lord's qualities, Sentient being, Pain and pleasure
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gVyhsE-q17U?t=154
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Just like, to give a crude example, we also experience pain and pleasure. If someone praises us, we feel pleasure. If someone criticizes us, passes a sarcastic remark, we feel pain."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the Lord's qualities, emphasizing that He is a sentient being who experiences pleasure and pain, just like us, and that He is Shad Aishwarya Purnam, possessing unlimited opulence.

So this chapter, the battle between Lord Bohr and Hiranaksha. So in the previous verses, we have read how Hiranaksha used very abusive words. And in this verse, it's mentioned, the Lord was pained by the shaft-like abusive words of the demon. But he bore the pain. So Prabhupada in the purport mentions, the Lord also has feelings. He is a sentient being. Just like we have feelings, we are sentient beings, we are amsa of the Lord. We being amsa of the Lord, the Lord Himself, who is Purna, who is the source of all these amsas, or who is the complete whole, the absolute truth, He is also a person, a sentient being. And Prabhupada mentions, because he has feelings, bhavag rahayu janardana. When someone offers prayers, the Lord is very pleased. We being amsa, we also have the tendency, if someone calls us by our name, or someone praises us, or glorifies us, we also feel some pleasure. Although that's not our constitutional position, to be glorified. However, we are the predominators, and our constitutional position is to glorify the predominator. So just like, to give a crude example, we also experience pain and pleasure. If someone praises us, we feel pleasure. If someone criticizes us, passes a sarcastic remark, we feel pain. So here, the demon used abusive words and it pained the Lord. All said and done, the demon also is amsha of the Lord. And the Lord is said to be Shad Aishwarya Purnam. He has unlimited strength, unlimited wealth, unlimited knowledge, unlimited fame, unlimited beauty, unlimited renunciation. And other good qualities, which we see, is also there. The Lord is embodiment of such good qualities. Titiksva Karunikaha. Titiksva means tolerant. So Lord is also very tolerant.

---
#### Tolerance, Free will, Bewilderment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gVyhsE-q17U?t=355
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Free will means we have a choice to do whatever we want."
**Summary:** This segment addresses the question of why the Lord allows suffering and evil in the world, explaining that He is very tolerant and has given jivas free will to choose, even to decry or blaspheme Him.

Sometimes we wonder, just two days back somebody asked me this question very sarcastically, where was Lord when the Muslims invaded and ransacked the temples, destroyed the temples, destroyed the deities? Actually, it can be very bewildering. Unless we have proper shravanam and we have understood, so this person who was asking me appeared to be quite, he was not an atheist, but from his question it was quite evident that he is bewildered. Where was the Lord? Sometimes it becomes difficult to reconcile. If Lord is all-powerful, he is the supreme controller, not a blade of grass moves without his sanction, then how do we reconcile all these things happening in the world? So the Lord is very, very tolerant. The jivas, who are his amshas, can say, the jivas, who are his amshas, can sometimes decry the Lord, can abuse the Lord, can become envious of him, can blaspheme the Lord. He has given the free will. Free will means we have a choice to do whatever we want. We can decry the Lord, we can blaspheme the Lord, we can glorify the Lord, we can pray to the Lord, we can abuse the Lord. He tolerates.

---
#### Karma, Free will, Atheism
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gVyhsE-q17U?t=479
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We can abuse the Lord, the Lord has given us a free will, but we will get back the reaction for our deeds."
**Summary:** This segment continues the discussion on the Lord's tolerance and free will, emphasizing that while we can abuse the Lord, we will face the consequences of our actions, and that the Lord allows even atheists to express their views.

On one side we see temples were ransacked, deities were, so to say, broken, the Lord tolerated. No doubt in this world every action has a reaction. Newton also pointed out this, every action has an equal and opposite reaction. When we see that we wonder why the Lord is not, is the Lord really there? The Lord tolerates. And other side when we see some calamities, when we see some people suffering very miserably, again we get bewildered. Why the Lord is so cruel? Why the Lord is so unkind? The fact is we all are getting what is due to us. We can abuse the Lord, the Lord has given us a free will, but we will get back the reaction for our deeds. So here, as I mentioned, Lord Shad Aishwarya Purnam, he has many, many good qualities, an aboundment of all such good qualities. One of the qualities he is abounding here is he bore the insult. Because he has given us a free will, we can abuse the Lord, we can blaspheme the Lord. There are great atheists in this world, who very strongly put forth arguments, God does not exist. He gives the intelligence.

---
#### Faith, Dependence on Krishna, Astrology
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gVyhsE-q17U?t=587
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Actions speak louder than words."
**Summary:** This segment introduces a story about a devotee who, facing challenges, sought help from an astrologer and wore a yantra, illustrating how even devotees can sometimes lack complete faith and dependence on Krishna.

Even we as devotees sometimes we may act in ways, although in our words we can say that yes, we are devotees of Krishna, we surrender to Krishna, we depend on Krishna. But sometimes our actions can prove otherwise. Actions speak louder than words. The Lord is there in the heart, he is witness to all our actions, all our feelings. Sometimes we may have an element of distrust or faithlessness. Sometimes when devotees also go through some rough patches or challenges in life, our faith in the Lord can shake. One time there was a devotee, Visala Das, he was foreign-bodied, he was from America. He had come to India and he was going through lot of challenges in his life. So he came to Vrindavan and because of all the ups and downs in his life, even his Krishna consciousness was getting affected. His chanting, his sadhana. So he, just like we go through challenges, so we try to find various remedies. We go to the doctor, we go to somebody. So he went to an astrologer. And the astrologer pointed out that there is an influence of the stars, Rahu, Rahu planet. And because of that, he is going through this challenge. And to counteract the influence of Rahu planets, he should wear a yantra. So as advised by the astrologer, he bought the yantra, he wore it. Quite a big one and it was quite visible. He wore it on his neck. So he was quite satisfied, okay, now at least. And his whole objective was that how somehow he could counter all the evil influences which is there in his life and then he can peacefully chant Hare Krishna, peacefully practice Krishna consciousness.

---
#### Surrender, Faith in Krishna, Prabhupada's teachings
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gVyhsE-q17U?t=754
**Verse References:** BG 18.66
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If you just surrender to Krishna, Krishna with a slight kick can annihilate one lakh Rahu planets."
**Summary:** This segment continues the story, describing how a senior devotee and Prabhupada corrected Visala Das, emphasizing the importance of surrendering to Krishna and having faith in His protection, rather than relying on astrology.

So he came to the temple just as he was entering the temple, Krishna Balram temple. Another very senior disciple of Prabhupada stopped him, he said, where are you coming from? And this yantra what he was wearing on the neck was quite visible. So he said, what is this? So he told, I had gone to the astrologer and he has given me this yantra and he has told that it will counter all the influence of the Rahu planet. So then he immediately stopped him there and said, but Prabhupada has never asked us to go to astrologer. Prabhupada said we should have faith in Krishna. Sarva dharman parityajya maam ekam sharanam raja aham tam sarva paap eva. He chanted that shloka. Krishna will counter all the evil influences, we have to just surrender to him. Krishna is saying maam ekam. Why are you going to an astrologer? So he said, why don't you go and meet Prabhupada? In the meantime this devotee went and told Prabhupada, Prabhupada this is what has happened, this devotee has gone to an astrologer and he has got this yantra, he is wearing that yantra to counter the influences of Rahu planet. So this devotee went and met Shloka Prabhupada. So Prabhupada saw him, he said where had you gone? So he told him, I had gone, who is he? How much did you pay him? So Prabhupada was quite unhappy. And then he said, Shloka Prabhupada quoted the verse, Sarva dharman parityajya, He said the whole Shloka 18.66 and Prabhupada said, If you just surrender to Krishna, Krishna will with a slight kick, annihilate one lakh Rahu planets.

---
#### Faith, Tolerance, Forgiveness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gVyhsE-q17U?t=888
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Lord is very forgiving."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the challenges of maintaining faith, especially during difficult times, and emphasizes the Lord's tolerance and forgiveness, encouraging devotees to learn from their mistakes and move forward.

So even we sometimes, again we are conditioned souls, faith is also, only a pure devotee has complete dependence on the Lord. We have examples of great devotees like Prahalad, he was thrown in a pit of snakes, imagine hissing snake, even one snake if we see from a distance we get fearful. So we may not have that faith and our actions prove it, our actions prove that. The Lord is there in our heart, he is witness to all our feelings, all our actions and sometimes the Lord is pained. We may chant, but then our actions prove otherwise. We may not have dependence on the Lord, we may not have faith in his protection. The Lord tolerates. Because the Lord is very tolerant, we are all continuing in Krishna consciousness. Otherwise even as devotees we end up doing so many nonsense things, which is very painful to the Lord, painful to the spiritual master. They are very tolerant, they are very forgiving, very kind, very merciful. Again we fall down, again they give us an opportunity. So long as we somehow, because just like a child when he is learning to walk, he cannot just run all of a sudden, he will fall down. And many times when we also make mistakes, we fall down, one thing is yes, we should feel there is an element of guilt, but beyond a point we shouldn't lament. Learn from the mistake, the Lord is very tolerant. Sometimes people also say, I have done so many sinful activities in the past, what is the hope for me? The Lord is very forgiving. We try our best not to repeat those mistakes, we try our best to not commit sinful activities intentionally. Unintentionally by the force of kala, guna, karma, sometimes mistakes happen, we move on. We beg forgiveness, we offer our prayers. We beg for strength and move on.

---
#### Faith, Devotional Process, Imitation vs. Following
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gVyhsE-q17U?t=1029
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We cannot artificially imitate that stage."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the devotional process and the importance of developing firm faith in the Lord, distinguishing between genuine faith and artificial imitation, using Prabhupada's teachings on prasadam as an example.

So here, just like Prabhupada, the devotee was telling, we are following a process. Adho, Shraddha, Tata, Sadhu, Sangha, Bhajan, Kriya, Anartha, all the misgivings, all the doubts, all the faithlessness what we have. Just like Prabhupada is saying, mayavadi fila, they don't even have faith in the Lord, the Lord tolerates. Then a stage comes, nishtha, firm faith. Then we don't doubt. Then we have strong conviction in the presence of the Lord, in the protection of the Lord. We cannot artificially imitate that stage. I gave example of Prahlad Maharaj. There is a very nice conversation in Perfect Question Perfect Answer book where one devotee asked Prabhupada, Prabhupada, many times we come across prasadam which is spoiled. You know, sometimes prasadam which is left over for many days, you may see some fungus or you may say something. So he asked Prabhupada, can we honor that prasadam, can we take that prasadam? So Prabhupada says, for a pure devotee, it does not matter, he will honor it. Even if it is apparently spoiled fungus. Because he is not in bodily concept. He is completely at the spiritual platform, transcendental. When he takes prasadam also, he is just absorbing the transcendence. He is unaffected. But he says, neophyte devotees or devotees like us should not imitate pure devotees. If the prasadam is spoiled, we cannot imitate pure devotees. Okay, let me also eat, I will not be affected. So, Prabhupada many times talked about imitation and following in the footsteps of pure devotees. There is a difference between the two.

---
#### Imitation, Competition, Devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gVyhsE-q17U?t=1162
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Conditioned souls, all the envy, jealousy, want to prove a point, want to show that they are great devotees."
**Summary:** This segment shares a humorous anecdote about devotees in Vrindavan trying to imitate yogis by controlling their bowel movements, illustrating the dangers of superficial imitation and the underlying motivations of conditioned souls.

One time in Vrindavan, devotees, somehow the word started spreading that if a person passes stool one time in a day, he is a yogi. If he passes stool two times, he is a bhogi. And more than two times, he is a rogi. So sometimes devotees also, they want to show off, there is some kind of a competition. So some devotees would hold on. And somehow the news reached Prabhupada. And one devotee goes and says, some devotees, even though they may have stomach ache, but somehow they hold on because they want to prove that they are yogis. So when Prabhupada heard that, said, is it? Devotees are doing it? Acha? He was quite surprised. Conditioned souls, all the envy, jealousy, want to prove a point, want to show that they are great devotees.

---
#### Imitation, Knowledge, Bhakti
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gVyhsE-q17U?t=1270
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Without understanding the intent, if you blindly follow, that's why bhakti is not just sentimental stuff."
**Summary:** This segment continues with Prabhupada's humorous story about a clerk who meticulously copied a fly smashed in an old book, highlighting the absurdity of blind imitation and emphasizing the need for knowledge and understanding in bhakti.

Pure devotee of the Lord has none of these agendas. Someone praises, it's fine. Someone blasphemes, it's fine. Someone, you know, because they are absorbed in the Lord. We conditioned souls are our satchidananda, we project in various things. So, so Prabhupada said, Acha? And then, he told a very humorous story. To demonstrate that how, devotees sometimes imitate. He said there was one time, a clerk in the office, he was assigned by his supervisor, the task of copying, making fair notes. There was a rough book. Sometimes in earlier times, unlike present times, you have soft copies, it's all in the computer, you can take a backup. There could be some gazettes or some kind of official papers and after 20 years, 25 years, 30 years, you make a fresh copy. You write it again. So he was assigned the task of making a fair copy of something which was there already in a book which was now worn out. So while he was copying, suddenly he got up from his seat and he was jumping all around the room trying to catch something. So the boss came in and he is wondering what's happening to him. He is supposed to be sitting in his seat and writing, suddenly jumping and trying to catch something. So he asked him, what are you up to? He said, I am trying to catch a fly. He said, why? He said, come. See in this book here, in this original book there is a fly which is smashed and pasted here. It could have happened 30 years back, 40 years back, somebody was writing and the book was folded, fly came in and it got smashed. And the fly was there in the book and he wants to fail that fly by copying what was there already. Without understanding the intent, if you blindly follow, that's why bhakti is not just sentimental stuff. Bhaktivedanta, it should be backed with proper understanding, proper knowledge. Surrender also is not a sentimental thing. Many times people are at a very sentimental level. So bhakti has to be backed with proper understanding. The faith in the Lord is not at a very sentimental level.

---
#### Knowledge, Faith, Devotee Protection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gVyhsE-q17U?t=1399
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Even though a devotee may take some preventive measures, apparently may appear to be taking some protective steps, still, he has complete faith in the protection of the Lord."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes that true faith is backed by knowledge, purification, and detachment, and that even while taking practical precautions, a devotee maintains complete faith in the Lord's protection, citing the Lord breaking his promise in the Mahabharata for his devotee's sake.

It has to be backed with tapasya, backed with purification, backed with anartha-livriti, backed with proper knowledge. And when we are backed with proper knowledge, then we will not imitate. On one side, yes, we have faith in the Lord. Other side, whatever needs to be done as a matter of duty will be done. Even though a devotee may take some preventive measures, apparently may appear to be taking some protective steps, still, he has complete faith in the protection of the Lord. He has the faith that although I may take so many steps, ultimately if the Lord sanctions, I will be protected. Otherwise, you may have best of doctors, you may have best of medicines, but if Krishna does not protect, the patient will die. So here Prabhupada also mentions in the purport that the Lord, irrespective of the words spoken by the boar, seeing that mother earth was frightened because mother earth was inundated, seeing the earth on the end of his tusk was frightened, he rose out of the water, just an elephant emerges with its female companion when assailed by an alligator. So then Prabhupada says, in order to give protection to his devotee, he is always ready to tolerate inserting words from the earthies. Even when the Lord took, Lord Krishna took the vow not to fight in the battle, not to take up any weapons, the Lord broke his promise. Generally a gentleman is supposed to hold on to his promise, is to keep up his words. That's the quality of a gentleman. Once he has said, he doesn't go back on his words, he has made a commitment. The Lord made a commitment. But for his devotee, he broke that commitment. Even at the cost of some people saying, what kind of a Lord he is? He is lying, he is stealing, he is running away from the battle.

---
#### Faith, Surrender, Lord's Control
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gVyhsE-q17U?t=1562
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Why should I worry?"
**Summary:** This segment narrates a story about a young girl on a turbulent flight who remained calm because her father was the pilot, illustrating the unwavering faith a devotee should have in the Lord's control and protection.

But if we understand from, in a disciplic succession we understand, it's all for protection of his devotees, for giving pleasure to his devotees. Even at the cost of his reputation, even at the cost of people using abusive words. One time there was a person, he was in an aircraft, he was flying, and he got late somehow, rushed into the aircraft, reached the seat where he was supposed to sit, put the luggage on the top, and sat. He was in the middle seat. The window seat he saw some middle aged lady, and on his left side, the aisle seat, he saw a young girl, seven years old girl. Sitting all by herself, all alone. The plane took off, and she started speaking to that girl. And he saw that this girl was quite blissful, busy, with her coloring book, crayons were there, and she was listening. And he could see that, you know, you can make out, on the next seat, somebody else was sitting, and on the front, back. The girl was apparently all alone, so he was wondering, how is it this young girl, seven years old, traveling all by herself? Because on the right side, somebody else was sitting, front, back, left, right, everyone, strangers. And the girl all by herself is traveling, so he was wondering, how is it traveling alone? And she was quite busy with, you know, her coloring book, and crayons, and all that. And suddenly, there was some turbulence in the plane. The pilot came on the PA system, and said, we're going through rough weather, and suddenly the plane started, you know, wobbling. Sudden dip happened, and started, you know, some unusual movement, which was not usual for all the passengers. And many of them got frightened, some of them started screaming, some of them started crying. The lady on the right side, sitting next to the window, you know, hands folded, started praying. And this person also, held onto the seat, and, you know, total commotion in the plane. It was dipping, and suddenly turning, and turn happened, and... And while all this thing was happening, the girl, the small little girl, was just sitting, playing with the coloring book and crayons. Totally unperturbed with what's happening. So then, after about 20 minutes, again the pilot came on the PA system, and says, I'm very, very sorry for all the inconvenience caused. We had gone through some rough weather. Now it's all fine, and very soon we'll be landing the airport. So everyone had a sigh of relief, and they were quiet, you know, took a deep breath, and trying to relax. And this person turned to the girl, that young little girl, and said, tell me little one, while all of us were, all so-called adults, were quite panic-stricken, and we were all screaming, you were completely... blissful, happy, undisturbed. By the way, why is it so? Said, my father is the pilot. He's in the cockpit. He's taking me back home. Why should I worry? So the small little girl had the faith that my father is there, and my father is driving the plane. He will help me reach my house safely. So even for a devotee, again, not imitation, nishtha, a stage comes when we may go through some rough times in our life, but we have firm faith the Lord is in control. It's easy to say, it's easy to preach, but difficult to practice. A pure devotee of Lord has that conviction. Just like that little girl had a strong conviction, my father is there, she has nothing to worry. He will take him home. Likewise, a devotee of the Lord, he fearlessly practices Krishna Consciousness, having firm faith that the Lord, my loving Father, Supreme Lord, is in charge of my life. He will take care. He'll take me back home, back to Godhead. So with these words, we'll stop here.

---

## HG Amitasana Dasa

### 2015-12-17 | SB 3.18.8 | HG Amitasana Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### Lord's power, devotion, appreciation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9WCUpqF7GPY?t=55
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Lord's activities could be appreciated only by the demigods headed by Brahma."
**Summary:** This segment discusses how the Lord's power is evident in floating the earth and planets, a fact appreciated by demigods and devotees, while demons cannot understand it.

The Lord placed the earth within his sight on the surface of the water and transferred to her his own energy in the form of ability to float on the water. While the enemy stood looking on, Brahma, the creator of the universe, extolled the Lord and the other demigods rained flowers on him. Those who are demons cannot understand how the supreme personality of God had floated the earth on water, but to devotees of the Lord, this is not a very wonderful fact. Not only the earth, but many, many millions of planets are floating in the air and this floating power is endowed upon them by the Lord. There is no other possible explanation. The materialist can explain that the planets are floating by the law of gravitation, but the law of gravitation works under the control of the direction of the Supreme Lord. That is a version of the Bhagavad Gita which confirms by the Lord's statement that behind the material laws or nature's law and behind the growth, maintenance, production and evolution of all the planetary systems, behind everything is the Lord's direction. The Lord's activities could be appreciated only by the demigods headed by Brahma. And therefore when they saw the uncommon prowess of the Lord in keeping the earth on the surface of the water, they showered flowers on him in appreciation of his transcendental activity. Thus ends the Bhaktivedanta Purport.

---
#### tolerance, free will, Lord's protection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9WCUpqF7GPY?t=157
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Lord has given us the free will, and He does not interfere."
**Summary:** This segment describes the battle between Lord Boar and Hiranaksha, focusing on Hiranaksha's abusive words and the Lord's tolerance, emphasizing the Lord's gift of free will and his protection of the pious.

So here in this verse, the chapter is the battle between Lord Bohr and Hiranapsha. The previous verse: the demon, Hiranapsha, who had golden hair on the head and fearful tusk, gave chase to the Lord while he was rising from the water, even as an alligator would chase an elephant. Roaring like thunder, he said, 'Are you not ashamed of running away before a challenging adversary? There is nothing reproachable for shameless creatures.' So here, Hiranapsha is chasing Lord, who is trying to lift Mother Earth from the ocean, roaring like thunder, and he is using abusive words and saying, 'Are you not ashamed? Are you not ashamed of running away?' He was thinking that the Lord, when he is rising, lifting the Mother Earth, and Hiranapsha is chasing the Lord, it appeared to him that the Lord is running out of fear. 'Are you not ashamed of running away?' And then he uses the word, 'nothing reproachable for shameless creatures.' There are different kinds of demons, atheists, all kinds of impersonalists, mayavadis, who may blaspheme the Lord, or sometimes use words which may be very painful for the Lord, but the Lord sanctions that. The Lord has given us the free will, and He does not interfere. Just like here, at that very moment when Hiranapsha is using these kind of words, he could have just smashed him, killed him, but he allowed him to express whatever he was feeling, even to the extent of abusing the Lord. Unmindful of the Lord, unmindful of all these words which were spoken by Hiranapsha, the Lord, to protect the pious, the devotees, the Lord placed the Earth within the sight of the surface of water, and transferred to her his own energy, in the form of ability to float on the water.

---
#### intelligent design, creation, Lord's direction
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9WCUpqF7GPY?t=352
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Behind everything is the Lord's direction."
**Summary:** This segment argues for an intelligent creator by pointing to the wonders of nature and the precise movements of the earth, contrasting this with materialistic explanations like gravity, which ultimately operate under the Lord's direction.

Actually, if you see around us, even with common sense, we can very easily understand that wonderful things which are happening in nature around us, in the world around us, there has to be an intelligent creator. But if we are hellbent, if we are determined, just like if the Sun is shining and we are in a dark room and we are hellbent not to open the doors, not to open the windows, then it'll be dark. Then there is no point in saying that there is no light or there is no sun. We all have seen that presentation. The earth is moving around the sun at a speed of 1,006,000 km per hour. Phenomenal speed and the mass is 6 x 10 to the power 21 metric tons. Generally when we see on a highway we see a big lorry fully loaded, 15 tons, maybe 20 tons. Generally on the highway we see boards saying that the lorry's speed limit is 40 km per hour, because they know that more the weight, bigger the lorry, higher the possibility of an accident if it crosses beyond a particular speed. So here we see 6 x 10 to the power 21 metric tons, huge mass, with big big mountains, oceans, continents, countries, is moving seamlessly in its orbit around the sun at a fixed speed, 1,006,000 km per hour, the fixed speed, day after day, day after day, so seamlessly, 10 days from now, 15 days from now, 1 month from now, what time the sun will rise you can calculate using mathematical equations. And that's only possible because it is so precise in its movement. And the distance between the earth and the sun, again, is so precise that if it comes closer to sun by 0.00001%, everything will be burned to ashes. And the same is the case if it recedes, everything will be frozen. No doubt scientists have given explanation, there are different laws, physical laws, but then, who made the arrangement? Just like Prabhupada here in the purport is saying, behind everything is the Lord's direction. The materialist can explain that the planet...

#### cause and effect, Krishna as the cause, atheism
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9WCUpqF7GPY?t=561
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "The cause of all causes is Krishna."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the limitations of materialistic explanations for the universe's creation, arguing that while science explores causes, Krishna is the ultimate cause of all causes, and atheistic viewpoints are possible due to Krishna's allowance.

So there are some explanation, it is not that man is not curious. Man is curious to know how this creation came about, how this universe came about. That's why we have this hydron colliders and, you know, search for God particle. Billions of dollars are being spent. Man is curious. And they have some explanation. The materialist can explain that the planets are floating by the law of gravitation, but the law of gravitation works under the control and direction of the Supreme Lord. We may have an explanation of a cause of a particular phenomena, but behind that cause there is another cause. And our shastras point out the cause of all causes is Krishna. Sarvakarana Karanam. For example, what's the cause of rain? It's clouds. What's the cause of cloud? Evaporation of water. What's the cause of evaporation of water? The sun. The sun rays, the heat. What's the cause of sun? Beyond a point, it's difficult even for the science to explain. Maybe they may say big bang. What caused the big bang? Who caused the big bang? A lot of energy was created and energy was transformed into mass. Where did that energy come about? And we see in this world such wonderful creation, beautiful birds, wonderful trees with aromatic flowers. Can it all come from a big bang? Beautiful butterflies with so many wonderful symmetrical wings, colorful butterflies. They all came from a big bang, an explosion. It's like saying how this temple came about. All the cement bags were put together, all the bricks, all the mortar, everything. And then a big blast happened and this temple came about. If we are determined, if we have an atheistic bent of mind and we are hell-bent not to accept the supremacy of Lord, Krishna allows some possible explanations. Let's be not fanatical. There are some explanations to say how God is not in picture, which may appear to some appealing also.

---
#### knowledge, illusion, divine grace
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9WCUpqF7GPY?t=735
**Verse References:** BG 15.15
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "From me comes knowledge, remembrance and forgetfulness."
**Summary:** This segment explains that without divine grace and a bona fide acharya, materialistic explanations can seem appealing, as Krishna allows for logic that obscures His presence, and emphasizes that the Lord's activities are best appreciated by demigods.

If you are not God conscious, if you are not blessed with divya gyan, if you are not blessed with the mercy of a bona fide acharya, it may appear quite appealing. Krishna also says, 'From me comes knowledge, remembrance and forgetfulness.' Wonderful logic and reasoning to prove that God is not in picture. Krishna has given those logics. So, although it's quite apparent, it doesn't require rocket science to accept the supremacy of the Lord. Mayabdhyakshena prakriti suyate saachara acharam: Under my supervision, the nature is working, producing moving and non-moving beings. But then, naham prakasha sarvasya: I am not revealed. I don't reveal myself to everyone. The command of the Lord is not revealed to everyone. So here it is said, the Lord's activities could be appreciated only by demigods, headed by Brahma. And therefore, when they saw the uncommon prowess of the Lord, he shored flowers on him in appreciation of his transcendental activities. Why do we glorify the Lord? Glorify the Lord for his uncommon activities, uncommon pastimes, uncommon mercy, unlimited strength, unlimited wealth, unlimited opulence, unlimited knowledge. So when we see such a great person, naturally when we come across somebody great in this world, we glorify his excellency, your honor. So, Krishna also says, Mayaparita jnana: Even so-called intelligent man, their jnana, their knowledge gets stolen away by illusion.

---
#### illusion, conditioning, Maya's trap
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9WCUpqF7GPY?t=856
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "This is the case how Maya also traps us."
**Summary:** The speaker tells a story about wild boars being trapped by a wise man's plan, illustrating how Maya gradually traps our intelligence through conditioning and comfort.

One time, in a particular village, there were some wild boars. Boar we all know, we are talking about the boars here, but Lord in the form of boar is not an animal. So there were wild boars and they would, in the night time, come inside the village and ransack the village, outside the house. If anything is lying, or any food item is lying, they would eat it up, they would scatter things all around, create a big mess, and they'll go away. So everyone was quiet, and they were wild boars, they were also dangerous. By chance, they come at odd time, and somebody is outside, somebody is not slept, even that could be fatal. Fear of life. So they were all very panic-stricken, fearful, and they tried different means to dissuade the boars, to keep them away from the village, but then, they also needed something to eat. They would come at odd times. So they were all fed up, so to say, making different plans, how to keep them away. One fine day, a wise man in that village came to that panchayat. Panchayat is, you know, where all the senior people in the village, they enact policies of what should happen in that village, what should not happen in the village. So he came to that panchayat, who were all discussing among themselves how to keep these wild boars away. So he said, 'I'll give you a simple plan. Just follow what I am saying.' So since they were all very intelligent people, panchayat, so they were a little egoistic also, and, you know, how can someone come with a simple plan which they are not aware of? They were reluctant to accept his advice, but as a last resort, they have tried different things. Okay, let us try this also. So this wise man said, 'you don't want the boars to eat your food, isn't it?' They said, yes. He says, 'let's do the opposite. Let's tell all the villagers to gather a lot of food, each one should bring food from their house, and let's keep the food in the open field. And let the boars eat to their heart's content, whatever they want, let them eat.' So Prima facie was a very foolish advice. On one side, they are trying to safeguard their food, other side, you know, they are saying that, invite the boars to eat the food, and everyone should bring food from their house. Anyway, they had tried different options. As a last resort, let's try this also. So all the villagers were told, they all brought a lot of food items, kept in the center of the field, so to say, and party was organized for the boars to come and enjoy the food. So the smell of the food was very, you know, very nice smell was emanating, and the boars, no time they could sense the food is lying there in the field. An open invitation for them. Initially they were reluctant, apprehensive, who knows it's a trap. Suddenly if you are walking on the road and you find heaps of gold lying there, it's uncommon. So, so the boars were also reluctant and apprehensive, who knows it's a trap, and they were not willing to come. Slowly, one boar came, ate little and suddenly went back, quickly, lest it should not get trapped. The second one came, he also quickly took something and went away. This way, you know, all the boars came and took away. And this phenomena repeated day after day. Slowly what happened to the boars? They got conditioned, and they became very convinced that there is no danger, because every day they are coming, every day they are eating, and every day they are going back. There is no one stopping them. No one is getting trapped. So it is quite, you know, we also build our perceptions. Perception of the world based on our experiences. So they also build a perception that it is great. Lord is all kind, Lord is very merciful. Free food is available, and they would every day come, eat to the heart content, and they will go back. Slowly a time came, they became habituated to free food. They became, so to say, went in the comfort zone. When they are hungry, they would walk into the field, food was lying there, they don't have to worry about anything, eat to the heart content, and go back. So this wise man, then told this panchayat leaders, now start putting poles around the field. And the poles were erected, many, many poles were put. And once you are habituated, you get used to the comfort, then you don't sense the dangers, because you are habituated, it has become your perception that there is no problem here. So they would, in spite of all the poles being erected, they would come every day, and they would go back. Then this wise man said, now start fencing. Put the fence around the poles. So that work also started. Some of the boars saw, but it didn't strike them. Already the perception had built that free food is available for them. And they would go back. A day came, and all the poles were erected all around the field, and the fencing was done, and the boars would come and go away. Then this wise man told, now put a gate. The gate was built. And all the boars passed through the gate, again oblivious of the fact that something is happening in the environment around them. And one fine day, all the boars were in the middle of the field trying to eat, and the wise man had now closed the gate. Fencing all around, the gate is there, and the gate is closed. All the boars are inside the field. All were trapped. This is the case how Maya also traps us.

---
#### Maya, illusion, material education, spiritual perception
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9WCUpqF7GPY?t=1290
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The more we are materially educated, and if we are not bad with Shastra Chakshu, Divya Gyan, more and more there is a possibility of we being an atheist."
**Summary:** This segment discusses how Maya traps our intelligence, leading to a sense of controllership and pride in material accomplishments, potentially leading to atheism, and emphasizes the importance of spiritual perception.

Maya also traps our intelligence. Even the scientists, so-called scientists, today if you see, there are lot of accomplishments, technological innovations. When we innovate and find out something, a sense of pride comes. Sense of controllership comes. I am in control. If you see a big aircraft, A320 and Dreamliners, you can see huge big aircraft, it's just taking off. It's a big achievement in one sense if you see. So when we see that we are able to control nature, we are able to control things, we are able to handle things, minus Lord, using mathematics, using mathematical equations, using our prowess, using our intelligence, Maya Prithagyana, slowly it starts dawning us, we are God. God is not in picture. We are able to fathom everything using our intelligence. That's why the more so-called material education, just like Prabhupada says, the materialist can explain the planets, the more we are materially educated, and if we are not bad with Shastra Chakshu, Divya Gyan, more and more there is a possibility of we being an atheist. Because Maya, just like the boars, they got trapped. Little by little, it was building up. Likewise, little by little, ahankara starts building up. I know everything. I am in control of things. Finally, death comes. All the knowledge is gone, all the intelligence is gone, all accomplishment is gone. Just like that other example we took of how the frog in a beaker, slowly the temperature raising of the frog is not realizing. It's trying to get accustomed, accustomed, accustomed to that new temperature. And finally, it dies in that thing. Same is the case with this thing. Our real knowledge, every living entity has that understanding. It is dormant in us, but that knowledge is covered. How it happens? Slowly, little by little, little by little, little by little, because we see that we are able to manage things without God. We see that we are able to control things without God. We see that we are able to explain things without God in picture. Then the point comes, where is God?

---
#### modern civilization, helplessness, humility, spiritual perception
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9WCUpqF7GPY?t=1460
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "It's better to be humble, to realize that we don't know, to always accept the supremacy of the Lord, to always see the world with shastra chakshara."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the dangers of modern civilization's illusion of control, highlighting our eventual helplessness and the importance of humility, accepting the Lord's supremacy, and sharpening spiritual perception through daily Bhagavata seva.

So it's a danger in one sense, when we see, modern civilization in one sense is dangerous because, in one sense it appears that we are in control of things. From time to time, just like you see now, what happened in Chennai, although we may have, you know, city planners, and lot of people who would have put their mind to design the city, and various kinds of planning would have gone, but then, times come when we realize that we are helpless. Adi-bhoti kleshas, Adi-devi kleshas, Krishna once in a while gives us a sample that, you know, don't be puffed up. And the final lesson, anyway, everyone gets it. We are helpless. We are helpless, lying on the death bed and, no matter what we are, how great we are, how intelligent we are, how accomplished we are, what degrees we have, helplessly we leave this world. So it's better to be humble, to realize that we don't know, to always accept the supremacy of the Lord, to always see the world with shastra chakshara. Even as devotees, it's very important, that we, our perception is constantly sharpened, spiritual perception is constantly sharpened by daily, nityam bhagavata sevaya. Just like it happens, maya pritha jnana, our real knowledge will be stolen away by maya. And it happens very slowly. We'll not even realize.

---
#### Srila Prabhupada, pure message, compassion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9WCUpqF7GPY?t=1560
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is my duty to speak the truth."
**Summary:** The speaker shares a story about Srila Prabhupada's directness and unwavering commitment to delivering the pure message, even when it meant upsetting others, emphasizing his compassion and duty as an acharya.

One time, Shri Prabhupada, Prabhupada, as we all know, was very heavy on scientists, on the mayavadhis, on the impersonalists, because Prabhupada was an acharya who wanted to give the pure message to the world, irrespective of whether people like it or not. Scientists, pafta people, or so many different kinds of people having different theories, Prabhupada was not in a mood to please everyone. Sometimes when we speak, you know, you know, to please the audience is also there in the back of our mind. So Prabhupada was not in a mood of, you know, having a big number of followers. He was in the mood of representing the parampara and delivering the pure message, whether you like it or you don't like it. One time it so happened that Prabhupada was in Calcutta and there is one, just like these days we have God-men, there was a lady, Yoga Shakthi Ma, who came, who wanted to meet Prabhupada and invite him for, a conference on, World Conference for Peace and Yoga. So initially Prabhupada was reluctant to meet this lady, but then, seeing that she was desperate to meet him and invite him for the program, Prabhupada agreed to meet her. So she came in, Prabhupada was in Calcutta and that lady came with three male followers and typically, saffron robed, the hairs are all, you know, spread out. So she came, met Prabhupada and said that, I am organizing this World Conference for Peace and Harmony and I am inviting all various spiritual leaders. So I want you also to come and speak in this conference. So by then, Prabhupada was very popular, this conference was relatively very big, lot of foreign devotees coming in, Mayapur project had started. So it was a big organization by then. And she also handed over a pamphlet, which had the address mentioned of their center. It was somewhere in Calcutta, an apartment. And, the pamphlet said, World Peace and Happiness through Karma Yoga, Jnana Yoga, Ashtanga Yoga, Bhakti Yoga, all the different yogas were mentioned. And that's why she was calling different spiritual heads, representing different yogas. Each one of them trying to give their opinion and share their experiences, how different yogas can bring about world peace and harmony. So Prabhupada met them, and...

---
#### pure devotion, illusion, duty of an acharya
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9WCUpqF7GPY?t=1776
**Verse References:** BG 8.15
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is my duty to break the illusion."
**Summary:** This segment continues the story of Prabhupada meeting Yoga Shakti Ma, highlighting his direct questioning about God, his analogy of spraying perfume in a toilet, and his forceful assertion that this world is a place of suffering, emphasizing the duty of an acharya to break illusion.

Immediately told them, you're talking about world peace through God consciousness, through yoga, through this thing and all that. By the way, do you know who is God? Sometime Prabhupada was very direct. So when he asked, do you know who is God? So they all fumbled. It was like, the answer was not apparent. So one of them said, Hari Om. So Prabhupada says, do you know who is God? And seeing that they're all fumbling, they have no clear idea about God, and they're talking about yoga society. Prabhupada says, yogi naam, it's very clear, yoga means to connect with God, link with God, you don't know who is God. What is the point in talking about yoga? So then, Prabhupada said, if you go to a toilet, and you spray perfume there, scent there, doesn't make toilet a wonderful place for you to stay there. Toilet is a place you go, relieve yourself and come out. And he started telling, forcefully, that Krishna in the Bhagavad Gita says, dukha alayam ashashvatam. This is not a place for peace and happiness. It's dukha alayam ashashvatam. It's a place, Krishna says, become my devotee, come back to me. By spraying perfume in the toilet, the toilet doesn't become a place where you can go and settle down peacefully and happily. So when he said that, one of them, apparently, it was very evident, became a little unsettled and unhappy with what Prabhupada was saying. It was like Prabhupada was chastising them. All their efforts, what they were trying to put, bring in happiness, harmony, peace in the world. So, one of them got, so to say, rattled and got up and left that place. Slowly, even the Yoga Shaktima and all her followers also got up and this thing. So when they all got up, Prabhupada's disciples, they were a little wonderstruck. Somebody came to meet Prabhupada, Prabhupada spoke so forcefully and they got upset, so to say. Prabhupada says, I know they have got upset, but it is my duty to speak the truth. It is my duty to break the illusion. They are in illusion that this world can be made a peaceful place. Whereas Krishna has clearly said it's dukhalayam. I want to break their so-called illusion. This is the duty of an Acharya who breaks the illusion. We all are in illusion. We all are in Maya. Maya Prithagyana. Breaks the hard knot of ignorance. And Prabhupada was unmindful of the fact that they got disturbed. He says, my duty is to speak the truth and I have done that. I don't care whether they like it or they don't like it.

---
#### compassion, truth, devotion, reality
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9WCUpqF7GPY?t=1974
**Verse References:** SB 1.2.6
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Only when we are devotees of the Lord, we are able to appreciate the glories of the Lord."
**Summary:** This segment concludes by emphasizing Prabhupada's compassion and his willingness to upset people to break their illusions, highlighting the importance of devotion in seeing the truth and appreciating the Lord's glories.

So sometimes Prabhupada was forceful, although as an Acharya he was also very kind, very gentle, very loving for his disciples, for his devotees, or for that matter, he was very compassionate. It doesn't mean that he was reckless. He was not at all considerate to others' feelings. The very fact that he was compassionate, he took all the pains to go to America. Had he been reckless, not caring for people, then he would have as well settled peacefully in Vrindavan. Sometimes we just hear this in isolation. It may appear that Prabhupada was very, so to say, proud of his knowledge and was reckless. He would, you know, upset people and things like that. No. Wherever he saw that somebody is, you know, deep in illusion, he would cut that knot of illusion, even at the cost of upsetting them. That's why Prabhupada in many, many places uses fools and rascals. Sometimes harsh words are required. Sometimes doctors even use knife. The boil is there, it may give an injection. Apparently it looks painful, but that is required for the patient to get cured. Sometimes we all behave and speak goody-goody, not to upset anyone, not to, so to say, tell something which will hurt a person's this thing. So Prabhupada as an Acharya, his duty was to speak out the truth on behalf of the Lord, and he did that. So here also we are seeing that Brahma, Prabhupada says, devotees like Brahma who are, they are able to see the truth. Therefore when they saw the Lord came and lifted the Mother Earth and because of his prowess, the Mother Earth could start floating on the water, he appreciated. He showered flower on him, appreciation of his transcendental activities. Only when we are devotees of the Lord, we are able to appreciate the glories of the Lord. We are able to see the truth, we are able to see the reality. Otherwise, illusion, maya, overpowers our understanding. With these few words, we'll stop here. Grantharaj Srimad Bhagavatam Ki. Srila Prabhupada Ki.

#### Srimad Bhagavatam, Hiranyaksha, Lord Varaha
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/t-Oa1FsWlxg?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A demon may be a great soldier and a commander of a large number of infantry, but in presence of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, he is powerless and is destined to die."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the chapter and verse from Srimad Bhagavatam, focusing on Lord Varaha's challenge to Hiranyaksha, emphasizing the demon's powerlessness before the Supreme Personality of Godhead.

Om Namo Bhagwate Vasudevaya. Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto No. 3, Chapter No. 18, Text No. 12. Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto No. 13, Chapter No. 14. Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto No. 13. You are supposed to be the commander of many foot soldiers and now you may take prompt steps to overthrow us. Give up all your foolish talk and wipe out the cares of your kith and kin by slaying us. One may be proud, yet he does not deserve a seat in an assembly if he fails to fulfill his promised word. A demon may be a great soldier and a commander of a large number of infantry, but in presence of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, he is powerless and is destined to die. The Lord therefore challenged the demon not to go away, but to fulfill his promised word to kill him. Thus ends the Bhagavad Gita.

#### Hiranyaksha, anger, foolish talk
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/t-Oa1FsWlxg?t=108
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Give up all your foolish talk and wipe out the cares of your kith and kin by slaying us."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the context of the verse, where Lord Varaha challenges Hiranyaksha's blasphemies and foolish talk, urging him to act on his threats and alleviate the concerns of his relatives by fighting.

So this chapter, the title is The Battle between Lord Bohr and Hiranyaksha. So in this particular verse, Lord is speaking to Hiranyaksha and saying that you are supposed to be commander of many foot soldiers and you may now take prompt steps to overthrow us. And in the previous verses we know that how Hiranyaksha challenges the Lord and blasphemes him. The Lord tolerated his piercing ill words, but in order to reply him, he expressed his terrible anger. So he used many ill words against the Lord, he challenged the supremacy of the Lord. And now the Lord is mentioning, give up all your foolish talk – there's no point in just talking. Big, big talks. And wipe out the cares of your kith and kin. Cares of kith and kin, near and dear ones, the relatives, because the battle is about to ensue. So they are concerned what's going to happen. Just like Dhritarashtra was concerned what will happen to his kith and kin. Wipe out the cares of your kith and kin by slaying us. How will the cares get wiped out? By slaying us. How Dhritarashtra also when he was anxious, he was anxious what will happen to his sons. And his anxiety will get allayed or rather removed if his sons win the battle. So likewise your kith and kin, the cares can be wiped out if you slay us. One may be proud yet he does not deserve.

#### humility, Krishna consciousness, false ego
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/t-Oa1FsWlxg?t=230
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When we become Krishna conscious or come to our constitutional position, there is no question of being proud."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the constitutional position of living entities as amsa of the Lord, emphasizing that true understanding leads to humility and service to Krishna, while false ego, or Maya, causes one to challenge the Lord.

He does not deserve a seat in an assembly if he fails to fulfill his promised words. Our constitutional position is that we are amsa of the Lord. Being an amsa of the Lord, whatever we have in our possession, whatever we have in our possession, whatever we have in our possession, our gross body, subtle body, including the atma, we, belongs to the Lord. The gross body is made of panchabhuta, which is again energy of the Lord, the subtle body, and even the atma, mamaivamso, Krishna says. So in that sense when we become Krishna conscious or come to our constitutional position, there is no question of being proud. When we have a notion of a God who sicasting within our consciousness, God is a pursuit of knowledge and knowledge. And on the other hand one will recognize or realize that everything including him belongs to the Lord. And a natural outcome of that understanding would be since the Lord is the proprietor of my body, my abilities, my skills, whatever I have belongs to the Lord, hence it has to be used in the service of the Lord. Now, this understanding gets covered by Maya, the illusory energy of the Lord. When the living entity desires to be the Lord, to challenge the Lord, to rebel against the Lord, this understanding what we have that I belong to the Lord, all my abilities are coming from the Lord, gets covered, and to the extent that we even challenge the Lord, that's what is happening in the case of Renaksha, he is challenging the Lord, forgotten that whatever he has, so the Lord is saying, don't talk unnecessarily, give up your foolish talk. Now the Lord is completely aware of the actual situation, he knows out of false ego he is puffed up.

**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the concept of ahamkara (false ego), explaining that it obscures the understanding that everything belongs to the Lord. He emphasizes that spiritual life is about annihilating false ego and reviving the real ego of being a servant of Krishna.

The Lord is all-knowing. So he is saying, give up your foolish talk and wipe out cares of this thing, you want to fight, you think that you are powerful, you can kill me, go ahead, do it. And he says, one may be proud, because of this ahamkara. I heard in one place, there was a man, he was a man, he was a man, he was a man, he was born in our place, but he has everything in life, he belongs to His head. He doesn't have anything left in this life that, what has become eternal now, he is full of past. You can name here 준비, पंचरबूता is a secret. अःकारक् everybody is so complex, thirteen percent of that is a secret. Things that are protect. अःकारक् It's like tube-desection for meat. that this is mine. Ahamkara does not come from the Lord. Manas buddhi ahamkara. That false ego does not come from the Lord. Manas buddhi also is coming from the Lord. So that's why we are here in this material world because we have that ahamkara. Aham ameti. And the whole spiritual life, all spiritual processes are how to annihilate, wipe out that ahamkara, purify that ahamkara and revive our real ahamkara, real ego. That is I am servant of Krishna, I am amsa of Krishna and I should serve Krishna. And when that real ego gets awakened then we are qualified, we are eligible to go back to the spiritual world and blissfully engage in the service of the Lord.

#### Arjuna, battle of Kurukshetra, protection of women
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/t-Oa1FsWlxg?t=485

**Summary:** The speaker transitions to the story of Arjuna's victory in the battle of Kurukshetra, highlighting his capabilities and the eventual reinstatement of the Pandavas. He then describes Yudhishthira's instruction to Arjuna to bring the queens from Dwaraka to Hastinapura for protection.

Arjuna, as we all know, he fought valiantly in the battle of Kurukshetra. Although the opponents were Karna, Bhishma, Dronacharya, great stalwarts, maharathis, yet Arjuna won that battle. And he was blessed with different arms by different devatas. He was very powerful. He was you know the pet sort of student of Dronacharya. He was very capable. So he won the battle. Although opponents were great, he was very capable. He was very capable. He was very quick. He was very alert, he was very warriors and soldiers and you know so many maharathis, still he won it. Won the battle. And when the battle is over they are reinstated back to the kingdom. Hastinapura, the Pandavas becomes the king. And then we know that how eventually Krishna also winds up his past times and leaves Delhi. Finally, Yudhishthira Maharaj tells Arjuna that you go to Dwaraka now that the Krishna has left, the queens will be all alone, unprotected. You can bring them to Hastinapura so that, you know always women, Prabhupada also says in the Vedic culture, should always be under the protection of men. So Arjuna was sent to Dwaraka to bring back the queens so that they can be in Hastinapura and since the Pandavas are ruling, they can be under their protection.

#### Arjuna, defeat, Maya
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/t-Oa1FsWlxg?t=612
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Arjuna could not protect the queens. He got defeated."
**Summary:** The speaker narrates the incident where Arjuna, while escorting the queens, is attacked by cowards and, surprisingly, fails to protect them. This leads to Arjuna's realization about the influence of Maya and the importance of Krishna's presence.

So Arjuna goes to Dwaraka to bring the kings, the queens and on his way back, he was coming, you know, the chariots were there and the queens were there and many, many queens. So whole big caravan kind of thing. And on the way, he was attacked by some cowards men, Gwalas. And they were not maharatis, they were having, wielding, they were not having some weapons, they were having some sticks in their hands. And weaklings, skinny, you know, seeing them compared to the soldiers in the battlefield who were armed, who were trained. You know. Akshounis, so to say, of soldiers with elephants, with all kinds of horses, well-trained, with all kinds of arms and ammunitions. Compared to that, they were just, you know, ignoble cowards men, weaklings with sticks in their hands. And Arjuna tried to fight against them fiercely so that he can protect the queens because they were attacked. And Arjuna could not protect the queens. He got defeated. Then Arjuna, this is in the Bhagavatam, he makes this wonderful statement. How a devotee can also sometime may get puffed up, may get puffed up, may forget his constitutional position. Because maya is very powerful. But then very soon, the devotee realizes, just like Prabhupada gives an example, marginal energy. The shore of an ocean, sometimes it is under water, sometimes it is, when the waves recede, then it is, there is no water. So likewise, we also get covered by maya, our consciousness. And then we associate with devotees. We associate with devotees. We hear Bhagavatam. We read Prabhupada books. Again, the maya recedes, the covering, so to say, recedes.

#### Krishna's presence, Arjuna's realization, powerlessness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/t-Oa1FsWlxg?t=751
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "But there is one difference. In the battle of Kurukshetra. Krishna was there with Arjuna. And here in this particular situation. Krishna is not there."
**Summary:** The speaker analyzes Arjuna's statement, highlighting the similarities between the Kurukshetra battlefield and the attack by cowards, but emphasizing the crucial difference: the absence of Krishna. This absence rendered Arjuna powerless despite having the same weapons and abilities.

So Arjuna says, tad vai dhanus. Now you see, Arjuna is comparing, there are many, many similarities between what was the situation in the battle of Kurukshetra and what was the situation when these cowards men attacked him. There are many, many similarities. Tad vai dhanus. The similarities, the dhanush is the same. Dhanush means bow and arrow. And it is not an ordinary dhanush, gifted by some devata to Arjuna. Tad vai dhanush. The dhanush is same. The same dhanush which Arjuna used to kill maharatis, big, big soldiers and, you know, Bhishma, Drona, all these kind of people. Same dhanush. Sa isha waha. The horses were not ordinary horses. The same horses what were there in the battle of Kurukshetra were there with Arjuna now. Sa isha waha. Sa ratho. The same chariot driven by the same horses. Sa ratho. The ratha is also the same. The horses driving the chariot is also the same. Sa hayaste. And same Arjuna, who is holding the bow. Arjuna is also same. Bow is the same. Chariot is the same. Horses are the same. Arjuna is same. Sa hayaste. Soham rathi. The one who is rathi is referring to himself. Nripatayo yata aanamanti. And then Arjuna says sarvakshanena. In a moment. Tad abhud asad eeshariktham. But there is one difference. That same Arjuna who could kill big big kings. Nirpa. Nirpa means kings. Could destroy them. Could kill them. Could vanquish them. Could defeat them. Same Arjuna driven by the same chariot. Same horses. Same Nandiva bow. Eeshariktham. But there is one difference. In the battle of Kurukshetra. Krishna was there with Arjuna. And here in this particular situation. Eeshariktham. Krishna is not there.

#### Krishna's absence, devotee's humility, dependence on Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/t-Oa1FsWlxg?t=910
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Minus Krishna is like pouring ghee on the ashes. There is no use."
**Summary:** The speaker uses analogies to illustrate Arjuna's realization that without Krishna, all his powers and abilities are useless. He then emphasizes that true humility is a natural outcome of Krishna consciousness, where one recognizes that everything comes from and belongs to Krishna.

So then Arjuna says. Because of absence of Krishna. Rest everything is same. Because of absence of Krishna. Basman hutam. Just like. Just like. On ashes you pour ghee. It does not inflame. Fire doesn't come out. No use. So same Arjuna. Same Nandiva bow. Same chariot. Driven by same horses. Minus Krishna is like pouring ghee on the ashes. There is no use. There is no fire coming out. Basman hutam kuh karadham. Just like seed you put in a barren land. No use. Seed may be very good. Barren land. It doesn't sprout. Evoktam usyam. Just like. Magic wand. You know you can. Use the magic wand. To show a magic. That money comes out. But no use. Magic wand doesn't bring out money. It's just a magic. Temporally it appears to be like that. No money. No actual money comes out. So likewise. Arjuna is saying. In the absence of Krishna. All my powers. All my fame. All my abilities. Because of which I could this thing. In a moment's notice. Shanena. All became null and void. So as devotees also we should always remember. Krishna consciousness means that. Sometimes we try to be humble. We sometimes are told. Don't be proud. Sometimes we are told, don't get puffed up. Being puffed up is a natural outcome when our pure consciousness is covered by Maya. It's a by-product, or rather it's an outcome of... In other words, somebody who is not Krishna conscious cannot be genuinely humble. The reverse, if somebody is Krishna conscious, he doesn't have to artificially impose, don't know why I should be humble. A natural outcome would be humility. Whatever I have, whatever strength I have, whatever abilities I have, whatever intelligence I have, whatever speaking power I have, whatever singing power I have, it's all coming from Krishna. It belongs to Krishna.

#### Arjuna, Krishna, humility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/t-Oa1FsWlxg?t=1050
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "He realizes, it was Krishna who was there, because of Him I won. Now that Krishna is not there, it's all useless."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts Arjuna's humility with Hiranyaksha's pride, emphasizing that Arjuna, being a devotee, realized his dependence on Krishna for his victories. He then introduces a story about a young man admitted to the Louis Pasteur Institute to illustrate the dangers of pride.

I am just a Kshetragna, a knower of the field of activities. All credit goes to the Lord. It belongs to Him. Just like Arjuna, because he is a devotee of the Lord, the moment he got defeated, He is not puffed up that, no, because I am great, therefore I could defeat so many great people in the battle of Kurukshetra. He realizes, it was Krishna who was there, because of Him I won. Now that Krishna is not there, it's all useless. All that power, skills, abilities, Gandiva bow, chariot, horses, everything null and void. So here we see Arjuna, and in contrast we see Krishna. Both are amshas of the Lord. One is puffed up, challenging the Lord, wanting to fight against Him. And Arjuna is, because he is Krishna conscious, because he is a devotee of the Lord, realizes his position and he is saying, Without you, no value. One time there was a young man, he was . . . he got an admission in a very prestigious university. The Liu Pasteur Institute. So this is in Paris. I am sure all of you would have heard about pasteurization, pasteurization of milk. So this person is credited with, so to say, the invention of this pasteurization process. So this person got an admission. It is like, you know, very prestigious like Harvard, MIT and all those things. So he got an admission in there and he was so excited. Some lakhs and lakhs of students apply for it and some selected few get admission. So he was one among them. And he was doing Ph.D. He got admission for doing post-doctoral.

#### pride, humility, Louis Pasteur
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/t-Oa1FsWlxg?t=1173
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Wasting time. And here I am, young man, full of energy, full of life. Full of achievement."
**Summary:** The speaker continues the story of the young man in Paris, highlighting his pride and condescending attitude towards an old man chanting on rosary beads. The story culminates in the revelation that the old man was Louis Pasteur himself.

So he was very happy, very excited. And then he reached Paris. And in Paris, like here we have metro. There also they have, like a metro, local trains within the city. So he boarded the train and he was few stations ahead of him. He had to get down and then from there take a cab to the institute. And since he was young, you know, very ambitious. He had a goal to, you know, make it and he has finally done it. Naturally, you know, some element of confidence was exuding him, bordering towards pride. He was sitting in the train. The train started moving and then suddenly he sees an old man next to him in the seat. Just with eyes, you know, half closed. Chanting on the rosary. And softly murmuring. You know, he was a Christian. And Christians also use the rosary beads and, you know, hail Mary. So they was, he was very softly chanting. So this young man looks at him, this old man. And naturally a feeling of thing comes, useless fellow. Wasting time. Like many times people may feel when devotees, they see, you know, chanting and all those things. Wasting time. And here I am, young man, full of energy, full of life. Full of achievement. Now I am going to do Ph.D. and then after that I am going to pass out. I am going to do research and make a difference in the world. And see this poor fellow, wasting time, simply. So he, anyway. So he asked him, what are you up to? You know, very sarcastically, what are you up to? She said, I am chanting hail Mary. So the conversation ensued. The old man also became inquisitive. Where are you heading? Where are you going? So he said, I have got admission in Louis Pasteur Institute. I am going there. So the old man, some more conversation happened and said, God bless you. Wish you all the very best. The train stopped. The old man gave his card and the old man got down the station. And this young man again continued and in his mind it was their useless fellows. And as the train started, he took the card from his pocket and saw the card. And of the card, it was written, Louis Pasteur.
