#### Entanglement, Sense Gratification, Disqualification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CxvZZv_G-DE?t=1235
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Another nature of this material enjoyment or sensual enjoyment is it disqualifies the living entity to enjoy real happiness. You cannot have both."
**Summary:** The speaker explains how entanglement in sense gratification disqualifies one from experiencing real happiness, referencing the bewilderment of intelligence due to bhoga and aishwarya.

If you ask anybody, many times when we conduct programs, especially the congregation, they are really entangled. A lot of people, they want to come. Children have exams, this is there, somebody is sick, somebody is hospitalized, some have to go out for something, parent-teacher meet is there. A lot of people when we analyze as to why they are not able to come. What does this mean? To come, they're completely entangled. Atha ghraya shetra suta aptavite janase mohoyam. You can see this Bhagavatam verse applicable. It's like cocoon caught in a silkworm caught in a cocoon. So bahudukha bhaja. This very happiness becomes a cause of entanglement, becomes a cause of misery, becomes a cause of all kinds of misfortune. Another nature of this material enjoyment or sensual enjoyment is it disqualifies the living entity to enjoy real happiness. You cannot have both. bhogaishwarya prasaktanam tayaprita cheta samit. The intelligence is too bewildered by bhoga and aishwarya. prasaktanam tayaprita cheta samit. If the intelligence is bewildered, that steady intelligence, that sobriety, samadhauna vidhya, they will not come about. So in other words, you cannot have the best of both worlds.

#### Desire, Soul, Service to the Lord
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CxvZZv_G-DE?t=1975
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The natural inclination for the soul, the natural desire, the natural quality of the soul is mamaivamsa, is amsa of the lord, is to serve the lord."
**Summary:** The speaker refutes the idea of becoming desireless, stating that desire is a natural quality of the soul, whose true inclination is to serve the Lord, leading to purification and engagement in devotional service.

Become desireless so Prabhupada explains one cannot be desireless because the natural quality of soul is symptom of life is desire a dead body is not having any desire it isn't desire no no no, sandalwood will be better for my soul it's just lying there put kerosene or you put petrol adulterated petrol or clean petrol, it just accepts no desire but a living being has desires so we cannot kill desires we cannot become shunya here Bhagavatam is saying the natural inclination for the soul the natural desire, the natural quality of the soul is mamaivamsa, is amsa of the lord is to serve the lord that's why it is said tapo divyam shudhyet when our desire becomes purified, when our senses becomes purified naturally we will engage in the service of the lord so when the spirit soul is engaged in devotional service to the personality of Godhead, without any motive that is far better than salvation.

So liberation means or some kind of thing where you contol you try to you know you have some some kind of yoga practices where you even control your breath control your mind control your senses sit in a rigid posture one can get some kind of a mukti salvation but even higher than that is when we engaged in positive devotional service in a number line you have minus three minus two minus one zero is higher than negative numbers but zero is not the best you have positive numbers so devotional service different kinds of liberation is like zero trying to be desireless is trying to achieve the state of zero which is definitely better than minus numbers but much better than that is positive numbers so what Prabhupada is giving us what Bhagavatam is trying to give us is positive engagement in Krishna consciousness positive engagement for the mind and the senses.

Right now the senses and mind are engaged in negative things positive engagement when the positive engagement happens that's why Prabhupada says always be engaged we should not be free even for a moment because moment the senses and mind get an opportunity they'll engage in sense gratification that's our position right now it's so habituated to sense gratification moment you get an opportunity to talk we'll talk rubbish so always engaged in the service of the Lord when we are engaged in the service of the Lord there is no opportunity for negative engagement that is the process what we are following and if we back this understanding with proper hearing proper context and engage in positive devotional service definitely we'll get purified and we will achieve that happiness brahma saukhyam tuvanam real happiness which is unending and which will completely satisfy the soul.

## HG Amitasana Dasa

### 2017-03-16 | SB 3.25.35 | HG Amitasana Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### omnipresence of the Lord, archa-vigraha, deity worship
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/VHR4_1182G4?t=300
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The deity, although we know that it is made from stone, he is not stone."
**Summary:** The Lord mercifully descends in archa-vigraha forms, which appear to be made of material elements, but are non-different from Him due to His omnipotency, allowing devotees to serve Him and attain the same benefit as serving Him personally in Vaikuntha.

So, the post boxes are installed in different localities to facilitate that people can easily post letters. They don't have to come all the way, travel long distances to and come and put the letter in the post box. It's a very crude example. Likewise, the Lord, understanding that it is difficult for us to approach Him. He is all spiritual. Prabhupada gives an example that when a man dies, we cannot see how the spiritual form leaves the body. What we see generally and what we interact with is the body. We are very gross in our vision and we don't see spirit, soul. But we know soul is a reality. And the Supreme Soul, Supreme Lord, He is all spiritual. He has transcendental body, all spiritual body. And with our blunt material senses, with our eyes, we cannot perceive the Lord. We cannot see the Lord. We know that the Lord is present everywhere. He is omnipresent. But still, there are many people who believe that God does not exist. Where is God? Even people like Rinnekar Shripur, very powerful people, they challenged openly, where is God? The Lord has created this world that if someone wants to live life with the understanding that God does not exist, there are enough reasons for him to be convinced of that fact. That's a different thing that Maya has to take away his intelligence. Maya pritha jnana. Otherwise, little bit we are introspective, we can easily make out that, yes, behind this wonderful creation there has to be an intelligent creator. Nothing wonderful comes about from nowhere. So as devotees, we know the Lord is there. He is present everywhere. But unfortunately, we have material vision. We don't have spiritual senses awakened. And hence, we cannot perceive the Lord in our present, with our present material senses. So just like the crude example of a postal department installing post boxes, there is the Lord, mercifully descends in archa-vigraha forms, which are made of material elements, apparently, looks like made of stone, wood, metal. But because of omnipotency of the Lord, they are all spiritual. They are non-different from the Lord. Prabhupada mentions in one place. The deity, although we know that it is made from stone, he is not stone. By offering food to the deity and by decorating and serving the deity, one gets the same result that one derives from serving the Lord personally in Vaikuntha. Just like posting the letter in the general post office, and posting the letter in the local post box, there is no difference. There may be some minor delay. But there is no difference. So it is the magnanimity of the Lord that the Lord descends in the form of archa-vigraha. And here it is mentioned, My devotees always see the smiling face of my form, like the rising, morning rising sun. So this is the benediction.

#### deity worship, holy name, mercy of the Lord
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/VHR4_1182G4?t=613
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Anywhere we can invoke the presence of the Lord just by uttering the holy names."
**Summary:** Serving the deity is equivalent to serving Krishna in the spiritual world, and in Kali Yuga, the Lord is even more magnanimous, descending in the sound form of the holy name, allowing devotees to invoke His presence anywhere.

If we make a garland and offer it to the Lord, it is the same as someone offering garland to Krishna in the spiritual world in Vaikuntha, Goloka Vrindavan. If someone looks at the lotus feet of the Lord, of the deity, and meditates on it, it is as good as meditating on the lotus feet of the Supreme Lord in Vaikuntha. If we remember the deity, it is as good as being in the personal presence of the Lord. Just like Arjuna was very fortunate in the battle of Kurukshetra, he had Krishna with him. Likewise, through various transcendental forms like the deity, like the holy name, if a devotee wants, ye yatha mam prapadyante tam satayiva bhajam yam If a devotee wants, if he is determined that I want Lord always with me, I want the company of the Lord, I want association of the Lord, I want to be conscious of the Lord, the various transcendental forms which a devotee can take help of. In fact, in Kali Yuga, the Lord is all the more magnanimous. Deities means still you have to go to the temple. We are very fortunate we are staying in the ashram, in the temple premises in that sense. But, let's say a devotee who is practicing Krishna consciousness outside, he has to come all the way to come have darshan of the deities. But in Kali Yuga, the Lord is even more magnanimous. He has descended in a sound form. Naam roopa. Shabd roopa. Kali kale naam roop avatar. Anywhere we can invoke the presence of the Lord just by uttering the holy names. So here it is said, they like to see my various transcendental forms which are all benevolent. Very benevolent. Benevolent means very very merciful, very very kind. It is because of mercy of the Lord we have these forms available to us.

#### deity worship, prayer, stone-heartedness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/VHR4_1182G4?t=744
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The deity, although we know it's made of stone, he is not stone. He is stone to the stone-hearted."
**Summary:** Devotees can take help of prasadam, the holy name, and the deity when feeling spiritually down, revealing their hearts and offering prayers to the deities, who reciprocate with devotees, unlike atheists who cannot understand these exchanges.

Prasadam, the holy name, the deity. Sometimes if we feel that we are spiritually going down, we should take help of these forms. Prabhupada in one place, if you are very disturbed, sit tightly in one place and chant the holy names till you feel clear consciousness. Or, one is disturbed, one can go and reveal his mind to the deities. The deities, here it says, the exchanges of feelings between the deity and the devotees are not understandable by atheist. We can offer prayers. We can reveal our heart. Sometimes we feel, you know, there is no one I can share my feelings. We can go and offer prayers. Prayers, yes, the prayers what we have, you know, sung by acharyas, but we can also speak to the deities. We can talk just like you talk to somebody else. Devotees express their mind before the deities and in many instances the deities also give answers. So we should also, Prabhupada says, the deity, although we know it's made of stone, he is not stone. He is stone to the stone-hearted. So when we chant, when we read, when we hear, what we are doing is our heart which is stone-hearted right now. We have no devotion. We have no love for Krishna. We have no attraction for Krishna. We have attraction for all this material illusory energy, the variegatedness connected by maya. If one is stone-hearted then Krishna will stand like stone forever. So why do we do this sadhana? Why do we do this chanting and all that? Slowly this heart which is stone-hearted, there is a transformation happening. For a stone-hearted the deity will be like stone. It will stand like a stone forever. But if one is soft-hearted by chanting Hare Krishna mantra, why we chant? The heart is becoming soft. Then he will talk with Krishna. That is the difference.

---
#### surrender, reciprocity, stone-heartedness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/VHR4_1182G4?t=891
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "As you surrender I shall reciprocate."
**Summary:** Krishna reciprocates based on our surrender, and the speaker illustrates this with a story about a man who becomes a billionaire to discover the source of a beautiful sound, only to find that the key to unlocking it lies in softening his heart.

Krishna is always present. So Krishna says, As you surrender I shall reciprocate. If you don't believe that I am there, if you have no respect for me, no love for me, no attraction for me, ok, I am stone there. No reciprocation. Although deities are very magnanimous, even if somebody with no intention comes and folds his hand, the deities, he gets the benefit, ajna tsukruti. But then if we consciously do with the right understanding, then here it says, the deities sometimes express their mind. Many instances the deities also give answers. So the Lord is present. The Lord's transcendental forms are present. It is left to us how much we take advantage of them. The Holy Name is there. It is left to us how much we chant and purify our consciousness. One time there was a person. He was traveling on a bike. He was going to a distant place. And it was late in the evening. Somehow the bike stopped. Maybe some defect, some problem with the bike. He tried putting it on and moving on, but somehow it didn't move. It didn't move any further. And it was late in the evening, so he started looking for some place where he can get the bike repaired and continue with his journey. And it's already there. It's already late in the night. And somehow it was a very deserted place. It was not like a typical town where you see many shops around. So he got a little scared. How is he going to spend the night? Where is he going to get the bike repaired? Fortunately he saw at a distance there was a nice little house with some lights. So he could understand somebody is staying there. So as he approached the house, he saw it was quite palatial. It was quite, you know, big compared to the place where he was in. So he was hesitant. Should he go and knock the door? Should he, you know, what kind of people will be there? So reluctantly he went to that house and knocked on the door. And, a gentleman opened the door. So he immediately told that, you know, I was passing this way. My bike has, you know, I'm not able to move it. I have to get it repaired. I don't see any repair shop nearby. If you don't mind, can I stay overnight? Morning I will leave. I will get my bike repaired and then move on. So the person said, Sure, sure. It's my good privilege that you have come. Please come. You could see that it looks like a gentleman. The bike was there. So he invited him. And, he helped him with some food and they talked for a while. And then he slept. So morning he woke up and, he saw the owner of the house doing some kind of meditation. So the owner said, why don't you also come do some meditation. I'll teach you this meditation. So he taught him a little bit. After the meditation got over, he heard a very, very beautiful melodious sound. Very, very, he's never heard that kind of a sound. So it was such a pleasant and melodious sound that, you know, he asked that person, what is the source of this sound? Where is it coming from? He said, sorry, I can't tell you. If you are a billionaire, only then, you will be able to understand. I'll be able to share with you the source of this sound. Unfortunately, he was not a billionaire. He was not a very rich man. So he felt dejected, disappointed with the answer. Anyway, he left. Few years later, it so happened that he was passing that way and somehow, you know, because they had very pleasant exchange, he was received very nicely. They developed some kind of friendship. He again knocked on that door. And again, the same person opened the door, invited him in. He says, very nice to see you after many years now. Why don't you stay with me overnight? So he agreed. Again, next day morning, they did that meditation. Just after the meditation got over, he again heard that very pleasant, melodious sound. This time, he became even more curious. He says, last time also, you did not tell me. But please, this time you should tell me what is the source of sound? Where is it coming from? So he says, sorry, I can't tell you. You have to be a billionaire. Otherwise, you can't understand. So this time, this man leaves with a resolve, with a determination. He says, I will become a billionaire. A lot of people want to become rich for various reasons. He said, come what may, I will become a billionaire. And I'll come back to this place. And then, I want to know what is the source of this sound. So, cut the story short. He worked very, very hard and eventually become a billionaire. And first thing, when he became a billionaire, when he accumulated billions of dollars, he came to this house. Directly, in a Rolls Royce. And same person opened and he said, now I am a billionaire. I have come only for one purpose. I want you to show me the source of that sound. So the person, you know, he was really amazed at the determination of this person. Just to know that sound, you know, he has worked so hard, he has become a billionaire, he has come in a very big car. So, he invites him in and he says, behind that door, there was a big wooden door in that house. He says, behind that door there is a source of sound. Go ahead, open the door and you can see for yourself. So this person was, you know, all the years he has worked hard and now is the time when he can see the source of the sound. He eagerly went forward, tried opening the door the door was locked. Big lock on the door. So he was disappointed, what is this? So he looked back and he says, no, here is the key. Open the door. So he opened the door and turned the knob down, pulled the door out and he was eagerly waiting when he can see the source of sound. And as soon as the door opened, he saw the source of sound. And he guessed what he saw. He saw another door made of stone. So he was disappointed. He looked back and he says, what is this? He says, no, the source of sound is behind this door. So he took the key, opened the door and put the knob, pulled it out and he was waiting. As soon as the door opens, he sees the source of sound. And he says, no, the source of sound is behind this door. So he took the key, opened the door and he sees the source of sound. He sees another door. This time the door is made of precious stones. So on one side he is excited that very soon he will get to know. The other side is disappointed that unnecessarily many many doors are coming in the way. Many obstacles are coming in the way. So finally this person says, you don't have to wait any longer. This is the last door. This is the key. Please open it. And you are very fortunate that you will have the source of sound right in front of your eyes. So he opens the door and pulls it out and there in his front he sees the source of sound making that beautiful melodious sound. Can anyone tell me what is the source of sound? Sorry, I can't tell you because you have to be a billionaire to know the source of sound.

#### stone-heartedness, qualification, sadhana
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/VHR4_1182G4?t=1442
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "For a stone hearted the deity is like a stone."
**Summary:** Unless one's heart is softened through regular chanting, reading, and hearing, they lack the qualification to see the Lord, and deity worship becomes a burden.

So here Prabhupada says, for a stone hearted the deity is like a stone. Unless we have the required qualification. Premanjana Chaturita unless we have anointed our eyes with love of God unless by regular chanting, reading, hearing our heart has become soft we will have no qualification to see the Lord. So that's why Prabhupada time and again insists the sadhana, the chanting. This is like bhaktilata bija it's like the bija which has been put in. Brahmanda brahmite kono bhagyavan jiv guru krishna prasade paaye bhaktilata bija So we got the seed of devotion it is left to us sometimes if the gardener is not very attentive the seed may just sprout and it may just wither away. The plant may just dry up. So as devotees if we have to be blissful in Krishna consciousness if we have to enjoy the company of the Lord if we have to relish the association of the Lord if we have to experience the reciprocation of the Lord if we go in front of the deities there is a joy in our heart we have the right we are able to express our feelings for that we will have to diligently follow the process. Prabhupada says if we have not heard sufficiently if there is no proper shravanam deity worship will become galagraha galagraha means a pain in the neck that's why

#### shravanam, holy name, faith
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/VHR4_1182G4?t=1610
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Likewise, for an inexperienced person, the name of the Lord is an ordinary sound vibration."
**Summary:** Proper hearing (shravanam) and chanting are essential for developing the right qualification to appreciate the Lord's presence in the holy name, just as a child needs to be taught the value of a currency note.

No unclean things, lamp, nobody has cleaned it, nobody has washed, oil is pouring out. So when we chant, when we hear proper shravanam, kirtanam, all these things, then we develop the right qualification. Even the holy name of the Lord, many people say, what's the use? It's an ordinary sound. How can it be the Lord? One time there was a small little girl, she was disturbing her mother when the mother was cooking. And while the cooking was on, she would be all, you know, creating ruckus sometimes, crying, disturbing the mother. So mother called the girl and said, do one thing, go around the house, if you see any paper lying on the floor, please help me clean up, it's time for father to return. Please help me clean up the house. So the girl also felt involved, the mother had given something responsible to do. So she started moving around the house and picking up the pieces of paper which were lying on the floor and throwing in the dustbin. So all along, mother was keeping an eye on the girl. And she was happy that, you know, she was doing something meaningful. And as soon as the mother was actively preparing the list, As soon as she saw one time, she was about to pick up a piece of paper, tear it, and mother says, stop, stop, stop. So the girl became a little surprised, what is this? So many pieces of paper I have thrown. She was very happy, she was encouraging me, and now this piece of paper, she's saying, stop, stop, stop. So the girl couldn't understand, so mother said, this is not an ordinary piece of paper. This is a 500 rupee note. Maybe the father in the morning, when he would have gone to the office, he would have slipped out of his wallet. He says, wait, wait, wait. So the girl could not understand. After all, it's a piece of paper. So then mother had to explain to the girl how it's not an ordinary piece of paper. So she said, because sometimes you have to speak to a person in their own language. Beta, this piece of paper, how to explain what is 500 rupee note? With this piece of paper, on your birthday, you can get 500 chocolates. Oh, is it? The girl is inexperienced. For her, all pieces of paper, including currency notes, are the same. Likewise, for an inexperienced person, the name of the Lord is an ordinary sound vibration.

---
#### holy name, omnipotency, consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/VHR4_1182G4?t=1779
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When we chant, we can chant mechanically. We can also chant with the consciousness that the Lord has descended on my tongue."
**Summary:** The Lord's omnipotency allows Him to descend in the form of His holy name, and chanting with the consciousness of His presence on the tongue is an opportunity for devotees to associate with Him and transcend material existence.

How is it different? Like any other word we utter, it's basically some vocal cords interacting and some sound being produced. Just like in a mundane example, we have pieces of paper which are worthless. You can put in the dustbin, you can trash it, and you also have pieces of paper which are worth. Now there's a 2000 rupee note. With that 2000 rupee note, you can buy reams and reams of paper. So it's a paper only. Likewise, by the Lord's omnipotency, just like here it is said, my benevolent forms, the Lord has descended in the form of his holy name. For the faithless, for the person whose heart is stone-hearted, it's an ordinary sound. The deity is made of stone, is made of wood. For a devotee whose consciousness is awakened because of shravanam, kirtanam, by chanting, by reading and all that, that sound vibration is none other than Krishna himself. So MPB also many times in his seminars, in his workshop on japa, he mentions that when we chant, we can chant mechanically. We can also chant with the consciousness that the Lord has descended on my tongue. He is there. Just like when a person who is very, very prominent, a VIP comes, assuming that we have a president's visit or prime minister visit to the temple, we'll all become so attentive to receive him. So many arrangements will be made. Depending on the stature of the person, we will make arrangements. Now if we don't have proper shravanam, when the Lord descends in the form of holy name, we will be very casual, very reckless, very inattentive. When we have that consciousness, that understanding that the Lord has descended, it is Lord's magnanimity that the Lord has come on my tongue. And it's an opportunity for us. His intention is so that we can associate with him, we can touch with him, touch him and come out of this material existence. Same crude example, the post office installing post boxes. The Lord agreeing to come just by uttering his name.

---
#### surrender, chanting, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/VHR4_1182G4?t=1946
**Verse References:** BG 18.66
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Chanting time, let's give that exclusive time to the Lord. Don't talk anything else. Don't hear anything else. Don't think anything else."
**Summary:** The Lord expects surrender during chanting, specifically of our speaking, hearing, and thinking abilities, and even if we struggle, our sincere attempt is a form of tapasya that purifies us.

And we as devotees have to take advantage of such opportunity which the Lord has given us. Yesterday we were hearing, this is a very interesting thing which Madhavapura said that when we chant the holy name, Krishna says surrender unto me. Maa mekam sharanam maa sucha, do not fear, I'll take care. So many times, you know, it's a very vague thing, what is surrender? Although we know shastras give principles of surrender, so many things. But very practical thing which was discussed yesterday, the Lord is being merciful, he is coming out of his magnanimity. He is descending in the form of his name. He is not expecting too much. He is expecting us to surrender. Which Krishna also says in the Gita. And especially during the chanting time, he is expecting surrender of our, speaking ability. Many times we stop chanting, we start discussing with someone else something. Something may be relation with service, sometime it may not be related to service. Just because we want to discuss something. It could be related to service also. Surrender our hearing. And surrender our thinking. The Lord has descended. In the Gita he says, surrender, I will take care, maa sucha, do not fear. Even when we have to think about our service. Even if we have some pressure, some anxiety. Surrender at that moment. And Lord will take care. Many times during chanting time we are thinking of service. During service time we have one hand in the japa mala. So in that sense, even so called karmis they say, do one thing at a time. He says, human mind is wired to work very efficiently when it does one thing at a time. Although these days we do multitasking. We are hearing the phone, we are talking to someone, we are trying to sign some checks or whatever it is. So chanting time, let's give that exclusive time to the Lord. Don't talk anything else. Don't hear anything else. Don't think anything else. As far as possible. And when Krishna says that we are sincerely trying. He is happy with that. Even if we may not be able to do. At least are we making a sincere attempt? Are we struggling? That struggle is tapasya. Tapo divyam shudhye brahma saukhyam tona. Krishna says, with that tapasya will get purified.

#### sincerity, determination, mercy of the Lord
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/VHR4_1182G4?t=2127
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "God helps those who help themselves."
**Summary:** Krishna helps those who are sincere, eager, and determined to improve their chanting and hear Bhagavatam attentively, revealing what they should do to purify themselves further.

Are we struggling? Are we really desiring very hard? The example we gave, that person, just he was so eager to hear that sound. People in this material world for some material mundane goals work so hard. At times so sincerely. They are so determined. They are so passionately, you know, pursuing something which they eagerly want. So can also, we also be determined. I have to improve my quality of chanting. I have to chant sincerely. I have to hear Bhagavatam class very attentively. What should I do? What should I do? Krishna knows that we are conditioned soul. Our mind is all infected with viruses of various sorts, various kinds of this thing. But when he sees that we are sincere. When he sees that we are eager. When he sees that we are trying differently. God helps those who help themselves. Then he, he also helps us. Dadami buddhi yogam tam yena mam upayanti te. He will reveal what we should do to purify further. So here it is said that the Lord is very magnanimous. He has descended in the form and devotees of the Lord, they take advantage. They relish such wonderful forms which are all benevolent and they talk favorably with me. So we will stop here.

## HG Amitasana Dasa

### 2017-03-24 | SB 3.25.37 | HG Amitasana Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### material enjoyment, Maya, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/5OXiircBEOo?t=30
**Verse References:** BG 9.4
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu has taught that one should not desire material opulence or material reputation, nor should one try to enjoy material beauty. One should simply aspire to be absorbed in the transcendental service of the Lord."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the allure of material opulences offered by Maya, contrasting them with the transcendental service of the Lord, and emphasizing that devotees do not aspire for material pleasures, even those available on higher planets.

The vibhuti or opulences offered by Maya are of many varieties. We have experience of different varieties of material enjoyment even on this planet. But if one is able to promote himself to higher planets like Chandra Loka, the Sun, or still higher, Maharloka, Janaloka and Tapaloka, or even ultimately the highest planet which is inhabited by Brahma and is called Satyaloka, there are immense possibilities for material enjoyment. For example, the duration of life on higher planets is far, far greater than on this planet. It is said that on Moon the duration of life is such that our six months are equal to one day. We cannot even imagine the duration of life on the highest planet. It is stated in the Bhagavad Gita that Brahma's twelve hours are inconceivable even to our mathematicians. These are all descriptions of the external energy of the Lord, or Maya. Besides these, there are other opulences which the yogis can achieve by their mystic power. They are all material. A devotee does not aspire for all these material pleasures, although they are available to him simply by wishing. By the grace of the Lord, a devotee can achieve wonderful success simply by willing, but a real devotee does not like that. Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu has taught that one should not desire material opulence or material reputation, nor should one try to enjoy material beauty. One should simply aspire to be absorbed in the transcendental service of the Lord, even if one does not get liberation but has to continue the process of birth and death, or even if one does not have the opportunity to do so. Actually, however, to one who engages in Krishna consciousness, liberation is already guaranteed. Devotees enjoy all the benefits of the higher planets and the Vaikuntha planets also. It is especially mentioned here, Bhagavatim Bhadram. In the Vaikuntha planets, everything is eternally peaceful, yet a pure devotee does not even aspire to be promoted there. But still he gets the advantage. He enjoys the benefits of the higher planets, and the higher planets also. He enjoys all the facilities of the material and spiritual world, even during the present lifespan. Thus, because he is completely absorbed in the thought of Me, the devotee does not desire even the highest benediction obtainable in the upper planetary system, including Satyaloka.

---
#### Maya, material variegatedness, desire
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/5OXiircBEOo?t=193
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Material advancement means increasing the variegatedness, increasing the opportunities to enjoy materially, and the living entity gets trapped enjoying this variegatedness."
**Summary:** This segment explains that the material world is created to facilitate the Jeeva's desire to enjoy independently of the Lord, and Maya provides unlimited variegatedness to trap the living entity, constantly offering new options for enjoyment.

So Prabhupada in the Purport mentions that the vibhuti, or opulences, offered by Maya are of many varieties. So in the spiritual world, there is spiritual beauty, there is spiritual opulence, there is spiritual variegatedness. And Jeeva, who has rebelled against the Lord, who wants to enjoy independent of the Lord, be an independent enjoyer, this material world is created. And to facilitate his desire to enjoy material pleasures, the Lord very mercifully has created many variegatedness, material variegatedness. Prabhupada here says, offered by Maya are of many varieties. It is said that in earlier times, as far as what you can hear, when I joined, if you have to hear some kirtans, they were audio cassettes, Prabhupāda lectures, there was a box with six Prabhupāda audio cassettes from Bhaktivedanta archives, A side, B side, 30 minutes, 30 minutes, and many times the tape will get intertwined. So, material advancement means increasing the variegatedness, increasing the opportunities to enjoy materially, and the living entity gets trapped enjoying this variegatedness. If he gets bored with one type of material, then he gets bored with the other type of material. If he gets bored with one type of happiness, the Māyā is already ready with another option. Okay, try this. Why not try this? Why not try this? If you're bored with India, why not Singapore? If you're bored with Singapore, that's why these days, because of travel, people are traveling all over the world, vacations.

#### Maya, Krishna consciousness, desire
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/5OXiircBEOo?t=358
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Only when the living entity comes to a state, anya abhilashita shunya, there is no other abhilasha, there is no other desire... Till that time he is trapped in this material world."
**Summary:** This segment discusses how Maya diverts our focus from the Lord to material variegatedness, making it difficult to concentrate on Krishna, and emphasizes that only when one is free from all other desires (anya abhilashita shunya) can they escape the material world.

So, what Māyā has done is our focus, which was supposed to be on the Lord, thinking about the Lord, immersed in thoughts of the Lord, the Lord has taken those, that attention from the Lord, our thought of the Lord, and taken that energy and put it on unlimited variegatedness. Because for many, many lifetimes we have dabbled in different forms of material variegatedness, that's why, even during chanting time, when we want to focus the mind on the Lord, on the Holy Name, we find it very difficult. By default, mind thinks of something material. In our case, maybe not material, maybe thinking of service, but to bring it back on the Lord, to focus on the Lord, to think of the Lord, to absorb our mind on the Holy Name, it's a big challenge. Because Māyā for many, many lifetimes taken attention from the Lord and put it on this material variegatedness. And there is no limit to that. We have experiences of different varieties of material enjoyment, even on this planet, but if one is able to promote himself to higher planets like Chandra-loka the standard of happiness is even higher, even higher. And what Maya is doing is basically an agent of the Lord. If we want to enjoy independent of the Lord, there are various options available so that we don't go and disturb the Lord. So if we pass one test, there's a next test ready. If we pass that test, next test is ready. Higher and higher form of enjoyment. Subtler and subtler form of traps. Only when the living entity comes to a state, anya abhilashita shunya, there is no other abhilasha, there is no other desire. He has finished, he has exhausted all hopes that some form of material variegatedness will give him pleasure. Till that time he is trapped in this material world.

#### Maya, Kali Yuga, material pleasures
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/5OXiircBEOo?t=520
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Maya on other side is tempting us with various forms of material pleasures. And on one side is the real pleasure that is Krishna, Krishna's beauty, attraction to Krishna, thinking of Krishna."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the increased difficulty in Kali Yuga due to the unlimited ways Maya offers for sense enjoyment, and contrasts the fleeting nature of material pleasures with the real pleasure found in Krishna consciousness.

And in Kali Yuga, it is all the more difficult because as I said, Maya is, you know, unlimited ways in which a living entity can enjoy his senses. These days you have televisions with 600 channels, 700 channels. What sports you want to see? What game you want to see? And now with Jio and all that, earlier, you know, you missed a match. At best, you would call your friend and say, please record that match for me. Please record that serial for me. Now any serial, any match, any time for the last six months, one year is played, it's at your fingertips. And a living entity who has inclination to enjoy materially, he is ready. Huge options are now available. For example, the duration now in higher planets, this is about material, this earthly planet. Now in higher planets, there are apsaras, there are, you know, the standard of living is much higher, duration of life is much higher. Here 20-30 years, you know, the skin starts wrinkling, eyesight starts becoming weak, the senses become weak. Even if we want to enjoy, you know, we have limited, you know, limited time. Imagine in other lokas, the duration of life is the standard of enjoyment is higher, the subtle forms of enjoyment, and the duration is very, very long. Prabhupada says, we cannot even imagine the duration of life on the higher planet. All these are descriptions of the external energy of the Lord or Maya. So we should understand that we have a very big project. In fact, Prabhupada says it's a war against Maya. Maya on other side is tempting us with various forms of material pleasures. And on one side is the real pleasure that is Krishna, Krishna's beauty, attraction to Krishna, thinking of Krishna. Our natural inclination is towards material energy. If we get fed up with material energy, with one kind of enjoyment, there is promise of another kind of variegated, another kind of variegated, another kind of variegated.

#### Krishna consciousness, Maya, habitual response

**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

That's why even when we come to Krishna consciousness, at times we feel frustrated, why I am getting attracted? Theoretically, we all know. But because it has become a habitual response, moment we see a sense object, there's a habitual response. So that's why it is said. We have to practice the principles of Krishna consciousness with enthusiasm, with patience and with determination. Generally, when we enjoy material pleasures or become, achieve material opulence, become materially well situated. Again, that is another trap of Maya. We can feel that now I am in control. We can start feeling that now I am safe. Bhagavatam Deva says, Deha apta kalatra deeshu, atma saineshu asat svapi. Again, it's a trap of Maya when we are surrounded by a lot of wealth, wealth, wealth, family members who are very supportive, good bank balance, a lot of insurance policies. One gets a sense that, you know, I am safe now. So Bhagavatam says that, asat svapi, asat svapi, it's not a fact. The security is not real.

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "By the mercy of the Lord, even just we have begun our devotional service, somehow we are able to see the scam, we are able to see the traps. Although still inclinations are there for material pleasure, at least theoretically we know what's the truth, what's the reality."
**Summary:** This segment uses the example of Jayalalitha's death to illustrate the illusion of material security, emphasizing that Maya covers our real knowledge, and that it is only by the mercy of the Lord that we can begin to see through the traps of Maya, even with our imperfections.

Thank You very much. Thank You. Although one sees that this is not real security, we have example of, you know, recently Mrs. Jayalalitha, chief minister of Tamil Nadu, she was in the hospital, attended to be the best doctors, 18 doctors, specialists were monitoring round the clock various parameters. The best of doctors in the world were consulted, the doctors from Ames were flown in, the best medical facilities were at the disposal, but then, asatsvapi. So, pramatta, we get maddened, that is the beauty of maya, it covers our real knowledge. And how do we come out of maya, how do we come out of illusion? Krishna says, it's my divine energy. It's not easy to break this illusion. Even learned men, even intellectuals, even big, big politicians, even scholars, philosophers, they are in the trap. By the mercy of the Lord, even we are not pure, even just we have begun our devotional service, somehow we are able to see the scam, we are able to see the traps. Although still inclinations are there for material pleasure, at least theoretically we know what's the truth, what's the reality.

#### devotional service, Maya, false ego
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/5OXiircBEOo?t=894
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This is exactly what happens when somebody becomes very opulent. He thinks that he is in control."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the importance of following the teachings of great acharyas to escape Maya's traps, and illustrates the dangers of false ego and material opulence through the story of the richest frog.

So that's the mercy of the Lord, and if we follow the footsteps of great acharyas, follow their teachings diligently, then we can be completely out of this trap. We can be very rightly situated, just like here it says, completely absorbed in thought of me, and then the devotee does not desire any material happiness, any material benediction. He just wants association of Lord, just want to engage in service of the Lord. One time there was a frog, he was living with other frogs, his family. And other frogs, other community of frogs, by the side of a river. And these frogs were facing a big problem. And the problem was, these elephants, they would come, herds of elephants would come to drink water or take bath in that river. And when they would come, you can imagine elephants, so big, so huge. And when they would come they would not see here, there, they would just keep walking towards the river, and all the frogs, you know, at times they were caught unaware, and they would get trampled under the feet of elephants. They would try to save their lives, but there were so many elephants that, you know, they would get crushed. So one day, this frog was sitting by the side of this river, and he said, we have to do something. And that day, somebody had come to take bath. And suddenly people, when they come, take bath, you know, offer some flowers to the river or put some money. So that frog happened to get a fifty paisa coin, which was lying by the side of the river, somebody had offered. So he took that coin and said, he started thinking, looks like I am the richest frog in the world. After all, which frog will have fifty paisa? I am the richest frog. And then, he put the coin on the ground and majestically sat on the coin and said, I am the richest frog. You know, with lot of pride, lot of ego. I am the richest frog, now I am the richest frog. And then, he started, because I am the richest frog, I should do something for my community. For other frogs. They have been harassed by these elephants and now I will do something for my community to solve once and for all the problems what they are facing. So, one fine day, sitting on the coin, very confidently thinking he is the richest frog, he has to serve his brethren. And there he sees from a distance elephants approaching the river. So from a distance, he says, stop! Stop! So elephants, where do they notice? Small frog sitting in some corner. He says, stop! I, the richest frog, order the elephants, don't move any further. The elephants kept moving towards the water, kept moving towards the water. He says, don't you know who I am? I am the richest frog, I am ordering, stop! Not one step, you should take more. The elephants kept moving, kept moving, kept moving. He says, no, you cannot do it. I am ordering. I, the richest frog, am ordering. You cannot come towards the river. And by that time, elephants had approached and tap on his, and the richest frog is over.

#### Maya, false ego, social work
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/5OXiircBEOo?t=1124
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "That is another trap of Maya. Sometime people ask, should we do some kind of a social work? Should we help somebody who is in need? Should we feed a person who is, who is hungry? Yes, our idea is, some spiritual element has to be added."
**Summary:** This segment explains how material opulence can lead to the false belief of being in control and a well-wisher, and cautions against performing social work with the understanding of being the doer, emphasizing the importance of adding a spiritual element and recognizing oneself as an instrument of the Lord.

This is exactly, although this is a figurative story, it looks quite, how can a frog claim like this? This is exactly what happens when somebody becomes very opulent. He thinks that he is in control. He thinks that, I am the well-wisher of my community, my society, my country, the world at large. I can help people. Krishna says, Many times, so-called philanthropists, they want to help others, thinking that, taking the position of the Lord. Although, yes, doing good to others is, is, is very nice. But not with the understanding that, I am the Lord. I am the well-wisher. I am the doer. I will help my brethren. That is another trap of Maya. Sometime people ask, should we do some kind of a social work? Should we help somebody who is in need? Should we feed a person who is, who is hungry? Yes, our idea is, some spiritual element has to be added. If you have to feed, yes, we can feed Prasadam. But, another important thing is, with what consciousness we are helping. If we are helping that, I am the well-wisher, I am really the one who can help him, that's a wrong understanding. We may even help a person, but with the understanding that, he is amsa of the Lord, I am amsa of the Lord. On behalf of the Lord, I am helping him.

#### devotees, Maya, humility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/5OXiircBEOo?t=1226
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Devotee always should take a humble position. Oh Lord, wherever I may be, let me be just situated humbly, the shelter of your lotus feet."
**Summary:** This segment warns devotees against the traps of power, fame, and adoration, emphasizing the importance of humility and seeking shelter at the Lord's lotus feet, and reiterating that any desire other than serving the Lord is a trap.

So, as devotees, many times we can get positions, we can get power, we can get fame, we can get adoration. And, we have to be very, very cautious. It says, power corrupts. We have examples of many devotees also who, sometimes were given services which required lot of control, lot of power, lot of work. Lot of position, lot of fame. And, Maya trapped them. So, devotee always should take a humble position. Oh Lord, wherever I may be, let me be just situated humbly, the shelter of your lotus feet. Wherever I take birth, in this life or any other life, let me have just one benediction. Let me be engaged in your loving, devotional service. In this life or any other life, wherever I may take birth, let me be always be in association of your devotees. Any other desire we have, it's a trap. That's why pure devotional service, it said, Anyabhilashita shunya.

#### devotional service, humility, anarthas
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/5OXiircBEOo?t=1308
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "At least, devotee should internally believe that. I am the most fallen."
**Summary:** This segment encourages introspection and recognizing one's own anarthas (unwanted qualities), emphasizing that even if one cannot suddenly become pure, acknowledging one's imperfections is crucial for spiritual progress and maintaining humility.

So, we should little bit be introspective. No doubt, suddenly we cannot become pure. Suddenly we cannot become full of pride-less ego, annihilate the ego. But then, at least we know these are the anarthas. They say, the bottom most stage is unconscious incompetence. At least, theoretically we should know conscious incompetence. We know that I am having material desires. We know that I am lusty. We know that I still have hopes that I can find pleasure. And I am not able to control those things. That will make a devotee, he will not think that, you know, I am great, I am senior, I am this. He will be properly situated. Knowing that, I have so many anarthas. I have a long way to go. Grame, that says, grame kahe pandita. People are claiming that I am a big pandita. But, what a fallen person I am. At least, devotee should internally believe that. I am the most fallen. Acharyas were not saying out of just, just to, you know, depict or demonstrate their humility. They actually believed it. They believed just like Krishnadas Kaviraj Goswami is saying that, I am more fallen than Jagayan Madai. So, here it is said that devotees do not, they are just absorbed in thoughts of the Lord. They don't want to enjoy material pleasures. They are not looking for heavenly pleasures. They are not even looking for going back to Godhead. They are not even looking for liberation. Actually, however, one who engages in Krishna consciousness, liberation is already guaranteed. But still, a devotee is not even aspiring for liberation. He is just aspiring for one thing, Krishna, please engage me in your loving devotional service. Let me not forget your lotus feet. Let me always be conscious of you. Just give me this benediction.

Just let me be always in association of devotees. In the Vaikuntha planets, everything is eternally peaceful. Yet, a pure devotee does not even aspire to be promoted there. But still, he gets that advantage. He enjoys all the facilities of material and spiritual world, even during his present lifetime. So, Bhagavatam, just like it said, nashta prayesho bhadre su nityam bhagavata sevaya We get calibrated. Day after day, we are getting, so to say, covered by the material energy. Just like day after day, we get dirty, we perspire, we take bath, we get cleansed. Likewise, day after day, unless we hear Bhagavatam, we'll go tangent, we'll go off. We have to understand what's the goal, what we have to aspire for, what is our end result. End result is to come to a state where we just desire the Lord. That's the goal. One time, there were some hatha yogis. They had, there was some kind of conclave. They had all assembled by the side of a river.

#### siddhis, yoga, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/5OXiircBEOo?t=1536
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So, who is the most accomplished yogi whereby he is dear to the Lord, he is nearest to the Lord?"
**Summary:** This segment begins a story about hatha yogis trying to determine who is the most accomplished by demonstrating various siddhis (powers), such as walking on water, flying, and knowing the past, present, and future.

So, while they had all assembled, one of them said, let's try to figure out who is the most accomplished yogi. Thereby, because yogi is very dear to Krishna. Yogi naam api sarve Shyam Krishna says. So, yogis are dear to Krishna. So, who is the most accomplished yogi whereby he is dear to the Lord, he is nearest to the Lord. Now, because they were hatha yogis with lot of siddhis and all that, they thought that most accomplished yogi means who has more siddhis, more, you know, siddhi powers. So, while this discussion was on, one of the yogi came forward. He says, I have been doing tapasya meditation for so many years. I have the siddhi that I can just walk on water. And without waiting for anyone, he just got up and started walking. By the side, there was a river, continued walking. He walked on the river and then came back. So, everyone was astonished. What a wonderful achievement. He can walk on water. Then another yogi, he said, this is nothing. Just see what I can do. So, he took one twig, that dhatun you have, that neem dhatun, the twig. He took that. He sat on that and started flying. And all of a sudden, all the people are watching. He is going in circles in the sky. And then he comes down, lands safely. So, people started discussing, this is more than the previous one. Flying in the sky. So, then another person said, this is nothing. He says, I, because of my siddhi, can tell the past, present and future. So, he called one person. He says, come, you come. So, the person came. And he started narrating in detail all what has happened to him in his life. His wife's name, his children's name, what happened, when he got married, when he studied, where he studied, how much marks he got. He says, is it correct? He says, yes. Then he started telling what present problems he is facing. He says, is it correct? Yes. Then he started what's going to happen with him. So, everyone was astonished. That person fell at his feet. You are great. That's what happens when astrologers, you know, we go and they tell something, we feel, my God, how powerful they are. So, one person said, this is nothing. He says, I have a siddhi.

#### humility, devotion, ego
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/5OXiircBEOo?t=1772
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A person who has annihilated his ego... thinks that I am the doer."
**Summary:** The story concludes with an old woman revealing that true accomplishment lies not in siddhis but in annihilating the ego and recognizing oneself as an instrument of the Lord, emphasizing the importance of humility and devotion over material achievements.

He says, You can light a fire. Lot of woodwork collected and fire was made. He went and sat there. Nothing happened to him. The fire was burning. He was sitting in that fire and he just walked out of fire, unharmed. So, while this all was going on, everyone trying to demonstrate who is greater yogi and who is more accomplished and who is more dearer to the Lord. An old woman just walked out from that group. He says, none of you are dear to the Lord. He says, this is not at all pleasing to the Lord. After all, these powers we have, abilities we have, skills we have, it's all coming from the Lord. So, she says, none of you are dear to the Lord. So, the people became curious and said, who is dear to the Lord? Who is the most accomplished yogi? He says, a person who has annihilated his ego. A person who has annihilated the fact, thinking that I am great. He says, Krishna says, kartaham itim aparita jnana kartaham itim anyara thinks that I am the doer. And then she said, that is not very easy to achieve. The siddhis and all, you can achieve very easily. Relatively speaking, it's much easy. But to achieve that state of consciousness where one thinks that I am nothing, I am the most fallen, everything what I have is coming from the Lord. Belongs to the Lord. He is the real enjoyer. To come to that consciousness, to annihilate that ego, to annihilate that pride, to annihilate that ghamand, that's not very easy. That's why Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, when he says, trinad api suni chena, taror api sahisnuna, maani na maana dena. We are followers of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. Actually, the achievement what we are aspiring for, it's very exalted. Where one actually thinks that he is very fallen, lower than a straw in the street. Somebody tramples, it just gets up. So we also should not aspire, even in demotic communities, many times we fall in trap, some position, some power, some fame, for the service of the Lord, we may get, but that should not be our goal. To facilitate the service of the Lord, we may get some responsibility. But if we get carried away, if we start thinking that I am a big devotee, I am a senior devotee, I am this devotee, that devotee, again, we get into the clutches of Maya. So for devotees, the safe position is, he always thinks that he is servant of the servant of the servant, of the servant of the servant, of the servant of the servant of the Lord. And just Lord, always keep me in association of devotees, and I get the good fortune of always serving you, life after life. Wherever you want to keep me, you want to embrace me, you want to make me brokenhearted, you want to keep me in this material world, you want to take me back to Godhead, you want to give me liberation, that's your choice. I just ask for only one thing, and that is, just keep me in your service. I will stop here. Granth Raj, Srimad Bhagavatam ki, Srila Prabhupada ki.

## HG Amitasana Dasa

### 2017-04-28 | SB 3.26 3 | HG Amitasana Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### God, creation, anadi
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Fu5uIKUt9AM?t=540
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "One of the definitions or understanding of God is He is anadi."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the concept of God as the cause of all causes, without beginning or end, and the source of all creation.

Everything has a cause, but the Lord is sarvakarana karanam, is the cause of all causes. When there was nothing, He was there. Sometimes people find it difficult to comprehend, and a very commonly asked question, generally when you address somebody who is totally new to Krishna consciousness, who created God? Okay, we can understand that God created everything, but who created God? It's like that chicken and egg story. Chicken comes from egg, and as it grows up, the hen gives egg. So what came first? People are perplexed. So one of the definitions or understanding of God is He is anadi. He is not an ordinary mundane person. Anadir adir govinda sarvakarana karanam. He is the cause of all causes. He is without beginning and is without an end, and He is the beginning of everyone. So everything what we see in this creation, has come from Him.

---
#### omnipresence, energy, material energy, spiritual energy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Fu5uIKUt9AM?t=632
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The entire creation is pervaded by the Lord. Nothing but the Lord and His energies."
**Summary:** This segment explains how the Lord, although a person, is present everywhere through His energies, both material and spiritual, pervading the entire creation.

Although He is without beginning, but He is the beginning of everyone. So then further Prabhupada mentions that, although the Lord is a person, He is present everywhere. He is perceivable everywhere, because He is self-effulgent. This is again, very difficult for many people to comprehend. Because all along we have an understanding of a person who is limited in time and space. If you see a person in a particular place, you can very confidently conclude that He is not in another place. But here it is said that the Lord is all-pervading. If He is a person, how can He be all-pervading? Because all persons whom we have seen are limited by time and space. If they are present in place A, they cannot be simultaneously present in place B. So here it is said that the Lord is present everywhere by His energies. The entire creation is pervaded by the Lord. The entire creation is pervaded by the Lord. The entire creation is pervaded by the Lord. Nothing but the Lord and His energies. Energies, broadly speaking, material energy and spiritual energy. The Lord is present anywhere and everywhere. And He is present through the distribution of energy. In the Bhagavad Gita also it is confirmed that the Lord is all-pervading and exists everywhere by two kinds of energy, one material and other spiritual. Both material and spiritual energies are spread everywhere and that is a proof of existence of the Supreme Personality of Godhead.

#### material energy, maya, modes of nature, bondage
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Fu5uIKUt9AM?t=761
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This maya, material energy, which is composed of three modes of material nature, binds the living entity, entangles the living entity."
**Summary:** This segment discusses how living entities in the material world are influenced by material energy (maya) and its three modes, which bind and entangle them.

So in the material world, we living entities who have rebelled against the Lord are under the influence of material energy. And this material energy, Krishna says, deviyesha gunamayi mamamaya dhuratyah. This maya, material energy, which is composed of three modes of material nature, binds the living entity, entangles the living entity. And that knot is very, very stringent. prakrte kriyamanani gunayi karmani sarvasah. We helplessly act under the influence of the modes of material nature which is influencing us. Just like we see typically a rope made of three strands intertwined. So likewise, these three gunas, the influence of these three gunas act like a rope which binds the living entity.

#### modes of nature, analogy, printing, consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Fu5uIKUt9AM?t=840
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Likewise unlimited combinations of behaviors, perspective of life, consciousness is possible by the influence of these three modes of material nature."
**Summary:** Using the analogy of CMYK printing, this segment explains how the three modes of material nature combine to create unlimited variations in behavior and consciousness.

If we see any printed picture, we see books with color plates, we see calendars, we see posters, magazines, brochures, all very colorful these days. The printing technology has advanced. There was a time when a lot of things were done black and white. It was very expensive to print in color. But these days we see a lot of colorful prints. Actually, if you go to a printer, all these colorful prints, all these beautiful calendars, all colorful posters, they are just made of... anyone has an idea how many colors? Four colors. C, M, Y, K. Cyan, magenta, black and yellow. If we closely see the... that's why we call it DPI, dots per inch. Higher the DPI, you will not be able to see the dots. It's so close, so densely packed. And different colors are produced by the different size of dots and how densely they are close to each other. If you see a picture which is by and large reddish in color, you'll have more of magenta dots and densely populated. If you see Radha Krishna calendar and if you see dress of Radharani, it's yellow in color. If you closely see with a very powerful lens, you will see it's made of yellow dots. And if you see orange, it is combination of yellow and magenta dots. And human eye cannot perceive it. So just like we have unlimited shades possible by combination of three colors, four colors, likewise unlimited combinations of behaviors, perspective of life, consciousness is possible by the influence of these three modes of material nature.

#### modes of nature, behavior, sattva guna, tamo guna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Fu5uIKUt9AM?t=979
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Different behaviors are all nothing but the combination of modes which are influencing us."
**Summary:** This segment explains how our behavior and perspective change as we are influenced by different modes of material nature, contrasting the experiences of someone in tamo guna versus sattva guna.

And we helplessly act under the influence of these modes. We see many times our behavior changes as we advance spiritually. Many times our perspective of life changes. Our understanding of world changes. It is because of the modes which are influencing is changing. If we are in tamo guna predominantly, we'll have one perspective of life. We'll have a particular kind of behavior. In general, we will be lazy. We will be clouded with different kinds of ignorance. As we rise in the modes, in rajo guna, in tamo guna, sorry, in sattva guna, our perspective changes. That's why a symptom of sattva guna is enlightenment, is knowledge. Just like an example is given, if you take an alkali, we have all studied in chemistry, alkali and acid. If you take an alkali and you combine with another alkali, there is no reaction. You take a powerful acid and combine with another acid, there is no reaction. You take an alkali and an acid, you mix together, there is a powerful reaction. Likewise, a person who is influenced by tamo guna, if he goes to a tamasic place, will he feel out of place? No. He is comfortable. There is no reaction. If you take a person who is predominantly in sattva guna, you take him to a place where tamo guna is predominant, he will feel very uncomfortable. He will feel like, let me just get away from this place as early as possible. See, different behaviors are all nothing but the combination of modes which are influencing us.

#### modes of nature, behavior, analogy, birds, comfort zone
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Fu5uIKUt9AM?t=1139
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Birds of same feather flock together."
**Summary:** Using the analogy of birds and their preferred environments, this segment illustrates how people with different inclinations and behaviors, influenced by the modes of nature, are comfortable with different things.

Prabhupada used to say, birds of same feather flock together. Swans, you will find them in very clear water bodies, nice landscapes, lakes. They feel very comfortable there. But you will not find many crows there in that place. Crows are more comfortable where there is filth, where there is some kind of a garbage, where there is some kind of dead corpse. So they enjoy that. Likewise, in this material world also, we see people have different inclinations, different behaviors. They are comfortable with different things. Some people are very comfortable with where there is dirt all around. Some people don't take bath for days together. They are comfortable with that. Can you see how the modes of material nature which influence us, conditions us, binds us, and makes us behave in a particular way? In Mumbai, we have a place where fruits are available near IIT market. And we have to pass through a place where they also sell, it's called fish market. And it smells so bad, so bad. Most of us cannot pass through that place. So whenever we have to go to buy fruits, we take an additional half a kilometer detour and reach that place. And whenever we are going, we sometimes talk among ourselves, how do people tolerate this smell? But if you see there, some people are very, very comfortable there, nicely living there, because they've got condition. The modes influencing them has conditioned them and that has become a new normal for them.

A person who is in tamo guna, when something tamasik is happening, he'll feel uncomfortable. A person who is in tamo guna, when something tamasik is happening, he'll feel uncomfortable. A person who is in tamo guna, when something tamasik is happening, he'll feel uncomfortable. A person who is in tamo guna, when something tamasik is happening, he'll feel uncomfortable. A person who is in tamo guna, when something tamasik is happening, he'll feel uncomfortable. A person who is in tamo guna, when something tamasik is happening, he'll feel uncomfortable. he will feel uncomfortable. Tamo guna, tamo guna, no reaction. Washing happens once in a month. So these are all symptoms of tamo guna. Forget about getting out of it.

#### transcendence, spiritual practice, shuddha sattva, allurements
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Fu5uIKUt9AM?t=1458
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Genuine spirituality involves on one side some do's like chanting, reading, hearing, participating in kirtans which elevates our consciousness and some don'ts which generally pulls the consciousness down."
**Summary:** This segment explains how contact with the Lord through spiritual practices elevates us in the modes, leading to transcendence, but also warns of the allurements of material energy that can pull us back down, emphasizing the need for both 'dos' and 'don'ts' in spiritual life.

Here the Lord is mentioned as transcendental to the material modes, his shuddha sattva. So when we touch the Lord, when we come in contact with the Lord, when we chant the names of the Lord, when we touch that supreme personality of Godhead who is supremely pure, who is transcendental to the material modes, we also gradually elevate in the modes and transcend the modes. The example is given just like you take an iron rod, put it in fire, it becomes hot. Keep it in fire, it becomes warmer. And when time comes, it becomes red hot. It acts like fire. So likewise the Lord who is all spiritual, when we who are under the influence of material energy, come in contact with the Lord by the process which is prescribed by the acharyas, we slowly come out of the modes of material nature. We transcend the modes and come to shuddha sattva. But unfortunately because we are conditioned, there is a natural inclination for us to fall back. Material energy is very lucrative for us. It's very tempting for us. The allurement, sense objects, it's very alluring which pulls us down, pulls our consciousness down. So that's why genuine spirituality involves on one side some do's like chanting, reading, hearing, participating in kirtans which elevates our consciousness and some don'ts which generally pulls the consciousness down.

#### material energy, kleshas, surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Fu5uIKUt9AM?t=1561
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If you surrender to me, these modes of material nature which are working under my direction, under my supervision, it is my energy, I will relieve you from these modes."
**Summary:** This segment describes how material energy batters living entities with three kinds of miseries (kleshas), making it difficult to comprehend their situation, and emphasizes that surrendering to Krishna is the only way to be relieved from these modes.

And this material energy which binds the living entity actually prahupada here mentions the living entity which is under the influence of this modes of material nature is battered by this material energy by three kinds of kleshas. Actually battered. We have Three kleshas, adhyatmik kleshas, adhidevik kleshas, adhibhautik kleshas. Miseries due to our body and mind, miseries due to other living entities, and miseries due to natural calamities. And in spite of being battered by the material modes, deviesha gunamai, mamamaya dhuratya. Dhuratya means it is very difficult for the living entities, struggling. But because we are under the influence of lower modes, we are not able to comprehend that. We still are too eager to exert our supremacy, that yes, I am in control. I don't need the Lord. I can manage my life on my own. I can maintain an existence independent of the Lord. Whereas Krishna says, mamamaya dhuratya, mam eva ye prapadyante. Mam eva ye prapadyante. If you surrender to me, these modes of material nature which are working under my direction, under my supervision, it is my energy, I will relieve you from these modes.

#### false ego, material energy, story, lion, elephant
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Fu5uIKUt9AM?t=1665
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Why are you getting angry if you don't know the right answer?"
**Summary:** This segment tells a story of a lion trying to assert his dominance in the jungle, highlighting how false ego persists even after being battered by material energy.

One time there was a lion in the jungle. So generally said to be the king. So he wanted to just reinforce his supremacy as being the king of the jungle. So one day he happened to see a monkey in the jungle. So he went to the monkey and said, hey monkey. So the monkey was shaken and growling. The lion said, hey monkey. He says, who is the king of the jungle? So in a very shaky voice, in a timid voice, the monkey said, you sir, you sir, you are the king. So he was quite happy with that answer. He said, don't you forget. He said, don't you forget. He said, don't you forget. So then he started walking away. There he saw a zebra. So again he wanted to double check, reinforce his supremacy. He said, hey zebra. This time he rode. And he said, who is the king of the jungle? So zebra again with fear, body shaking, he says, you sir, you. You are the king of the jungle. He says, don't you forget that. Kept moving. And then he came across an elephant. Huge, gigantic elephant. So again, as usual, rolled and growled. He says, hey elephant, who is the king of the jungle? So elephant didn't reply. He took him in the trunk and threw him against the tree. In comics you come across, you know, when a person gets this thing, he sees stars and all this. So, you know, he got smashed against a tree. Then this elephant walked towards this lion. Stepped on his tail. Again picked him up and smashed him on the ground. And started walking away. So the lion got up. And he said, why are you getting angry if you don't know the right answer?
