## HG Amitasana Dasa

### A Chance To Change | HG Amitasana Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### Krishna's nature, Qualitative oneness, Spiritual world
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/LyHytEHamtU?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So being Amsha of the Lord, qualitatively we are one with the Lord and we also are Sat, Chit and Ananda."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that living entities are qualitatively the same as Krishna, possessing Sat, Chit, and Ananda, and in the spiritual world, they are engaged in loving service to Him.

In the Bhagavad Gita, Krishna mentions that the living entities, the spirit soul, the Atma is his Amsha. Prabhupada explains that as Amsha of the Lord, we are qualitatively the same as that of the Lord. Prabhupada gives the example of how a drop of water in the sea has the same characteristics as the entire sea or the entire ocean, but quantitatively we are different. A drop of water is very minuscule compared to the entire ocean. So being Amsha of the Lord, qualitatively we are one with the Lord and we also are Sat, Chit and Ananda. Sat means eternal, Chit means full of knowledge and Ananda, blissful. These Amsha of the Lord in the spiritual world, in the association of the Lord, are blissfully engaged in the service of the Lord. In the spiritual world, the Lord is the center. There is no Aham, there is pure love, and everyone is engaged in serving others. A devotee may engage in serving other devotees, but keeping the Lord in the center. So there the mentality is Pehle Aap, Your service. Your seva enabling others to serve Krishna directly or indirectly everyone is engaged in serving the Supreme Lord in glorifying the Supreme Lord.

---
#### Free will, Rebellion, Material world as prison
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/LyHytEHamtU?t=116
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So likewise the original crime, ichcha, I want to be the lord."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the material world is like a prison house created for rebellious souls who desire to be the Lord, giving them a chance to reform.

I'm sure of the Lord when he develops an ichcha, we have a free will. We have a power to choose. Krishna has given us the free will, so when that Amsha desires, eacha, what is that eacha? Desires to be glorified, to be served, to be the center of attraction, to be famous, in other words, take the position of the Lord. Now, to fulfill that desire, some souls cannot be forced to serve that Jeeva who has developed that desire, just like in the material world when some souls rebel against the established law, when they don't want to abide by the law, when they don't want to go by the overall scheme of things, the way things have been planned, and if he violates that law, violates that arrangement, if he rebels against the law or against the government, then the government is forced to make a prison house. Why a prison house? The government can always isolate such people who have violated the laws, who are criminals, who have committed crimes, who have committed some crime, who have rebelled against the government, who are not willing to abide by the laws. They can put them in a line and shoot them, but the government wants to give them an opportunity to reform. Yes, that mentality has come, criminal mentality has arisen. A person who is a law abiding citizen has committed a crime. Now rather than just shooting him, let's give an opportunity to reform him of his criminal mentality. So what does the government do? The government invests money, invests energy in building a prison house because there is a necessity. There are some citizens who will break the laws, in other words, who will commit a crime. To reform them, a prison house is made. So likewise the original crime, ichcha, I want to be the lord. I want to be the lord. I want to not follow the law, be the law myself. When that Ichcha comes, then that soul, rebellious soul is put in this material world.

---
#### Loss of original quality, Opportunity for reform, Desire for recognition
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/LyHytEHamtU?t=287
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So this material world is created where an opportunity is given to such rebellious soul to reform."
**Summary:** The speaker describes how the rebellious soul loses its original qualities of Sat, Chit, and Ananda upon entering the material world, which serves as an opportunity for reform, but the soul still desires recognition and glory.

When it comes to this material world, the original quality of the living entity, which is Sat Chit Ananda, which is eternal, Chit, knowledge, knowledge of what? Knowledge of the understanding of the self, understanding that I am Amsha of Krishna, understanding that I am servant of Krishna, understanding of Krishna, Chit, and Ananda, blissfully engaged in that service, constitutional position. So the living entity comes in this material world. So somebody may wonder, are we all prisoners? Just like, you know, when we see some prisoners in some prison, we say, you know, these are all criminals. Criminal is not a very, in that sense, respected, that somebody is a dacoit, somebody has committed a murder. When suppose sometimes go to a prison for preaching or some other thing, we see these are all criminals. So are we also criminals? Yes, in that sense, we also are criminals. The crime what we have committed is we have rebelled against the Lord, the Lord who is the Lord of all things, is the center of attraction, who is to be glorified, who is to be served, we want to take that position. Somebody doesn't want to follow the Lord, but I don't care, law by himself. So this material world is created where an opportunity is given to such rebellious soul to reform, to be a saint, without being charged of what is right, without being punished, to be a God, without being a theotokos, without being reformed in this world. I don't want to be called a God, but I don't want to be called a god. I want to be treated like a God, like a saint, and I can be a saint. I want to be known as a God, but I am not a saint. I don't want to be called a god, so I have no place to call a saint, but I want to be known as a God, the one who brings us together.

#### Krishna consciousness, Maya, Purification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lxjaSNrbCi4?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna consciousness, when a person is Krishna conscious, dependent on Krishna, remembering Krishna, then one of the symptoms would be he will be happy."
**Summary:** We are in a transition phase, not fully Krishna conscious nor completely conditioned, but slowly purifying our consciousness through practice, leading to liberation and happiness when dependent on Krishna.

So, we are now in a transition phase, actually, if we analyze. We are not fully Krishna conscious. We are not like Prabhupada, a sent person, always acting for Krishna's pleasure, always remembering Krishna. And to some extent, we are realized that we are in Maya. By Prabhupada's mercy, we have started practicing principles of Krishna consciousness. Neither are we fully Krishna conscious, nor are we completely conditioned. Slowly, by practicing these principles, we are elevating from the lower modes, and our consciousness is getting purified. The more and more we get purified, the more and more we will feel liberated. More and more we will feel free from the clutches of Maya, the clutches of material existence. How do we know? If we are also struggling, we are not happy, full of anxiety, then that means we are in the clutches of Maya. Krishna consciousness, when a person is Krishna conscious, dependent on Krishna, remembering Krishna, then one of the symptoms would be he will be happy. Whatever is happening, Krishna is there, Krishna is taking care, Krishna is taking me through this.

#### varnashrama, social order, self-realization
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zJBroLaZLu8?t=194
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In varnashrama system, classification is done so that as per the capacity of person, education and rules and regulations could be introduced so that he can elevate to higher standard of life."
**Summary:** The speaker begins narrating the story of King Yayati, starting with the story of King Nahusha and his son Yati's refusal to rule, then digressing into an explanation of the varnashrama system and its purpose for societal and spiritual elevation.

So this history, this past time is very beautifully mentioned in the 9th canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. There was a very great king, Nahusha. He had six sons. And Nahusha molested the wife of Indra, and thus she, being very disturbed, approached brahmanas like Agastya. And understanding the situation of the poor woman, they cursed Nahusha to become a python. Now the great king is missing, you need another king. So his other sons were requested to occupy the position of father and to rule over the kingdom. So the eldest son was Yati. And he told, no, I don't want to occupy this position. Because the power to rule brings with it many material infatuations. And the person loses the sight of self-realization, which is the ultimate objective of human form of life. Realizing, I am not this body. Simply by putting petrol and grease in the car, the driver won't be satisfied. Similarly, by attaining objects of enjoyment, I will not be satisfied who am different from this body. But a person gets infatuated by worldly powers when he becomes king. Devgana, Manavgana or Danavgana. So devtas and demigods are present everywhere throughout the universe. Devtas means who are devotees of Lord Vishnu and asuras means who are atheists. So as human beings also, we have such tendencies, either that of demigod or that of asuras or that of manavas. So at the time of marriage, these things are considered that the marriage should be there in proper ganas. If they are not compatible with each other, there would be so many fights. And then there are other considerations also like this horoscope matching is done very, very nicely. And there are these varnas and ashramas. So usually the Brahmin boy marries a Brahmin girl. Kshatriya boy marries a Kshatriya girl. Why this happens? Because this is scientific also. So unfortunately caste system became degraded and it was not, it was never a caste system. It was a varnashrama system that got degraded into a caste system where people unauthorizedly, they occupied the position of a priest and they were not allowing other people to come to the priesthood. And my son would become next priest, his son would become next priest, even though they may not be qualified. However, this is not the mood of varnashrama system. In varnashrama system, classification is done so that as per the capacity of person, education and rules and regulations could be introduced so that he can elevate to higher standard of life. Shudra is supposed to be expected to rise to Vaishya, Kshatriya and Varnashrama. And then eventually Brahmana platform. Just like we have so many grades in our schools.

#### varnashrama, marriage, heredity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zJBroLaZLu8?t=385
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So, the proper physiology, psychology is continued as a hereditary principle when this marriage takes place among the same qualified people."
**Summary:** The speaker continues explaining the varnashrama system, emphasizing the importance of marrying within one's varna to preserve specific qualities and inclinations, facilitating proper training and societal function.

1st, 2nd, 3rd, 4th, 5th, 10th, 10th plus 2, then graduation, post graduation. Now these different grade standards do not exist so that a pre-primary student should always remain at pre-primary level. No, one is expected to come to primary level. Rather promotion is the only objective of creating these grades. Similarly, in varnashrama also there is proper division because promotion is the only objective. If a very new boy is given a book of algebra, he would not be able to understand basic arithmetic also. So, thus gradation is there. Now you follow this much only, you are allowed to play, study little and then you can become more serious when you are able to tackle the subjects at your level. This is varnashrama system. So, thus proper classification is required so that gradually from Shudra, Vaishya people can come to Brahmana. From there they can become Vaishnava and understand God. So, God realization is ultimate objective of all these divisions. So, Brahmanas have a specific duty. Now, the duty should match a person's inclination. In today's education system we see most of the people are not interested. The toppers also get burnt out. They are not interested in education. But the society's design is like everyone is forced to study. So no, study is meant only for higher classes. Especially Brahmanas and Kshatriyas and Vaishyas also sometimes. But specially for the Brahmanas, topmost education is meant for. They are naturally inclined to study, to do research about absolute truth. So, everything is there in the DNA that everyone understands now. What is our inclination, likes, dislikes. So, Kshatriya needs certain kind of valour, chivalry and certain other features, fearlessness. All these things should be maintained. Thus, this DNA has to be preserved. So, the proper physiology, psychology is continued as a hereditary principle when this marriage takes place among the same qualified people. So, Kshatriya man, Kshatriya woman would marry. Brahmana man, Brahmana woman should marry. So, that the child who takes birth, he is also having such Brahmanical inclinations. And thus, it is easy for them to get trained. So, it is very simple, scientific system. So, to preserve, to preserve the genes, to preserve the species, specific mentality, because specific jobs can be very well undertaken by people of those mentalities, these kinds of marriages are done. And thus, people are also satisfied because the men, women have similar inclinations and they have got the same type of training.

#### marriage, anulom, pratilom, curse
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zJBroLaZLu8?t=544
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So she became very offended. She cursed Kacha. That although you have learned this art, but it will not be put into proper use."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses different types of marriages, including Anulom and Pratilom, and then recounts the story of Devyani and Kacha, including Devyani's curse on Kacha and Kacha's counter-curse.

But in some circumstances, marriage happens not between the same castes. And that is, they are of two types, Anulom and Pratilom. Anulom means that man belongs to a higher caste, Brahmana, and woman could be belonging to Kshatriya. That is also accepted. Just like Ardha Muni, he was a Brahmana, got married to Devahuti, the son of Manu, who was a Kshatriya. But Pratilom, man is Kshatriya, and that is not very congenial. That is not allowed as per Vedic culture. That will create disturbance in the society. But in this case, Devyani requested, please don't consider odds as per the Vedic rules and regulations for marriage. Because this is the arrangement of the providence. And then she explained how she got cursed by Kacha. Kacha is son of Brihaspati who went to get education from Shukracharya. Shukracharya was knowing a very special art of Mritsanjeevani. Any person who dies untimely or people die untimely death, that is not natural. So any person who dies untimely, they can be brought back to life. So Kacha learned this art under the direction of Brihaspati. And Shukracharya's, sorry, under the direction of Shukracharya, Brihaspati's son learned this art. And Shukracharya's daughter, Devyani, proposed for marriage. But then, he was taking Guru and Guru's family with great awe and reverence. Guru is on a very exalted position. So Kacha told, I cannot marry you. You belong to a very exalted, you are daughter of my Guru. So you are very elevated. I am fallen. I am, I belong to your servant category. So she became very offended. She cursed Kacha. That although you have learned this art, but it will not be put into proper use. And thus Kacha also counter cursed that you will never get a Brahmana husband. For no reason you are cursing like this. So thus she told, please see I can never get a Brahmana husband. So now it is arrangement of providence that you have come here and you have taken me out. Offer your hand. Now please accept me. So it is Kacha's duty to not to reject any proposal made by any woman. And thus, Yayaati accepted the proposal of Devyani.

**Summary:** The speaker explains the Vedic cultural context where many were inclined towards self-realization and sannyasa, leading to a higher proportion of unmarried women. Polygamy among Kshatriyas was a solution to ensure every woman had a husband.

So, in Vedic culture, because many, many people were inclined towards self-realization, just like we see the example of Yati. He could have become the king of entire planet, but he was very much inclined towards self-realization. And, and, kingdom of the entire planet was also not very attractive to him. Like this, the sons of Daksha, Daksha produced thousands of sons. And all of them wholesale were being converted into devotees, into sanyasis directly by Narad Muni. So, just imagine 10,000 men or less overnight. Now, who will find a match for women? So, thus in Vedic culture, it was, and in the nature's course also, women population is usually more than the male population. And more so, in the Vedic culture, when many males are going for sanyasis, now, every woman deserves a husband. So, the solution is, such people who can maintain many, many wives, like Kshatriyas, they were allowed to keep many wives. So, in this way, all the women were able to have a husband. Just like Vasudev, Krishna's father. When he married Devaki, he married all the other sisters also. I think they were six or seven in number, whatever. So, he married all of them. Sobhari Muni married all the 50 princesses. So, in this way, it was allowed because men were less to get married to women.

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of following religious principles and performing one's duty without attachment to the results, offering the fruits of action to Krishna, as taught in the Bhagavad Gita.

So, even though Shukracharya had warned, this is religion. Religion means even though death is certain, even though loss is certain, you have to follow, abide by the rules and regulations of the scriptures. Now, we do so much of calculation and despite all the calculation, there is always suffering in the life. So, we have to understand we are eternal beings. So, whatever good or bad has to happen for this life has been decided as per the activities of previous life. Now, in this life, if I am very religious, follow the rules and regulations of the Vedas, my next life would be glorious. If I am perfectly religious, what is perfection of religion? Savaipunsaam paro dharmo yatho bhaktir adhokshaje paro dharmo. The topmost religion is Bhakti of Adhokshaja, Krishna. So, if I am very religious, I will go back to Godhead, I will attain spiritual life. If not, I am able to attain this perfection of religion. At least, I can go to heavenly planets, have a sattvic body so that I don't degrade to animal species. So, thus, without caring for profit and loss, thus I request all of you, please do not care for profit and loss in this life. That we have to tolerate. Throughout the Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavatam, all the scriptures that are being mentioned, karmande vadikaraste ma phaleshu kadachana. You have right to do your duty. Duty you should do as per the scriptures. But the results one should offer to Krishna. Do not worry about the results. They have been pre-decided and they belong to Krishna.

So, thus king also became like a madman and without any shame, he started requesting his sons, you please take my old age, let me become young because I want to enjoy, I have not satisfied my lust. So, king approached the eldest of the sons, Yadu. And who is Maharaj Yadu? Maharaj Yadu is great. Yadu is a great devotee of Krishna. Yadu was a great devotee of Krishna and Lord Krishna was supposed to appear in the dynasty of Yadu. Thus, when Yadu was requested by Ayati to exchange his youth for his old age, Yadu told, my dear father, if a person doesn't enjoy, so he presented his decision very nicely. So, this is the way people are trained in Vedic culture. Even though you are denying, but you can present it in very proper words. So, he told that you have become old and timely and I am not very pleased with this old age which has come upon you. Because this material enjoyment, while the person is young, is usually required so that a person can renounce easily in the old age. And renunciation is the ultimate objective of human form of life. If a person is attached to the sense objects, life after life he has to take birth and suffer from unlimited diseases, old age, death and birth. And so that a person is able to cultivate the spirit of renunciation, material enjoyment is usually required while the person is young. Lord, who Supreme Lord is and depend on him directly. So, every person has got some authority. This spirit of dependence is cultivated. Don't remain absolutely independent. So, everybody has some authority in the Vedic culture. So, ultimate purpose of this material world is to take the living entities back to the spiritual world, to give them relief from unlimited anxieties, stress, birth, death, old age and disease. So, how this plan can be executed in this material world? This happens in the system of Varnashrama. In Varnashrama, the society is guided by the Brahmanas and the Vedas. So, how this plan can be executed in this material world? This happens in the system of Varnashrama. In Varnashrama, the society is guided by the Brahmanas and the Vedas. So, how this plan can be executed in this material world? This happens in the system of Varnashrama. In Varnashrama, the society is guided by the Brahmanas and the Vedas. And the Brahmanas are in constant touch with the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Their job is not to do any job ever, not to earn any money whatsoever, not to accumulate anything whatsoever, simply dependent upon Krishna. And either they will collect grains from the field which are left after the crops are harvested or they will go and beg in the house of householders so that there is an opportunity for householder to advance by giving them donations and they can preach Krishna consciousness to them. So, in such a way, Brahmanas would maintain themselves because they are free from all such lust, greed and other things. Brahmanas are worthy to be taken advice from. They have no personal vested interests. So, such exalted sages who have gradually moved towards attaining eternal life, being devotee of Krishna. And this is enacted with the help of a monarch, a proper administrator. So, Brahmanas guide the king. So, king can never violate the injunctions of the Brahmanas. Brahmanas are authorities for Kshatriyas. Then Kshatriya king is authority for all the subjects. The subjects can never deny the order of king. So, now everybody protests against everyone. The children are protesting against father. The subjects are protesting against the kings. This is not the Vedic culture. So, the order of king is supposed to be followed by the subjects. And among the subjects also, there are many households. So, in every household also, father is the head of the family and he is the authority for the whole institution. In this way, everybody has got authority to follow. For the employee, employer is the authority. In this way, everybody is told to follow some authority, cultivate the spirit of dependence. And in this way, the order of God percolates in the society.

**Summary:** The speaker explains that while obedience to authority is crucial, it can be superseded by the pursuit of Krishna consciousness, as exemplified by Yadu's refusal to obey his father's order, and Puru's agreement.

And if the king is not proper, then Brahmanas can remove the king also. Simply by cursing, they are so powerful. And it has happened in the history. So, thus, a son also is taught to be dependent on the father. A son can never deny the advice of the father. Then why Yadu Maharaj, such an exalted person, in whose dynasty Krishna chose to appear, he denied the instruction of his father only for the sake of Krishna consciousness. Because he understood if I become old and that would be impediment in Krishna's appearance in my dynasty. He was knowing Krishna is going to come. So, he did not want to delay this process. He was knowing devotees having extraordinary wisdom by the mercy of Krishna that my youthhood would come back and all these things will happen. But he did not want to delay the appearance of Krishna. He was eager to see Krishna descending in his dynasty. So, that Krishna's descent is not disturbed. Krishna's service is not disturbed. He disobeyed his father. So, this disobedience is not, it appears to be irreligious, but it is supremely religious. This is just like Kumaras define the order of Lord Brahma. Lord Brahma told the four Kumaras, his sons, to get married, but they were interested only in self-realization and they denied the order of Brahma, their father. And such a decision is not considered irreligious. Only for Krishna consciousness, we can disobey our authorities, but never otherwise. Otherwise, we have to always follow. So, Yadu, thus, for the sake of Krishna consciousness, denied the instruction of his father. And other sons, they were not knowing the religious principles that I should follow completely. So, they were not knowing the religious principles that I should follow completely, my father. Not worry that without any enjoyment, we will get old and die soon. You have to follow the father. They were not aware of the religious principles. So, they also denied. But Puru agreed. And he took the, and he told, nobody can ever settle the debt of the father. Because of father, we get this human body, which is very valuable for self-realization. So, I accept whatever order you give to me. And thus, he exchanged. He became old and father became young. ## HG Amitasana Dasa

### A Rich Man Who Lamented | HG Amitasana Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### Akshaya Patra, influence, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/M46NTi8DFIc?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So he introduced some industrialists to us in the initial days and somehow, you know, we got busy and he got busy and some of the connection for some time got lost."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces a story about an influential man who helped Akshaya Patra in its early days, highlighting his connections and eventual disconnection.

There was one gentleman who helped us in the initial days of Akshaya Patra. He was a very influential man. There is a committee at the national level which is chaired by the finance minister, and he was part of that committee, and many financial policies are reviewed, more so with respect to taxation in that committee. Since he was very, very influential and was very impressed by the Akshaya Patra thing, he would invariably speak to some industrialists, influential people, and he helped us a lot. Since he was in an influential position, he could call anybody, any industrialist. He could even call, let's say, Mr. Mukesh Ambani, because financial policies which the government makes impacts all businesses, so they will have some relations. So he introduced some industrialists to us in the initial days, and somehow, you know, we got busy and he got busy, and some of the connection for some time got lost. Then one day he calls a devotee and says, "Swamiji, when you come to Delhi, please try to visit my place," and the voice was very gloomy, sad. So the devotees tried to inquire, "How are you? Long time haven't met. Surely we'll definitely come to your place when we visit Delhi." It so happened that next few days when the devotees were visiting Delhi, they visited his place.

#### suffering, materialism, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/M46NTi8DFIc?t=137
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Swami ji, my last few years my life has become a hell."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the influential man's deteriorated condition and his lament about his life becoming a 'hell' despite his wealth and status.

Mind you, we're talking about not an ordinary person. Sometimes, you know, when an ordinary person faces a lot of difficulties, faces some challenges in life, he's not able to find a solution because he doesn't have money, cannot afford treatment, already is in a financial mess. Here we have an example of a person who is very influential among the, you know, top brass in the country, makes national level additions. So the devotees visited his house, the door was open, some servant came, and they invited the devotees inside. This person then comes in limping very, very slowly, and then he sits in the drawing room on the sofa. Some of the devotees who visited him could not, you know, make out just a few years back he was so much charged up, so full of enthusiasm, and here is completely failed, old. So then, in the course of the discussion, he says, "Swamiji," holds the hand and says, "Swami ji, you are all very close to Krishna. You all pray so much! When you pray, please pray that I die very soon." It was like never expected these kind of words from a person. She said, "What happened? Why are you saying so?" This is Swamiji, what to say, "My last few years my life has become a hell." He's living in a big palatial house in Delhi, servants are there, everything is there, all amenities are there.

#### suffering, materialism, conditional life, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/M46NTi8DFIc?t=259
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Money can buy medicines but cannot buy health."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the man's suffering due to health issues despite his wealth, and reflects on the limitations of material possessions in providing true happiness and freedom from suffering.

So my life has become a hell. So he says, "Somehow I have this severe backache, tried all kinds of treatments, continuously it pains, can't sleep, can't sit any time of the day, it pains. Now I am living for last few years on painkillers, and now a time has come even the painkillers have become ineffective because the body gets used to that's why many times the doctors prescribe a higher dose. Because of these painkillers which I've been taking every day, eight, ten, twelve tablets, all the organs are now because of side effects, after all it's chemical, it's not the body can't take it for a short while. So he says because of that I have gastric problems, all kinds of digestion problems, so life has become hell, Swamiji. I wonder what's the use of living like this." So these are the examples of how sometimes we get a feeling that yes, if somebody is rich, he can, he's that way blessed, he can afford treatment, he can get admitted in the best of hospitals, he can have, you know, but conditional life doesn't spare anybody. Prakriti sthani karshati. It's an illusion which makes us, no doubt money has a place, money can afford you, it's just like they say money can buy medicines but cannot buy health. Some sayings are there, money can buy house but not home, so there's a difference between the two. A lot of people living in palatial houses but there is no homeliness, there is no cordial relationship, the family members fights are going on. So this is the meaning of conditional life which, irrespective of how much bank balance you have, what status you have in the society, you have to go through. Hare Krishna. A warm welcome to all of you for today's live session. The topic for the day is Achieving Contentment. All of us without fail are looking for happiness, peace of mind, contentment, satisfaction. But in present times we see all of these things are for most of the people elusive. People are battling for these things in different ways. So in today's session we will understand from Gita's perspective what Lord Krishna has to say as far as achieving contentment, satisfaction and happiness is concerned. So we will start the discussion with prayers to Lord Shri Krishna, to Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, Srila Prabhupada to bless us so that we can have a very very meaningful, fruitful discussion with lot of wonderful insights which we can take and apply in our lives. So please join me. You can fold your hands and repeat these prayers along with me. Nama Om Vishnupadaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale Srimate Bhaktivedanta Svamin Nithinamine Namaste Saraswate Deve Gauravani Pracharine Nirvishesha Shunyam Nirmata Vys 앉wadi V assembhati Pashashamaz gaudya Rishashunyavadi Paashyatyade shatarinhe Jay Shri Krishna Chaitanya Krishnaya Krishna Chaitanya, Namne Gauratuse Namaha. Namo Brahmane Devaya, Go Brahmane Hithaya Cha, Jagad Hithaya Krishnaya, Govindaya Namo Namaha. He Krishna Karuna Sindho, Dina Bandho Jagatpate, Gopesha Gopika Kanta, Radha Kanta Tute, Tapta Kanchan Gaurangi, Radhe Vrindavaneshwari, Vrishabhanu Sute Devi, Pranamami Hari Bhakti. Satisfaction is concerned so that we remain focused and it's easy for us to recollect what we are doing. What we have discussed and also it is easy for us to remember things and apply them in our lives. The first aspect which we will cover today is to know the root cause of dissatisfaction in our lives.

#### senses, mind, material nature
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XI-G8rTjfrk?t=508
**Verse References:** BG 15.7
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In the Bhagavad Gita and our scriptures, the senses are compared to venomous serpents."
**Summary:** The speaker identifies the root cause of dissatisfaction as the uncontrolled senses and mind, explaining that we are parts of Krishna meant to be happy, but are battling material nature through our senses, which are likened to venomous serpents.

The root cause why most of the people are not experiencing contentment in life. So, the first point is know the root cause. Even in IT industry, in the software industry or for that matter even in the manufacturing field, whenever there is an outcome or a product which has malfunctioned, they try to do the root cause analysis. So, first let's do the root cause analysis. So, first let's do the root cause analysis as to why most of the people today are not experiencing contentment. In the Bhagavad Gita, Krishna talks about the senses and the mind extensively. Krishna says, mamayi amsho jiva loke, we are his amshas. We are his parents. We are his children. We are his parts. And just like Krishna is always happy, always blissful, always contented, always satisfied, we also as amshas, as parts are meant to be always happy, always satisfied. But that's not our present state. So, therefore Krishna says… mamayi amsho jiva loke! jivabhuta sanatana, we are eternal. But mana shashtha indriyani, mana means mind, shashtha indriyani, including the mind, mind and the five senses. Mana shashtha indriyani, prakriti, sthani, karshati, we are battling, we are waging a war with mother nature, material nature. It is like a chess game, the nature makes a move, we try to counter the move, just like now we have coronavirus challenge world over and the nature has retaliated in the form of misery and we are now fighting hard to circumvent that problem, to find a vaccine which will cure this. So, called viral infections or make us immune to them. And this battle goes on, we are waging a battle with material nature. So, mana shashtha indriyani with the mind and the five senses, totally six of them. So, the senses, extensively Krishna talks about, you know, we have the five senses, eyes, ears, nose, tongue, touch and we are trying to enjoy this world through our senses, by gratifying our senses. And our normal understanding of happiness is, what will make us happy if we can gratify our senses more and more, enjoy our senses more and more, eat the best of eatables. Although, you know, the body has limitations, we can't eat beyond a point, but the desire is that if we can eat more and more, more and more tasty things, we will have more and more pleasure and hence we will have more and more happiness. Now, in the Bhagavad Gita and our scriptures, the senses are compared to venomous serpents. Not just the serpents, not just the snakes, venomous serpents, poisonous serpents. In fact, it is mentioned that even a saintly person, this is mentioned in the seventh canto of the Srimad Bhagavatam, when Prahlad Maharaj, you know, Hrindakashyapu was killed by Narasimha Dev.

And the snake is even more dangerous because unlike other creatures, other animals which can make sound, which can make some kind of roar just like a tiger, you can see them approaching, you can hear them approaching, they make a sound, whereas a snake slithers, just moves, crawls without making a sound and can come and bite a person. And even kill a person. I remember when we were young, in our childhood days, we had, you know, we were living in a township and one of our friends, you know, we were playing together and he happened to go home just to change his dress, you know, after we play, you know, we get tired, we perspire, just take a bath, shower. And this person went home. And he was just stepping out of the house after having taken a bath and in the portico there was a car parked, his father's car. And as soon as he stepped out of the house, probably there was a snake underneath that car. He just bit that boy. And he, his body started becoming, you know, because of the venom. You know, his body was full of venom. Yes. he became unconscious and the body started becoming blue i don't know the full details cut the story short in next one hour when he was rushed to the hospital he was declared dead a young boy and at that time maybe 12 13 years old and died of a snake bite for no fault of his just stepped off the house snake was uh you know lying there underneath the car and then just hit him and he was dead the next one hour time so so the senses are compared to venomous serpents and the serpents generally you know if you have one snake which is venomous it's it's quite dangerous and here we have five senses in fact including the mind six mana shashtendriyani imagine you are in a room with six venomous serpents and they all slither they don't make a sound any moment it can bite how dangerous it will be so they are compared to venomous serpents.

**Summary:** The importance of sense control is emphasized, drawing a comparison to snake charmers who remove the poisonous fangs of cobras, and identifying lust as the venomous fang that needs to be removed to control the senses and mind.

We are waging a battle with the mother nature therefore in the vedic language literature right from the very beginning control of senses is emphasized in fact the first training a person would get is to control his mind control his senses in the brahmacharya ashram first 25 years was all about discipline control regulation and with that training even if a person enters into a grass the ashrama he will have the required knowledge to make the right choices as far as controlling the senses is concerned. So the venomous serpents are there and now snakes per se are not dangerous because as we all know a small percentage of the snakes are only venomous, majority of them are not poisonous snakes. But still by and large people are fearful of snakes because we do not know which snake would be poisonous and it bites and quite possible it can be even fatal. So but we see especially all of us have seen you know earlier there were snake charmers and people would go in a basket taking a cobra and you know and with that they will make that musical note and the cobra will play and they will collect some money, it is means of livelihood for some people and they would be carrying a cobra in a basket. But that person is poisonous. He is just playing around with that snake and after that whole display is over he just takes that cobra and holds it and puts in the basket again. Now what is the difference between why that person is relatively not fearful because although it is a cobra snake but that venomous fangs, venomous fangs which actually bites a person and transmits. That poison has been removed from that snake. In one sense if a snake is not poisonous or if that venomous fangs have been removed that snake we do not have to fear. Just like we do not fear with many animals, dogs, cats, so many animals. In that sense also snake can be harmless only thing what makes a snake dangerous is because of the venom. It bites and it can be poisonous and it can be fatal. But if you remove that poisonous fang then the snake need not be fear. So now we have the senses and the mind which are compared to venomous serpents, poisonous snakes. The question now is what is that venomous fangs? Which if we remove. What is that venomous fangs? If we remove we don't have to fear our senses. We don't have to fear our mind. Krishna in the Gita says mind if it is controlled is a best friend. The same mind if it is uncontrolled is a worst enemy. From the very beginning regulate your senses. It is lust only which is the all devouring sinful enemy of the living entity and then Krishna next verse says. Mind, intelligence are the sitting places of lust. First he talks about the greatest enemy and then he says where is this enemy situated? Where is he sitting?

It's in the mind and the senses, the intelligence. All the scams what we see in this world even the so-called rape cases, the Nirbhya case, the headlines. And of late lot of. So many of them. You know crimes are happening. It's if we do a root cause analysis it's because of our uncontrolled senses. And the understanding that if I gratify my senses gorgeously I'll be more happy. I'll be more satisfied. I will achieve contentment. A time will come I would have enjoyed so much with my senses to my heart's content that I'll be fully satisfied. I will experience contentment and that doesn't happen. Take the richest man in the world you think he's satisfied, he's contented, no. In fact it is said that lust it's like putting ghee in fire. The more you try to indulge, gratify the senses it's like putting ghee in the fire more the fire burns. It aggravates. So therefore the Vedic scriptures say from the very beginning regulate the senses, tapasya. Tapo divyam shudhyet brahma saukhyam tu anantam. Try to regulate the senses. How? By tapo divyam, by divine tapasya. Shudhyet which will purify our existence. And what will happen as a result? Brahma saukhyam tu anantam. We will experience real contentment. That's it. Real contentment. Real happiness.

**Summary:** The speaker transitions to a story about Srila Prabhupada in Mayapur, setting the scene with the early days of ISKCON and the presence of Western disciples who were new to the culture.

Once Srila Prabhupada was in Mayapur. This was in the early days of ISKCON. I think it was 1970s, 72. Srila Prabhupada had come back to India with his western disciples and some construction was going on in Mayapur. Mayapur is ISKCON world headquarters. It's very close to Calcutta. And right after Mayapur. Mayapur is ISKCON world headquarters. And right now, you know, it's a huge, it's a place with many, many buildings, you know, especially during Gaurapurnima, Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's birth and celebrations. It's generally in the March or April month. Devotees from all over the world thronged to this place, maybe thousands of them, lakhs of them. So at that time, there were only two buildings. One was the Lotus building. And the other was the Gaurapurnima. This was the first building and a long building, is the name of the building. So Prabhupada was staying in the second floor of that building and along with some of his western disciples who were travelling with Srila Prabhupada, Prabhupada had some devotees travelling with him so that he can orient them, train them, instruct them, give them association, spend time with them because they were completely new to this culture. Unlike us, we have heard about Gita, we know about Bhagavatam. For them, everything is alien, including the dress we are wearing. So the devotees were staying and then Prabhupada was in that second floor. There was many rooms and then some of the rooms were occupied by devotees and one of the rooms was occupied by Srila Prabhupada. And in that building on that floor, towards the end, there were some common bathrooms. Not every room had a separate individual bathroom, it was like a dormitory kind of accommodation.

Snake, snake and you know, Prabhupada was giving association to the devotees in one of the rooms and everyone heard him screaming and they all got up and they all came towards him. He says, I have seen a snake, sick, sweet, huge, big snake, it's there in the toilet and as usual there was lot of commotion. It's in the toilet. It's in the second floor and some of the devotees started wondering how did it come in the second floor. If it's in the ground floor, it's understandable. How did the snake come in the second floor? At that time, there was some enmity with, there was another Matha there, Gaudiya Matha, some God brothers of Srila Prabhupada. So some of them said, maybe Gaudiya Matha has planted the snake there out of enviousness, out of enmity. This reminds me example of Vishwamitra Muni. He was a very exalted sage. It says only 24 sages have a complete grip and understanding of the Gayatri Mantra because many of these mantras are very esoteric. Although they have mantras, have meaning, just like Bhagavad Gita, Krishna says it's most confidential knowledge. Just by knowing the verses, you don't know Gita. Just by mugging up Gayatri Mantra or chanting Gayatri Mantra does not mean that you actually know the depth of the meaning of that mantra. So he is the first one who knows the complete deep understanding of the Gayatri Mantra. So one time he was undergoing severe penance and austerity and he had taken a vow practically not to eat anything and was very just living on breathing. There is to take different kinds of austerities and penances and because of austerities and penances you at times get some siddhis, even to the extent that he was about to create a planet like that of Indra, Indraloka, his own planet, heavenly planet because of his siddhi, because of his tapasya. So Indra became rattled. He became fearful. What if he has, you know, he has a competitor, another so-called heavenly planet like Indraloka, and what if his power gets diminished? So he sent an Apsara called Menaka just to disturb this Vishwamitra Muni. And on one particular day Vishwamitra Muni was deep in his meditation and then he hears the ankle bells tingling. There was some disruption in his meditation. He opened his eyes and he saw this heavenly, beautiful damsel Menaka, completely charmed by the beauty. And Menaka is also celestial, sent by Indra. Through her powers had created all around, you know, a beautiful garden and nice cool breeze. And with that breeze, with that vayu, with that air blowing, you know, her dress, also, with that air blowing, you know, her dress, also, with that air blowing, you know, her dress, also, with that air blowing, you know, her dress, also, also, you know, and this Menaka, this Vishwamitra Muni when he opened his eyes, he could see this Menaka, this exquisite beauty, tinkling of ankle bells. So Manashashta Indriyani, the mind and the senses got agitated. Cut the story short, he, you know, broke his meditation, he enjoyed with Menaka and eventually we know that they were, Shakuntala was born from that copulation. #### senses, enjoying spirit, service to the Lord
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XI-G8rTjfrk?t=2066
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The poisonous fang, the poison in the senses is our enjoying spirit."
**Summary:** The speaker reiterates the analogy of the snake charmer, emphasizing that the poisonous fang is the enjoying spirit, and that we are meant to serve the Lord and enjoy in association with Him.

Just like we gave an example of a snake charmer who moves around with that snake in the basket and he is not worried, he just takes out the cobra with his hand and just places it there and starts moving, just, you know, plays that musical instrument and then after a while, he just puts it down. He puts it back in the basket, it's not worried, moves around with that basket. Why? Because he has removed that poisonous fangs from that cobra. Now what is that poisonous fangs? What is that poisonous fang? If we remove, then we do not have to worry about our senses, our uncontrolled mind. Okay. The poisonous fang, the poison in the senses is our enjoying spirit. We are amsa of the Lord, we are meant to serve the Lord, enjoy in association with the Lord. Just like these hands cannot enjoy independently. The hands have to serve the body, the whole, they are designed like that, they are part and parcel of the body. The hands puts the food in the mouth, it is enjoyed by the belly and the hands gets its due quota of nourishment or enjoyment. If the hands make plans for its independent enjoyment, selfish enjoyment and decides not to put the food in the mouth but enjoy independently. The whole body will suffer and even the hands will not get the required nourishment. So these senses are meant to serve the senses of the Lord. Hrishikesha Hrishikesha Sevanam Bhaktir Uchyate, when we serve the master of the senses, Hrishikesha, Lord, with our senses, that is nothing. Okay. Okay. Okay. So, this is nothing but Bhakti. Krishna is not being impractical. He says, okay, the senses are like venipa's serpents. So just you know, you bind them and don't use at all. The senses have some right role to play and that right role is to enjoy by serving the Lord. By giving pleasure to the Lord. Okay. So, the sense of the Lord, there is something called as rightful enjoyment.

---
#### rightful enjoyment, devotion, contentment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XI-G8rTjfrk?t=2239
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Selfish enjoyment, unrestricted enjoyment, unregulated enjoyment will lead to suffering and bondage."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that rightful enjoyment involves keeping the Lord in the center, offering food to the Lord and relishing the remnants as prasadam, and that selfish, unrestricted enjoyment leads to suffering.

Like for example, now eating, the sense of tongue. A lot of pain, agony to innocent animals. It may be tasty for the tongue. But a devotee can eat. But a devotee does not do so. He restricts his tongue by offering the food which is worth offering to the Lord and relishing the remnants of the food as prasadam. So, enjoying the tongue is not negated. Nobody is saying, do not enjoy nice foodstuff. But the process is, keep Lord in the center. Selfish enjoyment, unrestricted enjoyment, unregulated enjoyment will lead to suffering and bondage. This very source of enjoyment, so-called enjoyment, that's why it's called maya, that's why it's called illusion, is the root cause of our misery. Root cause of our dissatisfaction. Root cause of our unhappiness. Why we are not happy? Why we are not satisfied? In spite of person, you take a person who is earning 5 lakh rupees a month, he is also not satisfied. Sometimes we think that, okay, maybe because I am not earning enough, I am earning 20,000, 30,000, it's not enough to lead a decent life. But the people who are earning 5 lakh rupees a month, 10 lakh rupees a month, they are also not satisfied. Because? Uncontrolled. Mind, uncontrolled senses. Never satisfied.

---
#### hankering, lamenting, self-realization
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XI-G8rTjfrk?t=2361
**Verse References:** BG 18.54
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Brahma bhuta prasanna atma na shocchati na kankshati. Shocchati means lamenting or hankering."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the cycle of hankering for what we don't have and lamenting for what we lose, and contrasts this with the state of self-realization, where one is joyous irrespective of circumstances.

Always longing for more, more and more. Hankering for what we don't have and lamenting for what slips away from our hands. Brahma bhuta prasanna atma na shocchati na kankshati. Shocchati means lamenting or hankering. So present state with our uncontrolled senses. Uncontrolled senses. Uncontrolled mind, uncontrolled senses is we are always hankering for what we don't have and lamenting for what time takes us, takes away from us, slips away from us. It could be wealth, it could be our near and dear ones, it could be relationships, it could be things, it could be situations, it could be circumstances. And we hanker and we lament. And therefore, this contentment, happiness, satisfaction, is always elusive. Brahma bhuta prasanna atma na shocchati na kankshati. If we are Brahma bhuta, if we are self-realized, prasanna atma, we'll be joyous, we'll be happy. Irrespective of circumstances. You can have everything in the world, still be unhappy, not satisfied. Discontented. And you can have practically bare minimum and you can be very happy and satisfied.

## HG Amitasana Dasa

### ACHIEVING PEACEFUL MIND | HG AMITASANA DAS | GITA LIFE Live
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### peace, Bhagavad Gita, prayers
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FXBujCZFnQY?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Peace is a common denominator in lives of all the people."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa introduces the topic of achieving a peaceful mind, highlighting its universal desire and relevance, and sets the stage for a discussion based on the teachings of the Bhagavad Gita, beginning with prayers.

A warm welcome to all of you for today's Geeta Live session. The topic for the day is Achieving Peaceful Mind. Everyone of us are wanting peace in life. There are many things in life which may not be common to everyone. For example, some people may be very, very passionately pursuing, pursuing wealth. Some people may be very passionately pursuing some other goals. But one thing for sure, everyone wants peace. In fact, many people, although they may be passionately pursuing wealth, they have in their mind that once I become financially secure, I will retire and then lead a peaceful life. So, peace is a common denominator in lives of all the people. They all are aspiring for it, wanting it, chasing it, wanting to experience it. But we all know, many a times, peace is not only a form of wealth, but it's also an estranged life. So, peace is a common denominator. But we all know, many a times, peace is not only a form of wealth, but it's also a form of life. is elusive. Instead of we having peace, our life at times is in pieces. So, we will have the discussion based on teachings of Lord Shri Krishna in the Bhagavad Gita on this particular topic. We will start the discussion with prayers. I request all of you to kindly fold your hands. Please join me in offering the prayers so that we can seek the blessings of Prabhupad, our Founder Acharya, our beloved spiritual master, Lord Shri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, Panchatattva, Lord Shri Krishna and with their blessings we get into this understanding. So please join me in offering the prayers.

#### peace formula, Bhagavad Gita, Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FXBujCZFnQY?t=323
**Verse References:** BG 5.29
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "So, what is the formula for peace which Krishna is sharing with us in the Bhagavad Gita?"
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa introduces Krishna's peace formula from the Bhagavad Gita, contrasting it with other approaches and emphasizing the authority of the Supreme Creator.

So, how do we go about achieving the peace, which we all find is elusive. Some of us may be blessed with a peaceful mind, a peaceful life. So, Krishna in the Bhagavad Gita has given us a peace formula. You know, many times we have formulas. Formula for making a particular drug. You know, mix different chemicals. Formula for making, let's say, a soap or a toothpaste. Formula for making a Pepsi. It seems it's quite a secret formula. And if you know the formula, you can make that particular item. So, what is the formula for peace which Krishna is sharing with us in the Bhagavad Gita? Mind you, many, many people have talked about peace, have written about peace. There have been peace conferences organized. There have been missions trying to bring about peace in this world. Now, let's hear from the Supreme Creator. The One who has created this world. The One who has created us. What He has prescribed for us to achieve peace. Mind you, many, many prescriptions which are there in the marketplace, in the books, written by very eminent authors, can have flaw. Can have some lacuna in their understanding. May not be a sure shot way for achieving peace. So, what is the peace formula? So, always we have been focusing on three aspects, three principles. The first principle today we are going to discuss about the peace formula. Let's learn this formula and see if we can apply this formula in our lives and achieve peace.

#### analogy, body, enjoyment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FXBujCZFnQY?t=473
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Bhokta means, I am the enjoyer. I am the real enjoyer."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa begins explaining the first principle of the peace formula, 'Bhoktaram yajnatapasam,' meaning 'I am the enjoyer,' and illustrates this with a story about the limbs of the body going on strike against the stomach.

So, Krishna in the Gita says, bhoktaram yajnatapasam sarvaloka maheshwaram suhridam sarvabhutanam nyatvamam shantim rachati bhoktaram yajnatapasam Bhokta means, I am the enjoyer. I am the real enjoyer. This reminds me of a story of the limbs going on strike. In our body we have many limbs, hands, legs, the mouth, genitals. We have also the belly which actually does not move. All the other limbs are quite active in the sense that they are moving. Like the hand you see, it's moving. My mouth is speaking. And after this class I will have to walk back to the place. So I use my legs. So throughout the day various limbs are active doing different things. My eye is busy accumulating visual stimuli so that I can make sense and act based on what my eyes have captured. So one day they all got together. They had a meeting. In fact a secret meeting. Where they said this is not done. It's a great injustice to all of us. We all work the whole day. Morning to night. And this stomach just sits there and does nothing. In fact All the hard work what we put in. In fact the hand started saying the food which we pick up and put in our mouth. And the legs said all the hard work we do morning to night. All the walking I do. The stomach sits there. Does nothing. And simply enjoys. So they all got together and said it's not done. It's a great injustice. This cannot happen. We will not allow this to happen. And they all decided. To go on strike. Why strike? Just like the strikes happen. The workers do strike to teach management a lesson. To bring them on the right path. So here the limbs went on strike so that they can teach a lesson to the stomach. Now you see how you can enjoy. If we do not work. Let us see how you will enjoy. So they all went on strike. And the hand said I will not do anything. I will not write anything. I will not pick up anything. The legs said I will not walk. We are not going to put any food in the mouth. If at all we get food we will try to enjoy independently. And all the organs, all the limbs were in agreement. Sometimes you know when there is a union and there is some small section of people. Who are not in agreement. Who don't want to participate in strike. But here it was a clear majority. All of them unanimous decision. Yes we should teach stomach a lesson. And they all went on a strike.

---
#### analogy, body, interdependence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FXBujCZFnQY?t=688
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Looks like we have done a big blunder."
**Summary:** The story continues with the limbs suffering from weakness due to the strike, realizing their mistake, and resuming their functions, illustrating the interdependence of the body's parts.

So one day passed. Two days passed. No food was put in the mouth. And the stomach also was nothing there to digest. And all the limbs among themselves were discussing. And were quite happy. That very soon the stomach is going to learn the lesson. But it so happened that while the food was not being put in the mouth. Even all the limbs also started feeling some kind of weakness. Some kind of lack of nourishment. They started wondering. What is happening? And then the hand realized. Okay maybe we are not getting food. So the hand took up some. Rasgulla. And started squeezing. That rasgulla. So that all the nutrients. The minerals. The vitamins. Whatever is there we get from the food. Which is required for the nourishment of our cells. For the nourishment of the body. So that the hand can assimilate that from the food directly. But a time came when the hand even did not have the energy. To even squeeze that rasgulla. To pull out some kind of nutrients from it. And all the organs. All the limbs slowly. Little by little. Little by little became completely weak. Could not even move. So they all somehow got together. And said. Looks like we have done a big blunder. Looks like our understanding. Of how we are going to get nourished. Was wrong. And next day they called up the strike. And as usual the food was put in the mouth. And all the organs started doing their respective functions. The stomach started digesting the food. Which was put in the mouth. And all the organs started getting their respective nourishment. Now here one may say. Is it. Unfair. That all the limbs are working so hard. That they are not getting the nutrients. That they are not getting the nutrients. They are working so hard. But they are not enjoying the food directly. We have never in our life. Thought that this is unfair. It is very much acceptable fact to us. That each part of the body. Each limb of the body. Is working holistically for the whole. For the whole. Which is the complete body. And when each limb does its respective function. Then as per the design. Design of this body. Design of this mind body system. Different limbs get their respective quota of nourishment.

---
#### analogy, service, enjoyment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FXBujCZFnQY?t=869
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "We are his amshas. And amshas the parts are meant to serve the whole."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa connects the story to the concept of serving the Lord, explaining that just as the limbs serve the body, we are parts of the Lord and are meant to serve Him, ultimately leading to our own nourishment and happiness.

Something very similar is the case of. How the whole system. Our world has been designed by the Lord. For us to be happy. It may look that this is not done. This is not fair. But Krishna in the Gita says. We are his amshas. We are his parts. Just like hand is a part of this body. Limbs are the parts of the body. We are his amshas. And amshas the parts. Are meant to serve the whole. Imagine this hand does not function. And there is severe serious infection happening. And it stops functioning. And that infection starts spreading. Who knows the doctor may say. This hand is no longer useful. In fact we may have to amputate the hand. So likewise. We all are amshas of the Lord. And we have been designed. To serve the whole. The Lord being the whole. The Lord being the supreme creator. The Lord being. The supreme absolute truth. We are his amshas. And we have been designed. To serve the whole. To give pleasure to the whole. Now somebody may wonder. Why it is like that. Why the Lord is so mean. That he wants to enjoy. And he expects us to serve him. Same question we can ask about our limbs. But we never ask that question. It is accepted fact. So long as these are limbs of my body. They are meant to serve the whole. And it is not a master servant relationship. Where the servant is simply serving the master. The master is simply enjoying. And the poor servant is just starving to death. No. When the servant serves the master. He in turn gets his quota of nourishment. His quota of happiness. In fact if you see the servants. In houses of rich aristocratic people. They may be cooking very. So called elaborate dishes. But I am sure. After the master have honored the dishes. They also enjoy the same.

---
#### devotion, reciprocity, enjoyment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FXBujCZFnQY?t=1059
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Lord is enjoying the company of his devotees. And reciprocating that service by serving his devotees."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa explains that the Lord enjoys the service of His devotees and reciprocates by serving them, using the example of Arjuna and Krishna, and emphasizes that serving the Lord leads to our own enjoyment.

In the case of the Lord. It is slightly different. In fact. The design is such like. That we are meant to serve the whole. The Lord. And the Lord in turn. Is busy with only one thing. He has many many energies. To take care of universal affairs. He is simply enjoying the service of his devotees. And reciprocating that service. By serving his devotees. Sometimes we have an understanding of the Lord. Who is the supreme creator. He is the maintainer. He is the annulator. So he must be very very busy. Just like you know. You have sometimes big business men. They are very busy. You take an appointment. You have to struggle for days together. Now we extrapolate that. Imagine the Lord. Who is not just managing. A particular company. Not just a country. Not just one particular continent. Not just one universe. Many many universes. Material and spiritual. And the Lord. Has innumerable energies. Which is effortlessly managing everything. And what is the Lord doing? Lord is enjoying the company of his devotees. Who are serving him. And. In turn. He is busy serving his devotees. Just like Arjuna. Decided to serve the Lord. And the Lord in turn served his devotees. By taking a menial position of a chariot driver. So. When we serve the Lord. Just like the limbs. Serve this body as a whole. And they get the required nourishment. Likewise. When we serve the Lord. When we please the Lord. When we give enjoyment to the Lord. We get our quota of enjoyment. That's why Krishna in the Gita says. Bhokta Ram Yagya Tapasam. I am the Bhokta. I am the enjoyer. When the limbs try to enjoy independently. Without serving the whole. It cannot enjoy. What is lust? Lust means desire to enjoy. Independent of Lord. Because of lusty desires. Because of material desires. We make independent plans of enjoyment. And. We do not get enjoyment. In fact. Because of those plans. We attract. Miseries in our life. Yehi. Samrsparsha. Jaya. Jaya. Jaya. Jaya. Jaya. Jaya. Jaya. Jaya. Jaya. Jaya. Jaya. Jaya. Jaya. Jaya. Jaya. Jaya. Jaya. Jaya. Jaya. Jaya. Jaya. Jaya. Jaya. For little pleasure. Which we may get. By being enjoyer. We. Attract. Lot of miseries in our life. In fact. We attract lot of mess in our lives. Lot of anxiety in our lives. And the peace of mind which we all are striving for. Becomes completely elusive. So, bhoktaram yagyatapasam. So, Krishna says, one who understands that I am the enjoyer. So, there's nothing wrong in enjoying life.

#### enjoyment, spirituality, bliss
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FXBujCZFnQY?t=1241
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Genuine spirituality means enjoyment, but enjoyment with proper understanding of the things."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa clarifies that spirituality is not against enjoyment but involves enjoying with the Lord in the center, giving examples of food and music in their tradition, and explains that we are meant to be blissful but have violated the Lord's original plan.

Prabhupada was asked, what's the goal of your movement? Prabhupada said, the goal of our movement is to enjoy life. Sometimes people think spirituality is against enjoyment. Spirituality is all about giving up. Giving up enjoyment, giving up luxuries, giving up money, giving up wealth, giving up family members, renunciation. No. Genuine spirituality means enjoyment, but enjoyment with proper understanding of the things. Enjoyment keeping Lord in the center. For example, here in our institution, we all enjoy nice foodstuff. We don't eat only bland food without salt, without any spices. We eat normal food. But all the food we eat is offered to the Lord and we accept the remnants as prasadam. Our day starts with singing and dancing. A lot of people don't like singing. They like to dance. And there is a lot of enjoyment in singing and dancing. Our day at 4.15 starts with singing and dancing. Susukum kartum avyayam. Krishna in the Gita says, Rajvidya Rajguhyam. This path is, this knowledge is a king of knowledge. It's the most confidential knowledge. Susukum kartum avyayam. It is full of joy and happiness. We are amsa of the Lord. Sat Chit Ananda. We are meant to be blissful. That's what we constitutionally are. But if we introspect, today we are in anxiety. We do not have peace of mind. We are entangled. We are not happy. We are not blissful.

#### formula, violation, enjoyment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FXBujCZFnQY?t=1359
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "So we enjoy. Enjoyment is not denied but keeping Lord in the centre."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa states that we have violated the original plan of the Lord, like not following a formula or prescription, and emphasizes the importance of keeping the Lord in the center for true enjoyment, using our senses in relation to the Lord.

Somewhere we have violated. We have violated the original plan of the Lord. Just like when you have a formula and you do not follow the formula. You have a prescription from a doctor and you do not follow the prescription. For sure we will not recover. For sure we will not make the end product correctly. So likewise Krishna, the most knowledgeable, the most intelligent person in this world, person in this creation, in fact the one who has created this world, has given us a formula. Bhokta Aram Yagya Tapasam. So we enjoy. Enjoyment is not denied but keeping Lord in the centre. We enjoy nice melodious music in relation with the Lord. We eat nice palatable foodstuff after having offered that to the Lord. First giving pleasure to the Lord. We see beautiful forms. But the forms which are within the framework allowed by the Lord. There are many things which gives pleasure to us. Pleasure to our eyes. But it could degrade the soul. It could pull the soul down. We can see the beautiful forms of the deities. Beautiful pictures of the Lord. Which can give pleasure to us. Pleasure to our eyes. And also elevate the soul. All our senses can be used in relation with the Lord. Serving the Lord. And by giving pleasure to the Lord we get our desired quota of happiness. So first principle which we discussed is the peace formula. And today we have taken the first aspect of that peace formula. I want all of you to help me. What is that aspect of the peace formula? It is Bhokta Ram. Bhokta Ram Yagya Tapasam. I am the enjoyer. So the peace formula. The first principle is the Lord is the enjoyer.

**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public

**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa summarizes the first principle, emphasizing the importance of keeping the Lord as the prime focus in our lives, developing love and attachment to Him, and reads a verse from the Bhagavad Gita about knowing Krishna as the ultimate beneficiary and Supreme Lord.

So if we keep Lord as the prime focus in our life. Develop our love for Him. Attachment to Him. We will never be frustrated. We will never get into miseries or anxieties in our lives. So this is the first thing which I want to share. I will just read out one verse from the Bhagavad Gita. This is in the 5th chapter. 29th verse. Where Krishna says. A person in full consciousness of me. Which is nothing but Krishna consciousness. Knowing me to be the ultimate beneficiary of all sacrifices. Bhokta Ram Yagya Tapasam. The Supreme Lord of all planets and demigods. The Supreme Lord of all planets. He is the Sarvaloka Maheshwaram. He is the real proprietor. Everything is owned by Him. He is the true owner. Benefactor and well wisher of all living entities. One who understands this. He will become peaceful.

#### Nirmama, detachment, illusion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FXBujCZFnQY?t=1577
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Nir means not. Mama means mine."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa introduces the second principle, 'Nirmama,' meaning 'not mine,' explaining that we come empty-handed and leave empty-handed, but fall into the illusion of 'I and mine' under the influence of Maya.

The second principle which we are going to talk today. Is related to the verse which just now I chanted. Bhokta Ram Yagya Tapasam. Sarvaloka Maheshwaram. And the principle is. Please remember this. I will be asking you to. Pen it down in the live chat. To type it out later on. And the principle is. Nir Mama. Nir means not. Mama means mine. We somehow in this world. Come empty handed. Not somehow in the sense. We come empty handed. And all of us. Irrespective of how much wealth we have amassed. All of us without exception. Will go empty handed. Nobody has taken anything with him. Any of his degrees. Any of his wealth. They have gone back empty handed. But. But while we are here. Janasya moho yam aham ameti. What is aham ameti? We fall in the illusion. Under the influence of Maya. Aham ameti. I and mine.

#### attachment, renunciation, Nirmama
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FXBujCZFnQY?t=1670
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Nirmama is to cultivate the understanding that it is not mine. It is Lord's."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa elaborates on the concept of 'I and mine,' discussing how we develop attachments to people and things, and defines 'Nirmama' as cultivating the understanding that everything belongs to the Lord, which is a form of renunciation.

Before I took birth. The land was there. I come in this world. And. Develop attachment. Under the influence of illusion. This land is mine. This property is mine. He is my brother. She is my mother. Janasya moho yam aham ameti. I and mine. We develop attachment to people. To things. Considering them. It's mine. I and mine. We get attached to this body. So what is the concept of Nirmama? Nirmama is. To cultivate the understanding that. It is not mine. It is Lord's.
