#### renunciation, spiritual principles, false renunciation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FXBujCZFnQY?t=1771
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Such vairagya. Such renunciation is called Falgu vairagya. Or false renunciation."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa discusses renunciation in various religions and emphasizes that genuine spiritual practice involves purification and dissolving wrong perceptions, leading to the understanding that everything belongs to the Lord, and defines 'Falgu vairagya' as false renunciation.

One level of renunciation. Jnana and Vairagya. Every religion if you talk about. Every scripture of the world. Talks about renunciation. Even in Islam. You have. You know the fasting. And then Ramzan. And Eid. Kurbani. Even in Christianity. Every religion of the world. Talks about. Renunciation. Taithing. Giving up 10% of what you earn to the Lord. To the church. Jnana and Vairagya. To give up. So that we can. Cultivate. Nirmama. It is not mine. So this Nirmama. When we. Practice. Genuine spiritual principles. Purification. Anartha Nivritti. All that illusion. All that wrong perception. All the wrong ideas. What we have. Slowly starts dissolving. And one comes to the right understanding. That it all belongs to the Lord. So that's why. In the verse which we chanted. Krishna is saying. One who understands. That the Lord is the true proprietor. Everything I have. Including my body. Is made of material elements. It's been given to me. After I leave. The soul leaves. This material elements will be dismantled. I do not take. I do not take. This body along with me. So this has been given to me. The intelligence has been given to me. The strength has been given to me. Some skills have been given to me. My abilities have been given to me. A dead man has no abilities. It is coming from the Lord. And when we recognize. And understand that everything belongs to the Lord. So renunciation first is. To give up. It's not mine. It's half job done. There are lot of faiths which talk about renunciation. It is not mine. And to give up. And the most important aspect of renunciation is. It's not mine. But it is Lord's. There is a verse which says. Praapanchitika buddhya. Praapanchitika buddhya means. When we think this worldly objects. Buddhya. With this mentality. Hari sambandhi vastuna. All the objects have some sambandha. Have some relationship with the Lord. But if we think that they are material. There is no sambandha with the Lord. And if we have that mentality. Hari sambandhi vastuna. Parityago. If we give up such objects. Thinking that they have no relationship with the Lord. Money is Maya. And we give up money. Thinking that it is Maya. Falgu vairagya kathyate.

---
#### renunciation, materialism, understanding
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FXBujCZFnQY?t=1939
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "What kind of renunciation is this? What kind of sadhu is this?"
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa shares his initial difficulty in reconciling the typical understanding of a sadhu with devotees using laptops and driving cars, highlighting the difference between true renunciation and superficial renunciation.

I remember when I was. Very new to Christianity. Krishna consciousness. I had started visiting temple in Bangalore. As I mentioned in my previous. Gita live session. After the office hours. Every day in the evening. I would go to the temple. And. One of those early days. When I visited the temple. I was given one seva. One devotee took me to his cabin. And he said I have something to type. Can you help me. I think some festival was coming. Some. You know. Passes were to be sent. And some address labels were to be created. And I was supposed to type something. And there I saw in the devotees cabin. A computer. In fact. The computer was there. Because he was in charge of IT. Taking care of all the printing needs of the temple. And he also had a laptop. It was very difficult for me to reconcile. Typical understanding of a sadhu means. You know. He should be poverty stricken. Living in very simple surroundings. No gadgets. And here this person. Supposed to be a sadhu. Wearing saffron robes. And having a laptop. What kind of renunciation is this? What kind of sadhu is this? Typical understanding of a sadhu means. He should have a kamandaloo. He should have. You know. Clothes. And even the clothes should not be iron. Clothes should be tattered. You know. Some hole should be there. I mean. The more tattered the clothes. The more is the renunciation of that sadhu. That's the typical understanding. So for me it was little difficult to reconcile. How can they be sadhu and still be using a laptop? And then. Again. The next day when I was coming to the temple. I used to visit everyday. I saw one of the devotees. Driving a car. Coming up the temple. I said. My god. Here we have another sadhu driving a car. It was very difficult initially for me to reconcile. How can they be renunciants. They be sadhus. They be in renounced order of life. And still be driving a car. Still having a laptop. So that's why the right understanding is.

---
#### energy, service, consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FXBujCZFnQY?t=2079
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "When we understand the entire energy, the material energy is Lord's energy. It has relationship with the Lord. And that energy when utilized in the service of the Lord is no longer a material energy."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa explains that true renunciation involves understanding the relationship between material energy and the Lord, and utilizing that energy in His service, giving examples of Raja Rishis and Parikshit Maharaj.

If we have the objects and we give up thinking them as material without knowing the relationship with the Lord, such renunciation is called Falgu Vairagya, false renunciation. When we understand the entire energy, the material energy is Lord's energy. It has relationship with the Lord. And that energy when utilized in the service of the Lord is no longer a material energy. For example, I am using a microphone here. This microphone is being used in the service of the Lord to talk about the Lord, to present. This is the message of Bhagavad Gita. This microphone is no longer material. I can use this microphone without having to worry about that I am using material things and hence I might get attached and get entangled. So there were Raja Rishis, Parikshit Maharaj, Bharat Maharaj, Amrish Maharaj, they were living in palaces amidst opulence, Pandavas. But although they were amidst material opulence, living in palaces among comforts, they were great devotees of the Lord because they had the consciousness Nirmama. That's why Parikshit Maharaj, although he was the emperor of the whole world, when he was cursed, he got to know that in seven days he is going to leave his body. Without even thinking about it. Without even thinking for a moment. Because he was not attached. It is all Lord's. He renounced his kingdom, renounced all the luxuries, all the comfort, went to the bank of river Ganges and for next seven days, without even eating, without even worrying about any of those material comforts, just heard Srimad Bhagavatam and left his body. If we were to be given seven days notice, we will... In the next seven days, we will be busy settling accounts, you know, just writing our wills and taking care of all the properties and all those things. All our near and dear ones. But they were Nirmama, it is not mine, it is Lord's.

---

## HG Amitasana Dasa

### AIM OF HUMAN LIFE | HG AMITASANA DAS | GITA LIFE Live
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### purpose of life, prayer, blessings
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/tF-58Tj0uME?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Over and above all these material goals, aspirations, aims, we have a much higher purpose, much bigger purpose, overall purpose for this human form of life."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the topic of the aim of human life, emphasizing a purpose beyond material goals, and begins with prayers seeking blessings.

A warm welcome to all of you for today's Gita live session. The topic for the day is the aim of human form of life. This is going to be a very, very interesting discussion because it has to deal with the overall purpose of our life. Every one of us have some goals, some aspirations. We want to achieve some things in life. But from the Bhagavad Gita, from our Acharyas, we get a very, very important perspective. Over and above all these material goals, aspirations, aims, we have a much higher purpose, much bigger purpose, overall purpose for this human form of life. So we are going to discuss this today in detail. Before we start the discussion, let us start with prayers, seeking blessings. Blessings of Prabhupada, Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, the previous Acharyas, Lord Shri Krishna, so that by their mercy, by their grace, by their blessings, we can have a meaningful discussion today. So please join me in offering the prayers. You can also fold your hands and you can repeat the prayers along with me. Nama Om Vishnupadaya Krishna Pradaya.

---
#### purpose of life, analogy, self-awareness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/tF-58Tj0uME?t=262
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "There is some similarity between that person and our own lives. In that sense, we are also off."
**Summary:** The speaker narrates an experience of encountering a lost person on a train, drawing a parallel to how people are often unaware of the purpose of their own lives.

I remember many years back I was travelling on a train. Next to me was a person. He looked quite weird, looked a little lost. You can make out from the dealings, the facial expressions, the body language. Something was not okay with him. And since it was a long journey, I think I was travelling to Vrindavan, since it was a long journey, so I became a little curious and I asked this person. Where are you coming from, where have you boarded the train from? So he looked at me and seemed to be having no answer. He was lost. I asked him the next question, where are you going, where are you heading? Again he looked at me, thought for a while. So I became even more curious. This person does not know where is he coming from, where has he boarded the train from, where is he going to get down, what is his destination. As a last attempt I said, where are you travelling, why are you travelling, what's the purpose of your travel. Again he seems to be lost. There was no proper answer. If you were in my position, what would you think about such a person? Later on, I realized that this person was mentally off. And there was this relative of his who was also traveling in the same gogi. Later on, I got to know. The seats were quite apart. And I met him in the morning. And the doubt I had became very... He also said that yes, he has been going through this challenge. Now, many a times, if we analyze our own life, here we can see a person who has no idea of where he has boarded the train from, where he is going. What is the purpose of this? There is some similarity between that person and our own lives. In that sense, we are also off.

---
#### purpose of life, self-inquiry, knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/tF-58Tj0uME?t=444
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "But a very fundamental aspect of our existence as to who we are, where we were before our birth."
**Summary:** The speaker shares a story about a saintly person questioning a man about his origins, highlighting the lack of fundamental knowledge about our existence before birth.

There was once a saintly person who was giving a discourse. He was visiting a particular town. And after the discourse got over, generally people come and, you know, show some respect, offer obeisances and take the blessings. So, one gentleman approached this saintly person and said, Swamiji, can you please visit my home? Generally, in India, we have the tradition that saintly people visit the homes and sanctify the homes just by their presence, by a visit. So, he invited Swamiji, can you please visit my place? So, Swamiji asked this person, where do you stay? So, he said that, you know, I am staying in New Delhi. So, Swamiji asked, is that your home? He says, yes. So, Swamiji asked, how long you have been staying in Delhi? So, he said, for last five years. Swamiji asked this person, where were you staying before you moved to Delhi? He said, before we moved to Delhi, we were actually staying in Amritsar. So, Swamiji asked the next question, where were you staying before you moved to Amritsar? So, the person became little perplexed. You know, Swamiji is asking plethora of questions without knowing the reason behind why Swamiji is asking these questions. So, this man replied, before Amritsar, we were staying in Punjab, Sindh. Before partition, my great-grandparents, grandparents were from Pakistan. It seems to be, Swamiji asked the next question, where were you before Pakistan, Sindh? Before you were born there, where were you? So, this person now did not have a proper answer. He says, I do not know. Let me ask my mother and tell you. Then Swamiji told, our schools and colleges teach us many many things, many many subjects. But a very fundamental aspect of our existence as to who we are, where we were before our birth. All of us have taken birth. We all have coordinates, just like we have Google coordinates for a particular place. We all have some coordinates, I am so and so, Indian, born in so and so place, gender, we are clear. We all have coordinates. But if you analyze, these are temporary coordinates. At best, we know the coordinates of this life. Where we were before this birth, we have no idea. Where we are heading, what happens to us after death, we have no idea.

#### purpose of life, scriptural understanding, success
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/tF-58Tj0uME?t=640
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Our scriptures give us profound understanding, clear understanding what is the goal of human form of life."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the consistent answers received when asking about the purpose of travel with the varied responses when inquiring about the purpose of life, emphasizing the clear understanding provided by scriptures.

What is the purpose of our lives? You ask this question to 10 different people, 10 learned people, educated people, intelligent people. I guarantee you, you will get 10 different answers. When we are travelling in a train from point A to point B, if you ask this question, why are you travelling? What is the purpose of this travel? You will get some consistent answers. I am travelling from Bangalore to Vrindavan. What is the purpose of travel? To reach from point A to point B. But you ask this question, what is the purpose of your life? You ask this question to 10 different people, you will get 10 different answers. We all have our own understanding of what is purpose of life, what is goal of life, what is aim of our life. Our scriptures give us profound understanding, clear understanding what is the goal of human form of life. Especially human form of life. We are not eternally human beings. beings, we are Atma, many many lives we have taken. In each life we get a different body and our scriptures point out that there are 84 lakh species of life, 84 lakh Yonia. And after many many lives of transmigration in different life forms, plants, birds, aquatics, reptiles, finally a living entity gets a human form of life and our scriptures give us a very very clear understanding of what is the purpose of human form of life, what is the goal of human form of life, when can we say that we have led a meaningful and successful lives. Again mind you, the definition of success, the definition of what is meaningful life varies from person to person but let us understand

#### human birth, spiritual opportunity, sense gratification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/tF-58Tj0uME?t=941
**Verse References:** SB 11.9.29
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Only the Atma gets an opportunity to take a human birth."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Srimad Bhagavatam to emphasize the rarity and importance of human birth, highlighting that it is a unique opportunity for self-realization, which should not be wasted on mere sense gratification.

Only the Atma gets an opportunity to take a human birth. Srimad Bhagavatam says that, labdhva sudurlabham idam bahusambhavante, we have got this human birth after many many lifetimes. That's a different thing that why we do not remember our previous lifetime, the fact remains that we all have taken many many lives prior to taking birth. When Arjuna asked this question to Krishna, Krishna replied, you and me have taken many many births, I remember them all but you do not remember. The very fact that we do not remember does not mean that we have not taken previous births. I do not remember what I ate last week. I do not remember many of the things which I did in my childhood but the fact is that I did those things. I definitely ate something last week. So we do not remember, labdhva sudurlabham bahusambhavante, after many many lives we have got this human birth. And Bhagavatam says, although this human form of life is temporary, tunam yate tanapatet anumrtyuyavan, before it again falls into the clutches of death. As sure as possible. As sure as death. We are going to fall into the clutches of death. Nobody is an exception. Krishna in the Gita says, one who has taken birth is sure to die and for one who dies is sure to take birth again. So Bhagavatam is saying, before we fall into the clutches of death, we have a very very important thing to accomplish. Tunam yate tanapatet anumrtyuyavan. Nishreya sa visya khalus sarvatrasyat. If we just dabble in sense gratification, just enjoying the senses, just enjoying the worldly pleasures, we have actually wasted this human form of life.

To come to light, to come to knowledge, to come to that understanding who I am, what is the purpose of my life, why I am suffering. How do I get out of suffering? How do I get out of this cycle of repeated birth and death?

#### varnashrama, social structure, qualification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/tF-58Tj0uME?t=1350
**Verse References:** BG 4.13
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This classification as a Brahmana, Kshatriya, Vaishya or Shudra is not by birth. It is by Guna, Karma, Vibhagasha."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the varnashrama system, emphasizing that it is not a caste system based on birth, but a classification based on qualities, actions, and divisions of labor.

the life was structured in such a way there was a system called varnashrama the life was structured in such a way that there was a system of varna and ashrama there were four Varnas. What are the four Varnas? The four Varnas were Brahmana, Kshatriya, Vaishya and Shudras. And there were four Ashramas. What are the four Ashramas? Dhramachari Ashram, Grahasta Ashram, Vanaprastha Ashram and Sanyasa Ashram. So, everyone in the society was following this Varna Ashrama system situated in his proper Varna and Ashrama. Mind you, when we are talking about four Varnas and Ashramas, we are not talking about so-called caste system. The degraded understanding of caste system is that a son of a Brahmana just by having born in a family of a Brahmana claims himself to be a Brahmana. Bhagavad Gita says that this understanding is wrong. This classification as a Brahmana, Kshatriya, Vaishya or Shudra is not by birth. It is by Guna, Karma, Vibhagasha.

---
#### ashramas, spiritual development, sense control
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/tF-58Tj0uME?t=1444
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Keeping the overall purpose of life, Athato Brahma Jagyasa."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the four ashramas, starting with Brahmachari Ashram for training and sense control, followed by Grahasta Ashram for family life within boundaries, all aimed at self-realization.

So, first 25 years a person would get trained and spend in Brahmachari Ashrama. How to control the mind, how to control the senses. This knowledge, this understanding, importance of austerity, penances, sense control was taught. Keeping the overall purpose of life, Athato Brahma Jagyasa. Next 25 years, a person would enter into Grahasta Ashrama. Assuming that human life was 100 years divided into 4 phases, a person would engage in family matters, worldly pleasures but within some boundaries of framework. Not unrestricted, unregulated enjoyment. Because, unrestricted, unregulated enjoyment can lead us tangent from the purpose of the life. Then after 50 years a person understanding the goal of life is self realization, God realization, a person would start voluntarily detaching himself from family life and that stage was called Vanaprastha Ashram.

---
#### detachment, renunciation, spiritual focus
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/tF-58Tj0uME?t=1532
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Because people were learned, they were self-realized, they knew that purpose of human form of life is self-realization."
**Summary:** The speaker continues describing Vanaprastha and Sanyasa Ashrams, emphasizing detachment from family life and full dedication to spiritual focus, highlighting the loss of this understanding in society due to Kali Yuga.

That's why you see even kings, great kings at a certain point would renounce their kingdom and go to the forest. Practical example would be a family man who is staying with his wife, with his family after his sons have grown up. They start little bit spending more time in the forest. Spirituality, visiting various pilgrimage places, getting detached from all family affairs, giving up the responsibility to the grown-up son or a daughter, whatever. And last 25 years a person would dedicate his time, energy with full focus, with full concentration and that ashram was called Sanyasa Ashram. So broadly speaking human form of life divided into four parts, four parts of 25 years, Brahmacharya Ashram, Ghrastha Ashram, Vanaprastha Ashram and then finally Sanyasa Ashram. Why life was structured like that? Because people were learned, they were self-realized, they knew that purpose of human form of life is self-realization. But unfortunately this understanding in the society is lost. That's partly because of the influence of Kali Yuga. In Kali Yuga this understanding that there is a higher purpose to human form of life is lost.

#### social harmony, body analogy, qualification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/tF-58Tj0uME?t=1651
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Mind you, the caste system, understanding what we get from our scriptures is not based on birth, but based on the qualification."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the scientific nature of the Varna Ashrama system, comparing it to the different parts of the body, and reiterates that the classification is based on qualification, not birth.

So this Varna Ashrama system was very, very scientific system and everyone it is said that just like in our body, we have the head, we have the belly, we have the arms, we have the legs. In this Varna Ashrama system, we have the head, we have the belly, we have the arms, we have the legs. the Brahmanas were compared to the head, the Vaishyas were compared to the belly, the Kshatriyas were compared to the arms and the Shudras were compared to the legs. Mind you, when I am using the word Brahmana, Kshatriya, Vaishya, Shudra, sometimes somebody can feel, we are talking about caste system. Mind you, the caste system, understanding what we get from our scriptures is not based on birth, but based on the qualification. Son of a high court judge is not necessarily a judge. But if he gets the required qualification, he can also act like a judge. So, although we have different parts of the body, but still we have different parts of the body. A person may have lost his limbs, a person may not have his legs, but the most important, still a person can function. But if the person is not having his head or mind intact, even though he may have other limbs intact, we say the person is deranged or we may say the person is mad.

somebody I have asked somebody have asked me this question many many times I've got this question that if everyone starts doing bhajan everyone becomes a devotee like you then who will do agriculture who will do trading who will do other activities the answer is that society everyone need not be a brahmana in society everyone need not be spending all his time in patan patan yajan yajan understanding scriptures just like society requires doctors society requires engineers society requires architect society also require people who can spend their time in understanding our scriptures thoroughly spend time in self-realization and with that knowledge guide the Shatria's

#### human birth, self-realization, spiritual progress

**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Everyone was moving towards that ultimate goal of life which was self-realization."
**Summary:** The speaker recaps the earlier points about the rarity and importance of human birth for self-realization, and how the Varna Ashrama system facilitated spiritual progress for everyone.

So quick recap of what we have covered so far. We talked about that human form of life is attained after many many life forms. After having transmigrated in plants, birds, aquatics, reptiles, we get this human birth. And human birth, although it is temporary, we are not eternally human beings, still it is very meaningful because in this human form of life we can achieve self-realization, God-realization by inquiring into fundamental questions of life as to who I am, who is God, what's my relationship with Him. And in Vedic times, knowing that human form of life is very very precious, Asatoma, Satgamaya, that's the goal of life. The human life was structured in four varnas and ashramas. I described that, the four ashramas and the four varnas. And everyone, be it a Kshatriya or a Vaishya or a Shudra, irrespective what ashram he is in, by doing their respective duties, because overall the society was guided by qualified Brahmanas, everyone was spiritually progressive. And everyone was moving towards that ultimate goal of life which was self-realization.

#### spiritual master, satsang, knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/tF-58Tj0uME?t=2821
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Invariably, we are born in darkness of ignorance. And by satsang, by associating with pure devotees, by reading scriptures like Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavatam, Ramayana, Mahabharata and all that, we can slowly, little by little, dissipate the darkness of ignorance and come to light."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the present-day scenario where the Varna Ashrama system is not well-defined, emphasizing the importance of a bona fide spiritual master and satsang to dispel ignorance and gain knowledge.

Now coming to present day scenario, we may not have very well-defined varna and ashrama system prevalent in our society. All of us are engaged in our worldly duties. But by the mercy of a bona fide spiritual master, brahmande brahmite kono bhagyavan jeev, guru krishna prasade paye bhakti lata bheech. That's why Krishna sends such representatives, such acharyas like Srila Prabhupada, who come and enlighten us, enlighten the society. In general, make us aware that there is a higher purpose to human form of life. So for us to do justice to this human form of life, we have to regularly aspire for satsang. By the way, what's the meaning of satsang? Satsang means, sat means truth, sang means to associate with. Invariably, we are born in darkness of ignorance. And by satsang, by associating with pure devotees, by reading scriptures like Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavatam, Ramayana, Mahabharata and all that, we can slowly, little by little, dissipate the darkness of ignorance and come to light.

**Key Quote:** "If we have to do justice to this human form of life, goal of human form of life, then a very very easy and practical process, more so in this age of Kali, is to chant the names of the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker explains Srila Prabhupada's practical advice: to chant the holy names of the Lord and avoid four major sinful activities (meat eating, intoxication, gambling, and illicit sex) to facilitate spiritual progress.

So, Srila Prabhupada has given us some very practical do's and don'ts, some do's, some don'ts. Just like when you go to a physician, knowing that a person is diseased, he will prescribe some medication and will also prescribe some don'ts. Avoid these oily foodstuffs, avoid doing these things, and do certain things. So predominantly speaking, Srila Prabhupada has given us one major do, something which all of us should very diligently do it in our lives. And that is, to chant the holy names of the Lord. If we have to do justice to this human form of life, goal of human form of life, then a very very easy and practical process, more so in this age of Kali, is to chant the names of the Lord. And, in fact, this verse says, Naam eva hi kevalam, only. Kalau naasteva, naasteva, naasteva gatiranya. There is no other way, there is no other way, there is no other way. Although there are many many bona fide methods for God-realization or Self-realization or Asato Ma Satgamaya meditation, it was recommended in Satya Yuga. Elaborate sacrifices was recommended in Treta Yuga. Elaborate Deity worship was recommended in Dvapa Yuga. But in Kali Yuga, with the prevalent conditions, all elaborate sacrifices, elaborate Deity worship, meditation, it's very difficult for a common man. But a very very easy practical thing which can fast track our progress in Self-realization, is to chant the names of the Lord. So that's how the Lord came as Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu and inaugurated the Sankirtanamum about 500 years back. So this is one do, to chant the holy names of the Lord. And then, Srila Prabhupada has also given us the four don'ts, the four major sinful activities which can retard our progress towards the ultimate goal, the goal of life. And what are the don'ts? Meat eating, intoxication, gambling and illicit sex.

#### dharma, artha, kama, moksha
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/tF-58Tj0uME?t=3075
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Dharma leads to Artha. When we engage in dharmic principles, there will be opulence."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the ultimate goal of life as Dharma, Artha, Kama, and Moksha, emphasizing that following dharmic principles leads to opulence and regulated sense enjoyment, ultimately leading to liberation.

So we as, although we may be situated in different circumstances, engaged in various professions, various duties, if while we are doing our worldly duties, also make this chanting of the holy names, reading of scriptures, satsang, as part of our daily lives, we can also march towards this ultimate goal of life. Now before I end and take up some questions, what is that ultimate goal? What is that ultimate aim which I am talking about? Ultimate goal as per our scriptures is, I am sure all of you would have heard about this, Dharma, Artha, Kama and Moksha. Dharma leads to Artha. When we engage in dharmic principles, there will be opulence. There won't be any scarcity. Today because we are violating dharmic principles, we see all kinds of anomalies in the society. Dharma, Artha and even in this material world, we can enjoy regulated sense enjoyment, Kama, fulfill our material desires. We cannot suddenly nullify our material desires. Dharma, Artha, Kama and finally Moksha. Now people have very weird understanding of what is Moksha. Again Moksha can be of different forms, but Bhagavatam says the ultimate liberation from the cycle of birth and death is when the soul can extricate from this cycle of repeated birth and death, punarapi janma, punarapi marna and go back to the original home, the kingdom of God, which Krishna invites, yad gatva na nivartante tad dhamam paramam mama.

This soul which is transmigrating life after life, punarapi janma, punarapi marna, in human form of life, can extricate from this repeated cycle of birth and death and go back to the original home, kingdom of God. Now how do we go there? Gita says, yamyam vapi smaran bhavam tyajti yante kalevram Whatever your state of mind, whatever your consciousness is at the point of death, that will determine what kind of body we will get in the next life. We have example in Bhagavatam of Bharat Maharaj who became very attached to deer and next life he got a body of a deer. If we are fortunate, anta narayana smriti, if we can leave this body in complete Krishna consciousness, thinking of the Lord, yamyam vapi smaran bhavam tyajti yante kalevram, then we go back to our original home, kingdom of God, freed from this cycle of repeated birth and death. And, because of the 2 minutes of running in Olympics, he has got so much of, he has won the medal, Olympic gold medal, which is quite a big accomplishment. He got many endorsing assignments and all that and he has earned 59 million dollars. Just for running 2 minutes in Olympics.

We are going through challenging times, and a lot of people these days are anxious about so many things: about the pandemic, about their jobs, about the future. So many reasons why a person is anxious today. So today, we are going to discuss some very wonderful spiritual perspectives which Krishna has shared with us in the Bhagavad Gita. #### anxiety, self-realization, identification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oGSs1r_6m6E?t=247
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So likewise, when we, the spirit soul, pure soul, identify with this body, with the mind, with the senses, this pure soul appears to be quivering, appears to be in anxiety."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the root cause of anxiety using the analogy of the moon's reflection in a river, stating that anxiety arises from identifying with the body and mind rather than the true self.

So before we talk about how to dispel anxiety, let us first spend some moments understanding why we become anxious, what is the root cause of anxiety. There could be many reasons why a person can become anxious, but what is the root cause, the fundamental reason because of which we go through anxiety? Bhagavatam gives a wonderful example of the moon in the sky. When we see the moon in the sky, let's say on a full moon day, the moon is shining brilliantly, and if you see the reflection of that moon in a river, although the moon in the sky is stationary, shining brightly, but if you see the reflection of that very moon, which is stationary in the sky, in a river or in a pond or in some water body, you will see the reflection is quivering. Again, to what extent the reflection quivers, shakes, depends on how much current is there in the water, in that pond, in that river body, or whatever you can call it. If the water is completely stationary, hypothetical case, then even the moon, the reflection is stationary, there is no quiver. But if there is turbulence in the water, if there is waves in the water, then even the moon which is stationary appears to be moving. So likewise, we as amsa of the Lord, as part and parcel of the Lord, we are sat, chit, and ananda. We are blissful. We are not meant to be having anxiety. We are not meant to go through anxious moments in our lives. Our constitutional position is we are always prasannatma, blissful, just like the moon. It's not supposed to quiver, it's not supposed to shake. Even the reflection of that very moon is supposed to be, that moon which is stationary, the reflection also has to be stationary, but depending on the water body in which the moon is reflected, it appears to be moving. So likewise, when we, the spirit soul, pure soul, identify with this body, with the mind, with the senses, this pure soul appears to be quivering, appears to be in anxiety, appears to be going through a lot of tension, stress, worry.

---
#### self-realization, detachment, misidentification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oGSs1r_6m6E?t=465
**Verse References:** BG 2.55
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So that is our nature."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes the Gita on brahmabhoota prasannatma, explaining that self-realization leads to joy and freedom from hankering and lamentation, contrasting this with the misery caused by identifying with the body.

That's why in the Gita Krishna says, brahmabhoota prasannatma na shochhati na kaangshati. The moment we become brahmabhoota, we realize our true self, or we become self-realized, brahmabhoota, I am not the body, I am the spirit soul, brahmabhoota prasannatma. What happens to such a person who realizes his true identity? He becomes prasannatma, he becomes joyful. And what happens to him next? There is no hankering, there is no lamentation. Take any person in this world, be the richest man in this world, there is hankering for something which he does not have, and lamentation falls for something which a person has lost or may lose. Brahmabhoota prasannatma, so that is our nature. So Srimad Bhagavatam says, if one thinks that he has many enemies, he is mistaken. The real enemy is his uncontrolled mind and uncontrolled senses. So when we identify with our body, when we think that I am the body, dehatma buddhi, and my happiness is in enjoying the senses, this material world is created for an opportunity for the soul to gratify his senses, enjoy the senses, be an enjoyer, be the lord, be the controller, be the center of attraction. So when we identify with the body, then the body goes through a lot of transformations: birth, death, old age, diseases, and the soul identified with that body also goes through a lot of worries, a lot of anxieties, a lot of pains, a lot of trials, a lot of tribulations.

#### introspection, illusion, Bhagavad Gita
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oGSs1r_6m6E?t=643
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

**Summary:** The speaker encourages introspection to identify the root cause of misery as the illusion of identifying with the body, referencing Arjuna's dilemma in the first chapter of the Bhagavad Gita.

Now each one of us, if we do some bit of introspection, the root cause of all our misery, all our anxieties, now each one of us, if we do some bit of introspection, the root cause of all our misery, there will be something which is bothering us, each one of us. There is something which is bothering us, something which is, you know, creating some kind of worry within us. It is impacting our state of happiness. We have some kind of stress or tension. If we do root cause analysis, go back, what is the cause, it stems from the fundamental misunderstanding, illusion, and that is I am the body. Even in the Bhagavad Gita, in the first chapter, Arjuna was in a lot of dilemma, a lot of doubts, should he fight, not fight, because right in front of him were his own relatives, kinsmen, and he has to fight a battle in which a lot of people will die, and they were not ordinary people, his own relatives, his own friends, cousins, respectful elders, and he is in dilemma, and he gives a lot of reasons why it is better for him not to fight. Krishna hears all of his arguments very, very patiently, and in the second chapter, Krishna tells Arjuna, how come these impurities have come upon you?

#### devotion, faith, pastimes of the Lord
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oGSs1r_6m6E?t=1000
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So the Lord was very much moved by their devotion, by their love, by their so-called prayers."
**Summary:** The speaker narrates a story about queens from Kharoli who, through their intense devotion and prayers, moved Lord Krishna to promise to visit their town.

It was not like present time where you can take a Ola or Uber and you can travel far off distances or maybe you can have your own car and vehicles. So the queens would come in caravans, in palanquins, by foot, walking, all the way from Kharoli to Vrindavan. And since they were great devotees of the Lord, they would come quite often, especially on the Purnima days. One such time it so happened that the queens had come from Kharoli to Vrindavan and in Banke Bihari temple they were offering very intense prayers with lot of feelings, with lot of emotions, seeing Lord eye to eye. And the prayer was, O Lord! Every time we come and offer our prayers, we cherish the beautiful darshan. Alas, it would be so nice if you could come to Kharoli. Every day we could have your darshan. Here we have to anxiously wait for months together at times to have your darshan. What great fortune would be if you are there in Kharoli. Every day. Every moment. Every day we can have your darshan. So the Lord was very much moved by their devotion, by their love, by their so-called prayers. So the Lord told the queen, as we all know the Lord is Bhakta Vatsala. He is very, very dear to his devotees. He is very fond of his devotees. He loves his devotees.

#### faith, devotion, divine manifestation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oGSs1r_6m6E?t=1123
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "For a stone hearted person, Krishna is like a stone."
**Summary:** Continuing the story, the speaker describes how Krishna agreed to go to Kharoli, but only those with faith could see Him, illustrating that the Lord manifests according to one's devotion.

So the Lord told the queens, if you want so, if you are pleading so intensely, I will come. I will come. So the queens were very, very excited, ecstatic hearing the Lord is going to come. And the queen started moving back to Kharoli and Krishna told the queens, I will be walking behind you. I will come to Kharoli. Krishna agreed to come to Kharoli. Krishna agreed to come to Kharoli. Krishna agreed to come to Kharoli. Krishna agreed to come to Kharoli with them. So the queens were so excited, they went to the kings who were also there with them on that particular day and told that Krishna has agreed to come with us to Kharoli. We are so blessed, we are so fortunate. And when the queens actually turned back and saw, they saw actually Krishna walking behind them towards Kharoli. But when the queens told the king. The kings thought these queens are very sentimental, after all they are women and very emotional. And out of emotions they talk, how can the deity, how can Banke Bihariji come to Kharoli? How can he walk? After all he is a deity, made of stone. So when the king said, no Krishna is coming, Krishna is coming, you can see for yourself. So when the kings turned and saw. Krishna is coming. There was no Krishna. And when the queens turned and saw, Krishna was there. And finally when they reached Kharoli, they found, including the kings, they found that Krishna has also reached Kharoli. Srila Prabhupada says, for a person who is stone hearted, his heart is like a stone, Krishna is stone. For a stone hearted person, Krishna is like a stone. That's why many people say, why are you worshipping statues? Why are you worshipping idols? Because they actually see it's stone, it's, and that Kabir's Doha, Pathar Pooje Hari Mile Toh Main Pooju Pahar. But for devotees who have an understanding that Lord is omnipotent, he can manifest in the form of his Archa Vigraha, in the form of his deity, and the deity and the Lord are non-different. They have that faith. They have nurtured that faith, that conviction that Lord is there.

#### faith, devotion, separation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oGSs1r_6m6E?t=1272
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Every emotion which we go through has a pure form in the spiritual world."
**Summary:** The speaker continues the story, explaining how the Lord was received in Kharoli, but eventually, the residents of Vrindavan became anxious due to separation from Krishna, illustrating the pure form of emotions in the spiritual world.

I am serving my Lord in that form. It's a great opportunity for me to render service to the Lord, to have darshan of the Lord. So king and the queens, they received the Lord in Kharoli and they made elaborate arrangements for worshipping the Lord and receiving the Lord and all the residents of Kharoli. They all gathered. Abhisheka was done and the worship was done very gorgeously. The Lord was received. And then the Lord decided to go back. Just like we go to some place for one day, two days, we are received well and then you come back. And all the queens and all the residents of Kharoli fell at the feet of the Lord. He says, no, you have come all the way from Vrindavan, we will not let you go. We want you to be with us. Just like you are so, so magnanimous and you are giving darshan every day to the Vrajavasis, to the residents of Vrindavan, why not us? How can you deprive us of that opportunity? We will not let you go. So they fell at the feet of the Lord and started begging and pleading that we will not let you go. So the Lord decided, okay, my devotees are so much insisting, He decided to stay back. Now when the news reached, when the Vrajavasis, when the Vrindavan Vasis got to know the Lord is not there, so they became completely, quote unquote, in transcendental anxiety. Every emotion which we go through has a pure form in the spiritual world. Anger also has a place in the spiritual world. Even the Gopis, when Krishna would go to the forest. Gopis would be anxious about the Lord. What we see the emotions in this material world is a perverted reflection of the pure emotions what are there in the spiritual world.

#### devotion, separation, Vrindavan
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oGSs1r_6m6E?t=1404
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Without you we cannot stay. We cannot live."
**Summary:** The speaker continues the story, describing how the Vrajavasis, missing Vrindavan, pleaded with Krishna to return, highlighting the deep connection between devotees and the holy land.

So Vrajavasis became anxious about the Lord and started frantically searching for the Lord. And finally they got to know that Lord has reached Karoli and searching for the Lord to get the Lord back to Vrindavan. They also reached all the Vrajavasis. Huge numbers they also came to Karoli and started begging and requesting the Lord to come back to Vrindavan. Without you we cannot stay. We cannot live. Now the Lord is in dilemma and the Queens and the King of Karoli, they requested the Vrajavasis. Yes, Vrindavan is Dhama of the Lord. But why don't for few months... arrangements, you all stay here, we will make elaborate arrangements for your stay, why don't we together worship the Lord here, and we will make your stay very comfortable so that we can also have nice, wonderful darshan of the Lord every day. So the Vrajavasis, again devotees are not selfish, agreed for that proposal. And elaborate arrangements were made for their comfortable stay, and they all cherish the wonderful darshan of the Lord daily. But the Vrajavasis very soon started, although the Lord was there, but very soon they started missing Vrindavan. For the Vrajavasis, Vrindavan, very very dear to their heart. In fact for Krishna also, they say Krishna does not leave Vrindavan. So, the Vrajavasis, they started missing Vrindavan and started begging the Lord, pleading the Lord, please come back to Vrindavan, we want to see you in Vrindavan, we want to serve you, play with you, dance with you in Vrindavan, the gopis and the Vrajavasis. So finally, one night, because if in the daytime, taking their permission, they will not let them go, the Karoli Vasis, the residents of Karoli. So, the dead of the night. At the end of the night, without informing everyone, Krishna stepped out of Karoli and started moving towards Vrindavan. Morning when the residents got up and they realized that Krishna is not there, they became very much anxious and they started looking for Krishna.

#### faith, devotion, anxiety
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oGSs1r_6m6E?t=1550
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So from last 200 years, the sevakarthas of the Lord in the temple, they are always anxious that if some devotee comes, sees the Lord eye to eye, pleads and begs the Lord to come with them, the Lord may even leave them and go away."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes the story, explaining how Krishna was intercepted and brought back to Vrindavan, and how this event led to the practice of closing the curtains in the Banke Bihari temple to prevent the Lord from being taken away by devotees.

In fact, the king sent his armies in all directions looking, find where Krishna is. Finally they intercepted Krishna in a place very close to Bharatpur. Because Bharatpur is on the way when you go from Karoli to Vrindavan. So they intercepted Krishna and they almost captured him and they were trying to, you know, bring him back to Karoli when the kings of Bharatpur are also great devotees of the Lord, were devotees of the Lord. So there was a fight between the armies of kings of Bharatpur and Karoli and finally the Lord reached Vrindavan. So from last 200 years, the sevakarthas of the Lord in the temple, they are always anxious that if some devotee comes, sees the Lord eye to eye, pleads and begs the Lord to come with them, the Lord may even leave them and go away. So in that anxiety, so that the Lord may not leave and go, they always close the curtain that if a person is praying, he is already offering his prayer, there is a relationship, the bond getting established, before it gets become so intense, the curtain is closed. So just like the queens of Karoli had so much faith in the Lord, so much love, so much devotion to the Lord. Compared to that, we don't have that devotion, we don't have that love, we don't have that faith.

#### faith, devotion, spiritual growth
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oGSs1r_6m6E?t=1655
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This faith can be cultivated, can be nurtured."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that faith can be cultivated and nurtured, referencing the stage of Nishtha (firm faith) in the growth of Bhakti, and highlighting the conviction that the Lord is one's protector.

This faith can be cultivated, can be nurtured. In fact, the stages of growth of Bhakti, one of the stages is called Nishtha. Nishtha means firm faith. Avashe Rakshibi Krishna I Vishwasa Palana. That faith, that conviction that Lord is my protector. Lord will take care of me. Even if I am going through some problems, even if I am going through some difficulties. Maro bhi rakho bhi jo ichho tumara. Since you are my Lord unconditionally, whether you protect me or you kill me, you are my Lord unconditionally. This is a prayer of Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. So the devotees have so much faith in the Lord. They have that conviction. That Lord will surely protect. Even Prahlad Maharaj, when he was thrown in a pit of snakes, he didn't plead the Lord for protection. He had that conviction, that faith that Lord will surely protect. And Krishna in the Gita says, as you surrender, so shall I reciprocate. As you surrender, so shall I reciprocate. Now today we cannot, in the times of our problems or difficulties, we cannot go to the Lord and say, why you didn't protect me? Have we invested our time? Have we nurtured that relationship? Have we surrendered to the Lord? Any relationship requires time, energy, effort. Even a mundane relationship. So human form of life is an opportunity. Now how does this faith get nurtured? The topic is nurture. Nurture your faith. How does it get nurtured?

---
#### faith, association, contagion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oGSs1r_6m6E?t=1771
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Faith is contagious."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that faith, like a contagious disease, can be spread through association with those who have faith, emphasizing the importance of hearing from and associating with pure devotees.

Faith is contagious. Just like we have these days pandemic, you know, coronavirus and because of fear of infection, everyone is wearing a mask, sanitizing our hands, washing our hands because it is contagious. It can spread very easily. Likewise, faith or faithlessness is contagious. If you associate with a person, if you touch a person, hear from a person who has faith in the Lord, your faith in the Lord also increases. If you associate with a person who is faithless, who has no faith in the Lord, that faithlessness also rubs on us. Shri Prabhupada, when he went to America, he had such strong faith. He had such strong faith. He had such strong faith. He had such strong faith. He had such strong faith and conviction in the Lord, in the protection of the Lord. He was so surrendered to the Lord. Anybody who came in touch with him, even those very fallen people, drug addicts, alcoholics, because of that strong faith, that strong conviction, that love for the Lord, it got implanted in their hearts. So that's why it is very, very important. Apart from the words what we hear. When we hear someone, Shri Prabhupada used to say, we should not hear from a professional reciter. Even Bhagavad Gita and Bhagavatam, there are many professional reciters. They make a living out of speaking Bhagavad Gita and Bhagavatam. But if you ask, if you see there, there are a lot of bhajan singers, they sing bhajans. But they not necessarily be having faith in the Lord. It's their occupation. Right? It's means of livelihood. So hearing from a professional, because in the heart there is no faith in the Lord, there is faithlessness. Apart from what we are hearing, subconsciously, that faith or faithlessness also gets transmitted. And that at the subconscious level, it is not even without, it's not even with knowledge. So therefore, when we associate. With a pure devotee of the Lord, who is faithful servant of the Lord, who has faith in the Lord, who has conviction in the Lord, who has love for the Lord, that faith also rubs on us.

---
#### faith, dependence on God, surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oGSs1r_6m6E?t=1927
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Our constitutional position, our natural position is to be dependent on the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of nurturing faith in the Lord as the root solution to anxiety, arguing that dependence on the Lord is natural and that He reciprocates according to one's surrender.

That's why it is very, very important for us to on a daily basis, Shravanam here. The solution to anxiety, yes, we can always have some stopgap solution, we can, you know, do deep breathing. And you know, we can. These days, lot of. We can go on the net, there are lot of solutions. You can take some pills, you can consult a doctor, you can consult a psychiatrist. There are many things. But if you were to address the root cause. The root cause is we have to nurture our faith in the Lord, our dependence on the Lord. One time I was speaking in some seminar and then somebody asked. If we are to depend on the Lord and pray to the Lord when we are having some difficulties, anxiety. And things like that. Isn't religion like a crutch? You know, crutch, when disabled people who don't have amputee, who don't have a leg, they use a crutch. So isn't religion or God like a crutch? That we are using it for relieving our anxieties, relieving our worry. Our constitutional position, our natural position is to be dependent on the Lord. We are part and parcel of the Lord. So it is nothing wrong to be dependent on the Lord. In fact, if we are not dependent on the Lord, it's an artificial position. If it's raining outside, is it wrong? Is it? Will we call a person as a coward if he takes an umbrella to protect himself? So likewise, Krishna in the Gita says. If you become conscious of me, by my grace, you will cross over all obstacles of conditional life. As you surrender, so shall I reciprocate. If you don't want Lord, he doesn't interfere in our lives. If we want him, to the extent, we want him, to the extent, we surrender to him, to that extent, he reciprocates.

---
#### faith, mindfulness, anxiety

And the first principle is nurture that faith so I request the devotees if you could help type out this in the live chat so that it's a reinforcement for us the first principle we have learned if we have to keep anxiety at bay is to nurture the faith in the Lord let's have the devotees writing mukesh yadav nurture your faith in the Lord. The second principle which we're going to talk today is mind your thoughts. Mind your thoughts.

#### mindfulness, thoughts, silence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oGSs1r_6m6E?t=2116
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Most of the problem just like the Bhagavatam says it's because of uncontrolled mind and uncontrolled senses."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the nature of thoughts, emphasizing their randomness and connection to anxiety, and mentions the practice of silence (Mauna) as a temporary relief.

We all know that our mind, you know, constantly we are thinking, constantly we are producing thoughts. Somehow they are figured out on an average day an average person thinks 60,000 thoughts and the sad part is most of the thoughts are random and unnecessary. We are either in the past or in the future worrying about the future or lamenting about what has happened in the past. Seldom we are in the present anxious about the future that's how you know there was one book which became very popular called the power of now where it talks about be present and a lot of people who followed the instructions in that book got some bit of relief because most of the problem just like the Bhagavatam says it's because of uncontrolled mind and uncontrolled senses. There are lot of seminars and workshops and people go and practice silence, Mauna and they experience lot of relief from the mind. Like I told about the moon quivering, it's because of the water. Therefore, all the turbulence we experience in our mind, in our life is because of uncontrolled mind, uncontrolled senses. So, when people practice Mauna, silence, for a while, for few days, they experience shanti, tranquility, peace. And, many of them mistake that tranquility or peace or freedom from anxiety as some spiritual experience.

## HG Amitasana Dasa

### ATTAINING GOD'S FAVOR | HG AMITASANA DAS | GITA LIFE Live
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### mercy, grace, qualification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xuVstCHcGJE?t=23
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So if Lord is pleased on us, he smiles on us, our life is done."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the topic of attaining God's favor, emphasizing the importance of achieving the Lord's mercy and blessings.

We have a very, very interesting topic today, and the topic is attaining God's favor. How can we be qualified to achieve the Lord's mercy, his special blessings, his grace upon all of us? This is the most important thing. The Lord is all-powerful, he is the supreme controller, he is the cause of all causes. That's the understanding of God we get from our scriptures, from the Bhagavad-gita. So if Lord is pleased on us, he smiles on us, our life is done. So that is what we are going to discuss today: what steps can we take, what activities we can undertake so that we can achieve God's need of our lives, get qualified to attain God's favor.

---
#### guru, spiritual master, connection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xuVstCHcGJE?t=314
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Our scriptures say that the right way to go to the Lord or to approach the Lord is via bona fide spiritual master."
**Summary:** The first principle to achieve God's favor is to connect through the right connection, which is a bona fide spiritual master.

So the first principle to achieve God's favor is get connected through right connection. In other words, the principle is connect through right connection. Many times when we offer our prayers to the Lord, or when we go to a particular temple, or when we chant the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra, we directly start chanting Krishna's name. We directly start offering prayers to Krishna. We directly start worshipping Krishna. Many times we do small aarti at our place in our houses. We might directly offer the various ingredients directly to Krishna. Our scriptures say that the right way to go to the Lord or to approach the Lord is via bona fide spiritual master.

---
#### guru, spiritual master, obeisances
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xuVstCHcGJE?t=392
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Because only by his mercy, only by his recommendation, I will be able to approach Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes a Kabir Das Doha emphasizing the importance of offering obeisances to the Guru first, as it is through the Guru's mercy that one can approach Krishna.

A bona fide Guru. We all have heard about this Kabir Das Doha which says that Guru Gobind, Dau Khade, Kake Lagu Pao, Bali Hari Guru Aapne, Govind Diyo Milai. Which means that right in front of me both Guru and Gobind are standing. If I have to choose to whom I should offer my obeisances, first, the right understanding is I should first offer my obeisances to the Guru, the spiritual master. Why? Because only by his mercy, only by his recommendation, I will be able to approach Krishna.

---
#### connection, power, material world
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xuVstCHcGJE?t=463
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Now, the point what I am trying to make is, the right connection is very important."
**Summary:** The speaker shares a personal anecdote about trying to meet the Chief Minister of Maharashtra to illustrate the importance of having the right connections, even in mundane affairs.

Many years ago, we wanted to meet the Chief Minister of Maharashtra. At that time it was Sri Devendra Fadnavis. And we were trying quite a lot to get an appointment, to meet him. We were trying our bit because we were starting Akshapatra in Maharashtra. And we wanted to approach him and tell him about our plans to set up kitchens in different parts of Maharashtra. And if we could recommend to the various commissioners about Akshapatra. So we were keen on meeting him. We were trying various means to get an appointment. In fact, we went to Mantralaya also. We went to the Chief Minister's office. Met some personal secretaries, the officers in charge of various protocols. And by and large, we were quite unsuccessful. Even though we had some contacts, it was quite difficult. And every time we would go to the Chief Minister's office, we would see a lot of people waiting for him. Lot of people wanting to meet him. And he was quite busy. A time came when I started personally feeling it is next to impossible. It's very, very difficult. Now mind you, I am talking about the Chief Minister of one of the 29 states in India. Now you can imagine, without the right connection, if we were to meet the Prime Minister of the country, Narendra Modi. Or for that matter, you extrapolate, if you were to meet the President of United States of America. We are talking about Krishna, who is not only the President or Chairman of a particular continent, a particular country, a particular universe, of all the universes. The Supreme Creator, the cause of all causes. So I was getting quite frustrated, quite disgusted. And a time came when I thought, okay, let us give it up. Not required. Hoga toh hoga, nahi hua toh nahi hua. Now Lord is watching. And he understood that we are quite eager to meet the Chief Minister. Although whatever we want from Chief Minister can easily be done by the Lord. But then there are some protocols we have to follow while we are in this world. The Lord has invested His power in different personalities and we have to respect them. That way Aveda says, even a king, who is quite powerful, is having the power on behalf of the Lord. He is ruling on behalf of the Lord. The Lord has empowered him. The Lord has empowered the different Devatas. They are not independent controllers. So, then we met one person. It seems he was very close to the Chief Minister. So, we told him that we want to meet him. And he said, when do you want to meet him? It was like, you know, asking as if we had a choice. So, I told him, sooner the better. So, then he asked me, are you free today evening? Imagine for days together, for months together, we were trying to meet him. And then he is asking, do you want to meet him today? I said, why not? We are ready. And then, he told us, okay, come at 4.30 in the evening. We went there. Again, like our previous experience, it was quite a busy scenario. Lot of people waiting. This person comes with us, walks directly to Chief Minister, and after two minutes, comes out and tells us, please come in. Please come with me. And we go with him, and for 15 minutes, we were with the Chief Minister, talking to him. Now, the point what I am trying to make is, the right connection is very important. For that matter, in everything in our lives, we all have experience of how the right connections make a big difference. That's how, in power circles, people always trying to connect with so-called power brokers, the people who wield power, people who have the right connections for material gains.

---
#### guru, disciplic succession, mercy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xuVstCHcGJE?t=779
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If we get the mercy of the spiritual master, if we get his favor, bhagwat prasado, we get the favor of the Supreme Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that a bona fide spiritual master is like a power broker with a close connection to the Lord, emphasizing the importance of guru parampara and the mercy of the guru.

So, just like the example which I shared with all of you, likewise, the spiritual master, bona fide spiritual master, a bona fide guru, who is coming in a bona fide guru parampara, a disciplic succession, he has close connection with the Lord. In fact, morning, we have Mangal Aarti. Every day, we pray these eight stanzas, glorifying the spiritual master, guru. Our day starts with that. Every day, without fail, 4.15 in the morning, we start with Mangal Aarti, which have eight stanzas, glorifying the spiritual master. One of the stanzas says, yasya prasadat bhagwat prasado. If we get the mercy of the spiritual master, if we get his favor, bhagwat prasado, we get the favor of the Supreme Lord. We get the mercy of the Supreme Lord. Yasya prasadat. The next line says, if we do not get his mercy, if for some reason we are not qualified to get his mercy, although he is very, very compassionate, he is distributing his mercy to all the fallen conditioned souls, like us, but this Mangal Aarti prayer says that, yasya prasadat, by his mercy, we can easily get the mercy of Lord Shri Krishna. And if for some reason we are disqualified or we are not qualified to receive the mercy of guru, then we have no hope.

#### guru, devotee, mission

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

So, the principle is, connect through right connection. So the right connection is, a bona fide guru. Why is the right connection? Because, he is a confidential servitor of the Lord. He is fulfilling the Lord's mission. Krishna in the Gita says, yada yadahi dharmasya glanir bhavati bharata. Whenever there is decline in religious principles, I descend myself. Not only the Lord comes, the Lord also sends his representatives. Not every time, the President of United States comes to India, in case there is some important and urgent discussion. He sends the representatives. So, a bona fide guru is a confidential servitor of the Lord, he is very, very dear to the Lord. He is a transparent via media. What is the meaning of transparent via media? That means, whatever we offer to guru, he offers to Krishna. He has no personal self-interest.

#### Srila Prabhupada, books, compassion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xuVstCHcGJE?t=1060
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Srila Prabhupada would get up at one o'clock in the night and for four hours, he would be transcribing, he would be dictating Bhaktivedanta purpose, word by word meaning."
**Summary:** The speaker describes Srila Prabhupada's dedication to writing books, highlighting his tireless efforts and sacrifices to translate and explain the Bhagavad Gita.

Srila Prabhupada has written his books, books on Bhagavad Gita, teachings of Bhagavad Gita. In fact, Srila Prabhupada would get up at one o'clock in the night and for four hours, he would be transcribing, he would be dictating Bhaktivedanta purpose, word by word meaning. Even at times when he was travelling, you know, he was seventy years old when he went to the US, the movement, the International Society for Krishna Consciousness, was registered in 1965-66. And then, he was travelling extensively. Every two days, he was in a new country, meeting journalists, talking to people, talking to some eminent personalities, addressing students in prestigious colleges, meeting devotees, answering their questions, taking them on morning walks, so that he can engage in some meaningful conversations, some meaningful conversations related to Krishna conscious principles.

---
#### Srila Prabhupada, dedication, sacrifice
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xuVstCHcGJE?t=1134
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "And at times, five o'clock, when he would be ready to go for a morning walk, he would ask, where are my secretaries?"
**Summary:** The speaker continues to illustrate Srila Prabhupada's dedication by sharing anecdotes of his tireless work ethic, even when his secretaries were exhausted from travel.

So many devotees who were travelling with him, helping Srila Prabhupada during his travels, assisting him, they would get completely drained out, completely tired because of jet lag. And then Prabhupada would travel and then get to a new place, sometimes late in the night. Again, he would get up at one o'clock. And at times, five o'clock, when he would be ready to go for a morning walk, he would ask, where are my secretaries? And they would be at nine o'clock, at five o'clock, they would be completely tired, at times sleeping. One day, Srila Prabhupada, as usual, got up at one o'clock and was dictating Bhaktivedanta Purports. You know, in dead of night, it's quite silent. And the devotees assisting him had still not got up. So Prabhupada, as usual, you know, he would typically have some seven or eight commentaries of the previous Acharyas. And he would be reading each one of them very carefully, very meticulously. And he would be dictating Bhaktivedanta Purports. Giving word to word meanings.

#### Srila Prabhupada, books, guidance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xuVstCHcGJE?t=1214
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "These books will guide the mankind for next ten thousand years."
**Summary:** The speaker shares an anecdote about a devotee who disturbed Srila Prabhupada while he was dictating purports, emphasizing the importance and lasting impact of Prabhupada's books.

So one devotee, he got up and knowing that Prabhupada is already busy dictating the Purports, he wanted to just make Srila Prabhupada aware that he is available. Prabhupada could call him in case he needs any help. Because Prabhupada was in his room and the devotee was in the adjacent room. So, he got up late, maybe two o'clock, two-thirty and he saw from the window, Srila Prabhupada is already up and is dictating. So, he made some sound. It was quite, because it was quite crowd, quite peaceful surrounding, middle of night. So, it was quite clear for Srila Prabhupada that, yes, somebody is outside and the devotee assisting him has woken up. Thinking that Prabhupada has not heard, he again made that sound. So, Prabhupada called him, called this particular devotee inside. He told the devotee, I know that you have got up. Do not disturb. I am dictating these purports. Each word, I have to be very, very careful. These are not ordinary books, which I am writing. These books will guide the mankind for next ten thousand years.

#### Srila Prabhupada, mind of the Lord, guidance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xuVstCHcGJE?t=1321
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So if we were to get Lord's favour, if we were to understand Lord's mind, if we were to understand what the Lord wants from us... when we read Shila Prabhupada books, then we are directly connected with the Lord's mind."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that reading Srila Prabhupada's books connects one directly with the Lord's mind, desires, and intelligence, especially in the absence of Krishna's direct presence.

If you have read Srila Prabhupada books, if you have read Bhagavad Gita as it is, each and every verse, each and every word, there is word to word meaning, there is a translation and there is Bhaktivedanta purport. And mind you, person is, Srila Prabhupada is seventy years old, travelling, one o'clock in the night, whole, throughout the day, a lot of engagement, the day is fully packed. He would get up at one o'clock in the night and he would be dictating these purports. So, if we were to get Lord's favour, if we were to understand Lord's mind, if we were to understand what the Lord wants from us, if we were to get a clear understanding as to what is Lord expecting from us. See, as far as battle of Kurukshetra is concerned, Arjuna is concerned, it was very clear, Krishna is right in front of him. Arjuna had many doubts, he could directly ask Krishna and get the answers. Somebody may say, now we don't have Krishna right in front of us, how do we know what pleases Krishna? What to do when we have certain doubts? Many times we are in dilemma, we are in confusion, we don't know what is the right thing to do. We don't know what is the mind of the Lord, what is in Lord's mind, what is Lord's desire for me? So when we read Shila Prabhupada books, then we are directly connected with the Lord's mind.

Because he is a transparent via media, he is connected with the Lord, just like a live wire, a wire which is plugged into the power source, it carries the electricity, electric current. Just remove that plug, it's still a wire, but it does not have the current. So bona fide spiritual master is like a live wire, he is carrying the current of Krishna consciousness, Krishna bhakti, love of God. And anybody who touches such a live wire, such a pure devotee of the Lord, love matra sadhu sangha sarva siddhi hoye, even by a moment's association with such a pure devotee of the Lord, has the potency, has the power, to completely transform our life.

#### devotee, favor, connection

What we were striving for months together, in a jiffy, in a day, he said, Do you want to meet in the evening? And he took us, 4.30, even before checking up with the Chief Minister, he says, 4.30 you come to the Chief Minister's office. We went there and, he just walked into the office and called us in.

#### giving, sacrifice, relationship
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xuVstCHcGJE?t=1798
**Verse References:** —

**Summary:** The speaker introduces the second principle: give to get, explaining that any relationship requires sacrifice and investment of time, energy, and feelings.

The second principle is quite controversial. Lot of people are quite cautious when it comes to Sadhus and Mahatmas. People have an understanding that, they will throw us out. They will take money from us. They will take money from us. And we see a lot of scandals, with so many God men, you know, amassed a lot of wealth. So people are very cautious. But the second principle is, although it may look controversial, although it may look, people saying that, yes, we knew, now you will say this. The second principle is, give, to get. I repeat, give, to get. Any relationship requires sacrifice, requires investment of time, energy, feelings.
