And having read the Gita, understood some bit about spiritual life, we all want to spend good quality time pursuing our spiritual activities. And many times we think that these material activities are sucking lot of my time. I don't want to spend lot of time doing these things but I have no option. In fact, sometimes people are quite passionate about earning money in their business or in their work. They are very focused on their career growth. And then once I am financially stable, I have earned enough, I have good savings. Then I can completely retire and focus entirely on spiritual pursuits. In other words, they have broadly compartmentalized life as material life, which has to do with material aspirations, material duties and spiritual life which has to do with elevation of soul. That's how we have a book by Robin Sharma which talks about monk who sold his Ferrari. And most of the people believe that these two cannot go hand in hand. #### material activities, spiritual activities, duty
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9qz9Xp1h9WA?t=895

**Summary:** The speaker challenges the conventional understanding that material and spiritual life are mutually exclusive, citing Arjuna's reluctance to fight in the Gita as an example of shirking one's duty.

That's the conventional understanding that there are some material activities which we understand, may not be in my real self-interest but we have to do it. Sometimes we have to spend lot of quality time doing it. It could be our jobs, it could be taking care of our near and dear ones, maintaining those social relationships. So the right understanding, in fact, in the Gita also, in the first chapter, Arjuna, while the battle is about to begin, when Arjuna understood that he has to fight this battle, he being a Kshatriya, his duty was to fight that battle, he wanted to withdraw himself from that duty. In fact, he told, what's the use of fighting, let me just beg and earn my living. But the whole Gita was spoken that, yes, you are a Kshatriya, a family man, you have to do your duty. So in other words, we cannot shun away from our duties. We may be placed in a situation where we have to take care of our near and dear ones. So we cannot have a binary, okay, I will be, this is all material. I have to somehow, it's a bad bargain, we have to somehow engage and then wait for that opportune time where you can completely give up all this.

#### material duties, spiritual life, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9qz9Xp1h9WA?t=1008
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Right understanding is that while we are engaged in our so-called material duties, our worldly duties, we can cultivate and practice spiritual life."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that spiritual life can be cultivated while engaged in worldly duties, referencing Krishna's instruction to Arjuna to fight while remembering Him, and highlighting ISKCON's mission to elevate consciousness for all, not just renunciates.

Right understanding is that while we are engaged in our so-called material duties, our worldly duties, we can cultivate and practice spiritual life. Even Krishna tells Arjuna. Even Krishna tells Arjuna, maam anusmarya yudhya cha. You think of me, be conscious of me and fight. That's why Ishla Prabhupada, this society is called International Society for Krishna Consciousness. We can cultivate consciousness and remembrance of Krishna while we are engaged in our so-called worldly duties. International Society for Krishna Consciousness is not meant only for full-time missionaries. It's not meant only for people who are in the renounced order of life. It's meant for everyone, all sections of the society, how we can elevate our consciousness. In fact, Ishla Prabhupada wrote one book Elevation to Krishna Consciousness. How we can practice certain principles, how we can regulate our lives, how we can incorporate the teachings of Bhagavad Gita and practice remembrance of Krishna, being conscious of Krishna while engaged in worldly duties. It is not recommended that a person gives up his worldly duties. It is not recommended that a person gives up his worldly duties or renounces his worldly life and make an abrupt shift to spirituality. Because we have to gradually train our mind, gradually cultivate that remembrance of the Lord, cultivate that attachment to the Lord, cultivate spiritual knowledge and also cultivate detachment.

#### renunciation, detachment, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9qz9Xp1h9WA?t=1156
**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Prabhupada says even if a person abruptly gives up his family life and goes to a forest and renounces everything, his house, his family, his wealth and he goes to a forest only with a komandalu', a pot, That feeling of I and mine is still there, aham ameti, I and mine."
**Summary:** The speaker argues against premature renunciation, stating that the feeling of 'I and mine' persists even in renounced life, and that spiritual life can be cultivated while engaged in worldly duties through Krishna consciousness.

So, this is not a proverb, but it is not very paintball. Thinking, then abruptly. Prabhupada says even if a person abruptly gives up his family life and goes to a forest and renounces everything, his house, his family, his wealth and he goes to a forest only with a komandalu', a pot, That feeling of I and mine is still there, aham ameti, I and mine. But we have examples of great Raja Rishis who were kings living in palaces amidst opulence but they because of their elevated consciousness, Krishna consciousness, were free from that illusion of I and mine. In other words, what I am trying to mention is, while engaged in the worldly duties, while still having property, while still having wealth in our possession, we can cultivate gradually by following Krishna conscious principles, the teachings of Bhagavad Gita, the consciousness of Nirmama, it is not mine, it is all Lord's, I am not this body. Jnana and Vairagya. And yes, if we have sufficiently cultivated at certain stage in life, for example, even in Vedic times we had Vanaprastha ashrama and sanyasa ashrama, a person at a later stage in life when things have settled up can move to a Vanaprastha stage or a sanyasa stage. But it is not true that unless we renounce. But it is not true that unless we renounce. But it is not true that unless we renounce. Everything, our family, our work, our jobs, we cannot cultivate spiritual life. It is a wrong understanding.

#### balance, human life, spiritual dimension
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9qz9Xp1h9WA?t=1340
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So spiritual dimension is also a very, very important dimension of our life and many times if we are off balance. We have neglected. If we have neglected that part, we will not be leading a successful life."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of a car with four wheels to represent the physical, emotional, social, and spiritual dimensions of human life, emphasizing the importance of balancing all four for a successful and meaningful life.

Now we require a healthy balance. Why the balance is required? It's said that just like in a car we have four wheels, it's said that in this human form of life these four wheels represent our, one wheel represents our body, health, physical dimension of our existence, our life. Then we have an emotional dimension. We are emotional beings, our feelings, love. We expect some kind of appreciation and then we have a social dimension, we are not We are social beings, man is a social animal, he cannot live in isolation. We live in a community, we live in a society, there are dealings, our near and dear ones, our community members, our society members, the building in which we are staying there is a society. So that's another dimension of our existence and then we have the spiritual dimension. So just like a car if it has to run smoothly, all the four wheels have to be properly aligned which is called wheel balancing. Imagine a car which is running on four wheels and one of the wheel, the tyre is punctured, you will not have a smooth ride. So spiritual dimension is also a very, very important dimension of our life and many times if we are off balance. We have neglected. If we have neglected that part, we will not be leading a successful life. Again successful is quite subjective, people have different understanding of what is success. In real sense, in true sense, we will not be able to lead a successful and meaningful life.

#### materialism, spiritual life, balance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9qz9Xp1h9WA?t=1538
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The problem is with the in-between category."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of students taking an exam to illustrate different approaches to life, highlighting the confidence of both the completely unprepared materialists and the fully dedicated spiritualists, and the nervousness of those in the middle trying to balance both.

They say when people go for examination, there are two people who go with a very, very big smile on their face. Most of you would have guessed who these two people are. We have all gone for examination. We have all gone for meditation. We have all gone for meditation. We have written exams in our lives. Two people, two kinds of students go with a very big smile on their face. The first category is students who have not prepared at all. They have not studied. They are not worried about the consequences or results because they haven't studied. Even if they fail, doesn't matter. And there is another category of students who go with a very big smile. And that category is students who are prepared very well. They are quite confident. They are well prepared. Whatever the exam paper may be, they are confident they will be able to answer them well and score good marks. So they are also quite confident. They go with a big smile on their face. The problem is with the in-between category. They are nervous. They are tense. Because what if? Something what they have prepared doesn't come. What if the questions come from a section which they haven't prepared. All this kind of nervousness can be there. So people, now using the same example, those who are complete materialists, they also seem to be enjoying life. In Hindi there is a word called Bindas. They are, you know, watching television. There is no guilt feeling. They do everything what comes in their mind. There is no rules and regulations guiding them, sleeping at one o'clock in the night, watching movies, going to the malls, going to pubs, drinking, partying. And there is no element of guilt feeling. There is no desire to strike any balance. And at times it looks like they are fairly balanced. Because, you know, they have only one life to take care of and that is material life. It's like the students going for exam with a big smile on the face with no preparation. Their situation is like that. Yes, they have a big smile on their face. But they want to fail the exam of life.

#### time management, priorities, balance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9qz9Xp1h9WA?t=1943
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Is the jug full?"
**Summary:** The speaker begins an analogy of a time management workshop where a facilitator fills a jug with rocks, gravel, sand, and water to demonstrate how to prioritize tasks.

So I'll give you two examples. One example is of a training workshop which was being conducted. This workshop was to drive home the principles of time management. It was on time management. The workshop was on time management. So the workshop leader, the trainer, the facilitator took a glass jug, a see-through glass jug and he started putting the big rocks, some rocks, some stones in that jug. And after having placed that rocks, which he mentioned that this big rocks represents our commitments, some activities which take away a lot of our time, big chunks of our time. So he placed the rocks in that glass jug and he raised the jug in front of the classroom. All the students were sitting. And he raised the jug in front of the classroom. All the students were sitting. And he said, is the jug full? So all of them said, yes, the jug is full. And then he bent downwards and picked up some gravel, small pieces of stones, and started putting it on top of the jug and started shaking the jug. And those small pieces of gravel or stones started crumbling. settling down in between those cavities, there was big rocks and in between those cavities or those spaces were there, they started settling down. And again he raised the jug and said, is the jug full? By this time the students had got little more cautious, they understood he is up to something, they did not want it to appear foolish. So one of them said, no, we can, there are still spaces in this jug, we can fill it up still with something and very true, he bent and he put some sand on top of it and started shaking the jug and the sand started settling down in between those cavities. And then again he raised the hands, again is the jug full? People were more smarter by now, much more wiser. So one of them said, no, we can put water. Then he started putting water and the water seeped through all that sand, gravel and the stones. Then he raised the jug again in the air and said, is the jug full? Most of them said yes, but one of them was, quote unquote, even more cautious. He said, maybe we can add some color, maybe we can add some salt, yes, it is a fact that even if it is full with water, you can add some salt. Or add some color to it and it will not take more space, but the sand, the salt can disperse in the water.

#### time management, priorities, balance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9qz9Xp1h9WA?t=2139
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The goal of life is not to pack 24 hours and be watertight."
**Summary:** The speaker continues the analogy, explaining that the initial interpretation of the experiment, to pack the day with more activities, is not the intended message.

So after having done that, he asked the students, what do we understand from this experiment or this demonstration? So many students started coming up with different observations, different learnings. Which they assimilated from this demonstration. One of them said that, you know, we all have, we all think that we are busy, we all think that we are packed. We all think that we have no time, but still there are pockets of time in between our day, which we can fill up with more activities. In other words, we might feel our day is packed, but still there are some, some pockets of time, like while we are having lunch. So these days, a lot of people while having lunch. They may, may catch up with the news or catch up with television or catch up with some reading or catch up with some, you know, hearing of something. So one of them said that it's all about packing our day and no matter how busy we may be, still we have a lot of gaps which we can pack. So this person said, no, that's not the point which I want to drive. The goal of life is not to pack 24 hours and be watertight. No. In other words, in the sense, moment to moment we are busy with some activities and we have no time for recreation, no time to pursue our hobbies, no time to pursue activities which we like doing. That's not the goal of life.

#### priorities, spiritual activities, time management
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9qz9Xp1h9WA?t=2236
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Activities which are important but not urgent."
**Summary:** The speaker reveals the true message of the analogy: prioritizing the 'big rocks' representing important but not urgent activities like spiritual practices, before filling the remaining time with less important tasks.

That's not time management. So then this person, the facilitator told that what if I put the contents which I had put in this jug in the reverse order. What if I put the sand first and then the gravel and then the big rocks. If we try doing that in the reverse order, you will see that we are not able to accommodate as many big rocks which were accommodated in the first place. And then he said these big rocks represents our big commitments of life, big ticket items, quadrant two activities. Activities which are important but not urgent. For example, many of us may not be exercising daily, spending time for physical fitness because it is not urgent. It is important. We all know that we should go for a walk, we should spend time exercising, but we may not find time because it's not urgent, its life is going on without exercise also somehow I am able to manage. So we don't. Reading, reading Bhagavad Gita or reading for that matter any good book, it's not urgent. I may not read for next seven days. Life still goes on and one might wonder what's the need and even if I want to read, I may not have time because my life is packed with so many activities. So exercising, reading, chanting of the Lord's name, chanting of Hare Krishna Mahamantra, it's a quadrant two activity, it's very, very important, but it's not urgent. There are a lot of people who are not chanting, but still their life is on. So spiritual activities are quadrant two activities, they are important, but they are not urgent. If we have the right knowledge, we will understand the urgency because we don't have the right knowledge, we don't understand the urgency of practicing spiritual life. We always think that still we have a lot of time, I am still young. Why now? I understand Bhagavad Gita is important, but why now? Post 60, post 65, post 70 years after everything is settled up, my family, my children, everyone is married, my grandson is also married, I have no responsibilities left, my worldly duties are all over, then I can pursue.

**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "The sad part is most of our thoughts, in fact, they have quantified 77% on an average of what we think is negative."
**Summary:** The lecture delves into the nature of the mind, comparing it to a thought factory that produces a large number of thoughts daily, with a significant portion being negative and counterproductive, impacting health.

Our minds are compared to a thought factory. Just like you have a factory in which some products are being manufactured. Likewise, in the factory of mind, thoughts are being manufactured. On an average day, an average person thinks around 60,000 thoughts. It may vary from person to person. But on an average, it's 60,000 thoughts per day. It's a huge number. The sad part is most of our thoughts, in fact, they have quantified 77% on an average of what we think. Again, it varies from person to person. That's why I'm saying average. 77% of what we think is negative and counterproductive. Many of the diseases are psychosomatic diseases. In other words, mind plays a very big role in these diseases. It's not because of some bacteria. It's not because of some virus. I'm not talking about the coronavirus or the present pandemic. But in general, around 80% of the diseases are having a big contribution from our mind because of anxiety, because of worry, because of negative thoughts.

**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

**Summary:** The speaker references the 18th chapter of the Bhagavad Gita, highlighting Krishna's confidential instruction to 'think of me' (Manmana) and connects it to the principles of Krishna consciousness, emphasizing awareness of Krishna's instructions and restrictions.

In the 18th chapter of the Bhagavad Gita, Krishna gives his most confidential instruction. Sarvaguhyatamambhuyashunumeparamamvachah. Param means the highest. After having spoken the entire Gita, Krishna says, now I am going to tell you the highest instruction. Shunumeparamamvachah. And the next words Krishna says, Manmana. Think of me. Although it might look to be a very simple instruction, but it's very profound. That's why the name of our institution is International Society for Krishna Consciousness. Thinking about Krishna. Conscious about Krishna. Conscious about Krishna's instructions. Conscious about Krishna's restrictions.

#### relationships, conflict, perspective
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ey9oyqy2WvM?t=647
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Right from day one, there was a chemistry mismatch between this lady and the mother-in-law."
**Summary:** The speaker begins telling a story about a lady named Lily in China who has a difficult relationship with her short-tempered mother-in-law, leading to frustration and a desire to leave the house.

There was once a lady. This story is from China. The lady by name Lily. Not L I L Y. It is a Chinese name. L I dash L I. So she got married and after marriage she went to her husband's place to stay. And in that house, the husband and his mother was there. Right from day one, there was a chemistry mismatch between this lady and the mother-in-law. The mother-in-law was quite short tempered and for small small things, she would pass some comments. And she had some habits which was difficult for Lily to align with. She found her to be very like a dictator. She wanted things to happen in a particular way. So as days passed by, invariably there would be some altercations, some fights, some misunderstandings, some arguments between Lily and the mother-in-law. The husband in between tried to play the balancing act, tried to pacify Lily and also tried to orient his mother. But somehow as days passed by, things started deteriorating as far as the relationship is concerned. And a day came when this lady Lily became very frustrated, got very upset and she decided to even leave the house. As a last resort, she thought of her father's friend who was running a business, who was dealing with medicinal herbs. So she went to this person, Mr. Huang and said the whole thing, explained that how this lady is really getting on my nerves and it's very difficult to be with her, she is spoiling even my relationship with my husband, my married life is completely messed up, can you please help me? Mr. Huang heard her out and then said what do you want? She said in that house either she can live or I can live, both of us cannot live together. So can you help me? This time he was very upfront, he says can you help me get rid of her? So Huang was surprised, he says what do you want? He says can you give me some herbs? He said yes. He said yes. So Huang was surprised, he said yes. So Huang decided to help her. What he did was he gave her some herbs which are actually poisonous, which can eventually kill her. So Huang was quite surprised, nevertheless, since she has approached and she was quite concerned about what's going on in her personal life, he decided to help her. He went inside his office and came back with some herbs.

#### relationships, attitude, positive change
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ey9oyqy2WvM?t=872
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "These herbs will act very slowly all you have to do is in the meals you cook just little bit you add and administer this herbs and over a period of few months she will even die."
**Summary:** Mr. Huang gives Lily poisonous herbs to slowly administer to her mother-in-law, along with instructions to avoid arguments, be obedient, and focus on the positives, setting the stage for a transformation in their relationship.

He gave her some herbs. So Huang was so asked to help her. He gave her some herbs. What he did was he gave her some herbs. Packets of herbs he said i have got together these herbs these herbs yes they are poisonous but they will act very slowly over a period of few months maybe six months because if i give you something which will act immediately everyone without a doubt will have suspicion on you and you will be in problem because they know that you're not getting along and maybe you were instrumental in administering poison to her so this herbs will act very slowly all you have to do is in the meals you cook just little bit you add and administer this herbs and over a period of few months she will develop the poison in her body and she will even die she was ready to accept this doesn't matter even if it takes few months and mr huang also informed this lady that the next few months i have some instructions for you some do's and don'ts but while you cook the food and add these herbs you also have to follow this very important prescription i am your well-wisher and the prescription is that during these few months you should not get into any argument with your mother-in-law no argument point one point two be fully obedient. Whatever she says, whatever she wants, just respect her. And third, always look at the positives. Even if she is grumbling, even if she passes some comments, just be positive. Look at the positives, empathize with her, try to understand her perspective, try to understand what made her speak like this, give the benefit of doubt and stay positive, be grateful.

She is almost like my own daughter. I do not have a daughter but she is like a daughter, the relationship we have now is like between a mother and a daughter. I am very blessed, I am very fortunate to have her in our family. So they actually became very friendly, would spend lot of time together and just like a mother and a daughter, they became attached to each other.

But, if a very, very good computer, with good memory, with good RAM, with good hard disk, with very good processing abilities, is infected with viruses, it can mess up the whole thing. #### Bhagavad Gita, anxiety, spiritual life
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ey9oyqy2WvM?t=2076
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

**Summary:** The speaker continues to quote from the Gita Mahatmya, stating that reading the Bhagavad Gita can free one from anxieties and miseries, leading to a spiritual next life, and uses the example of Arjuna's initial state of mind in the Bhagavad Gita.

So here it says, one will be freed from all anxieties and miseries of life and one's next life will be spiritual. Just like in the Bhagavad Gita, Arjuna was inflicted, was inflicted with Dehatma Buddhi. His thoughts were all around I and mine, my family, my dynasty. What is the use of fighting this battle? What is the use of getting back the kingdom which will be tainted with the blood of my own near and dear ones? So everything was centered around I and mine. His thoughts were centered around I and mine. Then eventually at the end of first chapter, Krishna, Arjuna tells Krishna his situation and then says, my mind is reeling. I am unable to stand the bow, Gandiva is slipping from my hand. And then he eventually sits down, gives up his bow and says, Krishna, I will not fight. So after hearing all the words from Arjuna, Krishna tells, how come these impurities, these impurities have come upon you, impurities in your thinking, impurities in your perception. As Einstein said, the challenges we face cannot be solved by being at the same level of thinking we were when we created those problems.

#### Bhagavad Gita, transformation, perspective
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ey9oyqy2WvM?t=2174
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "What he did was, through the sublime instructions, he changed Arjuna's thinking, Arjuna's perception."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Krishna did not change Arjuna's circumstances but changed his thinking and perspective through the instructions of the Bhagavad Gita, inspiring him to fight and win the battle.

So eventually it boils down to thinking, our minds, the thoughts we produce. Now after hearing Arjuna, Krishna did not change the circumstances. Krishna did not tell Arjuna, okay, I will fight the battle for you, you relax, you take it easy. In fact, he inspired him to go ahead and do the same thing which was cause of anxiety for Arjuna. What he did was, through the sublime instructions, he changed Arjuna's thinking, Arjuna's perception. What he thought about, what he is about to engage in. What he thought about, the battle in which he is about to be part of. And by changing his thinking, his perspective changes, and then Arjuna stood up, fought valiantly and won the battle.

It's one stage even higher than being positive. So, that's what Gita Mahatmya is saying, that one's next life will be spiritual. If one reads Bhagavad Gita, this is Mahatmya number 2, verse number 2, Gita Mahatmya, if one reads Bhagavad Gita, very sincerely and seriously, many times we read it very mechanically as a matter of, you know, duty. If one reads Bhagavad Gita very sincerely and seriously, then by the grace of the Lord, the reactions of his past misdeeds, we suffer because of our past misdeeds, good or bad. If we are enjoying life, yes, it's a reaction of the good deeds we have done in the past, in this life or in our previous lives. Likewise, for the miseries we encounter, again, tattya nukampam sasumiksha māna bhunjāna evātmakṛtam vipākam It's my doing. The reactions of his past misdeeds will not act upon him.

#### Bhagavad Gita, protection, mind
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ey9oyqy2WvM?t=2375
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "This Bhagavad Gita can protect us from all these crazy thoughts, which can run through us."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of roasted seeds losing their potency to germinate to explain how reading the Gita can prevent past misdeeds from affecting us, and emphasizes that the Gita is more than just an antivirus, protecting us from crazy thoughts.

The example is given just like you might have seeds, but if you fry them in a frying pan, if you roast them, even though seeds may be good, but they lose the potency or power to germinate. After the seeds are roasted or you have fried them, you put them in a soil, give all the required conditions, the moisture, manure, make all other conditions conducive for the seed to grow, but it will not grow. So likewise, here in the Mahatma, it says, if one sincerely reads Bhagavad Gita with seriousness, then the reactions of his past misdeeds, many of the deeds we have done, we may not be even conscious of, we might have done unintentionally, but still the reactions are there. So here it says, reactions of such past misdeeds will not act upon a person who reads Gita daily, sincerely and seriously. So if we are equipped with this knowledge of Gita and we are reading Gita daily, it acts like, in fact, it's wrong to compare to antivirus, because we don't generally, you know, think very highly of antivirus. These days, lot of people say this antivirus, which are there in the market, the same company is probably producing viruses, and then they are marketing the software, it's a marketing gimmick. But this Bhagavad Gita can protect us from all these crazy thoughts, which can run through us.

**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa introduces the topic of controlling anger based on the teachings of the Bhagavad Gita, promising practical tips and techniques.

Hare Krishna! A warm welcome to all of you for today's Geeta Live session. The topic for the day is, Burn Anger Before Anger Burns You. If you remember, a couple of weeks back we had a topic, Ame Gussa Kio Aata Hai? And while we were discussing about anger, a lot of questions came forth about how to go about controlling our temper, more so in present day to day circumstances. We all get angry at times, sometimes situation can be very provoking. So today's session is all about how we can control our anger in such trying circumstances. I will share with you some tips, some techniques, some things which might work. And again, it's from the teachings of Bhagavad Gita. Krishna has given us many, many practical insights, how we can control our base tendencies, lust, greed, pride, anger, envy, jealousy. I will quote few of the verses from the Gita while I share these tips with you. So we will begin the session today with prayers to Lord Krishna.

---
#### anger, lust, Bhagavad Gita
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/0BaJirgBr3Y?t=290
**Verse References:** BG 2.62, BG 2.63, BG 3.37
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Why do we get angry? Because things don't happen the way we want to happen."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the origin of anger according to the Bhagavad Gita, tracing it back to lust and unfulfilled desires.

So today's discussion, again, like we have been doing in the past few sessions, I will divide it into subsections. In fact, five sections, to be very precise. First, we will try to understand from Gita the verses which Krishna directly talks about anger and control of anger. As I said, Bhagavad Gita is full of practical insights. In fact, it's like a manual which guides us how we go about leading our lives. Then we will talk about the tips, some things which we can do in our day-to-day lives to control ourselves, to control our anger in case situation arises where we lose our temper, where we are about to lose our temper. So we will talk about that. In fact, we have four such important sections. I don't want to start describing those sections now. So four sections deal with some four practical insights how we can manage or control our temper before it starts damaging us from within. So this is quite a lot. So this is quite a recap of what we had discussed in our previous session. How does anger arise? This is in the second chapter of the Bhagavad Gita, 62nd verse, where Krishna says, while contemplating the objects of the senses, dhyayato vishayan pumsa, sanghaste shupajayate, we develop attachment. We contemplate on the objects of the senses and we develop attachment. And from attachment, lust develops. And lust means desire to be an independent enjoyer, desire to enjoy my senses, desire to enjoy my mind, intellectual pleasures, mental pleasures. dhyayato vishaya pumsa, sanghaste shupajayate, sanghatsanjayate kama, lust. And lust, when the desires do not get fulfilled, things don't transpire the way I want things to happen. People don't behave the way I want them to behave for my pleasure, for my satisfaction, for my peace of mind. Anger arises. Why do we get angry? Because things don't happen the way we want to happen. So lust leads to anger. And then Krishna, the next verse, 63rd verse Krishna says, from anger, delusion arises. What happens when we become angry? Delusion. Bewilderment of memory. We lose our power of discrimination. What we have to speak, what should we not speak, what we should do, what we should not do, that discrimination, that ability to discern, we lose. That's why at times when we get angry, we speak certain words which later on, we regret. From anger, delusion arises. And from delusion, bewilderment of memory. Memory is bewildered. All good sense what we have is lost momentarily. When memory is lost, intelligence is bewildered. One falls down again into material pool. And then in the third chapter Krishna says, 37th verse, it is lust only, Arjuna, which is born in contact with the material mode of passion, which is all devouring sinful enemy of the living entity. This karma, which unfulfilled leads to anger, is the greatest enemy of the living entity. We have also covered about lust in one of our previous sessions where we talked about root cause of all our problems is lusty desires. Karma.

---
#### self-control, yoga, happiness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/0BaJirgBr3Y?t=588
**Verse References:** BG 5.23
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Before giving up the present body if one is able to tolerate the urges of material senses and check the force of desire and anger, he is a yogi and happy in this world."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Bhagavad Gita 5.23, emphasizing the importance of tolerating material urges and controlling desire and anger to achieve happiness and become a yogi.

So, now, no doubt each one of us have different degrees of lusty desires. We are not completely pure. We are on the path. We are treading the path whereby we will get purified in due course of time. We all have base qualities like I talked about lust, greed, pride, envy, anger, all these qualities which we know are not good for us. It is there. So, how we go about dealing with them while we are on the path of purification. So, here Krishna in the Gita, 5th chapter says, this is 23rd verse, before giving up this present body, if one is able to tolerate the urges of material senses and check the forces of desire and anger. See, it's a force. Anger is a force. That's why many times people when they get angry they they lose control over themselves momentarily. It's a force. They know I shouldn't be getting angry. They know it's not good for me. So, before giving up the present body that means in this very life if one is able to tolerate the urges of material senses, different desires which keeps popping up, urges of the material senses and check the force of desire and anger, he is a yogi and happy in this world. See, we all want to be happy. All of us striving for things, situations, achievements which will make us happy. So, here Krishna is saying, before giving up the present body if one is able to tolerate the urges of material senses and check the force of desire and anger, he is a yogi and he is happy in this world.

---
#### Krishna consciousness, purification, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/0BaJirgBr3Y?t=722
**Verse References:** BG 4.10
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Being free from attachment, fear, anger and fully absorbed in me, taking refuge in me, many many people in the past have become purified by knowledge of me."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces practical tips for controlling anger, emphasizing that the process of Krishna consciousness, taking refuge in the Lord, and gaining knowledge of Him are essential for purification.

So, now we will talk about some practical tips what we can do to help control our temper or not falling prey to anger which really burns us from within. Krishna in the Gita again 4th chapter 10th verse says Being freed from attachment, fear and anger, being fully absorbed in Me and taking refuge in Me, many many people in the past have become purified by knowledge of Me. Here it's very important for us to know, Krishna is giving us some important insights how we can purify ourselves of all these base qualities. No doubt we can have some quick fix solutions. Just like, there are some quick fix solutions even if you don't know them. But, there are some quick fix solutions even if you don't know them. So, like there are some quick fix solutions even for our health. If suppose we are having fever, we can take some crocin, paracetamol tablets. If we have some pain in the body, we can take some painkillers. It gives us some symptomatic relief. But mind you, it is not addressing the root cause. So likewise, there are many many books, many many articles, many many videos on anger management. Believe me or not, they are more like quick fixes. You can count 10 to 1. You can take a deep breath. You can leave the situation. All that helps. All these techniques can be made use of. But they are not addressing the root cause. And here in this particular verse, 4th chapter, 10th verse, Krishna is giving us a very practical insight. Being free from attachment, fear, anger and fully absorbed in me. Taking refuge in me. So the process of Krishna consciousness is all about taking refuge in the Lord. Absorbed in consciousness of the Lord. International Society for Krishna Consciousness. Many many persons in the past, what happens if we are conscious of the Lord? Take refuge of the Lord. What happens? Many many persons in the past have become purified by the knowledge of me. And thus they have attained transcendental love for me. In other words, if we have to get purified, we have to have knowledge about the Lord. We have to take refuge of the Lord. We have to become conscious of the Lord. This will attack the problem at the root. It will not necessarily give us some symptomatic relief. Refuge of the Lord, being conscious of the Lord and Krishna is saying having knowledge of me, many many people in the past have got purified by the knowledge of me.

#### forgiveness, humility, scriptural perspective
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/0BaJirgBr3Y?t=942
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Vishnu is completely seeing this act from another different perspective."
**Summary:** The speaker shares the story of Bhrugu Muni testing the Trinity, highlighting Lord Vishnu's reaction of humility and forgiveness when kicked, illustrating the importance of scriptural perspective.

Let me share with you story of Bhrugu Muni. I am sure all of you have heard about Bhrugu Muni. He is son of Lord Brahma. So Bhrugu Muni was assigned the task by the Devatas to figure out who among the trinity Brahma, Vishnu and Mahesh Lord Shiva who among the three are full of mode of goodness. So Bhrugu Muni first decided to go to Lord Brahma. Bhrugu Muni is son of Lord Brahma. So as soon as he reached the Brahmaloka to meet his father Lord Brahma, he deliberately did not pay his obeisances as soon as he reached and was about to meet Lord Brahma. Generally it is an etiquette when a son or a disciple meets the spiritual master or the father he pays obeisances and offers some prayers out of respect. So deliberately he did not offer any respect offer any prayers and just walked into the assembly. In today's time more or less it has become quite common but it was quite unnatural and moment Brahma saw that it was an act of impudence disrespect so he became very angry how can Bhrugu do like this come into the assembly and not pay respect not pay obeisances so he became very angry and wanted to curse Bhrugu Muni but somehow he checked his anger controlled himself and let go. It was not at all expected from Bhrugu Muni to act in that way as far as Brahma is concerned. Next Bhrugu Muni went to see Lord Shiva in Mahesh Dham Lord Shiva was sitting with Mother Parvati and as soon as Bhrugu Muni entered there Lord Shiva got up from his seat and moved towards Bhrugu Muni to embrace him out of love but as we all know Lord Shiva is his body is clad with ashes apparently looks like he is quite unclean so as soon as Bhrugu Muni approached Lord Shiva and Lord Shiva got up and came forward to embrace Bhrugu Muni Bhrugu Muni stepped back he says no no no you are quite unclean your body is covered with ashes it's okay it's okay and he was quite reluctant to get embraced by Lord Shiva now mind you offence to a person can happen in thought that means in your mind it can happen in words you can speak bad words and offend a person or it can happen in action so the first offence what Bhrugu Muni did to Brahma was in the mind not paying respects offending him in his mind but not offering prayers as far as offending Lord Shiva is concerned it was in words saying that you are unclean and stepped back and not allowed Lord Shiva to embrace him welcome him Lord Shiva became very angry and wanted to kill Bhrugu Muni but somehow Mother Parvati forbade Lord Shiva to get angry and kill Bhrugu Muni and somehow she let him go then Bhrugu Muni went to Shweta Deepa to meet Lord Vishnu so in Shweta Deepa Lord Vishnu was lying on the bed of flowers served by Mother Lakshmi and as soon as Bhrugu Muni reached there deliberately because he was trying to figure out who among the trinity Brahma, Vishnu and Mahesh was totally in mode of goodness full of all good qualities greatest among three so he deliberately this time went straight to Lord Vishnu and kicked him on his chest mind you I mentioned offence can be in the mind it can be in words if you compare all three offences in the mind it's relatively lesser compared to in the words and even higher than that is offence in action so here Bhrugu Muni went straight to Lord Vishnu and kicked him on his chest as soon as he kicked him on the chest it was natural that Lord Vishnu was angry it can happen with anyone suppose somebody comes and hits us naturally we will become angry but instead of becoming angry Lord Vishnu started smiling and said the following words let us note down the words which Vishnu said first he said O gentle Brahmana he is referring to Bhrugu Muni you have come all the way to my place looks like I have made some mistake in my receiving you O great Brahmana looks like I have offended you kindly forgive me for my mistake this is the first statement which Lord Vishnu made then he said great sages like you great Brahmanas like you when they visit places of pilgrimage they can even sanctify purify the places of pilgrimage generally we go to places of pilgrimage to get purified but the places of pilgrimage themselves get purified by the presence of great sages so Vishnu says that such great sages such great sage like you have come to my place to Vaikuntha whole place will get purified just by your presence how fortunate I am to have you here with me with us third thing Vishnu said that your lotus feet your feet are like soft lotus flowers and my chest is like a thunderbolt and you have kicked on my chest I hope it has not hurt your lotus feet I am sorry if it has hurt you if my chest has hurt your lotus feet they might have got hurt and fourth thing he said I am so blessed I am so fortunate that you have hit on my chest my chest is a place where Lakshmi Devi resides and we all know Lakshmi Devi is chanchala chanchala means is quite flickering that's why somebody is blessed with Lakshmi today and next day it's gone a rich man becomes a pauper and a pauper can become a rich man we hear a lot of stories like that so chanchala Lakshmi Devi is chanchala so Vishnu says that you have hit on my chest where Lakshmi Devi resides now my chest has got sanctified by the touch of your lotus feet now Lakshmi Devi will not touch my chest now let us understand yes Vishnu can get angry yes Bhrigu Muni went and hit the chest with his leg but Vishnu is completely seeing this act from another different perspective just like it is said you can see the glass half empty you can also see the glass half full so different situations which arise in our life different circumstances different things which people do to us which can irritate us which can arouse anger in us can be seen from different perspectives we generally have one perspective but there are many perspectives ideally it is recommended that we have shastra perspective perspective of our scriptures perspective of great sages perspective of Lord Shri Krishna and based on that perspective we act here I am not trying to justify and say what Bhrigu did was completely justified and was right I am just giving an example that how we can have a different perspective just like Vishnu here is sharing four different perspectives like Vishnu is saying you have come and sanctified this place I hope your feet is not hurt because of having kicked my chest now that my chest has been touched by your feet it has got sanctified

when problems arise we can curse God again depends on the perspective we have we can consider it as a blessing in disguise so that's why it is very very important here Krishna is using the word knowledge of me knowledge of shastras knowledge of Bhagavad Gita it is very very important if we have that perspective of life we will not be bewildered buddhi naasho that's why Krishna says a sober person is not bewildered by such a change when death happens a person who is not having transcendental knowledge is completely shattered is completely rattled whereas Krishna is saying one who has this knowledge who has that shastra perspective a sober person is not bewildered by such a change so many situations which can bewilder us which can completely rattle us, which can completely shake us which can completely raise anger in us will lose its venom if we have scriptural perspective to our lives to that particular act so here he says we cannot control the force of our anger therefore when we look at material things we cannot avoid feeling attraction or repulsion for them but the supreme lord is never affected in that way the lord is transcendental therefore one who desires to conquer the force of senses must take shelter of the lotus feet of the lord must take shelter of the lord refuge of the lord be conscious of the lord follow the instructions of the lord then he will be victorious

it is said that lord Vishnu somehow forgave Bhrigu Muni but Lakshmi Devi she became very angry because how dare he hit chest of her beloved lord it seems that from that day onwards Lakshmi Devi is not very favorably disposed to the brahmanas that's why you see many times brahmanas they may be devotees of Vishnu but they may not be very wealthy they may be in poverty stricken state it seems that Lakshmi Devi is angry at such brahmanas how dare he went and hit lord Vishnu so the first thing which we can keep in mind is the process of Krishna consciousness reading of Bhagavad Gita chanting of the holy names of the lord trying to remember the lord think of the lord be conscious of the lord this process this path what we are treading will purify us it may not be immediate solution it is not that in a situation when you get angry it will take time the process of purification will take time we are attacking a root problem lusty desires all the base qualities which have deeply rooted within us it takes time for us to get rid of them but this process will work Krishna is saying many many devotees in the past have got purified by knowledge of me so that's why Prabhupad insists that we daily read his books read Bhagavad Gita chant the holy names of the lord it will surely purify us if suppose somebody gets angry very often if one treads this path follows the path of Krishna consciousness he will surely in due course of time see that he is able to check the forces of anger

**Summary:** The speaker narrates the story of Sant Eknath, who remained calm and compassionate even after being spat on 108 times, illustrating the devotee's perspective on life.

once there was a sage by name Eknath I am sure all of you have heard about he was in Maharashtra Sant Eknath so he was quite well known for his saintly qualities his saintly behaviour was very equiposed very peaceful very calm always engaged in chanting the names of the lord his bhajans kirtans preaching the glories of the lord very devoted and he became that way quite famous lot of people from far off villages would come meet him to seek his blessings offer obeisances to him so one time a person who became envious of Eknath he was a very rich zamindar he became envious of Eknath that how he is becoming so famous I have lot of money I have lot of wealth but still people don't come to me to seek my blessings to meet me but here this sage has practically nothing he is just engaged in chanting the names of lord pulling the public and people are coming and falling at his feet so he hired a man and said somehow you make this person get angry I will pay you thousands of rupees I don't know the exact amount but somehow he was promised a huge sum of money you make this Eknath so called so called sadhu he is a fake baba he is acting as if he is a great devotee somehow let him show his true colours let him become angry generally sages are you know known not to get angry very calm very peaceful so this person was hired so one day Eknath was coming back from the river after having taken the bath and on his way back home when he was returning this person was hiding behind the bush and as soon as Eknath passed that way he got up and spit at Eknath so Eknath looked at him out of compassion he could have got angry but out of compassion maybe there is some compulsion some way he has offended this person he did not shout back did not get angry went back to the river holy river took a bath and again walked back home again the second time the man came intercepted him on the way and again spat at him again Eknath went back took the bath and again came back this process believe me or not happened 108 times 108 times Eknath went back to the river at the holy dip again was walking back to the home and this person would come and spit at him after 108 times this person's heart melted he came and fell at the feet of Eknath he says please forgive me I am such a cruel man such a sinful man for some petty gains I dare to do such an innoble act of offending such a great devotee like you actually my family is completely poverty stricken my children have not eaten food for many days this person promised me huge sum of money and I got lured by it forgive me pardon me for having the impudence of having acted like this offended such great sage like you so Eknath got up and said please do not worry my friend in fact I feel that I am such a fortunate person today I got an opportunity to take bath in the holy river 108 times how blessed I am I got an opportunity because of you to take bath 108 times second thing Eknath also mentioned I am so sorry if you had hinted me if you had told me that you are in poverty and you have been promised with such huge amount you will get such huge amount just that I become angry you should have told me you should have hinted me I would have got angry much earlier I would have acted as if I have got angry so that you could have got that money much easier I made you wait for so long I am sorry again I am not saying that this is kind of a situation where ideal devotees great devotees who have completely conquered their all base qualities their false ego they act in this way but just to give a sense of how great devotees have a different perspective to life says that I am so blessed that I got an opportunity to take bath 108 times and then he says you could have hinted me I would have acted as if I have got angry you shouldn't have to wait for so long I made you wait for so long

**Key Quote:** "We are on the path of purification."
**Summary:** The speaker addresses a question about devotees who still get angry, explaining that purification is a process, and comparing it to a hospital where patients are cured over time.

one time I was talking about anger management and how great devotees by practicing the process of Krishna consciousness they can control their base qualities so one lady in the audience got up and said Swamiji I know one person who has been practicing Krishna consciousness for last 5 years he chants and this and that but he is very angry gets very angry on one side you are saying that by following this process of Krishna consciousness by chanting the names of the lord by reading Bhagavad Gita you will get purified and you can control all your base qualities other side I see a person who is chanting and doing all these things and he is short tempered and she asked this question in front of about 150 odd people sitting for a moment I started sweating I am suggesting something as a solution and here the lady is standing in front of everyone and saying the solution won't work and she has evidence she is saying that I know a person who is a devotee who is chanting and who is reading Gita who is apparently visiting temple attending satsang and he is getting angry he is short tempered so how do we reconcile yes at times we might encounter devotees who are short tempered I will explain why first and foremost we are on the path of purification it is not that somebody becomes a devotee joins the mission after he has become totally pure all of us with whatever qualities we have just like a person joins the hospital to get cured of his disease different people in the hospital are diseased to different degree yes the very fact that he has joined the hospital and is taking medication will help him cure in due course of time that is point one second thing even in the hospital at times a person might enter depending on severity of the disease it may take time for him to get cured sometimes a person is admitted in a hospital for months together at time year together for example Rishi Kapoor when he went to the US for his treatment of leukemia cancer he was there in the hospital for about a year and then he came back again he was in the hospital so some diseases you are in the hospital for a year or so or even more just because and in spite of being in the hospital many times a person succumbed to the disease we cannot say the hospital was bad we cannot say the doctors were not good we cannot say the medicines were not good there are so many factors which come into play to decide the end result you may have the best of doctors like present in covid case a lot of people who are getting admitted in the hospitals the doctors are trying their best they may have good ICU support they may have ventilators but in spite of that a person may not survive we cannot blame the doctors in a school you may have the same teacher the same course book the same classroom the same tutorials one person gets 95% marks another person fails with maybe 35% marks we can't blame the school we can't blame the teachers Krishna in the Bhagavad Gita is saying many many persons in the past have got purified by following this process taking refuge in me by knowledge of me and controlling the urges of mind and senses have got completely purified Bhagavad Gita is not a some some philosophy which some person has written some 100-200 years back 5000 years back Krishna spoke this knowledge of Bhagavad Gita and in the Bhagavad Gita Krishna spoke I spoke this knowledge to Sun God Vivaswan millions of years ago this is an authorized knowledge it's an authorized process just that it may take little time depending on how badly we are overcome overpowered by lusty desires by attachments by mode of passion we all are diseased to different degrees just like a fan when you switch off it doesn't come to a halt immediately depending on at what speed it was rotating it will take time for it to halt it was if it was rotating at number 5 very fast it will take a longer time for it to come to a halt so that way this process is authorized for sure we will get purified of these base qualities just that we have to be patient with faith with conviction Krishna had said so Prabhupad has said devotees are saying I will get purified one should follow this process and yes purification will happen

another thing which we all can do which we all can strive for is a sattvic lifestyle or things which will increase sattva guna in us there was a lady by name Anushka Mehta this lady was working as a marketing manager in a very big MNC and very fast paced lifestyle she would have lot of meetings she would rush for the office in the morning have breakfast on the way and obviously when you are fast paced lifestyle you compromise on all your things no exercises and then on the way on the move you are eating at times you are skipping your breakfast at times you are skipping the lunch meetings are unscheduled throughout the day and she was known for her bad temper small small things would irritate her she would get angry and many times her colleagues will leave the job attrition was very high in the office she tried she got fed up because there was lot of feedback coming in from different forces that her temperament her attitude is not good and it started dawning on her also that she has to really improve otherwise it is going to very badly hit her career so she started even visiting some counsellors taking some counselling sessions started you know reading some books on managing anger controlling anger but nothing seemed to be working and as I mentioned because of fast paced life her lifestyle was completely out of sync she would be eating all kinds of you know burgers pizzas she knew that she is eating all kinds of junk food just gave a suggestion and having tried different things and nothing was working out as a last resort she asked her mother tell me what should I do what should I do to improve and the mother generally mothers are quite wise they know what's good for us based on the wisdom based on the experience so she suggested some changes in her lifestyle in her eating habits requested her packed lunch from the home she personally cooked right kind of dishes lot of salads lot of sattvic foods home cooked food in the evening requested her to come back home ourselves from the grips of tamo guna raja guna and elevate to sattva guna sattvic aahar the right kind of foods foods which are sattvic in nature if we eat will also have the right kind of is solution to all our problems Krishna may not directly talk about the problem of depression may not directly talk about problem of greed may not directly give you a solution about but it is talking about the root of all of our problems because of passion there is attachment because of attachment there is lust because of lust there is anger because of anger there is bewilderment because of memory because of bewilderment there is loss of intelligence how we can by practicing these instructions principles which Krishna is sharing with us gradually elevate from tamo guna to raja guna to sattva guna and transcend even sattva guna to shuddha sattva and when we are situated in sattva guna and shuddha sattva automatically we will be in a good position to control the urges of our mind urges of our tongue urges of forces of desire forces of anger and we will be

#### self-control, emotional regulation, relationships
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/0BaJirgBr3Y?t=3705
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "When we are getting angry in that moment, check if we can have a good sense to press the pause button, extricate from that situation and come back and handle the situation with a cool head."
**Summary:** The speaker shares two practical tips: avoid settling scores or dealing with situations when angry, and do not brew hatred or ill feelings against someone who has offended you.

Now last two tips, which I would like to share before we end the discussion today and take up some questions This is something which is Which is very at the ground level At times we will get angry We will lose temper Somehow at that time we should have good sense tends to not Settle scores Not deal with situation because at that time. We are bewildered memory is lost Intelligence is bewildered So many times when we get angry or Minutes together for hours together. We are dealing with situation in an angry mood Try ask for as possible be extricate from that situation if we know that as possible, we extricate from you're getting angry try for that moment if you can step out of that room go to some other place if you are an email somebody has sent an email that has irritated you do not reply to that email in that moment relax calm down let that time pass drink some water this is very very important one time i was taking a class and in the class somebody asked me a question and i was talking about gurus and by mistake i happened to take name of a saintly person a guru who is quite famous but may not be necessarily genuine as far as what he's talking about so in that you audience there was one person who was follower of who was quote unquote a very staunch follower of that guru although i did not blaspheme but definitely i somehow spoke lowly of that person to the extent saying that he is not genuine so this person got offended and in the whole assembly you know an argument ensued he started saying how can you say that and then i started talking and in between i became quite forceful and even lost my temper and because i have knowledge of geeta i somehow could defeat him in the argument i could defeat him in front of everyone so i won the argument but i lost the battle and after the class journey we used to have some

## HG Amitasana Dasa

### Be An Instrument in the Hands of Lord | Bhagavad Gita 11.31 - 11.37 | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### Bhagavad Gita, Universal Form, Inquiry
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NJ8dSEFQ9SI?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "O Lord of Lords, so fierce of form, please tell me who you are. I do not know what your mission is and I desire to hear of it."
**Summary:** Arjuna, upon seeing the Vishwaroopa, asks Krishna about his identity and the purpose of this form.

So, we are covering text number 31 to 37 today from the 11th chapter of Bhagavad Gita as it is, based on teachings of Srila Prabhupada. We will chant these words together. Please repeat after me. We will go word by word first. Aakyahi meko bhavan ugra rupo. Namostu te devavara prasida. Vignatum ichchami bhavantamadyam. Nahi prajnami tavapravityam. Translation. O Lord of Lords, so fierce of form, please tell me who you are. I offer my obeisances unto you. Please be gracious to me. I do not know what your mission is and I desire to hear of it. These are the words spoken by Arjuna on seeing Vishwaroopa or the universal form. So, he is saying, O Lord of Lords, so fierce of form, please tell me who you are. What he is seeing right in front of him is the universal form which is gigantic with all the devatas and all the people gushing into the mouths, very fearsome form. I offer my obeisances unto you. Please be gracious on me. I do not know what your mission is and I desire to hear of it. I do not know what your mission is, what this form is all about. Why have you given me this vision? I desire to hear of it.

#### Mission, Time, Destruction
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NJ8dSEFQ9SI?t=130
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Time I am, destroyer of the worlds and I have come to engage all people."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that everything has a purpose, including the Lord, and that the Lord's energy of time devours everything.

So, now, when we all come here on Sunday, we all assemble, or for that matter, any activity we do, there is a purpose. We don't do anything whimsically. Even for a company, you know, generally the directors or the promoters sit together and then they do some bit of thinking on what is the mission, what's the vision, what's the values. And if the company is aligned to the mission and vision, then they say that it is in alignment, otherwise mission is something else, doing something else. So Lord also has a mission, this purpose. This world is created for a purpose. Now Arjuna is inquisitive to ask, I am interested to know what your mission is, what is all this about. Text 32, the blessed Lord said, time I am, destroyer of the worlds and I have come to engage all people with the exception of you, the Pandavas. All the soldiers here on both sides will be slain. Who developed or invented the atomic bomb? Anyone has an answer? Atomic bomb, atom bomb. Robert Oppenheimer, okay. So he quoted this verse when the first time the bomb was actually executed, time I am the destroyer of everything. So Lord has a very important energy which is called kala. Kala means color doesn't mean black, kala means time. And nature of time is it devours. Devours means now you see anything in this world, you take a new car, it's brand new, lustrous, very, very good to look at. Time devours. After 10 years, after 15 years, after 20 years, after 25 years, a time comes you have to junk it. Buildings, very nice, nicely built, 30 years, 40 years, 50 years, they demolish. Time I am, that Kala is the energy of the Lord.

#### Surrender, Devotion, Fearlessness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NJ8dSEFQ9SI?t=300
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The true security is in understanding that Lord is with me. That's why a devotee actually is fearless."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the necessity of surrendering to the Lord, willingly or unwillingly, and how a devotee willingly surrenders, finding true security and fearlessness in the Lord's protection.

Now, every one of us have to surrender to the Lord, knowingly, unknowingly, willingly or unwillingly. So, one time, a person said, why I have to surrender? I don't surrender to God. I don't believe in God. Why I should surrender? Why Krishna is demanding surrender to me? So, Prabhupada said, in fact, you have no choice. When old age comes, you have to surrender. You cannot say, I will not surrender to old age. I will not become old. When diseases come, you have to surrender. When death comes, you have to surrender. Mrityu sarva haras chaham. Krishna is saying Mrityu. Mrityu means death, all devouring death. Sarva haras means it devours everything. Chaham, I am that. Now, I will explain as we move forward. Sometimes, it may appear that, you know, this is instilling fear in us. The Lord is saying, I am the death. I am fear personified. Surrender to me, otherwise you will be killed. Let's understand the context. So, everyone has to surrender. Now, what a devotee does is, he willingly surrenders. He accepts the supremacy of the Lord. Accepts that, yes, we are subordinates. Say to the Lord, yes, you are the master. Yes, you are the controller. Yes, we are controlled. Yes, we are not all powerful. We have some powers. We have some abilities. We can control few things. But you are the all powerful. You are the supreme controller. So, that way, with this knowledge and natural consequences, the devotee becomes humble and his dependency on the Lord increases. He depends on the Lord with the understanding that, if the Lord protects, I am safe. Not because I have lot of money. Not because I have lot of strength. If I have the mercy and grace of the Lord, I am safe. If I don't have the mercy of the Lord, in spite of having everything, I am not safe. What happens is, this is the meaning of illusion or Maya. When we have money, bank balance, near and dear ones, well-wishers around us, there is a sense of, I am safe, security. The true security is in understanding that Lord is with me. That's why a devotee actually is fearless.

#### Security, Illusion, Material Attachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NJ8dSEFQ9SI?t=488
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "We all are looking for stability, security, sense of steadiness, and we are trying to secure ourselves through various means."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that people seek security through various means like wealth, health, and relationships, but these are ultimately temporary and illusory.

He knows Lord is with me, therefore I am safe, not because of my money. And that's the reason why in spite of ups and downs, money is there, not there, the devotee has an equanimity, steadiness, because he knows Lord will surely protect me. We all are looking for stability, security, sense of steadiness, and we are trying to secure ourselves through various means. Bhagavatam says we all have different means to secure ourselves. We all have, we build just like a king to secure ourselves, or for that matter country to secure ourselves, we build the armed forces, we buy weaponry, we buy ammunitions. Now in newspaper you are hearing, India is spending billions of dollars to secure ourselves. So Bhagavatam says we all have a sainiya or army around us, which we build around us. What is that army consisting of? Let's contribute. That army is built of what? Which gives us a sense of security. That's the first thing. First is Deha. Deha means body. Today, for good reason, nothing wrong about it, people are very health conscious. One hour, two hours in the gym. Why? If I have a good, strong, healthy body, diseases won't attack me. I am safe. Isn't it? That's the first thing. Now, what is the consciousness? Why are we doing what we are doing? If I have a strong and stout body, I can ward off diseases. I will not become old easily. I can, to some extent, defer aging. So it gives me a sense of security. I don't have any ailment. I am sixty five. I am healthy, by the Lord's grace. We say it – Lord's grace. So janpayya ileesi, we say Lord's grace. So, we pursue health. It gives me security. I am good health, good body, strong, stout, healthy. Deha, then Aapta. Aapta means we build network around us of friends, relatives, acquaintances, so that I am safe. They will help me. Sometimes we help others also because today I am helping, tomorrow when I need, they will be there to help me. Otherwise, if I don't help me, sometimes even in our so-called selfless action, there is a selfish motive. Yes or no? Even our so-called selfless action, even helping others, there is a selfish motive. And the background could be my own security. Deha, Aapta, Kalat Radishu. Kalat Radishu.

#### Illusion, Death, Dependence on Lord
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NJ8dSEFQ9SI?t=692
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "You may have the best of doctors, the best of medicines, but if the Lord does not protect, the medicines will not be able to protect you."
**Summary:** The speaker continues discussing the illusory nature of material security, emphasizing that even with the best resources, one is helpless without the Lord's protection, and that a devotee understands this and builds dependence on the Lord.

Kalat means my wife, my family, my children, the near and dear ones. So, Bhagavatam says, Atma Saineshu Asat Swapi. This Atma Sainya, which we build around us for our security, it's Asat. Asat means it is not permanent, it is not enduring, it will not really protect us. Asat Swapi. Tesham Pramatta Nidhanam Pashyana Peena Pashyati. Tesham, seeing all that, Pramatta, Pramatta means maddened, intoxicated. Tesham Pramatta Nidhanam, Nidhanam means eventual death. Nidhan, Nidhan means to death. Tesham Pramatta, Pramatta means when we are in illusion with that. Tesham Pramatta, Nidhanam, Pashyana, Pashyana means to see. Pashyati, Pashyana, Pashyana Peena Pashyati. Although we know that death is inevitable, but we are not able to see. Because I have all this, because I am now secure, I have near and dear ones, I have money, I have bank balance, I have doctors around me, I have an insurance policy, I have LIC, I have all the people who will support me. Tesham Pramatta Nidhanam Pashyati. Tesham Pramatta Nidhanam, Nidhanam, Pashyana Peena Pashyati are not able to see. And that's why that real dependence on the Lord does not awaken, because faith is in our money, faith is in our near and dear ones, faith is that they will protect me. And whole life we are building that Saini around us, we are investing on that Saini. I am secure now. Now Bhagavatam says you may have the best of doctors, the best of medicines, but if the Lord does not protect, the medicines will not be able to protect you. And many times in our lives, we see real life examples. Isn't it? Life teaches us. We have everything, the best of doctors, the best of medicines, the best of money to support us, but we are helpless. Right in front of eyes, somebody near and dear ones is suffering, suffering, suffering and finally dies. So, a devotee, understanding that he, no doubt, as a matter of duty, it's not that money is not important, it's not that near and dear ones are not important, but he simultaneously understands that my real security is the Lord and he builds that dependency.
